- **`plan text`** (default `'free'`): the plan label the space is on (free / starter / pro / business / white-label), surfaced cheaply and ridden on the JWT alongside `space_id` ([ENTITY-SPACES-SYSTEM.md](ENTITY-SPACES-SYSTEM.md) §5.2) so RLS and the resolver read it without a join.
- **`entitlements jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'`**: the per-space feature-gate bag (e.g. `crm`, `email`, `gamification`, `white_label`, `native_app`). A new capability is **one key**, never a migration ([ENTITY-SPACES-SYSTEM.md](ENTITY-SPACES-SYSTEM.md) §1.4). The resolver reads entitlements once per request.

Defaults make every existing row a Networked, free, no-add-ons space, so the root keeps behaving exactly as today. Mode changes (Networked <-> Private <-> White-Label) become **flag edits**, never data migrations: the rows never move, only which shared seams the space's events flow into and which chrome renders ([ENTITY-SPACES-SYSTEM.md](ENTITY-SPACES-SYSTEM.md) §1.5).

**Scope / non-goals.** These three columns only. Adding the new `event_space` value to the `spaces.type` CHECK is a separate decision (owner gate D-2). Custom-domain / TLS ops (`custom_domains`) and space-level billing (`space_subscriptions`) are later-phase tables ([ENTITY-SPACES-SYSTEM.md](ENTITY-SPACES-SYSTEM.md) §4.2). This ADR does not specify the entitlement key catalog; keys are added per feature as they ship.

**Consequences.** The mode spectrum (isolation + branding) is expressible from the `spaces` row alone, read once into the JWT. Feature gating is a jsonb key, so the schema stays stable as the dream-suite grows. The security boundary is identical in every mode (mode changes only visibility and branding, never `space_id` + RLS). Documented in [DATABASE.md](DATABASE.md) ("Entity Spaces (tenancy)").

## ADR-323: The entity profile is the Detail template composed from registered modules, typed by a role blueprint, at `/spaces/<slug>` (per-entity layout scope `space -> route -> section -> global`)

**Status:** Accepted · Phase 1 / Epics 1.4 + 1.5 of entity-spaces ([ENTITY-SPACES-BUILD.md](ENTITY-SPACES-BUILD.md) §B.1 to §B.5, [ENTITY-SPACES-SYSTEM.md](ENTITY-SPACES-SYSTEM.md) §2.2). Shipped on the feature branch. Resolves the two ADR placeholders the build doc reserved (per-entity layout scope; the canonical profile route).

**Context.** An entity profile (a practitioner, studio, non-profit, coaching academy) needed a home in-app. The options were a bespoke profile layout / second card system, or composing the existing page kit. A bespoke layout would have duplicated the header/card/grid primitives, diverged in polish, and made "change a module once, every profile updates" impossible. The kit already renders Circles, Channels, Events, and member Profiles from the same `Detail` template + a module registry.

**Decision.** A profile **is** the existing `Detail` template (`components/templates/detail-template.tsx`): a context band (brand logo + name, a `spaces.type` badge, a streamed `StatCard` strip, a dynamic primary CTA, the blueprint's tab row) over a tabbed module body. The body is a set of **registered entity modules** (`components/widgets/entity/*`: `entity-about`, `entity-stats`, `entity-offerings`, `entity-practices`, `entity-community`, `entity-team`, `entity-cta`), each a self-fetching Server Component that reads only the active Space's own rows. A **role blueprint** (`lib/spaces/blueprints.ts`, the data descriptor per ENTITY-SPACES-SYSTEM §2.2) selects, per `spaces.type`, which tabs/modules/CTA/stats render. The canonical in-app route is **`/spaces/<slug>`** (`getVisibleSpaceBySlug` resolves it visibility-aware; Private spaces 404 for non-members with no existence leak). The active Space is threaded to the parameterless modules via a request-scoped context (`lib/spaces/active-space.ts`, `React.cache`), and the per-entity **layout scope** extends the cascade to `space -> route -> section -> global` (the `space` layer is `page_settings` keyed by `space_id`, ADR of Epic 0.5). `/spaces/<slug>` registers as a `'scoped'` rail in `lib/layout/page-chrome.ts`; the bare `/spaces` directory stays `'global'`; `/spaces/new` + `/spaces/<slug>/settings` are `'none'` (Focus).

**Consequences.** "Looks good" and "easy to change site-wide" are both **structural**: a module is defined once and updates every profile of every type; a new role is a blueprint descriptor, not a new layout. Adding `event_space` needs no new route, only a blueprint entry. Unknown/unregistered types fail closed to an About-only blueprint. Wave A shipped the Practitioner blueprint; Wave B added Business/Organization/Coaching reusing the same seven modules (ADR carried in the build doc §B.3). Tradeoff: modules are parameterless by the registry contract, so the active-Space request context is load-bearing: a module rendered outside a profile route returns `null` rather than guessing.

## ADR-324: Entity-Space write side: `spaces.about`/`tagline`, the create wizard + owner Focus settings, brand accent as a validated DAWN token, and the per-space Vera draft seam

**Status:** Accepted · Phase 1 / Epics 1.6 + 1.7 + the AI seam of entity-spaces ([ENTITY-SPACES-BUILD.md](ENTITY-SPACES-BUILD.md) §B.6). Shipped on the feature branch.

**Context.** Wave A made profiles render read-only. Owners then needed to (a) create a Space, (b) edit its identity, and (c) get AI help drafting copy, without a free-form page builder (P3: templated + tunable, never a blank canvas) and without leaking a private Space's existence. There was also no column to hold a profile bio: the `entity-about` module and the Vera bio drafter had nowhere to read/write.

**Decision.**
- **Bio columns.** Add `spaces.about text` + `spaces.tagline text` (migration `20260711030000`), both nullable, the home for the profile bio + one-liner and the persistence target for Vera drafts. The context band reads `tagline` as its subtitle; `entity-about` reads `about` with a quiet on-voice placeholder when null; the directory card + `lib/spaces/discovery.ts` project `tagline`.
- **Create wizard** `/spaces/new` (Focus): pick type (derived from the blueprint registry so it auto-includes every wired role and offers no type the shell cannot render), name, `isSafeSlug` handle, brand, network/private. `createSpace` (`lib/spaces/provision.ts`) is auth-gated, validates type + slug uniqueness, resolves the root Space's `entity_id` at runtime, inserts the row, and seats the owner as a `space_members` `admin`.
- **Owner settings** `/spaces/<slug>/settings` (Focus): gated on `getSpaceCapabilities(space, caller).canEditProfile` and **404s when denied** (not 403) so the surface never reveals a Space exists. `updateSpaceProfile` (`lib/spaces/profile-settings.ts`) re-checks permission server-side.
- **Brand accent is a validated DAWN token name, not raw hex** (`TOKEN_ALLOWLIST` / `isSafeSlug` from `lib/theme/validate.ts`). Tokens-only, contrast-safe, re-themeable; a pre-existing raw-hex accent reads as "None" until re-picked (the live theme resolver does not consume `brand_accent` yet, so nothing breaks).
- **Vera draft seam.** "Draft with Vera" (about) and "Suggest with Vera" (tagline) call the existing `lib/ai/space-copilot.ts` actions on the cheap (`haiku`) tier through the consolidated AI gateway (ADR-318); each **fills the field for the owner to edit**, never auto-saves, and degrades to a deterministic fallback when AI is off.

**Consequences.** A Space goes from non-existent to a non-empty, owned, editable profile entirely through templated surfaces, with no hand-rolled layout and no raw page builder. Authorization is server-enforced at every write; the settings surface is existence-safe. Not-yet-typed columns (`about`/`tagline`/`visibility`) are read via the untyped admin cast (ADR-246) until the generated types are regenerated. Deferred: the `space_follows` ledger (Follow is a UI seam today), the deeper layout editor for profile module order, role-specific deep modules (memberships / donations + receipts / curriculum / 1:1 booking), and `event_space`.

## ADR-325: The fifth role (Event Space) + Practitioner 1:1 booking as the first deep feature

**Status:** Accepted · Phase 1 deepening of entity-spaces ([ENTITY-SPACES-SYSTEM.md](ENTITY-SPACES-SYSTEM.md) §2.4, §2.8). Shipped on the feature branch (PR #926). Follows ADR-323 (the profile shell) and ADR-324 (the write side).

**Context.** Four roles (practitioner/business/organization/coaching) shared the profile shell with no role-specific depth, and `event_space` was parked behind a `spaces.type` CHECK. Two next steps: complete the role set, and prove the "deep feature" pattern on the smallest, highest-value surface (Practitioner 1:1 booking).

**Decision.**
- **Event Space role.** Expand the `spaces.type` CHECK to allow `event_space` (migration `20260711040000`, a drop-and-readd of the CHECK) and add the blueprint descriptor, reusing the seven entity modules: tabs About/Events/Practices/Community/Tickets on the wired segments, CTA "Get tickets", attendees/events/circles stats, shared `dawn` skin. The create wizard auto-includes it (it derives types from the blueprint registry). Deep ticketing/capacity/lodging/waivers stay deferred.
- **Practitioner 1:1 booking v1.** Two service-role tables (migration `20260711050000`): `space_availability` (recurring weekly windows in one IANA timezone per Space, local-midnight minutes, slot length) and `space_bookings` (absolute-UTC instants, confirmed/cancelled). A **partial unique index on `(space_id, starts_at) WHERE status='confirmed'`** is the double-book guard; a cancelled row frees the slot. RLS is enabled with **no client policies**: all access is through gated server actions on the service-role admin client (mirrors `space_members`, ADR-320). Slot generation is a **pure, DST-aware** function (Intl-based, no tz library) separated from IO so it is unit-tested; `createBooking` re-validates the slot server-side and maps the unique-index race to a friendly message. The member books on the `/spaces/<slug>/book` tab; the owner sets availability on the `/spaces/<slug>/settings/availability` Focus surface and sees upcoming bookings there.

**Consequences.** All five roles now exist; the "add a role" cost is confirmed to be a blueprint descriptor plus a one-line CHECK expand for a new type, never a refactor. The booking subsystem establishes the deep-feature pattern: a `space_id`-scoped, service-role, server-action-gated table set with a pure testable core, additive to the profile (the CTA module swaps in the surface). Booking v1 defers calendar sync, buffers, no-show policy, payments/packages, and per-member timezone (each an additive column, not a refactor). The other roles' deep features (memberships, donations + tax receipts, curriculum journeys) follow the same shape.

## ADR-326: The `spaces` SELECT policy is visibility-aware (closes the Private-space metadata leak)

**Status:** Accepted · security fix (migration `20260711080000`). Verified on the live DB. Found by a post-merge security audit of the entity-spaces feature.

**Context.** The only SELECT policy on `spaces`, `spaces_read_active`, was `USING (status = 'active')`, visibility-blind. A browser anon-key client ships to users (`NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`), so anyone could directly `select * from public.spaces where visibility = 'private'` and read every Private space's metadata (slug, name, brand_*, type, owner_profile_id, entitlements). The app's own reads go through the service-role admin client (`lib/spaces/store.ts`) plus the `getVisibleSpaceBySlug` gate, so the table itself never enforced visibility; this was the one place where "Private 404s for non-members" held only in app code. Member lists, bookings, and availability were never exposed (those tables are service-role-only).

**Decision.** Replace the policy with `status = 'active' and (visibility is distinct from 'private' or public.is_space_member(id))`. `is_space_member(space_id)` is a `SECURITY DEFINER` helper (owner OR any active member; the ADR-056 pattern, same as `is_space_admin`) so it reads spaces/space_members without re-entering their RLS; EXECUTE is granted to `authenticated` AND `anon` because the policy is evaluated for both (for anon, `auth.uid()` is null so it returns false). Network and unset spaces stay publicly readable (the directory/profile/discovery behavior is preserved); Private spaces resolve at the DB layer only for the owner or an active member. Separately, `getSpaceVisibility` now fails CLOSED (returns `'private'` on a read error) so a transient failure 404s rather than briefly exposing a Private space.

**Consequences.** The "Private 404s for non-members" guarantee is now enforced at the database, not just in app code. The app is unaffected (admin-client reads bypass RLS). Verified: with a demo Space temporarily set Private, an anon read returns 0 rows and the owner returns 1. The deferred per-`space_id` RLS isolation on circles/events/practices/journeys (ADR-321) is a separate, still-pending item before real sub-tenant data lands.

## ADR-327: Business memberships v1 (tiers + join, billing deferred to Phase 4)

**Status:** Accepted · Phase 1 deep feature (migration `20260711070000`). Shipped on the feature branch. Follows the booking pattern (ADR-325).

**Context.** The Business role had the profile shell and a "Become a member" CTA but no membership depth. Honoring the bootstrapping constraint (no paid infrastructure yet), build the owner-defines-tiers / member-joins flow WITHOUT charging.

**Decision.** Two service-role tables mirroring booking: `space_membership_tiers` (name, `price_cents` + `interval` that are DISPLAY-ONLY in v1, `benefits` jsonb, sort, is_active) and `space_memberships` (member_profile_id, tier_id, status active/cancelled, with a **partial unique index `(space_id, member_profile_id) where status = 'active'`** as the one-active-membership guard; a cancelled row frees a re-join). RLS enabled with no client policies (service-role only, gated server actions, ADR-246 untyped casts). The Business "Become a member" CTA (the `book`/"Join" tab in `entity-cta.tsx`) renders the tiers + a Join; the owner manages tiers and sees the member list at `/spaces/<slug>/settings/memberships` (Focus). **Honesty (CONTENT-VOICE skeptic test): v1 takes NO payment, and the copy says so plainly.** Joining records a membership; the price is what it will cost once billing ships.

**Consequences.** A second role gains real depth on the same generalizable shape (a `space_id`-scoped service-role table set + a pure-testable core + a CTA that swaps in the surface). Deferred to Phase 4 (all additive, never a refactor): Stripe billing/dunning/proration, class packs/drop-ins, member-only content gating (a payments table + a subscription-id column). Known v1 caveat: `setMembershipTiers` is replace-set, so removing a tier orphans an existing membership's `tier_id` (reads fail safe to a generic "Member" label); switch to upsert-by-id before billing ships. USD-only in v1 (a currency column is an additive future expansion).

## ADR-328: Per-`space_id` content read-isolation (the ADR-321 contract phase)

**Status:** Accepted · security/isolation hardening (migration `20260711090000`). Verified on the live DB. Closes the gap a post-merge audit flagged as the main remaining isolation item.

**Context.** ADR-321 added a `space_id` tenancy column to `circles`/`events`/`practices`/`journey_plans`/`programs` and backfilled existing rows to the root space, deferring the RLS "contract" that enforces space isolation. Each table's SELECT policy enforces the row's OWN visibility (event status, circle archive, program approval) but is space-blind: it does not consider the OWNING SPACE's visibility. No leak exists today (all content is owned by network spaces: the root plus the network demos), but a Private space's content would become readable by non-members the moment one owns content.

**Decision.** Add a `can_view_space_content(space_id)` `SECURITY DEFINER` helper (ADR-056 pattern) that is true when the row is unpartitioned (null `space_id`), the owning space is not Private (network/unset), or the viewer is the owner or an active member. Add an `AS RESTRICTIVE FOR SELECT` policy to each of the five content tables using the helper. Restrictive policies AND with the existing permissive ones, so a row is readable only if its own rules allow AND the owning space is visible to the viewer. For network/root/unpartitioned content the guard returns true (zero behavior change); only Private-space content is walled to its owner + active members. Verified on the live DB: for anon, the helper returns false for a private space's content, true for network content, true for null; for the owner of a private space it returns true.

**Consequences.** Read isolation is enforced at the database, additively (existing permissive policies untouched; root/network content reads exactly as before). Scope is the five `space_id`-bearing content tables. Deferred follow-ups (each additive): WRITE isolation (only a space's editors write its content), the layout infra (`pages`/`page_settings`, read via the admin client), child tables (rsvps/memberships/blocks, which inherit visibility through their parent today), and automated CI contract tests (this round was verified on the live DB). Perf: the helper short-circuits on the network-visibility check (an indexed `spaces` PK lookup) for the common case; a denormalized space-visibility column on the content tables is a possible later optimization if large scans show cost.

## ADR-329: Per-`space_id` content WRITE-isolation (the write pair to ADR-328)

**Status:** Accepted · security/isolation hardening (migration `20260711100000`). Verified on the live DB.

**Context.** ADR-328 closed READ isolation; the write side stayed open. `circles` and `events` have session-client write policies (host/guide/mentor community authoring) that do not check `space_id`, so a community host could craft a direct session-client write setting `space_id` to another (for example, a Private) Space and inject or alter content there. (`practices`/`journey_plans`/`programs` have no session-write policy today; the guard is added there defensively so the invariant holds table-wide.)

**Decision.** A `can_write_space_content(space_id)` `SECURITY DEFINER` helper (true for null, the ROOT space, or the owner / an active editor+ member, mirroring `getSpaceCapabilities.canEditProfile`) plus an `AS RESTRICTIVE` policy for INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE on each of the five content tables. Restrictive ANDs with the existing permissive write policies: a write is allowed only if the existing rules allow AND the writer controls the row's owning Space. The UPDATE guard checks BOTH the old row (`USING`) and the new row (`WITH CHECK`), so content cannot be moved across Spaces either. Verified safe for existing data: every circle/practice and every community event is root-owned, so the root branch returns true (community authoring is unchanged); only the seeded sub-Space offerings become newly gated, to their Space's owner/admin.

**Consequences.** Write isolation is enforced at the database, additively (existing permissive policies untouched; all community authoring unchanged). Verified on the live DB: a random authenticated user can write root content (true) but not a sub-Space's content (false); the Space owner can (true); null is true. Together with ADR-326 (the `spaces` row), the service-role tables, and ADR-328 (content reads), the Space tenancy boundary is now enforced at the DB for both reads and writes. Deferred (additive): child tables (rsvps/blocks), a per-row author carve-out if a flow ever needs non-admins to author within a Space, and automated CI contract tests (this round was verified on the live DB).

## ADR-330: Space Vera co-host hardening, a per-feature budget cap + AI disclosure

**Status:** Accepted · hardening (no migration). Closes two gaps a post-merge audit flagged in the per-Space Vera draft seam (`lib/ai/space-copilot.ts`).

**Context.** The Vera profile-draft seam (`draftSpaceBio` / `suggestTagline` / `draftOfferingBlurb` on the cheap `haiku` tier) recorded usage via `recordAiUsage` but never checked `featureOverBudget`, so its spend was **uncapped** (it gated only on `aiEnabled`). And it surfaced **no AI disclosure**: the "Draft with Vera" / "Suggest with Vera" affordances filled the owner's fields with no statement that the copy is AI-generated (EU AI Act Art. 50 transparency, deadline Aug 2026).

**Decision.** (1) Gate the `draft()` core on `featureOverBudget('space-copilot')` (the codebase pattern, `lib/ai/usage.ts`) right after the `aiEnabled` check, falling back deterministically when the budget is spent; register a `'space-copilot'` daily cap ($2) in `FEATURE_DAILY_CAP_USD`. (2) Add a plain disclosure beside the Vera affordances in the owner settings form: "Vera is AI. The Draft and Suggest buttons write a starting point you review and edit; nothing is saved or published until you do."

**Consequences.** Vera drafting can no longer bill unbounded (a daily ceiling halts a runaway, failing to the deterministic fallback). The owner is told plainly that Vera is AI and that drafts are human-reviewed before saving. Deferred: per-SPACE cost accounting (`ai_usage` has no `space_id` yet, so the cap is per-feature-global, not per-space) and a public-facing disclosure if AI-drafted copy is ever published unedited.

> **Correction (2026-06):** `ai_usage` now HAS a nullable `space_id` (migration `20260712020000`; see [DATABASE.md](DATABASE.md) "Entity Spaces"), so the "no `space_id` yet" sentence above is no longer true. NULL stays the correct default (platform-level / non-space usage); a space-scoped feature stamps it, so the per-feature-global cap can now be enforced **per Space** off the `(space_id, created_at)` index. Per-Space cost accounting is therefore unblocked.

## ADR-331: Default a NULL content `space_id` to the root space on insert (the safe interim tenancy guarantee, not NOT NULL)

**Status:** Accepted · security/isolation hardening (migration `20260712030000`). Closes the last `space_id` tenancy gap a post-merge audit flagged after ADR-328/329.

**Context.** ADR-321 added a NULLABLE `space_id` to `circles`/`events`/`practices`/`journey_plans`/`programs` and backfilled existing rows to the root space; ADR-328 (reads) and ADR-329 (writes) then added the isolation helpers. But both helpers short-circuit on `space_id IS NULL` and treat an unpartitioned row as visible AND writable network-wide. So any insert that OMITS `space_id` silently escapes tenancy: the row is born owned by no space and is readable + writable by everyone. The ADR-321 backfill fixed existing rows but nothing stops a community create-flow (which does not yet always stamp `space_id`) from inserting a fresh null. A hard `NOT NULL` would close the gap but REJECT every create-flow that does not set `space_id`, breaking community circle/event/practice/journey/program creation.

**Decision.** Add a `default_space_id_to_root()` `SECURITY DEFINER` trigger function (ADR-056 pattern, `search_path` pinned) that, `BEFORE INSERT`, sets `NEW.space_id` to the root id (`select id from public.spaces where type='root' limit 1`) when `NEW.space_id IS NULL`, and attach a `BEFORE INSERT` trigger on each of the five content tables (`drop trigger if exists` first, so idempotent). It resolves root at insert time, so it is **portable** (no hardcoded uuid, works in every environment with a seeded root) and **safe** (it ONLY fills nulls; any insert that already sets `space_id`, a sub-space create-flow, is untouched, so no existing flow changes behavior). The migration also re-runs the defensive backfill (`update <each table> set space_id = root where space_id is null`; expected 0 rows today). **`NOT NULL` is deliberately NOT set**: that harder contract is deferred until the app's dual-write of `space_id` is confirmed in prod (the ADR-321 expand -> dual-write -> backfill -> contract order); this trigger makes the column behave as NOT-NULL-with-default-root WITHOUT the breaking rejection.

**Consequences.** A null `space_id` can no longer escape tenancy: an omitted value now persists as explicit root ownership (exactly how the isolation helpers already treat null, but durable rather than an ambiguous null a future helper change could re-interpret). Existing and legacy create-flows keep working unchanged (they set `space_id`, or they get root). Together with ADR-326/328/329 the tenancy boundary is now closed at insert time too. Deferred (additive): the `NOT NULL` contract once app dual-write is verified, and an UPDATE-side guard if a flow ever nulls `space_id` after insert (the read/write helpers already wall such a row, and ADR-329's UPDATE `WITH CHECK` blocks cross-space moves). Documented in [DATABASE.md](DATABASE.md) ("Entity Spaces (tenancy)").

## ADR-332: Per-Space QR codes + a constrained splash landing (Phase 2)

**Status:** Accepted · Entity Spaces Phase 2 (migration `20260713000000`). Code: `lib/qr/space-codes.ts`, `lib/qr/splash.ts`, `app/q/[slug]/route.ts`, `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/settings/qr/`.

**Context.** The managed QR system (`qr_codes` + the `/q/<slug>` resolver) was single-tenant: codes had no owning Space, so an entity Space could not run or measure its own codes. Phase 2 needs per-Space codes, a per-plan code cap, an owner-facing splash landing, and per-Space scan analytics, all inside the established tenancy model (service-role tables, app-code authz, ADR-246/321/328/329) and the kit/voice canon. A full page builder (Puck) is reserved for the Phase 5 public micro-site, so an in-app splash must stay a small constrained shape.

**Decision.** Add two additive columns to `qr_codes`: `space_id` (FK spaces, on delete cascade, NULLABLE interim per ADR-321/331, backfilled to root) and `splash` (jsonb). Scope every code read/write by `space_id` through gated service-role helpers (`lib/qr/space-codes.ts`), each re-checking `getSpaceCapabilities(...).canEditProfile`; staff get a read-only preview. Enforce the per-plan code cap in APP CODE (free 3 / starter 25 / pro 100 / business 500, unknown falls to free), never a DB constraint, since the cap is a product policy that changes with plans. Model the splash as a fixed block list (heading, blurb, image url, up to 5 validated links) with pure normalize/validate (`lib/qr/splash.ts`), explicitly NOT a Puck block tree. The `/q/<slug>` resolver, when a code carries a valid splash, redirects to its primary CTA or renders an escaped self-contained HTML landing (the existing standalone public-route pattern), after the unchanged scan-log + referral/A-B steps; splash-less codes are unchanged.

**Consequences.** Codes are tenant-isolated (a caller for Space A never sees Space B's codes or scans); cross-space splash edits are rejected by re-resolving the code's own Space. Raising a cap or adding a plan is a one-line app change, not a migration. The splash is safe by construction (links validated, content escaped, no free-form markup). The standalone-HTML splash uses inline styles because it renders outside the React/DAWN shell (consistent with the other raw public route handlers); a themed splash can move to a real Next page later. Interim `space_id` nullability defers the NOT NULL + RLS contract; the column is reached via untyped casts (ADR-246) until `database.types.ts` is regenerated.

## ADR-333: Per-Space CRM as an additive nullable scope, with `client_notes` as fail-closed personal data (Phase 2)

**Status:** Accepted · Entity Spaces Phase 2 (migration `20260713010000`). Code: `lib/crm/pipeline.ts`, `lib/crm/client-notes.ts`, `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/settings/crm/`.

**Context.** The CRM (`crm_deals`/`crm_activities`/`crm_stages`/`contacts`) was a single GLOBAL host-gated operator tool (`requireCrm()`, `lib/crm/pipeline.ts`, `/admin/crm`). Phase 2 needs a Space owner to get their own per-space pipeline + contacts + private client notes WITHOUT disturbing the global tool, and notes are third-party personal data subject to GDPR/CCPA.

**Decision.** Add a NULLABLE `space_id` to the four CRM tables (the ADR-321 interim pattern, never NOT NULL in v1), backfill existing rows to the root space, and make the data-layer `spaceId` param OPTIONAL: absent preserves exact global behavior (no filter, whole table); present filters `space_id` for per-space isolation. Add `client_notes(space_id, contact_id, author_profile_id, body)` with RLS ENABLED and ZERO policies (service-role only), every read and write gated on the Space owner (`getSpaceCapabilities.canEditProfile`) and double-scoped by `space_id`. The owner surface is a Focus page at `/spaces/<slug>/settings/crm` gated on canManage or staffViewing; staff preview is read-only and reads notes empty.

**Consequences.** The global `/admin/crm` tool is untouched (a regression test asserts no `space_id` filter is applied on the no-arg path). A caller for Space A can never read, write, or delete Space B's CRM rows or notes (delete is scoped by id AND space_id, so a cross-space id is a no-op). `client_notes` never leaks cross-space and never to a non-owner; erasing a contact cascades its notes. `space_id` stays nullable until a future contract migration; the CRM tables remain untyped casts (ADR-246) until `database.types.ts` is regenerated.

## ADR-334: Event Space check-in reuses `nodes`/`captures` with additive space scoping (Phase 2)

**Status:** Accepted · Entity Spaces Phase 2 (migration `20260713020000`). Code: `lib/spaces/checkin.ts`, `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/settings/checkin/`.

**Context.** An `event_space` Space needs door check-in. The platform already has a server-authoritative physical-engagement pipeline (`nodes` + `captures`, the `/n/<nodeId>` claim path, `captureNode` in `lib/engagement/capture.ts`, with verify -> exactly-once ledger -> capture row -> reward). Building a separate check-in table would fork a proven, anti-cheat-hardened flow.

**Decision.** A check-in is an ordinary node capture. Add NULLABLE `space_id` to `nodes` and `captures` (backfilled to root) for per-Space tenancy, and an additive `nodes.kind` marker ('standard' or 'checkin') so each Space has exactly one reusable check-in node. Reject overloading `nodes.type` (which drives the engagement source and reward currency). A Space's check-in node is a `qr` node, so scans route through the unchanged claim pipeline; the only new code is service-role owner helpers (`ensureCheckinNode`/`listCheckins`/`countCheckins` in `lib/spaces/checkin.ts`) gated on `canEditProfile`, plus a Focus roster surface. Reads fail-safe; node-create fails-closed; the roster resolves the node by `space_id` then reads its captures, so Space A never sees Space B.

**Consequences.** Free, additive, reversible, no scan-flow fork. `space_id` stays nullable (the NOT NULL contract is deferred). New captures are not yet space-stamped at write time; v1 reads by node linkage, and a one-line additive change to `captureNode` (`space_id: node.space_id`) is the noted follow-up when scoped capture writes land. Distinct-checker analytics is an additive later step. Columns are reached via untyped casts (ADR-246) until `database.types.ts` is regenerated.

## ADR-335: Per-Space email send backbone (fail-closed, on the existing Resend integration) (Phase 3)

**Status:** Accepted · Entity Spaces Phase 3 (migration `20260714000000`). Code: `lib/spaces/email.ts`, `lib/suppression.ts`, `lib/unsubscribe-tokens.ts`, `app/api/unsubscribe/route.ts`, `app/api/webhooks/resend/route.ts`.

**Context.** Frequency already has a global email stack (Studio campaigns, the `email_suppressions` list, the unsubscribe flow, the Resend webhook). Phase 3 needs per-Space sending without forking that stack, and email is anti-spam sensitive, so it must be fail-closed by default.

**Decision.** Extend the existing stack additively. (1) A per-Space kill-switch `spaces.email_enabled` (default false): a Space sends NOTHING until an owner explicitly enables it after affirming anti-spam permission. (2) `email_suppressions` gains a nullable `space_id`: NULL = a global suppression honored by every Space; a set `space_id` scopes to one Space. The email-only primary key is replaced by a synthetic `id` plus a unique index on `(coalesce(space_id, zero-uuid), lower(email))` so an address can be both globally and per-Space suppressed. (3) A new `outreach_sends` ledger records one row per recipient (status queued/sent/delivered/bounced/complained/failed/suppressed + resend_id), the source of truth for the per-Space daily cap and analytics. (4) `campaigns` gains nullable `space_id` (backfilled to root) + `scheduled_for`. (5) A conservative v1 cap of 500 sends per Space per UTC day. (6) RFC 8058 one-click unsubscribe is scoped per Space via an HMAC token over `(spaceId, email)`, recording a space-scoped suppression; the global member unsubscribe is unchanged. The send path gates on `canEditProfile`, is fail-closed on the kill-switch, the cap, and suppression, and reuses the shared verified Resend `send.` subdomain.

**Consequences.** The global email flows are byte-identical (nullable columns, global suppression = NULL `space_id`, the global send path checks global rows only). A Space A caller never sends as / reads Space B. Out of scope (counsel/cost gated): a custom per-Space sender domain with DKIM, SMS/A2P, the AUP/DPA legal copy, and SES at scale. Tables stay service-role only (RLS on, no policies); columns reached via untyped casts (ADR-246) until `database.types.ts` is regenerated.

## ADR-336: Per-Space email campaign authoring (Phase 3 composer + audiences)

**Status:** Accepted · Entity Spaces Phase 3. Code: `lib/spaces/campaigns.ts`, `lib/spaces/audiences.ts`, `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/settings/email/`.

**Context.** A Space owner needs to write an email, choose recipients from their own contacts, and send or schedule it, on the per-Space tenancy model and the kit/voice canon, calling the ADR-335 send backbone.

**Decision.** The owner authors from `/spaces/<slug>/settings/email`, a Focus surface gated on `canManage || staffViewing` (staff preview read-only), composing kit primitives + the existing block/compose pattern. Campaign CRUD (`lib/spaces/campaigns.ts`) is over the shared `campaigns` table reached by untyped cast; every read filters `space_id` and single reads pin it, so a Space A caller never reads, edits, schedules, or sends Space B's campaigns. Audiences (`lib/spaces/audiences.ts`) resolve over the Space's own `contacts` (Phase 2 `space_id`), optionally narrowed by a tag via `network_contact_tags -> network_contacts.linked_contact_id`, returning the exact `{ contactId, email }[]` the send seam consumes. The body renders to a minimal HTML email with a Space-appropriate footer carrying a per-recipient unsubscribe placeholder the send seam swaps. The per-Space email enable gate requires a plain anti-spam acknowledgment ("I have permission to email these people and will follow anti-spam rules"); the counsel-gated AUP is deferred.

**Consequences.** Authoring is fully tenant-isolated and the send goes through the ADR-335 backbone (kill-switch, cap, suppression, RFC 8058, ledger). A contact with no linked `network_contact` appears only under "Everyone in this space," never under a tag. Email HTML uses inline styles + hex (it renders in mail clients, outside the DAWN shell, like the existing `campaignEmail`).

## ADR-337: Per-Space email deliverability reads are app-gated, fail-safe, and union global suppressions (Phase 3)

**Status:** Accepted · Entity Spaces Phase 3. Code: `lib/spaces/email-analytics.ts`, `components/spaces/email/analytics-panel.tsx`, `components/spaces/email/suppression-list.tsx`.

**Context.** A Space owner needs to see their own send deliverability (sent / delivered / bounced / complained) and who has unsubscribed or bounced, reading the ADR-335 `outreach_sends` ledger + `email_suppressions` without any cross-tenant leak.

**Decision.** The read lib (`lib/spaces/email-analytics.ts`) counts `outreach_sends` per `space_id` and lists a Space's effective `email_suppressions` (its own rows UNION the global NULL-space rows), gated on `canEditProfile` (a janitor may also read, mirroring `listSpaceMemberships`), via untyped casts. `sent` is the attempted denominator (all statuses except `queued`/`suppressed`); `complaintRate > 0.1%` surfaces as the anti-spam health flag. Every read fails safe to zeros/`[]` so the surface renders before the ledger has rows and a tenancy leak is impossible.

**Consequences.** A Space's stats and suppression view never include another Space's rows; global suppressions are labeled as such. The panels are self-contained Server Components embedded in the email surface. Distinct-open/click tracking and richer charts are additive later.

## ADR-338: Tenancy hardening sweep (default-to-root extension, FK covering indexes, merged space_members SELECT policy) (audit follow-up)

**Status:** Accepted. Migration `20260714010000_tenancy_hardening.sql`, applied. Closes the post-Phase-3 audit findings (Supabase advisors `unindexed_foreign_keys` + `multiple_permissive_policies`, and the default-to-root coverage gap on the Phase 2/3 tenant tables).

**Context.** ADR-331 attached the `default_space_id_to_root` BEFORE INSERT trigger to the five original content tables so a NULL `space_id` could not escape tenancy. The Phase 2/3 work (ADR-332/333/335) then added a nullable `space_id` to more tables (`qr_codes`, `contacts`, the CRM trio, `campaigns`, `captures`, `nodes`) without that trigger, so an insert omitting `space_id` could again land unpartitioned. Separately the advisor flagged seven foreign keys on our tables with no covering index, and two duplicate permissive SELECT policies on `space_members` (`read_own` plus `read_for_space_admin`) that both evaluate on every SELECT.

**Decision.** Reuse (do not redefine) the existing SECURITY DEFINER `default_space_id_to_root()` and attach its BEFORE INSERT trigger to the eight uncovered tenant tables, idempotently. Exclude `ai_usage` and `email_suppressions`: their NULL `space_id` is meaningful (platform-level usage; a global suppression that must apply to every Space), so stamping root would corrupt it. Add `create index if not exists` covering indexes for the seven advisor FKs. Merge the two `space_members` SELECT policies into one (`space_members_read`) whose USING is the exact OR of the prior predicates (own row OR `is_space_admin(space_id)`), preserving semantics while clearing the advisor. Do not set any column NOT NULL and do not change `qr_codes` RLS: both are separate decisions deferred to a later migration once app dual-write is confirmed.

**Consequences.** A NULL `space_id` can no longer escape tenancy on the Phase 2/3 tables either; the two meaningful-NULL tables keep their semantics. FK deletes and joins on the indexed columns stop being sequential scans. `space_members` SELECT is evaluated by one policy. Behavior is otherwise unchanged: inserts that set `space_id` are untouched, writes stay service-role only, and `space_members` reads return the same rows. Verified live: 13 default-root triggers, one `space_members` SELECT policy, seven new indexes. Deferred (additive): the NOT NULL contract, and any `qr_codes` per-`space_id` RLS isolation.

## ADR-339: One canonical role-type set, split into a full union and a provisionable subset (HARD-01 / HARD-02)

**Status:** Accepted. Code-only reconciliation (no migration); the `spaces.type` CHECK already allows `event_space` (ADR-325). Touches `lib/spaces/types.ts`, `lib/spaces/blueprints.ts`, `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/settings/page.tsx`, with the contract recorded in `docs/SPACES.md`. Closes the entity-role audit gap behind A-PLUS-ROADMAP §2/§9.

**Context.** The role types had two drifting sources of truth. The hand-written `SpaceType` union listed `lab` and `partner` but was missing `event_space`, even though `event_space` is a live role (its `spaces.type` CHECK is enabled, it has a blueprint, and the directory + create wizard already offer it, ADR-325). The blueprint registry was the mirror image: it had `event_space` but no `lab`/`partner` descriptor. The missing union member forced a `(space.type as string) === 'event_space'` cast in the settings hub to light up the Event Space check-in card, defeating exhaustiveness checking exactly where it matters. There was no single place that said which types exist versus which a member can actually stand up.

**Decision.** Define one canonical role-type set with two tiers. (1) The **full set** (every value a `spaces.type` row may hold) is `root`, `practitioner`, `business`, `organization`, `coaching`, `event_space`, `lab`, `partner`; all eight are members of `SpaceType`. Add `event_space` to the union and drop the now-needless cast in the settings hub (a plain, exhaustively-checked `space.type === 'event_space'`). (2) The **provisionable subset** (a member can create one via the wizard) is defined operationally as the types with a registered blueprint in `lib/spaces/blueprints.ts`, since the wizard already derives its choices from `blueprintForType`: `practitioner`, `business`, `organization`, `coaching`, `event_space` (plus `root`, the platform host, which is never wizard-provisioned and has no member-facing blueprint). Keep `lab` and `partner` in the union (live role values; existing rows and the discovery/label paths must type-check) but **defer their blueprints to item ADMIN-05**, so they are intentionally not provisionable and `blueprintForType` fails closed for them. The blueprint registry stays the single source of truth for "which types are provisionable"; the union is the single source of truth for "which types exist". Record the contract in `docs/SPACES.md` and lock it with tests (lab/partner fail closed; the provisionable set has exactly its blueprints).

**Consequences.** The two sources now agree on one contract instead of drifting, and the settings hub keeps full exhaustiveness checking on `space.type` (no `as string` escape hatch). `tsc` stays clean. ADMIN-05 (make Lab and Partner provisionable) becomes a pure additive step: add a descriptor to `blueprints.ts` and the wizard picks it up automatically, with no edit to `SpaceType` and no change to this contract. The deferral is explicit in code comments, in `SPACES.md`, and here, so a future change cannot silently delete `lab`/`partner` from the union or assume they are provisionable. No schema or runtime behavior changes (the CHECK and the wizard choices are unchanged today).

## ADR-340: Entity-role preview is a second view-as axis that routes into a representative Space (JAN-01)

**Status:** Accepted. Code: `components/layout/view-as-control.tsx`, `lib/view-as.ts`, `app/(main)/view-as-actions.ts`, `lib/spaces/entity-roles.ts` (pure helpers), `lib/spaces/representative.ts` (server reader), with tests in `lib/view-as.test.ts` + `lib/spaces/entity-roles.test.ts`. Closes the Janitor-selector gap in MASTER-PLAN §6 (Stream D).

**Context.** The "view as role" selector previewed only the COMMUNITY ladder (`ROLE_HIERARCHY`) plus a hardcoded Visitor. The entity roles are the separate `SpaceType` axis (the provisionable subset per ADR-339, now all seven member-facing types after ADR-341), and an entity role has meaning only INSIDE a Space, so staff could not preview the app as any entity role. The two axes must not be conflated: an entity role is not a rung on the trust ladder, and previewing one must never alter community capabilities or escalate any privilege.

**Decision.** Add a second labelled "Preview as entity" group to the selector, driven by the provisionable blueprint set (sourced from `provisionableTypes()` via a new pure `previewableEntityRoles()`, never a divergent hardcoded list), so it stays in lockstep with the create wizard and auto-grows as blueprints land. Model the target as a discriminated union `ViewAsTarget = CommunityRole | 'visitor' | { kind: 'entity'; type: SpaceType }`, carried in the SAME `freq-view-as` cookie under an `entity:` prefix so the two axes can never collide. `applyViewAs` returns the caller's real community role UNCHANGED for an entity target (no downgrade, and crucially no escalation): the entity preview is expressed purely by ROUTING the staffer into a representative Space, not by mutating the community capability resolver. The server action `previewAsEntity` validates the requested type against the provisionable set (`isPreviewableEntityRole`, which fails closed for `root` and any unblueprinted/garbage value), sets the cookie, and resolves a representative Space via a new fail-safe reader (`representativeSpaceOfType`) that reads ONLY the public-directory set (`visibility='network'`, active, root excluded) through the shared discovery helper. When no networked Space of that type exists yet, it returns no href and an on-voice note ("No space of that role is on the network yet…") rather than erroring. The cookie's mere presence already strips the staff axis (`lib/staff.ts`) and floors the trust level, so an entity preview is downgrade-safe exactly like the ladder preview.

**Consequences.** A host-and-above staffer can preview the app as any provisionable entity role and land in a representative Space of that role, with the community-ladder preview behavior unchanged. The validator accepts only valid entity-role values and can never escalate (a forged `entity:` cookie naming `root` or an unknown type is rejected at parse time, and `applyViewAs` never raises the community role for an entity target). The selector group, the validator, and the representative-Space pick are all derived from the blueprint registry, so lab/partner (ADR-341) appear automatically and a role no one has provisioned simply shows the graceful note. The pure entity-role helpers live in `lib/spaces/entity-roles.ts` (client-safe, no Supabase) so the client selector can import them without pulling server-only code into the bundle; the admin-client reader stays server-only in `representative.ts`. Per CONTENT-VOICE the new copy ("Preview as entity", the labels, the empty note) carries no em or en dashes.

## ADR-341: Lab and Partner role blueprints (make the two deferred roles provisionable) (ADMIN-05)

**Status:** Accepted. The pure-additive follow-up ADR-339 designed for: a descriptor per role in `lib/spaces/blueprints.ts`, with its test (`lib/spaces/blueprints.test.ts`), the wizard filter (`app/(main)/spaces/new/page.tsx`), and a note in `docs/SPACES.md`. No migration (the `spaces.type` CHECK already allows `lab`/`partner`), no edit to `lib/spaces/types.ts` (the union is unchanged), no edit to `lib/spaces/accent.ts` or the settings hub.

**Context.** Under ADR-339, `lab` and `partner` were live members of the `SpaceType` union but had no blueprint, so the create wizard filtered them out and the profile shell fell back to an About-only page. The blueprint registry is the single source of truth for "which types are provisionable", and ADR-339 deliberately deferred the two descriptors to this item. A **Lab** is a physical Frequency place (entity partition `labs`) where people gather for in-person sessions, practices, and community; a **Partner** is a brand that runs a Frequency loyalty Partner program (entity partition `partner`), publishing perks and a place to join.

**Decision.** Author a `RoleBlueprint` for each, following the established shape + the §2.10 extensibility contract, so both provision onto a non-empty, legible profile. Both reuse the same five wired route segments (`about`, `offerings`, `practices`, `community`, `book`) and the same eight entity modules as the other roles; only the labels, CTA, hero stats, and accent vary.

- **Lab** (venue-shaped, "come in and take part"): tabs About · What's on · Practices · Community · **Visit**; CTA **Visit**; hero stats `offerings` (What's on) · `circles` · `members` (Regulars), leading with the calendar a Lab always has so the first stat is never an always-zero count; About leads with `entity-offerings` (a room is its calendar). Default skin `dawn`; default accent `--color-success` (green, the last free accent family, reading as the "in-person, growing room" hue).
- **Partner** (brand-shaped, "join the program"): tabs About · Perks · Practices · Community · **Join**; CTA **Join**; hero stats `offerings` (Perks) · `members` · `circles`, leading with the perks a Partner publishes day one; About leads with `entity-about` (a brand leads with its story). Default skin `dawn`; default accent `--color-broadcast` (it **shares the Business brand family by design** - see below).

**Owner controls.** Both **compose the universal four** (Members / QR / CRM / Email) the settings hub already renders for every Space type; neither adds a role-specific deep owner control in v1. A Lab runs on the shared event / circle / QR-door tools it already has, so a bespoke surface would duplicate them; a Partner's loyalty-program engine is a later, money-adjacent phase (the same pattern as memberships v1, ADR-327: ship the legible profile + the shared owner tools first, defer the money engine). The member-facing CTA renders through `entity-cta.tsx`, which shows the Space's own upcoming sessions for any non-practitioner/non-business role (the generic, graceful fallback ADMIN-04 will later replace with a deep engine), so both CTAs are legible today.

**Wizard.** The wizard derived its choices by filtering `components/spaces/space-type.ts`'s `DIRECTORY_TYPES`, which lists only the five public directory roles (not `lab`/`partner`, which are internal/operator roles never shown in the public directory). To keep the blueprint registry the single source of truth for "provisionable" without editing the directory map, a `provisionableTypes()` helper now derives the wizard's choices straight from the registry (the type value + the blueprint's own `typeLabel`), in canonical role order. So `lab`/`partner` are offered the moment their blueprints register, the public directory stays unchanged, and no list is hardcoded.

**Accent constraint.** `lib/spaces/accent.ts` (out of this item's scope) ships exactly six remappable accent families; five are taken one-each by the other roles, leaving one free (`--color-success`), which Lab takes. **Partner shares the Business `--color-broadcast` family on purpose** - a Partner is a brand running a loyalty program, so the "product / brand" hue fits - rather than introducing a seventh family in `accent.ts`. A Partner-specific accent family is a later step there. The per-role-distinctness guarantee therefore holds for the five core roles; Lab is distinct, and Partner's shared accent is the documented exception.

**Consequences.** All seven member-facing role types are now provisionable through one wizard onto a legible, role-distinct profile; the contract test now expects the full seven (`root` remains the only blueprint-less type). The change is purely additive: no migration, no union edit, no settings-hub or `accent.ts` edit. `tsc`, eslint, the test suite, and the build stay green. When the Partner loyalty engine or a Partner accent family ships, each is a further additive step that does not revisit this contract.

## ADR-342: Organization donations owner control (forms only, no money) (ADMIN-01)

**Status:** Accepted. Migration `20260716000000_space_donations`, applied. Code: `lib/spaces/donations.ts`, `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/settings/donations/`.

**Context.** The Organization role had a blueprint and a Donate CTA but no owner control to configure it. Phase 4 (Stripe Connect, charges, tax receipts) is Held, so v1 delivers the owner surface and structured data only, with no payment, exactly as Business memberships v1 (ADR-327).

**Decision.** A `settings/donations` owner Focus surface where an Organization owner configures one donation ask per Space (a fund label, a short description, and a set of suggested amounts), stored in the additive `space_id`-scoped `space_donation_asks` (one row per Space via a unique index on `space_id`; RLS enabled with no client policies, service-role only, ADR-246 untyped casts). The fund label and amounts are display only; nothing takes a payment and there is no Stripe path, and the copy says so (skeptic test). Writes re-check `canEditProfile` server-side; the janitor preview is read-only; render gates on `resolveSpaceManageAccess`. The hub registers the card gated to `organization`; the route is Focus in `page-chrome.ts`.

**Consequences.** An Organization owner can configure donation asks today, ready for the member Donate engine (ADMIN-04) to read. Real charges are additive later (a payments table + a charge id), never a refactor. The single-row-per-Space shape keeps the editor simple; relaxing the unique index makes multiple funds an additive change.

## ADR-343: Coaching enrollment v1 (program definition + enroll, billing deferred) (ADMIN-02)

**Status:** Accepted. Migration `20260716000100_space_enroll`, applied. Code: `lib/spaces/enroll.ts`, `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/settings/enroll/`.

**Context.** The Coaching role had the profile shell and an Enroll CTA but no owner admin. Honoring the no-paid-infrastructure constraint (ADR-327), build the owner-defines-program / member-enrolls flow without charging.

**Decision.** Two additive service-role tables mirroring memberships: `space_programs` (one row per Space via a partial unique index on `space_id`; name, description, free-text schedule, optional dates, capacity with 0 = no cap, is_published; NO price column) and `space_enrollments` (member_profile_id, program_id, status active/cancelled, with a partial unique index `(space_id, member_profile_id) where status='active'` as the one-active guard; a cancelled row frees a re-enroll). RLS enabled with no client policies; all access via gated server actions in `lib/spaces/enroll.ts` (`canEditProfile` on writes; `enrollInProgram` refuses an over-capacity or duplicate enrollment). v1 takes NO payment and the copy says so. Member-facing copy says "program"/"enroll", never "cohort" (NAMING).

**Consequences.** A third role gains real depth on the same generalizable shape. Deferred to Phase 4 (additive): paid enrollment / billing, multiple concurrent cohorts (drop the one-program index, add a cohort label), structured sessions, waitlists. v1 caveat: `setSpaceProgram` is replace-by-delete, so re-saving cascades enrollments; switch to upsert-by-id before paid enrollment ships. The member enroll engine is ADMIN-04.

## ADR-344: Event Space ticketing as free / RSVP tiers, no money (ADMIN-03)

**Status:** Accepted. Migration `20260716000200_space_tickets`, applied. Code: `lib/spaces/tickets.ts`, `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/settings/tickets/`.

**Context.** An `event_space` Space had check-in (ADR-334) but no box office. The §5 money boundary keeps real paid ticketing in Held Phase 4, so v1 ships the owner surface and structured data only.

**Decision.** Two additive `space_id`-scoped tables: `space_ticket_tiers` (name, `kind` CHECK free|rsvp, nullable capacity, description, sort, is_active) and `space_ticket_rsvps` (status going|cancelled, partial unique `(tier_id, member_profile_id) where status='going'`). NO price column exists, on purpose, so nothing implies a charge; a future paid kind + price column + payments table is a one-line CHECK widen, not a rebuild. RLS enabled with no client policies; all access via `lib/spaces/tickets.ts` gated on `canEditProfile` (writes) or public active tiers (member surface). RSVP enforces tier kind = rsvp, capacity, and one-going-per-member. The owner surface is a Focus page gated on `canManage || staffViewing` AND `space.type === 'event_space'`; staff preview is read-only.

**Consequences.** Free, additive, reversible, no payment path; Space A never reads/writes Space B's tiers or RSVPs (every query scopes `space_id`; capacity + uniqueness are tier-scoped). The member reserve/cancel surface ships with ADMIN-04. A replace-set delete-then-insert can orphan an RSVP's `tier_id`; `listSpaceRsvps` falls back to a generic "Ticket" label (switch to upsert-by-id before paid ticketing).

## ADR-345: Practice un-log is today-only, with an exact-debit grant column and three anti-cheat layers (WEBSITE-CHANGES-PLAN B.1 / B.2 / D4 / D5)

**Status:** Accepted. Migration `20260717000000_practice_log_zaps_awarded.sql`, applied. Code: `lib/practices.ts` (`unlogPractice`, `logPractice`, `MAX_PRACTICE_LOGS_PER_DAY`), `lib/zaps.ts` (`reverseZaps`), `app/(main)/practices/actions.ts` (`unlogPracticeAction`, the rate limit). Realizes WEBSITE-CHANGES-PLAN §3 decisions D4 (today-only un-log) and D5 (all three anti-cheat mechanisms).

**Context.** A member who logged a practice by mistake had no undo, and the log path had no farming guard. Two constraints shaped the scope. (1) A FULL historical reversal is unsafe: it would have to un-bridge spent streak freezes and un-pay milestone rewards, and the streak writer is monotonic by design. (2) The Zap economy had no debit primitive: `awardZaps` rejects an amount of zero or less so totals only ever rise, and the `after_zap_transaction` trigger is the single place totals move. Separately, the live payout for a log can drift between the log and the un-log (the `zap_config` weight-class amounts are tunable, and a practice's `weight_class` / `reward_zaps` can be edited), so an un-log cannot recompute the grant from current config without risking an over-debit.

**Decision.** Un-log is scoped to the caller's OWN log for the current UTC day only (D4); a past day's log is intentionally not reversible. `unlogPractice` runs an exact, ordered reversal: delete the durable `practice_logs` row, delete the `engagement_events` idempotency row keyed `practice_log:{profile}:{practice}:{day}` (CRITICAL: leaving it silently blocks re-logging the same practice today, since `recordEngagementEvent` would treat the next log as a duplicate no-op), debit the EXACT Zaps the log granted, re-derive the streak, then best-effort reverse the one-shot welcome-back / spark grants this log created so a re-log can re-roll them. Three supporting mechanisms:
- **The exact-debit column.** `logPractice` writes the amount it actually awarded onto the new `practice_logs.zaps_awarded` column at log time; `unlogPractice` reads that stored amount and debits precisely it. A null (pre-feature row) reverses nothing rather than guessing, so an old row can never over-debit.
- **`reverseZaps`, the negative-aware sibling of `awardZaps`.** It appends a single COMPENSATING negative `zap_transactions` row (`-amount`), so the existing trigger subtracts it from season + lifetime zaps the same way a grant adds. It is a safe no-op for an amount of zero or less, and the action type defaults to a debit-marked label so the Vault "how you earned" log reads it as a reversal. Idempotency is the caller's responsibility, which `unlogPractice` satisfies because the log row + idempotency row are deleted in the same pass (the log cannot be un-logged twice).
- **The dedicated streak re-derive.** `recomputePracticeStreakAfterUnlog` rebuilds current / longest from the remaining logs, never the monotonic forward writer; today-only scope means it never has to un-bridge a freeze or un-pay a milestone.

Anti-cheat ships all three D5 layers: a per-member rate limit on the log/un-log action (`rateLimitOk('practice_log', profileId, 10, '1 m')`, fail-open when Upstash is unset so dev/preview are never blocked, and the same window on un-log so a log/un-log loop cannot be spun); the per-day total-logs cap `MAX_PRACTICE_LOGS_PER_DAY = 25` across all of a member's practices (counted before the idempotency row is written, fail-open on a read error, and a practice already logged today is exempt so its idempotent re-run still no-ops cleanly rather than tripping the cap); and the timer-completion proof for timed practices, where the timed sit / Movement session must complete through `completeSession` (which clamps and records the real elapsed seconds into `practice_sessions`) before the practice logs, closing the "log a timed practice with no real sit" hole.

**Consequences.** A member can cleanly undo today's accidental log: the log row, the idempotency row, the exact Zaps, and the streak all reverse, and a re-log works immediately and can re-roll its bonuses. The economy gains its first and only debit primitive (`reverseZaps`), still funneled through the one trigger, so rank stays monotonic everywhere except this small, bounded, today-only path. Historical reversal stays out of scope (it would have to un-pay milestones / un-bridge freezes). A failed `zaps_awarded` write or a missing column leaves NULL, which the un-log treats as zero, so the worst case under-debits rather than over-debits. The cap and rate limit only ever bite a farming loop, never a real daily program. The `zaps_awarded` column is reached through the untyped admin handle until `lib/database.types.ts` is regenerated (ADR-246).

## ADR-346: The Movement timer is a second timer kind, with `timer_kind` superseding `uses_timer` as a GENERATED mirror (WEBSITE-CHANGES-PLAN C.6 / C.8 / D6)

**Status:** Accepted. Migrations `20260717000000` (the un-log column, ADR-345) and `20260718000000_practice_timer_kind.sql`, both applied. Code: `lib/movement.ts` (the pure timer engine), `components/on-air/movement-session.tsx` + the Movement setup components, `app/(main)/on-air/actions.ts` (`completeSession`). Realizes WEBSITE-CHANGES-PLAN §4 decision D6 (four Movement modes + the interval engine now, steps deferred, a `timer_kind` enum).

**Context.** A practice routed to exactly one timer (the Mindless sit/breathe on the On Air rails) or to none (a one-tap Log it), encoded as the binary `practices.uses_timer`. The Movement workstream adds a SECOND timer beside Mindless: a Walk, a Yoga flow, open Play, or a Workout interval engine. "Timer yes / no" can no longer express which of two timers a practice opens, so the discriminator must widen from a boolean to a three-way kind. But many existing readers depend on `uses_timer` as written (`lib/practices.ts`, the timer-completion proof in `completeSession` per ADR-345, the `practices_ranked` view), so widening the schema cannot break them or let the two signals drift.

**Decision.** Introduce a `practice_timer_kind` enum `none | mindless | movement` and add `practices.timer_kind` (the authoritative discriminator) plus an optional `practices.movement_config` jsonb (the Movement mode + its tuning) in migration `20260718000000`. Keep `uses_timer` as a GENERATED STORED mirror `= (timer_kind <> 'none')` so every existing reader is untouched and the flag can never diverge from the kind. Because Postgres has no `ALTER ... SET GENERATED` for an existing plain column, the migration swaps `uses_timer` through a temp generated column (`uses_timer_derived`), drops the old plain column, and renames the generated one into its place; `practices_ranked` is dropped first and recreated identically so it reads the generated value transparently (the view does NOT expose the two new columns, which the library does not need; the editor + timer routing read them from the table via `getPractice`). Seeding maps pre-existing `uses_timer = true -> 'mindless'` (the only timer there was) and `false -> 'none'`; new rows default to `'mindless'`, matching the prior `uses_timer default true`. The timer engine itself (`lib/movement.ts`) is pure: every mode compiles to one flat phase array plus a rounds count, the live component walks wall-clock elapsed against it via `phaseAt`, and a Movement session ends through the SAME `completeSession -> logPractice` path the Mindless sit uses (the timer is a stage, not a second economy), so `lib/movement.ts` writes nothing. The four modes (Walk / Yoga / Play / Workout, with the Tabata / EMOM / AMRAP / Circuit interval presets) ship now; steps / distance via web sensors are deferred (iOS gesture-gated, no background). All member-facing labels are plain and carry no em or en dashes (proper nouns like Tabata and EMOM carry the magic).

**Consequences.** A practice now routes to one of two timers or to none, while `lib/practices.ts`, the `completeSession` timer-proof, and `practices_ranked` keep reading `uses_timer` unchanged and can never drift from `timer_kind`. `updatePractice` treats `timer_kind` as authoritative (never writes the generated `uses_timer`) and clears `movement_config` when the kind is not `'movement'`, so a stale Movement config never lingers on a Mindless / none practice; `forkPractice` carries both fields so a forked Movement practice keeps its mode + tuning. The Movement engine stays unit-testable and frame-cheap (no DOM, no audio, no time held in the engine), and a Movement sit tags its mode into the `practice_sessions.mode` free-text column so it reads back distinct from a Mindless sit while the economy and timer-proof treat it as the timed sit it is. `timer_kind` / `movement_config` are reached through the untyped admin handle until `lib/database.types.ts` is regenerated (ADR-246). Deferred (additive): steps / distance as a later best-effort opt-in.

## ADR-347: The settings drawer (the ADR-128 dock, rebuilt at the shell level) and a split-out all-roles QR & Share dropdown (WEBSITE-CHANGES-PLAN D.1 / D.3 / D.4 / D.6, decision D2)

**Status:** Accepted. Code: `components/layout/settings-drawer.tsx`, `components/qr/qr-share-dropdown.tsx` (reusing `components/qr/page-qr-manager.tsx`). Rebuilds the removed ADR-128 `PageAdminDock`; realizes WEBSITE-CHANGES-PLAN §5 decision D2 (rebuild the dock + split QR & Share).

**Context.** The side panel of per-page admin actions described in ADR-128 (right-edge, push / overlay, drag-resizable, persisted) was built then removed; only the spec survived, and the current inline `PageAdminBar` lacked the drag-to-widen, push-the-content behavior. ADR-128 also bundled QR / Share inside the same operator panel, so a plain member never had a clean home for sharing a page; and that bundling kept the share affordance gated behind operator access.

**Decision.** Rebuild the dock at the SHELL level as `SettingsDrawer`, and split sharing out into its own all-roles dropdown. The drawer is a right-edge, fixed, full-height panel that opens LEFTWARD into the slot the global right rail vacates; open + width persist in `localStorage` (`freq-settings-open` / `freq-settings-width`); a left-edge pointer-drag handle widens it (bounded 288px, the global rail width, to 560px). It opens on the `open-settings` window event (D.6, mirroring `open-capture`), so the trigger seam is decoupled from the panel. The shell owns the chrome reactions (D.3 / D.4): the drawer reports its open + widened state up via `onOpenChange` / `onWidenChange`, and the shell hides the right rail while open and collapses the left rail to its mini render once the drawer is widened past `SETTINGS_WIDEN_THRESHOLD` (420px); pages never toggle the rail themselves. The drawer holds only the NON-share settings (the registry-selected sidebar modules per scope kind, the Circle Quest / rail modules, page content for the right routes, and the operator "Page" group of Layout / SEO / Status, set apart by a hairline), each module re-gating server-side so selection here is by scope kind. QR & Share splits out (D.1) into `QrShareDropdown`, shown to ANY signed-in role on a shareable page: managers (host+ / staff) get the full QR designer + scan activity (`PageQrManager`), everyone else gets the read-only share kit (`PageShareKit`), preserving the exact per-role split that lived inside the old panel; only the home moved. Tokens only; all copy carries no em or en dashes.

**Consequences.** Managers regain the unobtrusive, drag-resizable, persisted admin dock from ADR-128, now living at the shell level where it can drive the rail chrome cleanly, and adding a Focus-style surface is a chrome decision rather than a page edit. Sharing is no longer operator-gated: every signed-in member can share a page, with the QR designer still reserved for managers. The split keeps each surface single-purpose (settings vs share) and the drawer empty-and-closed when there is nothing to manage (it renders nothing, leaving the shell's right rail visible). This supersedes the ADR-128 `PageAdminDock` component and the QR/Share half of the inline `PageAdminBar`.

## ADR-348: The Manage mega-menu is a launcher-only header surface with no persistent active-Space (WEBSITE-CHANGES-PLAN E.3 / E.4, decision D3)

**Status:** Accepted. Code: `components/layout/manage-mega-menu.tsx` (the client launcher), `lib/spaces/managed.ts` (the `listManagedSpaces` reader) + its `'use server'` wrapper in `lib/spaces/managed-actions.ts`. Realizes WEBSITE-CHANGES-PLAN §6 decision D3 (launcher only, no active-Space context).

**Context.** The biggest IA gap was that entity / Space management had no global entry: a member who owned or managed a Space had no header path into its back-end. The design alternative was a persistent active-Space context (a cookie + switcher) that both the mega-menu and the settings drawer would operate on. That was rejected (D3) as an unnecessary state machine: an active-Space cookie would couple the launcher to the settings drawer (ADR-347), force every surface to reason about "which Space am I in," and risk the operator world bleeding out of the left rail.

**Decision.** Ship a PURE LAUNCHER. A "Manage" trigger beside the wordmark folds out a wide panel listing the viewer's owned / managed Spaces, each a deep link to its `/spaces/<slug>/settings` hub with a type chip and an Owner / Manager badge, plus "New Space" and "Browse directory" actions and a "create your first Space" empty state. There is NO persistent active-Space context and NO cookie: clicking a Space just routes you into its back-end. The operator / admin world STAYS in the left rail as a telescoped group (it does not move into the mega-menu), and the settings drawer stays scoped to the page you are on, not to an active Space; this decouples Workstream D (the drawer) from E (the launcher). The managed-Spaces list loads LAZILY on first open via the `getManagedSpaces` server action (guarded so it fires at most once), so the header never blocks on it. The reader `listManagedSpaces` defines "managed" as a Space the viewer OWNS (`spaces.owner_profile_id`) OR holds an ACTIVE `space_members` role at editor+ (editor / moderator / admin), the same `canEditProfile` authority the owner back-end gates on; a plain viewer membership does not count. It is request-cached, re-resolves the viewer's own id at the call boundary (never trusts a client-passed id), reads only rows keyed to that viewer's ownership or own membership (no cross-tenant reach), drops suspended / archived Spaces, and is FAIL-SAFE to `[]` on any error or a pre-migration table so the launcher degrades to its empty state rather than throwing. Behavior (E.4): open on click, dismiss on outside-click / Esc / navigation / pointer-leave (with a ~400ms exit delay so a brief overshoot does not snap it shut), WCAG 1.4.13 compliant, desktop + tablet only (the mobile drawer reaches the same surfaces through the rail). Tokens only; copy carries no em or en dashes.

**Consequences.** Entity / Space management finally has a global header entry, with zero new state: no active-Space cookie, no switcher machine, and the operator rail untouched. The launcher can only ever list Spaces the caller is entitled to steward (tenancy-safe by construction), and fails closed to a friendly empty state on any error. Because there is no active-Space context, the settings drawer (ADR-347) and the launcher stay independent; if a persistent active-Space is ever wanted, it is an additive layer, not a revisit of this contract. The `spaces` brand columns and `space_members` are reached through untyped casts until the generated DB types catch up (ADR-246).

## ADR-349: Broad coverage standard, every page composes a kit template AND renders its assignable interior via `<PageModules>` (WEBSITE-CHANGES-PLAN Workstream F, decision D1 = Broad)

**Status:** Accepted (locked standard). Canonical spec: [`PAGE-FRAMEWORK.md`](PAGE-FRAMEWORK.md) §8 (the standard) + §8.4 (the migration recipe + the long-tail progress log). Realizes WEBSITE-CHANGES-PLAN §7 decision D1 (Broad now, run as a tracked, phased, multi-PR track in route-group batches).

**Context.** "Convert every page to the template system and wire in all blocks" was ambiguous. The survey found two already-fully-wired block systems with zero orphans (the in-app module engine + the Puck marketing editor) and only about three genuinely hand-rolled app pages, so the work was not "wire orphaned blocks" but "decide how broad the page-to-framework migration goes." The choices were Narrow (migrate the ~3 hand-rolled pages + set a standard), Broad (convert app-page interiors to header + `<PageModules>` so their sections become operator-assignable blocks, the framework's stated end goal), and also un-gating Puck for per-Space white-label authoring (Phase 5, money / white-label, deferred).

**Decision.** Adopt the BROAD standard and lock it: every page composes a kit template (the shell + chrome, §8.1) AND renders its assignable interior sections through `<PageModules>` (the module engine), rather than a hand-rolled `<h1>` + a hand-stacked body. The migration runs as a phased, multi-PR track in route-group batches, not one PR, following the §8.4 recipe. Part A (always do this) adopts a shell: pick the template by what the content is, replace the hand-rolled header with the template's `PageHeading` slots (exactly one page `<h1>`, from the kit), and register the rail once in `lib/layout/page-chrome.ts`. Part B moves each self-contained, self-fetching interior section to a registered module: write the module as an async Server Component that fetches its own data and returns `null` when empty, add its metadata to `LAYOUT_MODULES`, bind it in the registry, add the route's module set to `ROUTE_MODULE_IDS`, list the route in `MODULE_ROUTES` so the on-page Layout editor offers it, and render `<PageModules route="/route" />`. A section stays hand-composed ONLY when it depends on a page prop a nested module cannot get (a `searchParams` facet), surfaced via the request-header seam (`x-search`, as `/practices` does) or left in the page (as `/friends` keeps its `mode`-dependent buckets). `/practices` (a full interior of modules) and `/friends` (one section converted) are the exemplars. Per-batch progress is tracked in PAGE-FRAMEWORK §8.4's long-tail log. Un-gating Puck (D1 option c) stays deferred to Phase 5.

**Consequences.** New pages are a template choice + a chrome line, never a new layout, and their interior sections become operator-arrangeable blocks for free. The broad conversion is a tracked, batched, long-tail effort (the converted-pages log in §8.4 is the running record; hand-built interior sections are the migration target, not the pattern), so it never blocks other work and is always resumable. The `searchParams` exception is explicit, so a facet-dependent section is not forced into a module that cannot read it. Puck white-label authoring remains a Phase 5 (money / white-label) decision, untouched here.

## ADR-350: Owner live-site feedback reversals — the right rail shows on every page, the settings drawer is confined to the rail column, Manage becomes an admin "Spaces" nav item, the entity preview moves to per-Space buttons, the practice day is the member's LOCAL day, and the home feed posts to the wall

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-21). Supersedes the chrome-reaction half of ADR-347 and the header-launcher of ADR-348. Code: `lib/layout/page-chrome.ts`, `components/layout/{app-shell,settings-drawer,view-as-control,timezone-sync}.tsx`, `lib/nav-areas.ts`, `lib/{view-as,member-day}.ts`, `app/(main)/{view-as-actions,timezone-actions}.ts`, `components/spaces/view-as-space-button.tsx`, `app/(main)/admin/spaces/page.tsx`, `app/(main)/feed/page.tsx`, `app/(main)/network/{layout,page}.tsx`, `app/globals.css`.

**Context.** After seeing the shipped work live, the owner gave eight directives that reverse or refine several recent decisions: (1) the practice "Log" buttons reset at UTC midnight (~4-5pm Pacific) instead of the member's own midnight; (2) the header "Manage" mega-menu (ADR-348) felt out of place; (3/4) the JAN-01 entity-role preview in the bottom-left "View as" control was abstract (it previewed a representative Space of a TYPE, not a specific one); (5) entity Spaces should not be member-visible in the menu yet; (6) the rebuilt settings drawer (ADR-347) hid the right rail, widened past it, and collapsed the left rail, which read as the "admin menu" overrunning the page; (7) the Focus-surface model dropped the right rail on many pages, but the owner wants the rail on every page; (8) the Community hub's in-page sub-tabs (Community / My Contacts) duplicated nav that belongs in the left rail. Plus three polish bugs: a home-feed post defaulted to the member's primary circle, and the global focus ring boxed the composer textarea and the header wordmark.

**Decision.**
- **Rail on every page (7).** `railFor` now returns `'global'` by default; `'none'` is reserved for `/admin/*` (it mounts its own info rail) and the four genuine full-viewport takeovers (`/on-air`, `/scan`, `/sign-in`, `/print`). Settings, compose/edit forms, message threads, the entity-Space wizard AND owner settings all keep the global community rail. The Journey course builder (`/journeys/[slug]/edit`) is the ONLY modifier: it keeps the rail but starts it collapsed to a mini strip (`railStartsCollapsed`), per "the only time it's modified is in Journey editor mode."
- **Settings drawer confined to the rail column (6).** The drawer is a fixed 288px (`w-72`, the rail width) panel mounted INSIDE the right-rail `<aside>` (made `relative`), overlaying exactly that column (`absolute inset-y-0 right-0`, `max-w-full`) and never extending past the content's right edge. The hide-the-rail, drag-to-widen-past-the-rail, and collapse-the-left-rail behaviors from ADR-347 are removed; the left and right rails both stay put while it is open.
- **Manage → an admin "Spaces" nav item (2/5).** The header mega-menu is unmounted; a "Spaces" area is added to the left rail's Admin section right after QR Studio (`href: /admin/spaces`), gated EXACTLY like QR Studio (`defaultAccess: 'admin'` + `staffDomain: 'platform'`) so only Admin + Janitor see it. No member-facing Spaces nav ships yet.
- **Entity preview → per-Space "View as" on /admin/spaces (3/4).** The entity group is removed from the bottom-left `ViewAsControl`. `previewAsEntity(type)` is replaced by `previewAsSpace(spaceId)` (janitor-gated; resolves the Space and routes into its `/spaces/<slug>/settings` owner surface, which renders read-only for a janitor), surfaced as a `ViewAsSpaceButton` on each tenant card in `/admin/spaces`. The `ViewAsTarget` entity shape changes from `{ kind:'entity', type }` to `{ kind:'entity', spaceId }`.
- **Practice day is the member's LOCAL day (1).** `lib/member-day.ts` resolves "today" (YYYY-MM-DD) in the member's IANA timezone — preferring the durable, un-spoofable `profiles.home_timezone`, then an optional client-reported tz, then UTC. `logPractice` / `unlogPractice` / `getPracticesToLogToday` / `getPracticeMemberState` all key the idempotency row + `practice_logs.logged_for` unique constraint off it, so the day flips at the member's own midnight. A `TimezoneSync` shell component fills `home_timezone` once from the browser when it is empty, so server-side reads (the feed) resolve correctly without a client round-trip. All anti-cheat (daily cap, once-per-practice-per-day, the on-air timer proof) is untouched — only the day STRING is tz-aware.
