- **Home feed posts to the wall (bug).** The home composer is always scoped to the member's profile (their wall, `visibility: 'public'`), never their primary circle; the circle composer lives on the circle page. 
- **Network sub-tabs removed (8).** The in-page Community / My Contacts tab switcher is deleted; each surface keeps its own left-rail item, and Connections is relabeled "My Contacts" under Community.
- **Focus ring opt-outs (bug).** Two unlayered, higher-specificity rules in `globals.css` remove the global `:focus-visible` ring from the composer textarea (`[data-tour-anchor="composer"] textarea`) and the header wordmark (`.brandmark-link`); the ring stays everywhere else for keyboard a11y.

**Consequences.** The chrome model is simpler and matches the owner's mental model: one rail, present everywhere except admin and true takeovers, with the settings drawer as a rail-width overlay rather than a page-reflowing panel. Space management is a role-gated left-rail item (admin/janitor only) instead of a header surface, and previewing a Space is concrete (one specific Space, read-only) rather than a representative-of-type abstraction. The practice loop now respects the member's clock, fixing the "buttons reset at 4pm" report, with the day still server-resolved so it cannot be spoofed to backdate a log. This supersedes ADR-347's rail-hiding / widen / left-collapse reactions (the drawer's confine-to-rail design replaces them) and ADR-348's header launcher (the admin "Spaces" item replaces it). _Update (2026-06-23, dead-code sweep): `components/layout/manage-mega-menu.tsx` has now been deleted (it was unmounted and orphaned). Its `getManagedSpaces` Server Action wrapper (`lib/spaces/managed-actions.ts`) is retained as a stable seam._

## ADR-351: The settings drawer is a RESIZABLE push column — it slides over the rail, and its grab handle compresses the center content (refines ADR-350)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-21). Refines ADR-350's settings-drawer decision. Code: `components/layout/settings-drawer.tsx`, `components/layout/app-shell.tsx`.

**Context.** ADR-350 confined the drawer to a FIXED rail-width overlay (removing the ADR-347 drag-to-widen) to satisfy "the admin menu only ever covers the right rail." Seeing it live, the owner wanted the resize back, but done correctly: clicking Settings should SLIDE the panel out from the side with a grab handle, it should cover the right rail, it must never spill past the content's right column, and dragging the handle should COMPRESS the main content (a push, not an overlay over the body).

**Decision.** The drawer becomes a resizable PUSH column. The `SettingsDrawer` owns its open state, a persisted width (`freq-settings-width`, floor = the 288px rail width, cap = 600px so the body stays usable), a left-edge grab handle (pointer drag + a keyboard `role="separator"` with Arrow/Home/End, WCAG 2.1.1), and a slide-in (translateX). It reports its live `{ open, width, resizing }` up via `onStateChange`. The shell sizes the RAIL COLUMN wrapper to that width: at rest the column is the rail width and the drawer slides over the rail (covering it, nothing reflows); as the handle widens the drawer, the column grows and the center `flex-1` COMPRESSES to match. Because the drawer is its own pushing column (never an absolute overlay over the body), it can never spill past the content's right column. The width transition is dropped while `resizing` so the column tracks the pointer 1:1; the panel content is viewport-sticky (`sticky top-14`) so it stays in reach on a long page. This restores the ADR-347 resize intent but as a content-compressing push bounded to its own column, replacing ADR-350's fixed-overlay decision (the rest of ADR-350 stands).

**Consequences.** The settings surface behaves like a proper resizable drawer: slide-out with a grab handle, covers the rail, pushes (not covers) the body, bounded so neither the rail nor the body is lost. The shell once again reacts to the drawer (it sizes the column), but through a single reported state object rather than the old hide-rail / widen-past / collapse-left behaviors. The 288px floor guarantees the rail is always fully covered when open; the 600px cap guarantees the center never collapses to nothing.

## ADR-352: A shared best-practice mega menu for the public header and the admin nav

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-21). Code: `components/layout/mega-menu.tsx` (the shared primitive), `lib/site.ts` (`PUBLIC_MEGA_NAV`), `components/layout/primary-nav.tsx`, `lib/admin/nav.ts` (`ADMIN_NAV` + `canSeeAdminSection`), `components/admin/admin-mega-nav.tsx`, `app/(main)/admin/layout.tsx`.

**Context.** The header navigation was flat tabs + a thin single-column "Discover" dropdown that overflowed, and the admin bar was a flat scrollable row of ~8 section links with no path to the ~50 pages beneath them. The owner asked for a best-practice mega menu, scoped to the PUBLIC header (to organize the growing set of public pages) and the ADMIN nav (to better navigate admin pages); the in-app member header keeps the left rail as its primary nav.

**Decision.** One shared, data-driven `MegaBar` primitive serves both surfaces. The triggers sit in a row and share ONE panel that slides DOWN as a FULL-WIDTH row under the bar (owner directive 2026-06-21: "slide down as a row under the header"), not a cramped per-trigger floating dropdown. The panel anchors to the nearest POSITIONED ancestor, so it spans the full viewport under a fixed/sticky header (public) or the content column under a `relative` wrapper (admin); that single full-width row (a flex row of grouped columns + an optional featured tile) also fixes the column-overlap a per-trigger auto-width dropdown hit once it had multiple groups. It opens on hover-intent AND keyboard focus and closes on Escape / outside-click / focus-out / navigation (WCAG 1.4.13 + 2.1.1); a trigger is a button that toggles, or — given an `href` — a Link that navigates on click while still revealing the panel on hover/focus. The public header (`PrimaryNav`, used by the marketing + site headers) renders `PUBLIC_MEGA_NAV` (a "Discover" panel over the live-community pages with a "find your first circle" featured tile, and an "Explore Frequency" panel over the mission pages), collapsing the old flat tabs into two grouped triggers (no overflow). The admin bar (`AdminMegaNav`, fed by the gated `ADMIN_NAV`) makes each operator section a link-trigger that navigates to its root and reveals its real sub-pages grouped by theme (Community → Spaces/People/Activity, Programs → Content/Engagement, Growth → Marketing/Pipeline, Operations → Platform/Configuration, etc.); Dashboard + Leadership stay plain tabs. Gating is unchanged: `canSeeAdminSection` mirrors the prior per-item gate (role floor OR staff domain), and sub-pages inherit the section gate (they re-gate server-side). Both nav data sets are plain (no-React) modules so the server and the client menu share them; the bar root stays UNPOSITIONED so the panel reaches the right positioned ancestor for its span (the header full width, or the admin `relative` wrapper for content width). Tokens only; copy carries no em or en dashes.

**Consequences.** Adding a public or admin page is a data line in `PUBLIC_MEGA_NAV` / `ADMIN_NAV`, never a header edit. The public header no longer overflows and reads as a designed, grouped nav; the admin area exposes its full page tree two clicks deep instead of hiding it behind section roots. The shared primitive keeps both surfaces consistent and accessible. The old `AdminTopMenu` (flat row) and the `PrimaryNav` flat-tab/`Dropdown` internals are removed. The in-app member header keeps the left rail; it shows only the "Explore Frequency" panel (Discover is hidden there via `showDiscover={false}`).

## ADR-353: The practice completion economy: partial timed logs clear the day for 1 Zap, with a "Finish Practice" top-up, plus a Mindless MODE and an author duration lock (practice-timer redesign data model)

**Status:** Accepted. Migrations `20260720000000_practice_mindless_mode.sql`, `20260720000100_practice_log_completion.sql`, `20260720000200_practice_timer_data_fix.sql` applied 2026-06-21 (#966); `lib/database.types.ts` regenerated. Code: `lib/practices.ts` (`logPractice`, `getPartialPracticesToday`, `pillarTimerDefault`, `MINDLESS_MODES`, the `Practice` + `LogPracticeResult` additions, `normalizePractice`, `practiceFullReward`), `app/(main)/on-air/actions.ts` (`completeSession`), `app/(main)/practices/actions.ts` (`logPracticeAction`), `lib/on-air.ts` (`RevealPayload` additive fields). Realizes the practice-timer redesign data model + completion economy.

**Context.** A timed practice could only be logged as a binary all-or-nothing event: either you completed the full sit (full reward, streak tick) or you logged nothing. A member who sat most of a session but stopped early earned nothing and lost the day, which punishes the exact partial effort a daily-practice product wants to reward. Separately, a `timer_kind = 'mindless'` practice had no way to say WHICH flavour of Mindless it was (a meditation vs a breath set vs a journal vs a stillness sit vs a ritual vs a plain log), and an author had no way to pin a fixed session length. The constraint: every change had to preserve the existing one-tap / full-sit path byte-for-byte (the default economy), keep the today-only un-log (ADR-345) debiting exactly, and not touch `lib/movement.ts`, the on-air UI, the streak box, or the generated DB types.

**Decision.** Add a completion economy on top of the existing log path, gated entirely on a timed log carrying a positive `secondsTarget`; a one-tap log (no target) is untouched. Three additive `practice_logs` columns carry the state: `completed` (default true, so every existing + one-tap row stays complete with no backfill), `seconds_done`, and `seconds_target`. `logPractice` gains optional `secondsDone` / `secondsTarget` and routes off the done/target ratio:
- **One-tap / no timer** (target null/0): the unchanged FULL path — `completed=true`, full reward (`reward_zaps` override else weight class, via the one source of truth `practiceZapValue`), streak tick, bonuses.
- **Timed, no existing log, ratio >= 0.95**: a full sit — `completed=true`, the seconds columns, full reward, streak tick, bonuses (identical to one-tap except the columns are filled).
- **Timed, no existing log, 0.5 <= ratio < 0.95**: a PARTIAL — `completed=false`, the seconds columns, exactly **1 Zap**, but it STILL records the engagement event (>= 50% clears the day) and ticks the streak. (A ratio < 0.5 never reaches `logPractice`: the `completeSession` timer-proof gate blocks it.)
- **Timed, existing PARTIAL log (the "Finish Practice" top-up)**: decided BEFORE the engagement-idempotency gate (that key is already recorded from the first partial, so a finish would otherwise be swallowed as a duplicate no-op). If the new sit is full, the row flips to `completed=true`, `seconds_done` is raised, and the DELTA `fullReward - zaps_awarded_so_far` is paid (so partial + finish totals exactly the full reward), with NO streak re-tick and NO re-paid bonuses; if still partial, `seconds_done` nudges upward and nothing more is paid.
- **Existing FULL log**: the unchanged no-op (`logged=false`).

`LogPracticeResult` gains optional `partial?` / `finished?`; `RevealPayload` gains the same two optional fields (additive, so the untouched reveal UI ignores them) so `completeSession` can message "1 Zap now, finish for the rest." `getPartialPracticesToday(profileId, clientTimezone?)` returns each partial practice plus `{ secondsDone, secondsTarget }` so the UI can show "Finish Practice" and compute the time left; `getPracticesToLogToday` is unchanged (a partial practice has a log row, so it correctly drops out of "to log" and into the partial list). `completeSession` computes `secondsTarget` from the practice's `duration_min*60` (else the claimed seconds for an open / Free sit, so target = done → a full sit) and `secondsDone` from the real clamped elapsed seconds, and passes both; `mode === 'log'` (Just Log) passes a null target to stay one-tap full. The Movement path flows through the SAME `completeSession -> logPractice` call, so it gets partial / full / finish automatically. The schema also adds a `practice_mindless_mode` enum (`meditate | breathe | journal | stillness | ritual | log`) on `practices.mindless_mode` (NULLABLE: null = derive from the Pillar at read time via the new `pillarTimerDefault`) and `practices.duration_locked` (default false = the member may adjust the length). A guarded, idempotent data-fix migration routes the seeded demo practices to the right timer + mode by slug (Motion → Movement walk; stillness sits → Mindless stillness; journaling → Mindless journal; reach-out / action → none + log) and backfills any Mindless practice's null mode from its Pillar, mirroring `pillarTimerDefault`.

**Consequences.** A member who sits at least half a timed practice now keeps the day and the streak and earns a Zap, and can finish later to collect the rest, while the one-tap / full path is unchanged and the today-only un-log still debits exactly `zaps_awarded` (a partial row stores 1, a finished row stores the full amount). The full reward still SCALES by `reward_zaps` override else weight class exactly as before; partial is always 1 and finish pays the rest, so the totals reconcile. `mindless_mode` lets the launcher dress the Mindless timer per flavour with a null-safe Pillar fallback, and `duration_locked` lets an author pin a length. The new columns are reached through the untyped admin handle until `lib/database.types.ts` is regenerated (ADR-246); `RevealPayload` / `LogPracticeResult` carry the two new flags as OPTIONAL fields so no existing consumer breaks. All copy carries no em or en dashes.

## ADR-354: On Air opens NEUTRAL from a generic entry (the Free sit); only a practice-specific entry pre-selects a practice

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-21). Code: `lib/on-air/session-data.ts` (`loadOnAirSessionData` `defaultPracticeId`). Refines the practice-timer redesign (ADR-353).

**Context.** Opening either timer from a GENERIC entry (the header Mindless/Movement button, the Zap menu) auto-selected the member's first adopted practice and previewed ITS mode. With the new `timer_kind` routing that meant a log-only practice (e.g. One Small Reach) opened the launcher already in Just Log with a note field and no timer, even though the member had chosen nothing yet. The owner's directive: a generic open is a NEUTRAL state where the member can log anything or select a practice; only selecting a practice should jump to that practice's mode.

**Decision.** `loadOnAirSessionData` pre-selects a real practice ONLY when one is explicitly requested (`requestedPracticeId`: a practice page, the streak box's button, a Journey step, a `/on-air?practice` link). A generic open (no request) now defaults to the **Free sit** when one exists, never a random adopted practice, so the setup previews the neutral open sit (Meditate / Walk + the timer) and the "Select a practice" chooser drives the real choice, which routes to the picked practice's mode. The first-real-practice fallback survives only for the edge case where no Free sit practice is configured.

**Consequences.** A generic open reads as a calm, neutral timer instead of someone else's log screen; the per-practice routing (a specific entry opens in that practice's mode) is unchanged. Both timers share the loader, so both open neutral on a generic entry. No copy carries em or en dashes.

## ADR-355: Mega menu redesign — content-column panel, slide-from-under with linger/fade, and a full-width admin sub-header

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-21). Refines ADR-352 for the in-app member header and the admin nav (the public/marketing header is unchanged). Code: `components/layout/mega-menu.tsx` (the shared primitive), `components/layout/primary-nav.tsx`, `components/layout/app-shell.tsx`, `lib/site.ts` (`PUBLIC_MEGA_NAV`), `app/(main)/admin/layout.tsx`. Removes `components/admin/admin-mega-nav.tsx`.

**Context.** ADR-352 shipped one shared `MegaBar` whose panel spanned the nearest positioned ancestor: the full viewport under the public/admin bars. The owner wanted, for the in-app member header and the admin nav: (1) the panel to read as the width of the PAGE CONTENT COLUMN (between the left menu and the right rail), not the full viewport; (2) the panel to slide out from UNDER the header and, on disengage, to LINGER then FADE rather than vanish (also fading on click-away); (3) the in-app "Explore Frequency" trigger to sit on the header's horizontal line at the rail link's color (it was top-aligned and dimmed to 40%); (4) each "Explore Frequency" sub-category to become its own column (to be built out later); and, for admin, (5) a SECOND full-width sub-header that opens below the main header and pushes content down, whose slide-out panel still stays in the content column.

**Decision.** The redesign stays SELF-CONTAINED in `MegaBar` (no cross-tree provider/portal). The panel keeps anchoring to its sticky bar (so it follows scroll and tucks under the bar's bottom edge), and a new `panelAlign` prop chooses the span: `'viewport'` (default; the public header, which has no rails) reproduces ADR-352's centered full-width row, while `'content'` reproduces the shell's body grid INSIDE the panel — a centered `max-w-[105rem]`, the same `gap-8`, a left rail-width spacer (`w-48`), and an optional right rail-width spacer (`w-72`, gated by a `rightRail` prop) — so the visible card lands exactly in the content column between the rails. Motion: the panel now stays MOUNTED through close so it can animate out; pointer-leave starts a linger timer (`LINGER_MS`), then a fade (`FADE_MS`) before unmount; re-entering the bar OR the panel cancels it; Escape and outside-click trigger the fade instead of an instant unmount; honors `prefers-reduced-motion`. The member header (`PrimaryNav`, forwarding `panelAlign`/`rightRail`) renders "Explore Frequency" vertically centered (`items-center`) at `text-muted` with the dimming wrapper removed, and `lib/site.ts` splits that entry into one `MegaSection` (column) per sub-category (The Lab, The Community, The Quest, About), each holding its landing link as a placeholder for future sub-links. For admin, the app shell itself renders the full-width sub-header on `/admin*` — built client-side from the SAME `ADMIN_NAV` + `canSeeAdminSection` gate the server layout used (the shell already has the viewer's role/web_role/staff role) — as a `sticky top-14` band in normal flow (so it pushes the body down), with its triggers aligned to the content column and its `MegaBar` panel using `panelAlign='content'` (no `rightRail`, since admin drops the member right rail). The admin layout therefore stops rendering its own bar: `admin-mega-nav.tsx` is deleted and the Ask-Vera/search band now sticks at `top-14 md:top-[6.5rem]` (under the 3.5rem header + 3rem sub-header).

**Consequences.** The in-app and admin mega panels read as designed drop-downs over the page content column instead of full-bleed bars, and feel calmer (linger + fade, dismiss on click-away). Admin gains a true two-tier header. The public/marketing header is untouched (it keeps `panelAlign='viewport'`). Admin nav stays a single source of truth (`ADMIN_NAV`), now consumed in the shell as well as the (re-gating) pages. Trade-offs to revisit if needed: the content alignment reuses the shell's rail-width constants (`w-48`/`w-72`) rather than measuring, and the admin panel spans the full content area (it does not carve out the xl-only info rail); the sub-header is desktop-only (md+), with mobile admin still reached through the left drawer. No copy carries em or en dashes.

## ADR-356: The "Explore Frequency" mega-menu columns map to existing pages, with no overlap with the Discover menu

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-22). Code: `lib/site.ts` (`PUBLIC_MEGA_NAV`). Fulfills the placeholder left by ADR-355 (each "Explore Frequency" sub-category became its own column "to be built out later"). PR #969.

**Context.** ADR-355 split the "Explore Frequency" panel into one column per category (The Lab, The Community, The Quest, About), each carrying only its landing link as a placeholder. The owner asked to populate the columns. Two routes were possible: invent new sub-pages, or map each column to the public pages that already ship. The owner chose mapping to existing pages, so no column points at a route that does not yet exist.

**Decision.** Each column leads with its category landing link, then lists the existing public routes that belong to that theme, deliberately drawing only from surfaces the **Discover** menu does NOT already own (Circles / Events / Journeys / Interests) so the two panels stay distinct rather than duplicate each other:
- **The Lab** → `/the-lab`, How it works `/how-it-works`, Practice library `/discover/practices`.
- **The Community** → `/the-community`, Partners `/discover/partners`, Help center `/help`.
- **The Quest** → `/the-quest`, Join the Beta `/onboarding/beta`. (Intentionally thin: no public game sub-pages exist yet.)
- **About** → `/about`, Pricing `/pricing`, Privacy `/privacy`.

`/discover/practices` and `/discover/partners` live under `/discover` in the route tree but are NOT listed in the Discover menu (`DISCOVER_NAV`), so reusing them here introduces no menu overlap. `/demo` is deliberately excluded (it was merged into How it works). Every `href` was verified to resolve to a shipped `page.tsx`; the menu is data-driven, so the panel renders the extra items with no component change. Link labels and one-line descriptions carry no em or en dashes (CONTENT-VOICE).

**Consequences.** The "Explore Frequency" panel is now a real grouped nav over the mission/info pages, with each column thematically coherent and no duplication of the live-community Discover panel. Growing it stays a data edit in `PUBLIC_MEGA_NAV`. Two placements are judgment calls, easy to revisit as data lines: "How it works" sits under The Lab (it could read as The Community), and The Quest holds only its landing + the Beta CTA until public game sub-pages exist (Pricing could move there). No copy carries em or en dashes.

## ADR-357: DB-backed, editable menu system across every nav surface (the Menu Manager)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-22). Migration `20260721000000_menu_system.sql` (written, NOT yet applied to the live DB; apply, then regenerate `lib/database.types.ts`). Code: `lib/menus/` (`types.ts`, `defaults.ts`, `read.ts`, `actions.ts`, `db.ts`), `app/(main)/admin/menu/` + `components/admin/menu/*` (the Menu Manager UI), `components/layout/mega-menu.tsx` / `primary-nav.tsx` / `app-shell.tsx` / `marketing-header.tsx` / `site-header.tsx` / `marketing-footer.tsx` / `menu-role.ts` / `ghost-link.tsx` / `nav-icons.ts`, and the header/footer fetches in `app/(main)/layout.tsx` / `app/(marketing)/layout.tsx` / `app/(help)/layout.tsx` / `app/page.tsx`. Supersedes the single-rail `menu_config` editor (`app/(main)/admin/menu/menu-sorter.tsx` + `actions.ts`, removed); `menu_config` / `area_permissions` stay as the rail's pre-seed fallback and for `/admin/roles`.

**Context.** The owner asked for a fully featured Menu Manager: edit every menu position (public Discover + Explore Frequency panels, admin sub-header, in-app left rail, marketing footer), add/delete categories incl. nested sub-categories, edit per-item subheadings, set columns per menu (default six) with free-grid placement, drag links across categories, per-item role visibility, three modes per link (Active / Ghost / Hidden), per-item Ghost upsell, and left/right rail content cards, plus global open/dwell speed. The nav was hard-coded across `lib/site.ts`, `lib/admin/nav.ts`, and `lib/nav-areas.ts`, so none of this was editable. Decisions captured via the owner Q&A: source of truth = full DB-backed; scope = all positions; tenancy = global now with per-space and per-role layering later; link target = known routes or custom URL; role dimension = per-item role rules; Ghost = a greyed link opening an upgrade-to-Crew lightbox with a per-item tier + message; rail cards = featured-card shape; columns = free grid; speed = global timings; left rail = full DB-drive; migration written but applied by the owner.

**Decision.** Five tables (`menus`, `menu_categories` nested via `parent_id`, `menu_items`, `menu_rail_cards`, singleton `menu_settings`) are the source of truth. `space_id` is nullable (NULL = the global menu; per-space is a later phase with no further migration). Reads are PUBLIC (anon + authenticated) because the public header serves logged-out visitors; the data is non-sensitive nav structure and per-role/per-mode filtering happens in the renderer, not the DB. Writes go exclusively through the service role in janitor-gated server actions (`lib/menus/actions.ts`), mirroring `menu_config` / `area_permissions`; there is no client-facing write policy. `role_modes` keys are whitelisted to the known role set on write (prototype-pollution guard, CodeQL). The reader (`lib/menus/read.ts`) assembles rows into a normalized `ResolvedMenu` and is BEST-EFFORT: on a missing row, missing table (pre-migration), or any error it falls back to `lib/menus/defaults.ts` (which adapts the existing static nav), so nav always renders and `isDefault` marks a code-fallback menu. Per-viewer resolution (`components/layout/menu-role.ts`): effective mode = `roleModes[viewerRole] ?? mode`, then the `minAccess` floor downgrades below-access viewers to ghost (when authored as an upsell) or hidden; Active = a link, Hidden = dropped, Ghost = a muted `GhostLink` opening the upgrade lightbox with `ghostTier` (default crew) + `ghostMessage`. `MegaBar` renders `ResolvedMenu[]` (public = one trigger per menu, admin = one trigger per top-level category) with an explicit CSS grid when placement is set and the flex-wrap fallback otherwise; all motion/timings (now from `menu_settings` via the `timings` prop) and a11y are preserved. The left rail is DB-driven ONLY when its menu is non-default (`!isDefault`): until an operator seeds `left_rail`, the rail keeps its proven legacy gating (`menuAreaKeys` + `itemAccess` / telescope / `area_permissions`) unchanged, so the highest-blast-radius surface is zero-risk pre-seed; bespoke rail chrome (streak, notifications, profile, search, Manage launcher, view-as, compact/mini, mobile drawer) is untouched.

**Consequences.** A janitor can reshape every menu surface from `/admin/menu` without a code change, and adding a public/admin page can become a data edit. The migration is written but unapplied, and every surface falls back to the code defaults until it is applied AND a surface is seeded, so shipping this is safe in stages. Trade-offs / follow-ups: reads use the service-role admin client (the tables are not in generated types until regen, ADR-246 pattern, via the untyped `lib/menus/db.ts` handle) and should be tightened after `db push` + `gen types`; per-space and the per-role full-override layer are modeled in the schema but not yet built; the admin sub-header's per-section gating now flows through `minAccess`/mode rather than `canSeeAdminSection` (permissive, but `/admin/*` is already access-gated by the layout and pages re-gate server-side); the icon vocabulary for custom rows is the curated lucide set in `nav-icons.ts`. No copy carries em or en dashes.

## ADR-358: The Practice builder mirrors the Journey builder's Vera capabilities, retargeted to the atomic Practice

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-22). Code: new `lib/ai/practice-shape.ts` (`PRACTICE_SHAPE_PRIMER` + `withPracticeShape`), `lib/ai/practice-spark.ts` (`draftPracticeSpark`), `lib/ai/practice-edit.ts` (`planPracticeEdits`); new `app/(main)/practices/create-actions.ts` (`sparkPracticeAction`, `createPracticeFromSparkAction`) + new `app/(main)/practices/actions.ts` actions (`buildPracticeWithVeraAction`, `applyVeraPracticeChangeAction`); new route `app/(main)/practices/new/page.tsx`; new client components `components/studio/practice/{practice-spark,practice-composer}.tsx`; `PracticeBuilder` gains a `veraComposer` slot; `NewPracticeButton` now links to `/practices/new`; two feature caps added in `lib/ai/budget.ts` (`practice-spark` 2, `practice-edit` 4); `/practices/new` registered in `lib/layout/page-chrome.ts` `MANAGED_ROUTES`.

**Context.** The Journey builder (ADR-302, components/journey/v2/*) is the reference authoring surface: a guided **Spark** wizard drafts the entity from a few plain answers (deferred creation, nothing persists until a reviewed title is committed), then a full editor carries a **Vera composer** that builds the entity from a line and applies plain-language edits in place. The Practice builder (ADR-143, the Studio `PracticeBuilder`) had the editor and autosave but none of Vera's authoring capabilities: no guided spark, no "Build with Vera", no "Edit with Vera". The owner's directive: make the Practice builder "very similar to the Journey builder, use all the same Vera functions, but make it specific to Practices." The constraint: a Practice is an ATOM (one act, no phase/module/lesson tree, no weekly arc), unlike a Journey (a block-tree curriculum). So the Journey's structure-shaped Vera functions (composition of a four-Pillar week, per-slot coaching, the block-edit op list) do not transfer literally; the capabilities do.

**Decision.** Mirror the Journey builder's Vera capabilities and UX, retargeted to the Practice's flat field model, REUSING the shared Vera infrastructure rather than forking it. Specifically:
- **Reuse, don't fork, the AI kernel.** Every new path routes through `withVoice` (the locked voice primer), `completeRaw` (the forced-tool chokepoint), `MODELS`/tiering, and the `recordAiUsage` + `featureOverBudget` ledger, exactly as the Journey functions do. Degrade-to-null is preserved everywhere (AI off / over budget => the builder falls back to hand-entry).
- **New AI modules mirror the Journey ones, one-for-one in shape:** `practice-shape.ts` is the twin of `journey-shape.ts` (a `withPracticeShape` primer that sets the default Practice shape: name, card hook, one-line description, a 3-to-6-step guide, the five-minute rule, one Pillar). `practice-spark.ts` is the twin of `journey-spark.ts` but, because a Practice is an atom, drafts the WHOLE Practice in one pass (title, summary, description, body, pillar, cadence, duration) on Sonnet. `practice-edit.ts` is the twin of `journey-edit.ts`: a plain-language change returns the changed FIELDS (not a list of block ops, since there are no blocks) on Opus, re-bounded by the action before saving.
- **Deferred creation, like Journeys.** `/practices/new` renders `PracticeSpark` (who · the act · outcome · cadence · time → `draftPracticeSpark` → a reviewed draft). Only on commit does `createPracticeFromSparkAction` create the row (mapping Vera's suggested Pillar slug to `domain_id` via the existing `pillarIdsBySlug`), then redirect to the editor. The Crew+ author gate + Host+ auto-approve status logic is identical to the existing `createPracticeAction` (ADR-109). "Skip, I'll build it myself" creates a blank draft and opens the editor.
- **The editor carries a Vera composer.** `PracticeComposer` (the atom-level twin of `JourneyComposer`) shows "Build with Vera" until the practice has a guide (`buildPracticeWithVeraAction` drafts the whole practice from a line), then "Edit with Vera" (`applyVeraPracticeChangeAction` applies a plain-language change). It mounts in `PracticeBuilder` via a new optional `veraComposer` slot, so the Studio popup still composes standalone.
- **The existing `personalizePractice` (ADR-116, the claim wizard) stays as-is** and is unchanged; the new functions sit beside it.

**Consequences.** Authoring a Practice now feels identical to authoring a Journey: guided spark, review, then a Vera composer in the editor, all in the locked voice. No shared Vera code is duplicated (the kernel, voice primer, ledger, and budget are reused); the only new code is Practice-specific prompts + the three thin AI modules + the wizard/composer UI + the spark create-actions. The Practice builder remains on the Studio `PracticeBuilder` overlay (it is not converted to a full-page Journey-style shell), so it keeps the Studio kit and autosave; the Vera composer renders full-width inside that window above the fields, the structural echo of the Journey builder's full-width composer. `/practices/new` and `/practices/[id]/edit` keep the standard GLOBAL community rail (page-chrome default, like every member surface and like the Journey routes). Two new budget caps gate spend; both AI paths fail safe. No migration: the builder writes only existing `practices` columns (title, summary, description, body, cadence, duration_min, focus_details for the Pillar). No copy or Vera prompt carries em or en dashes; the prompts run the CONTENT-VOICE rules through the shared `withVoice` primer.

## ADR-359: The Menu Manager is a TEMPLATE PAGE of five independently-arrangeable blocks, scoped to one active surface via the x-search seam

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-22). Refines ADR-357 (the DB-backed Menu Manager) and ADR-294/270 (the per-route module engine). Code: new `lib/menus/active-surface.ts` (`activeMenuSurface`); five new layout-module RSCs `components/widgets/menu/{menu-surface,menu-groups,menu-speed,menu-layout,menu-rail-cards}-block.tsx` bound in `lib/widgets/registry.tsx`; their client editors `components/admin/menu/{menu-surface-picker,menu-groups-editor,menu-layout-panel,menu-rail-cards-editor}.tsx`; `lib/widgets/modules.ts` (five meta entries + `MENU_MODULE_IDS` in render order, replacing `menu-manager`); `app/(main)/admin/menu/page.tsx` (header subtitle via `resolvePageContent`); the Settings trim in `components/admin/page-settings/page-settings-module.tsx` + new `components/admin/page-settings/subtitle-editor.tsx`; `/admin/menu` added to `lib/layout/editable-content.ts` `CONTENT_EDIT_ROUTES`; a `/admin/menu` case in `lib/widgets/modules.test.ts`. Removes `components/widgets/menu/menu-manager-block.tsx`, `components/admin/menu/menu-manager.tsx`, `components/admin/menu/menu-editor.tsx`.

**Context.** ADR-357 shipped the Menu Manager as ONE coupled `menu-manager` module: a single self-fetching RSC that read all five surfaces' menus + the speed settings server-side and handed them to one big client builder, which held the active surface in `useState` and owned its own two-thirds / one-third layout. That made the page un-arrangeable: the on-page Layout panel offered a single block, so an operator could not reorder, hide, or role-gate the editor's parts the way every other module-driven page allows. The owner asked (repeatedly) for the page to BE a template page composed of five independently-arrangeable blocks, and for that page's Settings to drop everything except a Subtitle + the Layout editor.

**Decision.** Decompose the single editor into FIVE registered layout modules, in this render order: `menu-surface` (the surface picker, the ONLY block that sets the active surface), `menu-groups` (menu-level links + groups/sub-groups with their links, drag/drop within and across groups, per-item depth, modes, the per-role matrix — the bulk of the editor), `menu-speed` (the global open/dwell/fade panel, no surface dependency), `menu-layout` (the active surface's column count + Seed/Reset from defaults), and `menu-rail-cards` (the surface's left/right featured cards). Each is a self-fetching, janitor-gated RSC that returns null below janitor, so the page is a header + `<PageModules route="/admin/menu">` and an operator arranges all five from Settings → Layout. The retired `menu-manager` id is deleted from the catalog, the registry, and `MENU_MODULE_IDS`.

The four surface-scoped blocks (surface, groups, layout, rail-cards) stay in lock-step on one active surface WITHOUT shared client state by reusing the `/practices` `x-search` seam: the Surface picker navigates to `?surface=<key>` (a client `router.push`), the proxy stamps the query onto the `x-search` request header (proxy.ts), and `lib/menus/active-surface.ts` (`activeMenuSurface()`) reads it back, defaulting to the first surface (`public_discover`) when unset. A nested PageModule never receives the page's `searchParams`, so this request-header seam is how the picker re-scopes its three sibling blocks; each surface-scoped block keys its client editor by the resolved surface so a re-scope remounts it with fresh state.

The blocks edit INDEPENDENT slices via the existing actions (groups → categories/items; layout → columns + `seedMenuFromDefaults`; rail-cards → `menu_rail_cards`), so they need no shared state. The one piece of coupling is handled explicitly: **materialize-on-default runs in EXACTLY ONE place, `menu-groups`** (the primary editor) — a never-customized surface (synthetic ids) is materialized into real DB rows once on open, so per-item edits land on real rows. `menu-layout` and `menu-rail-cards` must NOT each fire their own materialize (three blocks racing `seedMenuFromDefaults` would clobber); they only read `getAdminMenu` and `ensureMenu` lazily on their own first write. The empty-row→default fallback + the single auto-materialize are preserved, as are all editor capabilities (drag/drop, role matrix, modes, per-item depth, columns, seed/reset, rail cards, the left-rail Profile pin).

The page's Settings are trimmed on `/admin` routes (`pathname.startsWith('/admin')`) to ONLY a Subtitle editor (the page description) + the Layout editor — Basics (title + header image), Status & visibility, and SEO/share-image are dropped, since admin workspaces own their own title/chrome, are access-gated by the `/admin` layout, and are not search-indexed. The Subtitle editor reuses the existing `page-content` get/save actions (no migration): `/admin/menu` is added to `CONTENT_EDIT_ROUTES`, the page reads its header description via `resolvePageContent('/admin/menu', fallback)` with the coded line as fallback, and the editor saves description-only (blank clears the override).

**Consequences.** A janitor can now reorder, hide, or role-gate each of the five menu blocks from the on-page Layout panel like any other module-driven page, and the surface picker re-scopes the surface-bound blocks through one request-header seam rather than a shared client store. Because the layout resolver (`moduleAssignments`) filters unknown ids and appends unplaced ids in registry order, any layout an operator had saved against the old `menu-manager` id is harmlessly ignored and the five new blocks render in default order — a clean, migration-free transition. The reads stay best-effort (`getAdminMenu` falls back to `lib/menus/defaults.ts` per surface), so the page renders pre-migration. Trade-off: the surface picker now costs a client navigation per switch (it was local `setState`), which the `x-search` seam requires; in exchange the page composes the framework instead of hand-rolling its own layout. No copy carries em or en dashes.

## ADR-360: One member-facing timer, Mindless with Be Still / Get Moving modes (the two timers unified)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-22). Supersedes the two-separate-timers framing of ADR-346 (the Movement timer as a second member-facing timer beside Mindless) at the MEMBER layer; the underlying `timer_kind` enum, `lib/movement.ts`, and `lib/on-air.ts` engines are unchanged. Builds on ADR-229 (On Air / Mindless), ADR-353 (Mindless modes + the completion economy), and ADR-354 (a generic open is neutral). Naming recorded in [NAMING.md](NAMING.md) (§The Quest On Air/Mindless entry + the Retired list); the member doc is [ON-AIR.md](ON-AIR.md) ("The unified timer" section); member help is `content/help/the-quest/on-air.md` (the former `content/help/the-quest/movement.md` redirects into it). All member copy follows [CONTENT-VOICE.md](CONTENT-VOICE.md) §3 + §10 (plain, skeptic test, no em or en dashes). **Docs-only ADR:** the timer-unification CODE is owned by a separate workstream; this records the decision + the member-facing names.

**Context.** ADR-346 added a SECOND member-facing timer, **Movement**, beside **Mindless**: a Walk / Run / Yoga / Strength / Stretch / Play interval timer sitting on its own door, while Mindless held the quiet sit. That gave the member two timer names to learn and two doors to find before doing the thing, and it implied a visible taxonomy (which timer is "mine"?). It also pushed toward surfacing crossover scoring (a practice that develops more than one Pillar, e.g. breathwork = Body + Spirit, yoga = Body + Spirit) as a member-facing rubric, which fits the precious-wellness register the brand explicitly rejects. The owner decided to unify the two into one member-facing timer.

**Decision.** There is now **ONE** member-facing practice timer, **Mindless**, tagline **"Get out of your head, and into your life."** with two modes the member toggles between:
- **Be Still** = the former Mindless sit (sub-modes Meditate / Breathe / Stillness / Ritual / Journal / Just Log).
- **Get Moving** = the former Movement timer (sub-modes Walk / Run / Yoga / Strength / Stretch / Play).

Mode labels are EXACTLY `Be Still` and `Get Moving`; the tagline is EXACTLY "Get out of your head, and into your life." **"Movement" is RETIRED as a separate member-facing timer name** (it is now the Get Moving mode; NAMING.md Retired list). **The mode auto-selects from the launching practice's `timer_kind`:** `mindless` → Be Still · `movement` → Get Moving · `none` → Be Still (defaulting to Just Log). A generic open (the Free sit / a non-practice-specific entry, per ADR-354) lands on **Be Still** and **remembers the last mode** used. The two existing engines are reused under one door, unchanged: Be Still runs `lib/on-air.ts` (the breath patterns), Get Moving runs `lib/movement.ts` (the interval engine, ADR-346). **Scoring stays INTERNAL and unchanged:** a practice may develop more than one Pillar through the existing `focus_details` map, but this is never surfaced as a visible rubric, and there is **exactly one Zap reward per session**: a session in either mode ends through the same `completeSession` → `logPractice()` path, and nothing else pays. "On Air" remains the INTERNAL name (routes, schema, `timer_kind`, this ADR family).

**Rationale.** One door = **one daily anchor, one streak, one identity, the lowest friction**, which is what habit formation wants (a single keystone behavior beats two competing ones). A single plain timer with a mode toggle also fits the **skeptical, anti-precious demographic** (CONTENT-VOICE §2) far better than a two-timer taxonomy with visible crossover scoring: the member does the thing instead of learning a naming scheme first, and the multi-Pillar crossover stays scoring metadata rather than a precious member-facing scoreboard. Keeping **one reward per session** preserves the load-bearing invariant that **On Air is a stage, never a second economy** (ADR-229): no mode and no Pillar-crossover multiplies the payout.

**Consequences.** The member learns one timer, not two, and reaches the right engine through a mode toggle that the practice pre-sets for them. The economy, the streak, the reveal, the completion/partial-finish path (ADR-353), and the neutral-open rule (ADR-354) are all untouched. Because `timer_kind`, `lib/movement.ts`, and `lib/on-air.ts` are unchanged, this is a member-presentation and naming change at the doc/help layer plus the (separately-owned) UI that renders one door with two modes; ADR-346's schema/engine decisions stand, only its two-doors member framing is superseded. Help consolidates onto `content/help/the-quest/on-air.md`; the old Movement help page points at the one timer. No copy carries em or en dashes.

## ADR-361: My Contacts as a lightweight in-person CRM that feeds the paid Spaces CRM

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-23). Owner-approved direction; **strategy-first** (this ADR + [CRM-STRATEGY.md](CRM-STRATEGY.md) precede any code). Builds on ADR-098 (the `network_contacts` capture tool, [NETWORK-CRM.md](NETWORK-CRM.md)), ADR-099 (scan → shared CRM → invite → credit), ADR-102 (the operator `crm_deals`/`crm_stages`/`crm_activities` pipeline), ADR-130 (the unified person), ADR-172 (My Contacts re-homed to `/network/contacts`), and the Spaces tenancy line (ADR-320…327, [SPACES.md](SPACES.md)). Gating rides the `businessCrm` surface in `lib/core/access-matrix.ts`. All member-facing copy is provisional pending [NAMING.md](NAMING.md) + [CONTENT-VOICE.md](CONTENT-VOICE.md). **This is a strategy/decision ADR;** the build lands across P1–P3 in [CRM-STRATEGY.md](CRM-STRATEGY.md).

**Context.** "We don't have much of a CRM yet" is only half true: a private personal contact book (`network_contacts`, with notes + tags) and a full operator pipeline (`crm_*`, ADR-102) both ship today — they are just not connected, and the personal book lacks the relationship layer that makes a personal CRM sticky. Separately, the owner wants My Contacts to be the **in-person / local** book (cards and in-person QR scans land there), with a super-lightweight pipeline (follow-up reminders, sorting, tags, notes) that is slick enough to make a business owner, practitioner, or org want to upgrade to a full CRM — i.e. the free tier should be a **lead generator** for paid Spaces, flowing seamlessly into the fuller CRM a Space runs. Category research (Dex, Clay/Folk, HiHello, HubSpot free→paid, Pipedrive, Attio) is consistent: the winning shape is a free *relationship keeper* and a paid *operating system*, with the free tier engineered to manufacture the upgrade moment.

**Decision.** Adopt a **freemium barbell**. (1) **Free — My Contacts** (`/network/contacts`): capture (card scan + **in-person QR** + manual), tags, notes, sorting, **follow-up reminders + last-contacted + a daily "reach out today" list**; private, single-owner. Tabs are `All · Card · QR Scan · New · Active · Archived` (two facets — source: Card=`card_scan|poster`, QR Scan=new `source='qr_scan'`; and status: New/Active/Archived). (2) **Paid — Spaces CRM**: multiple pipelines + per-segment stage templates (sales / client-journey / supporter-lifecycle), automation/sequences, team sharing + roles, email/calendar sync, reporting, branding removal — reusing the existing `crm_*` tables scoped per `space_id`. (3) **In-person QR capture is one-way + met-context** (owner-chosen): a signed-in member scanning another member's personal `/q/<slug>` connect code creates a private `network_contact` (`source='qr_scan'`, `linked_profile_id` = code owner) pre-filled from the owner's public profile and stamped with met-context (event/Space, date, city) in the existing `details` jsonb, plus a follow-up affordance at the moment of scan; the **reciprocal handshake is a consent-gated fast-follow**, not in the first cut. (4) **Graduation is a scope flip, not a migration**: a member running a Space can bring their `network_contacts` into the Space's `contacts(space_id)` and seed `crm_deals`, reusing `linked_contact_id` + `crm-sync.ts`; the upgrade is surfaced contextually at the free-tier ceilings (second pipeline, a teammate, an automation). The data model follows the canonical Person · lifecycle-status · activity-timeline · deal · pipeline/stage · owner+workspace-scope primitives, modeling lead/contact/client as a **status**, not a separate table, so personal → Space needs no data move. The only genuinely new primitives are a `network_contact_reminders` table and a `last_contacted_at` column; `'qr_scan'` is added to the `source` check constraint.

**Rationale.** Building on the two ends we already own (personal `network_contacts` + operator `crm_*`) is far lower-risk and faster than a greenfield CRM, and keeps a single person spine (`linked_contact_id`) rather than forking a third contact table. The free features chosen (capture, notes, reminders, the reach-out list) are the ones that create a daily habit and the "aha"; the paid features (pipelines, automation, collaboration) are the ones a person needs precisely when they are running a practice or business — i.e. when a Space is the right home — so the line is a natural upgrade trigger rather than an artificial crippling. One-way + met-context QR capture is the slick, zero-consent-friction moment that turns a meeting into a CRM entry, while deferring the reciprocal exchange keeps the first cut small and consent-clean. Modeling lifecycle as status and scope as owner→`space_id` is the standard way to let a personal book graduate into a team CRM without a rewrite. Riding the existing zaps doctrine ("pay for real outcomes, not rows", NETWORK-CRM.md) keeps the gamification honest.

**Consequences.** P1 adds the keep-in-touch layer + tab facets behind one small additive migration; P2 extends the `/q/<slug>` resolver for in-person capture with no new tables; P3 wires the paid `/spaces/<slug>/crm` over the existing pipeline and the graduation flow. The free tier becomes a measurable lead funnel into Spaces (instrument the ceiling-hit upgrade events). Two open items: the duplicate contact surfaces (`/network/contacts` vs the Friends `ContactsList`) must be reconciled to one home before the CRM layer is added twice, and the reciprocal-handshake consent UX is designed in P2. Comms stays governed by [COMMS-CRM-ARCHITECTURE.md](COMMS-CRM-ARCHITECTURE.md) consent/suppression lanes — the free tier adds no marketing email. No member copy ships until it passes the naming + voice canons. No copy carries em or en dashes.

**P3 shipped (2026-06-23).** The graduation seam is live with **no migration** (the four CRM tables already carry `space_id` from ADR-331/333). Added: per-segment stage templates (`lib/crm/stage-templates.ts` `defaultStagesForSpaceType`, pure + tested) seeded idempotently by `ensureSpaceStages` on first CRM open; the graduation action `importContactsToSpace(spaceId, { status?, tag? })` (`lib/crm/graduation.ts`, owner + `spaceHasEntitlement(space,'crm')` gated, idempotent, fail-safe) bridging `network_contacts` into `contacts(space_id)` via the new space-scoped `syncContactToSpaceCrm` and seeding one open deal each; the per-Space CRM board (`app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/crm/`, Dashboard template, rail `'none'`) gated twice (the `crm` entitlement AND owner/admin) with a tasteful locked/upgrade state instead of a dead end; and a light dismissible upgrade prompt on My Contacts. The pricing/entitlements model (`lib/core/access-matrix.ts`, `lib/spaces/entitlements.ts`) was NOT restructured: this work only READS `spaceHasEntitlement`. An operator turns CRM on per Space by setting `spaces.entitlements {crm:true}` today; automating that is the separate pricing track's job.

## ADR-362: Pricing entitlements on three independent flags, operator-managed, shipped OFF by default

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-23). Implements pricing **P1** — the entitlements + admin-config foundation for the owner's "Frequency — Pricing Model & Feature Gating Spec." Builds on `lib/core/entitlement.ts` (`isPaid`/`canCashIn`/`deriveTier` over `profiles.membership_tier`), the `community_role` ladder decoupled from billing (ADR-207), the Space `plan`/`entitlements` columns + default-deny `spaceHasEntitlement` (ADR-322), the env-gated Stripe layer (`lib/billing/stripe.ts` `billingEnabled`), and the operator-config precedent (`platform_flags`/`platform_settings`/`page_chrome_overrides` and their admin surfaces). Sibling doc: [PRICING.md](PRICING.md). Stripe wiring is **P2**; wiring the gates into live surfaces is **P3**. All operator/member copy passes [NAMING.md](NAMING.md) + [CONTENT-VOICE.md](CONTENT-VOICE.md) (no em dashes).

**Context.** The product needs a real pricing model (member Crew/Supporter; Space practitioner/business/organization/white-label; take-rates; a Vera free cap; founder price-locks) but must NOT start charging or make live Stripe calls before that layer is built, tested, and an operator deliberately turns it on. We already own the two halves of the entitlement spine — the personal tier (`membership_tier`) and the Space plan (`plan`/`entitlements`) — so the risk is in conflating axes (binding the earned community role or the game to billing) and in a half-built layer accidentally charging or locking people out.

**Decision.** Model pricing on **three independent flags** and ship the whole layer **OFF**. (1) **billing_tier** is what someone pays for: personal = the existing `profiles.membership_tier` (reused, no new column), space = the existing `spaces.plan` (reused). (2) **community_role** is earned and never set by billing (unchanged). (3) **gamification_access** is derived from `billing_tier` by default (member = earn-only, crew+ = full) but is an INDEPENDENT, overridable switch via a new nullable `profiles.gamification_access_override` (resolved by `resolveGamificationAccess` = `override ?? derive(tier)`). The editable VALUES (prices in cents, take-rate bps, Vera cap, trial, annual discount) live in a new `pricing_settings(key, value jsonb)` table seeded from the spec; the feature→entitlement map lives in code (`lib/pricing/gates.ts` `FEATURE_GATES`, the source of truth) with a new `pricing_feature_gates(feature, min_entitlement, enabled)` table as a FAIL-SAFE override layer merged OVER the code map exactly as `page-chrome.ts` merges chrome overrides (`mergeGate`↔`mergeChrome`). The switches reuse `platform_flags`: a master `billing_live` (default OFF), per-tier/plan `*_enabled` (OFF), per-role `gamification_full_*`. The live gate is `billingLive()` = `billingEnabled()` (env keys) AND `billing_live`, so billing is OFF even with keys present. `setSpacePlan(spaceId, plan)` (the function P2's webhook will call) writes the plan and additively expands `spaces.entitlements` from `planEntitlements(plan)`, but is gated on `billingLive()` (a no-op while OFF) and does NOT restructure `spaceHasEntitlement`. A janitor-gated `/admin/pricing` console (Operations → Platform) edits every value, the gate matrix, the switches, and the founder lock, and shows a read-only Stripe status. Two `profiles` columns support the founder lock: `is_founding_member` + `locked_price_id` (display in P1, honored at checkout in P2).

**Rationale.** Three flags keep the axes from colliding: paying never grants earned standing, and the game is grantable/withholdable independent of billing (comps, demotions) without touching tier or role. Reusing `membership_tier` + `plan`/`entitlements` avoids a parallel tier system and keeps one source of truth per axis. Putting the feature map in code with a DB override mirrors the proven `page_chrome_overrides` pattern (operator-tunable without a deploy, fail-safe to code). Shipping OFF with `featureAllowed` short-circuiting to grant-all while `billing_live` is false makes the entire layer behavior-preserving: it can be merged, reviewed, and demoed with zero risk of charging or locking anyone out, and P2 turns it on by flipping one switch. The whole thing is additive + idempotent and every read is fail-safe to the seeded defaults.

**Consequences.** P1 adds one additive migration (`20260723010000_pricing_foundation.sql`: the three `profiles` columns, `pricing_settings`, `pricing_feature_gates`, the flags, RLS service-role/admin-gated with a rollback comment), `lib/pricing/*` (plans, gamification, gates, settings, space-plan; pure helpers unit-tested), and the `/admin/pricing` console. Nothing charges and no Stripe call is made. The new tables are not yet in `lib/database.types.ts`; the readers reach them untyped (ADR-246) and fail-safe to the code defaults until the migration is applied + types regenerated. Existing gated surfaces are deliberately NOT rewired in P1 (risk of changing live behavior); the helpers are exposed for P2/P3 to consult once billing is live. Open items for P2: Stripe checkout for tiers/plans, the webhook calling `setSpacePlan`, and honoring the founder lock at checkout.

## ADR-363: Pricing P2 — Stripe products/prices + subscription checkout + webhook entitlements, still shipped OFF

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-23). Implements pricing **P2** on top of ADR-362 (P1). Wires Stripe to the entitlements layer: a Product/Price catalog synced from the operator pricing values, subscription Checkout for member tiers / space plans / paid space memberships, and webhook reconciliation back to `setSpacePlan` + the membership payment status, with the founder lock honored at checkout. Sibling doc: [PRICING.md](PRICING.md) (P2 section). Reuses the env-gated Stripe layer (`lib/billing/stripe.ts`, `checkout.ts`, `connect.ts`, `fees.ts`, `tips.ts`, `tickets.ts`) and the P1 gate (`billingLive()`). P3 (wiring `featureAllowed` into live surfaces) is unchanged. All copy passes [NAMING.md](NAMING.md) + [CONTENT-VOICE.md](CONTENT-VOICE.md) (no em dashes).

**Context.** P1 built the entitlements + admin-config foundation but made no Stripe call and stored no resolved Price ids. To charge, we need: Stripe Products/Prices that mirror the editable `pricing_settings` values; subscription Checkout for the three paid surfaces (member Crew/Supporter, a Space owner buying a plan, a member joining a paid Space tier); the webhook to flip the right entitlement; and the founder price-lock (`is_founding_member` + `locked_price_id`) actually applied. The hard constraints: it must STILL ship OFF (no charge / no live Stripe call until env keys + `billing_live` + the per-tier switch are all on), it must not duplicate Stripe objects on re-sync, and `pnpm test`/`pnpm build` must never make a Stripe call.

**Decision.** Add one additive migration (`20260723020000_pricing_stripe.sql`): `pricing_stripe_prices(key PK, stripe_product_id, stripe_price_id, archived)` as the resolved Product/Price map (service-role RLS, no client policy, like `pricing_settings`); `space_membership_tiers.stripe_product_id`; `space_memberships.stripe_subscription_id` + `payment_status CHECK(pending|active|past_due|canceled) default pending`; `spaces.stripe_customer_id` + `stripe_subscription_id`. Add `lib/billing/pricing-keys.ts` (PURE price-key, take-rate, and founder-lock selection math), `pricing-prices.ts` (the map IO, fail-safe), and `pricing-products.ts` `syncPricingProductsToStripe()` — admin-TRIGGERED only (the `/admin/pricing` "Sync products to Stripe" action), gated on `billingEnabled()`, idempotent (Products looked up by a `frequency_pricing_key` metadata; immutable Prices reused on amount+interval match), founder Prices stored `archived=true`. Add `space-plan-checkout.ts` (`createSpacePlanCheckout`, customer = the space owner, metadata `kind:'space_plan'`, gated on `billingLive()` + the per-plan switch) and `space-membership-checkout.ts` (`createSpaceMembershipCheckout`, a Connect destination charge with the application fee = the SPACE plan's take-rate read from `pricing_settings` via the new `fees.ts` `spaceTakeRateCents`, metadata `kind:'space_membership'`, gated). Extend `createMembershipCheckout` to honor the founder lock (`locked_price_id` → synced founder Price → public Price → env fallback). Route the EXISTING membership webhook (`app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts`) by `metadata.kind` through `space-subscriptions.ts` FIRST: `space_plan` → `setSpacePlan(space_id, plan|free)` + persist the subscription/customer ids; `space_membership` → upsert the subscription id + `payment_status`; a subscription with no `kind` falls through to the untouched member Crew/Supporter path. Extend `/admin/pricing` with the sync action + a read-only resolved-price panel (sync disabled when env missing).

**Rationale.** Reusing the existing env-gate (`billingEnabled`) + master switch (`billingLive`) keeps the OFF invariant identical to P1: every checkout returns null and the sync no-ops until an operator deliberately turns billing on, so P2 merges and demos with zero risk of a charge. Metadata-keyed idempotency (Products by `frequency_pricing_key`, Prices by amount+interval) makes re-syncing safe and matches Stripe's immutable-Price model. Routing by `metadata.kind` in the one subscription webhook reuses the proven `stripe_webhook_events` idempotency claim and the destination-charge pattern from tips/tickets, and leaves the member path byte-for-byte unchanged. Putting the price-key / take-rate / founder-lock decisions in PURE helpers (`pricing-keys.ts`) keeps the testable core free of Stripe so the unit tests never touch the network — satisfying the "no Stripe call in test/build" constraint while still covering the logic that matters.

**Consequences.** P2 adds the migration above (additive + idempotent, rollback comment, untyped reads per ADR-246 until types regenerate), `lib/billing/{pricing-keys,pricing-prices,pricing-products,space-plan-checkout,space-membership-checkout,space-subscriptions}.ts` + `fees.ts` `spaceTakeRateCents`, the `createMembershipCheckout` founder-lock extension, the webhook routing, and the admin sync UI. Nothing charges and no live Stripe call fires while billing is OFF; the sync is the only path that calls Stripe and it is env-gated and admin-triggered. Tests cover the pure logic (`pricing-keys.test.ts`, `space-subscriptions.test.ts`) with the Stripe client never instantiated. Open for P3: wiring `featureAllowed`/`resolveGamificationAccess` into live surfaces, a member-facing space-plan/membership checkout UI, and dunning/proration polish once billing is turned on.

## ADR-364: Pricing P3 — member-facing surfaces on the operator values, white-label as a high-touch lead (not checkout), the cash-in gate routed through `featureAllowed`; still shipped OFF

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-23). Implements pricing **P3** on top of ADR-362 (P1) + ADR-363 (P2). Puts the layer in front of people: the member upgrade surface, a Space owner plan picker, a Space membership join CTA, and a white-label request flow, all rendered from the operator-editable values and all GATED so the whole thing still ships OFF. Wires the `vault_cash_in` gate through `featureAllowed` where it is clearly safe and additive. Sibling doc: [PRICING.md](PRICING.md) (P3 + "Status & deferred"). Reuses the P1/P2 seams (`getPricingValues`, `billingLive`, `featureAllowed`, `spacePlanSellable`, `createMembershipCheckout`/`createSpacePlanCheckout`/`createSpaceMembershipCheckout`) and the existing `contacts` lead table. All copy passes [NAMING.md](NAMING.md) + [CONTENT-VOICE.md](CONTENT-VOICE.md) (no em dashes).

**Context.** P1/P2 built the entitlements + Stripe layer but exposed it nowhere member-facing; the `/upgrade` page still showed a hardcoded "$10" and the free-beta toggle, the Space plan was invisible to owners, and a Space membership "join" recorded a row with no payment path. P3 needs to surface tiers/plans/join from the operator config without (a) charging anyone, (b) firing a live Stripe call in build/test, or (c) changing today's behavior while billing is OFF. White-label ($2,000 setup + a monthly fee) is the deliberately-expensive door in the owner's network-effect strategy: it should be a high-touch lead a human closes, not a self-serve checkout that lets anyone flip on their own branded silo and weaken the shared network.

**Decision.** Add a PURE display layer (`lib/pricing/display.ts`: `formatCents`/`priceRow`/`memberTierRows`/`spacePlanRows`) so every surface renders `getPricingValues()` and never a hardcoded price, and a `memberTierSellable(tier)` sell-gate mirroring `spacePlanSellable(plan)` (`billingLive()` AND the per-tier switch, FAIL-SAFE FALSE). Rewire `/upgrade` to show the operator Crew/Supporter prices + a Founding-Member badge (`is_founding_member`) and gate the CTA on `memberTierSellable` (OFF → the unchanged free-beta toggle + a disabled price preview; ON → the existing `createMembershipCheckout`, founder lock already honored). Add a Space billing surface (`app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/settings/billing/`: a plan picker → `createSpacePlanCheckout`, linked from the Manage hub and matching the `success_url` P2 already used). Add a paid Space membership join: when `billingLive()` the join card opens `createSpaceMembershipCheckout`, with a fallback to the existing display-only `joinTier` (so OFF, and any not-payable case, preserves today's behavior exactly). Make white-label a LEAD: a `requestWhitelabel` action writes a `contacts` row (`source='whitelabel_request'`, space context in `meta`), reusing the beta-waitlist seam, with NO Stripe call. Route the Vault **cash-in** action (`redeemItem`) additionally through `featureAllowed('vault_cash_in', …, { billingLive })` on top of the existing `canCashIn(tier)` line. Gates that risk changing live behavior (leaderboard "compete", `vera_unlimited`, the `space_*` plan-feature gates which already resolve via `spaceHasEntitlement`, `gamification_full`) are deliberately NOT rewired and are tracked in PRICING.md "Status & deferred".

**Rationale.** Rendering from `getPricingValues()` keeps one source of truth (the admin console) and removes the hardcoded prices. Gating every CTA on the existing sell-gates means OFF is structurally safe: while `billing_live` is OFF, `memberTierSellable`/`spacePlanSellable` are false (disabled previews), `createSpaceMembershipCheckout` returns `billing_off` (the join card silently uses the unchanged `joinTier`), and `featureAllowed` short-circuits to grant-all (the cash-in action behaves exactly as today). White-label-as-lead is the network-effect call: a self-serve white-label checkout would let operators peel off into branded silos that don't reinforce the shared network, so the expensive door is a human conversation, captured as a `contacts` lead an operator follows up on — reusing the proven lead seam, no new table. Routing only `vault_cash_in` through `featureAllowed` (and explicitly deferring the riskier gates) honors the "if rewiring risks changing current behavior, expose the seam and defer" rule.

**Consequences.** P3 adds NO migration (reuses P1/P2 tables + the existing `contacts` table). New: `lib/pricing/display.ts` + `memberTierSellable`, the Space billing route (`page.tsx`/`plan-picker.tsx`/`whitelabel-request.tsx`/`actions.ts`), the `startSpaceMembershipCheckout` action seam, the join-card paid path, the `/upgrade` rewrite, and the `redeemItem` gate wire. Tests cover the pure display helpers + the `vault_cash_in` gate semantics (`lib/pricing/pricing.test.ts`); no Stripe client is instantiated in test/build (every Stripe call sits behind `billingLive()`/`billingEnabled()`). Founding-member founder pricing is honored at checkout (P2) and now surfaced (the badge). Deferred items are listed in PRICING.md "Status & deferred" (the leaderboard/compete gate, `resolveGamificationAccess` consumers, `vera_unlimited`, the `space_*` gates, household/Circle bundle, dunning/proration, conversion-mechanics polish).

## ADR-365: Accepted advisor exceptions + RLS/view hardening

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-23). Records the disposition of the standing Supabase advisor findings from the audit sweep. Migrations: `20260724000000_wrap_reminders_rls_auth.sql`, `20260724000100_practices_ranked_security_invoker.sql`. Source forward-fix: `20260723000000_network_contacts_crm_p1.sql`.

**Context.** The advisor reported two ERROR-level `security_definer_view` / `rls_disabled` items plus `auth_rls_initplan` warnings on the new `network_contact_reminders` table (shipped in ADR-361 P1). Each needs a verdict: fix in place, or accept with a reason.

**Decision.** (a) `spatial_ref_sys` reporting `rls_disabled_in_public` is a PostGIS extension-owned table; a non-superuser role cannot `ALTER` it to enable RLS, and it holds only static reference data (the spatial reference catalog). The advisory is benign and unactionable, so it is **accepted** as a standing exception. (b) The four `network_contact_reminders` policies wrap `auth.uid()` in `(select auth.uid())` so Postgres evaluates it once per query (initplan), matching the repo-wide pattern set by `20260615200000`. The fix lands as a forward-fix to the P1 source migration (so a fresh DB is born correct) AND an idempotent sweep migration (`20260724000000`) so an already-applied DB is corrected on the next run. (c) `practices_ranked` is reset to `security_invoker = on` (`20260724000100`): it was created that way (`20260606160000`) but `20260617000000` recreated it with `CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW` without restating the option, reverting it to the implicit `security_definer` and tripping the advisor.

**Consequences.** One of the two standing ERROR advisories clears (`practices_ranked`); the other (`spatial_ref_sys`) is documented as accepted. The reminders policies are perf-aligned with the rest of public RLS. Both new migrations are idempotent and carry rollback comments; neither changes access semantics. Migrations are not auto-applied; they ship with the branch and apply on the next deploy.

## ADR-366: Per-Space functions gated by member role, controlled by operator and owner

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-23). Migration: `20260725000000_spaces_feature_roles.sql` (`spaces.feature_roles` jsonb + `space_function_type_defaults`). Resolver: `lib/spaces/functions.ts` (pure). Surfaces: every `/spaces/<slug>/settings/*` page + their server actions; operator grid `/admin/spaces/[id]`; per-type defaults editor `/admin/spaces/defaults`; owner panel `/spaces/<slug>/settings/features`. Provision seed: `lib/spaces/provision.ts`.

**Context.** A Space's tools (CRM, email, members, QR, the per-type surfaces, plan/billing, profile/brand) were gated ad hoc: each surface re-derived its own "can this viewer open it" from `canEditProfile` / `isAdmin`, with no per-Space control over WHICH role may use a tool or whether a tool is on at all. Two needs drove a single model: (1) a Space owner wants to delegate one tool to a moderator without making them an admin, and turn off tools they do not use; (2) the platform operator wants to set sane per-type starting defaults and an absolute override. A LATENT bug made this urgent: `lib/spaces/store.ts` never projected `spaces.entitlements` onto the resolved Space, so `spaceHasEntitlement(space,'crm')` always read `undefined` and the per-Space CRM board was locked for everyone (the CRM-projection fix).

**Decision.** Gate every Space function on **two axes**, resolved by one pure function `spaceFunctionAccess(space, fn, viewerSpaceRole)`:
- **ON/OFF (entitlements).** Reuse `spaces.entitlements`. A PLAN-GATED function (CRM, email) reads its plan entitlement key (default-deny). A UNIVERSAL function keys the same blob by the function key, DEFAULT-ON (only an explicit `false` turns it off). No new column for the switch.
- **MIN-ROLE (feature_roles).** New `spaces.feature_roles` jsonb maps a function key to the lowest `SpaceRole` (viewer<editor<moderator<admin) that may use it, sparse against a CODE default (`DEFAULT_FUNCTION_ROLE`). An empty blob = every function at its code default, so today's gating is reproduced exactly.

**Control is two-tier.** The **operator** (janitor) holds an absolute switch per Space (`/admin/spaces/[id]`, the override beats the plan) and per-TYPE seed defaults (`/admin/spaces/defaults` → `space_function_type_defaults`, RLS-on, service-role-only, fail-safe to code defaults). The **owner** (owner/admin, `/spaces/<slug>/settings/features`) toggles functions WITHIN their plan and sets each function's min-role; they can never out-grant their plan (a plan-gated tool the plan lacks renders LOCKED with an upgrade nudge). A new Space **seeds** its `entitlements` + `feature_roles` from the per-type defaults merged over the code defaults (`seedSpaceConfigFromDefaults`, plan-gated tools never seeded on); fail-safe to code defaults, non-breaking for existing Spaces.

**Defense in depth.** Each surface gates BOTH the page render (a calm `FeatureLockedNotice`, never a 404 of a visible Space) AND its server action through the same resolver, so a lowered/disabled per-Space setting is enforced on the write, not just hidden in the UI. A platform janitor previewing a Space they do not manage keeps the existing read-only preview (the resolver is bypassed for the staff-view render; every write stays gated).

**Consequences.** Adding a gateable tool is one registry row in `lib/spaces/functions.ts` (+ one entitlement key if plan-gated), never a schema change. The resolver is pure and fail-safe (unknown function, null/unknown role, or a malformed blob all deny). The CRM board unlocks for any Space whose plan grants CRM now that `entitlements` projects. The hub no longer renders dead cards for tools the viewer cannot use. CRM + email cards on the settings hub now hide when the plan lacks them (previously always shown); this is the intended Phase-2 behavior, consistent with the CRM board's own gate. Defaults reproduce today's role thresholds: universal tools at editor (= the old `canEditProfile`), check-in at moderator, billing + CRM + email at admin.

## ADR-370: Deferred pricing gates wired through `featureAllowed`, all NO-OP while billing is OFF

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-23). Finishes the deferred-gates tail on top of ADR-362 (P1) / ADR-363 (P2) / ADR-364 (P3): the nine items in [REMAINING-WORK.md](REMAINING-WORK.md) "Pricing" + [PRICING.md](PRICING.md) "Status & deferred". Wires the gates the prior phases deliberately left as seams (leaderboard compete, the `resolveGamificationAccess` consumer, `vera_unlimited`, the `space_*` plan-feature gates, a standalone `gamification_full` gate), plus the Household/Circle bundle plan path, the dunning/proration/past-due member UX, and the season-reset conversion nudge. Migration: `20260727000000_pricing_deferred_gates.sql`. All operator/member copy passes [NAMING.md](NAMING.md) + [CONTENT-VOICE.md](CONTENT-VOICE.md) (no em dashes).

**Context.** ADR-364 listed these as deferred for one reason: rewiring a live surface onto the pricing gates risked changing today's behavior while `billing_live` is OFF. The whole pricing/billing layer ships OFF (the master `billing_live` flag defaults OFF; `billingLive()` = `billingEnabled()` AND `billing_live`), and the product must behave byte-for-byte as today until an operator deliberately flips it. The seams were ready (`featureAllowed` short-circuits to grant-all while OFF; `resolveGamificationAccess` was pure + tested but had no consumer); what was missing was the inert wiring + the schema for the two surfaces that need state (the bundle link + a personal dunning status).

**Decision.** Route EVERY gate through the OFF-preserving seam so each is a no-op while billing is OFF. (1) **Leaderboard compete** (#1): the individual board gates on `gamificationFullAllowed(tier)` (→ `featureAllowed('gamification_full')`); OFF grants, so the board renders exactly as today; ON for an earn-only member shows a calm `CompeteLocked` "join to compete" preview (the collective goal + own standing stay visible). (2) **`resolveGamificationAccess` consumer** (#2): a new server seam `lib/pricing/gamification-access.ts` (`resolveViewerGamificationAccess` / `resolveGamificationAccessWithFlags`) folds the per-profile override → the operator per-role `gamification_full_*` flags → the derive-from-tier default, consumed live in `getCrewContext`; with the seeded flags it returns exactly `deriveGamificationAccess(tier)` (today's line). (3) **`vera_unlimited`** (#3): a new `lib/ai/vera/usage-gate.ts` enforces the operator `vera_free_daily_cap` against a member's daily Vera count, routed through `featureAllowed('vera_unlimited')`; OFF grants so the cap never bites (no extra read changes the answer); ON, a free member over the cap degrades to the deterministic concierge (the existing fallback, never an error). (4) **`space_*` gates** (#4): a new IO wrapper `lib/spaces/function-access.ts` `spaceFunctionAccessLive` composes the pure `spaceFunctionAccess` (role + `spaceHasEntitlement`) with `featureAllowed('space_crm'|'space_email'|…)`; OFF grants so it equals the pure result; wired into the CRM + email surfaces (the lib write-action defense-in-depth keeps the pure check, unchanged). (5) **`gamification_full` standalone gate** (#5): `gamificationFullAllowed` is the single tier gate, reused by the leaderboard + the season-reset nudge. (6) **Household/Circle bundle** (#6): `lib/pricing/bundle.ts` (pure config + price keys) + `bundleSellable()` (mirrors `memberTierSellable`/`spacePlanSellable`) + `lib/billing/bundle-checkout.ts` (`createBundleCheckout`, gated, returns null while OFF); the migration adds `profiles.household_bundle_id` (member → bundle owner) + the `household_bundle` config + the `bundle_household_enabled` flag (OFF). (7) **Dunning** (#7): `lib/pricing/dunning.ts` (`resolveMemberPaymentState`, GATED on `billingLive()` → returns `active` while OFF; `prorationNote` pure copy) + a `PastDueBanner` on `/settings/billing`; the migration adds `profiles.membership_payment_status` (nullable; NULL read as active). (8) **Conversion** (#8): `lib/pricing/conversion.ts` (pure season-reset timing) + a `SeasonResetPrompt` shown only when `!gamificationFull` AND the season is within the reset window (inert while OFF). (9) **Type regen** (#9): NOT done here (no DB access in the worktree); the new columns are reached untyped (ADR-246) and the parent session regenerates `lib/database.types.ts` at integration.

**Rationale.** The ABSOLUTE INVARIANT is "OFF = everything allowed, exactly as before." Routing every new gate through `featureAllowed` (which grants-all while OFF) or gating the whole surface on `billingLive()` makes that structural, not a matter of remembering: a reviewer can see the no-op from the call. The two state-bearing items default to today's behavior (NULL `membership_payment_status` reads as active; NULL `household_bundle_id` = not in a bundle), so an unapplied migration is also today's behavior. Wrapping the pure resolvers rather than editing them (`spaceFunctionAccessLive` over `spaceFunctionAccess`; the gamification seam over the pure derive) keeps the framework-independent core testable and the IO at the edge. Tests prove the invariant directly (`lib/pricing/deferred-gates.test.ts`).

**Consequences.** One additive, idempotent migration (`20260727000000_pricing_deferred_gates.sql`: `profiles.membership_payment_status` + `profiles.household_bundle_id` + index, the `household_bundle` setting, the `bundle_household_enabled` flag; rollback comment, SAFE to re-run, untyped reads until regen). New modules: `lib/pricing/{gamification-access,bundle,dunning,conversion}.ts`, `lib/spaces/function-access.ts`, `lib/ai/vera/usage-gate.ts`, `lib/billing/bundle-checkout.ts`, and the `CompeteLocked` / `SeasonResetPrompt` / `PastDueBanner` components. Nothing charges and no live Stripe call fires while billing is OFF; every reader is fail-safe to today's behavior. **Open for integration (#9):** regenerate `lib/database.types.ts` so these columns are typed, then remove the untyped casts that read them. The columns/casts blocked on the regen are: `profiles.gamification_access_override`, `profiles.membership_payment_status`, `profiles.household_bundle_id`, `spaces.plan` (read onto the Space in `lib/spaces/store.ts`), `space_memberships.payment_status` (existing, P2), and the `pricing_settings` / `pricing_feature_gates` / `pricing_stripe_prices` tables (existing, P1/P2). Until then the readers degrade to the seeded code defaults.
## ADR-371: Round-2 concurrency + correctness hardening (QA pass 3)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-23). A round-2 audit found a cluster of check-then-act races, swallowed-error ledger gaps, webhook replay/idempotency holes, and a calendar-math overflow. Migration: `20260726000000_round2_concurrency_guards.sql` (additive + idempotent, rollback note, SAFE to re-run). All code fixes verified against `tsc`, `lint`, the unit suite, `build`, and `check:authz`.
