3. **Embeddings (best-effort, FAIL-SAFE): `lib/resonance/embeddings.ts` + migration.** A 384-d gte-small resonance embedding per person (Pillars/Journeys/practices), stored in `resonance_embeddings (pgvector)` with an hnsw `vector_cosine_ops` index + a SECURITY DEFINER `resonance_neighbors` RPC, mirroring `event_embeddings` exactly. CRITICAL: until the migration applies + the `vector` extension is enabled, every read/write degrades to a no-op (try/catch, safe-empty), so the engine runs on graph-traversal candidates only and NEVER throws. The embedding is additive precision, never a dependency.
4. **Double-opt-in intros: migrations + `lib/resonance/{edges.ts, matches.ts}` + Vera tools.** Migrations add `resonance_consent` (per-person opt-IN to matching + opt-OUT as target, opt-in default deny), `resonance_edges` (canonical `(a_pid,b_pid)`, score, reasons jsonb, affinity, `expires_at` so stale edges age out), and `resonance_matches` (`a_optin`/`b_optin`/`accepted_at`, stamped only when both opted in). All four tables are RLS-on / no-client-policy (the fail-closed service-role pattern); edges are SENSITIVE-class. Two new Vera tools (`lib/ai/vera/tools.ts` + dispatch in `execute.ts`): `suggest_resonance_match` (read/propose) and `send_intro_email` (write, suggest-only) which refuses to send unless BOTH parties opted in (`bothPartiesOptedIn`) AND the recipient passes `resolveSendGate`. Nothing sends until both tap yes. Mutations self-guard: consent writes scope to the caller's own profile id, `recordMatchOptIn` requires the opter to be a party (confused-deputy guard).
5. **Surfacing + the nightly step.** A "Resonance" section on the Person view (`resonance-section.tsx`) and a Space-scoped match-suggestions section on the Space cockpit (`space-resonance-section.tsx`), both ADDED behind their own Suspense (the existing pages unchanged), reading the PERSISTED edges (no graph recompute on a request); the Space cockpit's "Intros accepted" stat now reads real accepted matches. The nightly cron (`app/api/cron/refresh-traits/route.ts`) calls `refreshResonanceEdges` AFTER the trait refresh (so the re-ranker reads tonight's receptiveness); it also writes the new `resonance_match_count` computed trait (declared in `lib/traits/registry.ts`, computed by an APPENDED `resonanceMatchCount` in `lib/traits/compute.ts`, no existing trait function touched). Best-effort + fail-safe: a missing table/extension is swallowed and the trait refresh still completes.

**Rationale + the fail-closed choices.** Reciprocity is the harmonic mean, not a tunable knob, so it can never be turned off. Consent is opt-IN (a person-to-person surface, like `email_marketing` in `lib/consent/scopes.ts`): say nothing and you are not in the pool. The intro is double-opt-in AND send-gated, two independent fail-closed gates. The privacy moat is the WHY: edges carry only shared BELONGING ("you are in the same Circle"), never a stalking-adjacent signal. The pgvector layer is best-effort because graph traversal is the reliable baseline and needs no new infra; making the embedding optional means the whole feature ships value the day the migrations apply, with precision arriving when the extension is enabled.

**Seams CONFIRMED vs ADAPTED.** Confirmed as named: `lib/crm/person.ts` (`resolvePerson`), `lib/traits/registry.ts` + `lib/traits/compute.ts`, `lib/ai/vera/tools.ts` + `execute.ts`, `lib/comms/send-gate.ts` (`resolveSendGate`), `lib/playbooks/registry.ts` (`effectiveAutonomyTier`), the Space cockpit + Person view pages, the trait cron. ADAPTED: (a) **community edges** are `memberships`/`circles` + `journey_enrollments` + `member_practices` (NOT a `spaces`-only graph); Pillars are derived from `practices.domain_id` (the column kept through the naming-canon rename, ADR-208), since there is no member-Pillar join table. (b) **`resonanceScore` name** already exists in `lib/ai/vera/today.ts` for the Today ranker (a different single-candidate signature); the Phase 4 `resonanceScore(A,B,aff)` lives in `lib/resonance/score.ts` with no collision (separate module, separate import). (c) the Space cockpit's "Intros accepted" stat was a Phase 2 placeholder marked "coming with the Resonance Graph"; it is now wired.

**Consequences.** Additive + fail-closed throughout; no Phase 1-3 signature changed, no existing trait function modified (merge-clean for the sibling Phase 5/6 agents). Gated behind the EXISTING `spaceHasEntitlement(space,'crm')` + owner/admin; NO new capability keys added to `entitlements.ts` / `function-access.ts`. New code: `lib/resonance/{score.ts (+test), candidates.ts, embeddings.ts, edges.ts, matches.ts, surface.ts}`, `app/(main)/admin/marketing/contacts/[id]/resonance-section.tsx`, `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/crm/space-resonance-section.tsx`. Changed: `lib/traits/{registry.ts (+resonance_match_count), compute.ts (+appended resonanceMatchCount)}`, `lib/ai/vera/{tools.ts (+2 tools), execute.ts (+sendIntroEmail dispatch)}`, `app/api/cron/refresh-traits/route.ts` (+resonance step), `app/(main)/admin/marketing/contacts/[id]/page.tsx` (+Resonance section), `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/crm/{page.tsx (+Resonance section), space-cockpit-band.tsx (+intros stat)}`. Migrations (all after `20260801000000`): `20260802000000_resonance_consent.sql`, `20260802000100_resonance_embeddings.sql`, `20260802000200_resonance_edges.sql`, `20260802000300_resonance_matches.sql`. No Notion page (a build-log, not operator instruction). `tsc` / `eslint` / `check:authz` / full vitest (2209 passed) green.

---

## ADR-386: Resonance Engine Phase 5 - gamification fuel + winback and dunning playbooks

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-24). Wires the existing non-monetary economy (streaks, freezes, Gems, ranks, Circles) as ENGINE FUEL and adds the two retention playbooks the loop was missing, built OVER the Phase 1 loop (ADR-382) + the Phase 3 safety rails (ADR-384). Additive + fail-closed throughout: outbound stays suggest-only, only the in-product freeze save may auto-run (and only when the autonomy slider allows), gem grants stay modest. Phase 5 of `docs/NEXT-GEN-CRM.md` ("Gamification + practice as fuel").

**Context.** Phase 1 made the streak-save playbook VISIBLE but it only recorded the touch (the freeze was never spent). The economy was non-monetary (the right shape for resonate-not-extract) but unwired to the engine. The two highest-leverage retention plays (a value-led winback, a warm dunning note) were stubs, and the social-fuel moves the doc calls for (Circle-feed celebration, a cooperative Circle streak, a one-tap nudge, the zero-ties structural-risk signal) did not exist. The plan's seams were confirmed against the real code; all matched, two ADAPTED (below).

**Decision.**
1. **Two new traits, additive + pure.** `decline_slope` (computed number, week-over-week practice-frequency drop in [0,1]) and `notification_budget` (computed enum `generous` / `standard` / `sparing` / `paused`) declared in `lib/traits/registry.ts` and computed by NEW pure functions in `lib/traits/compute.ts` (`declineSlope`, `notificationBudgetTier`, `computeRetentionTraits`), APPENDED beside the existing functions (no existing trait function touched, so a sibling Phase 4 agent that also appends traits merges clean). `notificationBudgetTier` fails-safe toward RESTRAINT (the strongest "less contact" signal wins). Unit-tested (`lib/traits/decline-slope.test.ts`). The nightly refresh (`lib/traits/refresh.ts`) emits both beside the existing traits.
2. **The auto-freeze playbook actually spends a freeze.** `lib/practice-streak.ts` gains `saveStreakWithFreeze` (spend one banked freeze to bridge an at-risk day, idempotent + fail-closed: no freeze, not at risk, or already covered = clean no-op) + `revertStreakSave` (the Undo, refunds the freeze, never above the cap). `lib/ai/vera/execute.ts` `saveStreak` now calls it (Phase 1 only logged the touch), records the bridged day on the timeline for the Undo. It is the ONE `auto`-eligible playbook, reversible, audited via `playbook_runs` + the timeline, and STILL gated by the Phase 3 autonomy slider at the call site (`effectiveAutonomyTier`; no auto when a Space is `suggest_only`).
3. **Winback + dunning registry entries.** The winback (`reengage_winback`, keyed `next_best_action=reengage` + a high `decline_slope` at candidate-selection time) is now value-led: tag cooling, gift a modest handful of Gems (a welcome back, not a bribe), then DRAFT a value-led note (a new Journey in their Pillar / their Circle missed them); a price nudge waits for sequence end. Dunning (`failed_payment_dunning`) is keyed on a NEW `failed_payment` trigger kind: tag the payment, then draft a warm 72h note tied to what they would lose, never a threat. BOTH outbound legs are `suggest` and pass `resolveSendGate` (in `sendPlaybookEmail`), never auto-send. A new `give_gem_gift` governed tool (in `VERA_TOOLS` + `executeConfirmedTool`) makes the gift modest + capped (default 5, cap 10) and daily-capped via `awardGems`.
4. **Social fuel (`lib/circles/social-fuel.ts`, new).** PURE helpers (the testability law): `circleStreakWeeks` (a cooperative, LOCAL-ONLY Circle streak: consecutive weeks the Circle met a 50% quorum), `circleMatesToNudge` (who is about to break their own streak, capped, longest-run first), `structuralRiskFromCircleTies` + `structuralRiskBoost` (zero Circle ties = `unanchored`, the highest structural risk). IO: `celebrateInCircleFeed` (fire a Journey / Master-rank milestone into the member's CIRCLE feeds as peer recognition, system-authored, drafted via `withVoice` with a deterministic in-voice fallback, NOT a private modal) + `nudgeCircleMate` (the one-tap nudge, bound to a shared active Circle). Wired: `lib/quest/complete.ts` fires the celebration on a fresh completion (Master line when this finish newly raised them); a new `nudgeStreakMate` server action (`app/(main)/crew/leaderboard/streak-actions.ts`, session-scoped) exposes the nudge; `lib/ai/vera/today.ts` folds a MODEST multiplicative structural-risk tilt into `resonanceScore` (a `none`-action card stays 0; a member with no `structuralRisk` set is unchanged), reading Circle ties fail-safe in `buildTodayCards`. Unit-tested (`lib/circles/social-fuel.test.ts`).
5. **`playbook_complete` gamification event.** `processGamificationEvent` (`lib/achievements.ts`) gains a `playbook_complete` event + a `playbook_complete` `AchievementCriteria` (`lib/gamification.ts`) + a `playbooksCompleted` stat (counts `playbook_runs` status `done` for the member as subject, fail-safe to 0 pre-migration). Finishing a playbook (in the Today `runPlaybookAction`) fires it for the member behind the card, so the advocate state earns retroactive milestones (e.g. `connector` / `facilitator`, seeded in the achievements DB).

**Rationale + the guardrails.** Gamification is FUEL, not the point: every mechanic serves ACTIVATION (get into a room), never dwell. Gem grants are modest + capped so intrinsic motivation is not crowded out; the celebration is SOCIAL (the crowd-in channel), not a private modal; Vera never writes "earn N Zaps" (enforced by the deterministic celebration test). Outbound is always `suggest` and send-gated; only the in-product, reversible freeze save may auto-run, and only when the slider allows. The freeze save is idempotent + fail-closed (a misfire spends nothing); the gem gift is daily-capped (a misfire sprays nothing). Members with zero Circle ties are routed toward a connection move via a SMALL tilt (it tilts, never dominates, the churn x propensity x action core).

**Seams ADAPTED to the real code.** (a) **Trigger kind:** the registry's `PlaybookTrigger` only had `next_best_action` + `churn_risk`; dunning's failed-payment signal is neither, so a new `failed_payment` trigger kind was added (with `playbookForFailedPayment`) and the registry test widened to allow it. The winback's `decline_slope` keying is a candidate-SELECTION facet (who enters the list), not a third trigger, so the existing one-playbook-per-`reengage` law is unchanged. (b) **decline_slope baseline:** the stats RPC does not expose the prior week's practice count yet, so `refresh.ts` reads an OPTIONAL `verified_practices_prev_7d` (undefined -> slope 0), exactly the Phase 3 "wired but 0 for now" pattern (the breaker's unsubscribe term); the pure `declineSlope` lights up with no code change when the RPC adds the column. `notification_budget` defaults to `standard` in the batch (no per-member preference pull there); the playbooks read a member's live budget at action time.

**Consequences.** Additive + fail-closed; no Phase 1 to 4 signature changed (the new trait functions + `give_gem_gift` tool + `failed_payment` trigger + `TodayCandidate.structuralRisk` are all additive/optional). New mutations self-guard or are consciously delegated: `nudgeStreakMate` is session-scoped + binds to a shared Circle; the `lib/circles/social-fuel.ts` helpers carry `// authz-delegated:` notes (system/owner-scoped, like `lib/system-line.ts`). NO migration (the new traits ride the existing `member_traits` store; `playbook_runs` already exists; the Gem gift uses the existing `gift_received` action; achievements like `connector` / `facilitator` are DB-seeded). New code: `lib/circles/social-fuel.ts (+test)`, `lib/traits/decline-slope.test.ts`. Changed: `lib/traits/{registry.ts, compute.ts, refresh.ts}`, `lib/practice-streak.ts` (+saveStreakWithFreeze/+revertStreakSave), `lib/ai/vera/{execute.ts, tools.ts, today.ts}`, `lib/playbooks/{registry.ts, registry.test.ts}`, `lib/gamification.ts`, `lib/achievements.ts`, `lib/quest/complete.ts`, `app/(main)/admin/crm/today/actions.ts`, `app/(main)/crew/leaderboard/streak-actions.ts`. No Notion page (a build-log, not operator instruction). `tsc` / `eslint` / `check:authz` / full vitest (2212 passed) green.

---

## ADR-387: Resonance Engine Phase 6 - per-Space AI-depth upsell + outcome metering

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-24). The PER-SPACE business model for the Resonance Engine: AI-depth is the paid lever, surfaced contextually at the ceiling, metered on outcome-shaped units, never on extractive per-message counts. Built OVER the Phase 1 loop (ADR-382), the Phase 2 cockpit (ADR-383), and the Phase 3 autonomy slider (ADR-384). Strictly ADDITIVE, fail-closed, every gate default OFF (a Space with no AI-depth entitlement gets only the free wedge), mostly pure + unit-tested. Phase 6 of `docs/NEXT-GEN-CRM.md` ("Scaling to Spaces + the upsell engine").

**Context.** The Resonance Engine is built scoped to `space_id` from day one (every Space gets the same Today + cockpit). The open question was the business model: how to charge for it without violating resonate-not-extract. The doc's answer, locked here: AI-DEPTH is the lever (not seats, not raw message volume), the FREE WEDGE is never paywalled (Today suggest-only + summaries + read-only scoring, because that is the value that helps a member), and metering tracks OUTCOMES delivered to the operator (members re-activated, advocacy invites accepted, playbook actions run), reserved for Space/operator plans where it tracks delivered value. The plan's seams were confirmed against the real code; two were ADAPTED (below).

**Decision.**
1. **AI-depth capability keys (`lib/spaces/entitlements.ts` + `lib/spaces/function-access.ts`).** Three additive entitlement keys on the SAME `spaces.entitlements` jsonb, read DEFAULT-DENY through the existing `spaceHasEntitlement` primitive (no schema change, ADR-246): `crm.playbooks` (governed auto-execution of safe playbooks + larger volume + advanced segments, Practitioner+), `crm.resonance` (the read-only resonance surface, Business+), `crm.resonance_ai` (predictive alerts + the full Resonance Graph + managed matching, Organization+). The pure readers `spaceAiDepth` / `spaceMeetsAiDepth` / `spaceCanRunPlaybooks` / `spaceCanSeeResonance` / `spaceCanUseResonanceAi` / `spaceCanUseAdvancedSegments` FAIL-CLOSED to the free `wedge` (a null Space, a missing key, a malformed blob, or any non-`true` value all read as the wedge, never a lockout, never an over-grant). The LIVE wrappers (`spaceCanRunPlaybooksLive` etc. in `function-access.ts`) fold in the `featureAllowed` plan-ladder (gates `space_crm_playbooks` / `space_crm_resonance` / `space_crm_resonance_ai`); while billing is OFF, `featureAllowed` short-circuits and the result is exactly the pure entitlement read. `crm.autonomy` (Phase 3) is INTACT and orthogonal: depth is WHAT the plan unlocks, autonomy is HOW MUCH the engine acts on its own; auto-execution needs BOTH (`crm.playbooks` AND `safe_auto`). The plan ladder seeds the keys cumulatively in `lib/pricing/plans.ts`.
2. **Predicate-grammar extension (`lib/spaces/audiences.ts`).** Three APPENDED optional facets on `AudienceFilter` (`engagementDepth`, `resonanceTier`, `churnRisk`), each a coarse `member_traits`-derived band, with pure fail-safe normalizers and a `definitionToFilter` that reads them from BOTH camelCase and snake_case (forward-compat with the segment store). PURELY ADDITIVE: no existing `resolveAudience` / `AudienceFilter` caller changed. Like the `consent` facet, the facets are ACCEPTED + STORED but do not yet NARROW (the v1 contacts read has no `member_traits` join), so the grammar grows without a breaking change; the narrowing lands when the trait join does.
3. **Outcome-unit metering (`lib/spaces/ai-usage.ts`).** A small, fail-safe, DISPLAY-ONLY read over `playbook_runs` (status `done`, bound to `space_id`) that counts outcome-shaped units: playbook actions run, distinct members re-activated, distinct advocacy invites accepted (classified by playbook-id markers, decoupled from the Phase 5 registry). The plan-shaped SOFT ceiling (`playbookActionCeiling`, generous for the free wedge, unlimited for the top plans) powers the upsell trigger, NOT a hard gate: hitting it never blocks an action. FAIL-SAFE: any error / pre-seed state yields a zeroed, never-at-ceiling, `degraded` usage, so a hiccup never blocks the engine or wrongly nags. Metering is reserved for Space/operator plans (never the personal member tiers), per the ethos.
4. **Contextual upsell at the ceiling (`app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/crm/ai-depth-upsell.tsx`).** A tasteful, value-framed card on the Space CRM cockpit, shown ONLY when a free/lower Space is below the top rung AND has reached its soft ceiling (it is actually working the engine). Copy frames the NEXT rung as what the room gets ("let the engine run the safe moves", "see who in your room should meet", "open the full Resonance Graph"), with a concrete number, never a price pitch (CONTENT-VOICE §10, no em or en dashes). DISPLAY-ONLY: it links to the Space's own billing surface and never charges or auto-changes a plan. Owner/admin only (`caps.canManageMembers`); a self-contained, clearly-delimited block in `page.tsx` so a sibling Phase 4 section can sit beside it without a merge conflict.

**Rationale + the fail-closed inversion.** The DEPTH gates fail closed to the WEDGE (a paid lever degrades to the free floor, never an over-grant), which is the OPPOSITE of the function-access gate's fail-open-to-pure-result, because here a misread must never hand out a paid capability for free. The wedge being un-gated means that fail-closed posture never locks a member out of the loop. The metering and the upsell fail SAFE to silence (a degraded read shows nothing), because the failure mode that matters for resonate-not-extract is nagging a quiet operator, not under-counting.

**Seams ADAPTED to the real code.** (a) **`crm.autonomy` ownership:** Phase 3 (ADR-384) already implemented `crm.autonomy` as a jsonb key read by the pure entitlement readers, NOT via `function-access.ts` as the doc proposed; this phase leaves it intact and adds only the three NEW depth keys, treating autonomy as orthogonal to depth. (b) **Advanced-facet narrowing:** the doc implied the resonance/engagement-depth facets would filter immediately, but the v1 `resolveAudience` contacts read has no `member_traits` join, so the facets follow the existing `consent`-facet precedent (accepted + stored, narrowing reserved) to keep the change purely additive. (c) **No migration:** the doc listed potential data-model additions, but the entitlements jsonb (depth keys + plan seed) and the existing `playbook_runs` audit table (metering source) cover Phase 6 entirely, so no migration was added; the new feature-gate keys live in the `FEATURE_GATES` code map (the `pricing_feature_gates` table only overrides, fail-safe).

**Consequences.** Additive + fail-closed throughout; no Phase 1/2/3 signature changed (the autonomy readers + `featureAllowed` + `planEntitlements` shapes are unchanged, only extended). No new mutation (the upsell is display-only; metering is a pure scoped READER), so no new authz gate; `lib/spaces/ai-usage.ts` is a scoped read bound to `space_id`. New code: `lib/spaces/ai-usage.ts (+test)`, `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/crm/ai-depth-upsell.tsx`. Changed: `lib/spaces/entitlements.ts` (+AI-depth ladder readers, +test), `lib/spaces/function-access.ts` (+live AI-depth gates), `lib/spaces/audiences.ts` (+3 advanced facets + normalizers, +test), `lib/pricing/gates.ts` (+3 space AI-depth feature gates), `lib/pricing/plans.ts` (+AI-depth keys on the plan ladder, +test), `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/crm/page.tsx` (+the self-contained upsell block). No migration (the entitlements jsonb + `playbook_runs` cover it). No Notion page (a build-log, not operator instruction). `tsc` / `eslint` / `check:authz` / full vitest (2211 passed) green.

---

## ADR-388: `/pricing` re-authored as a normal pricing page (Member featured, every tier listed, Coming Soon)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-24). The public `/pricing` page drops all beta / founder / "free during beta" / countdown framing and becomes a NORMAL, professional pricing page. The free **Member** tier is the featured card. Every section of the pricing model is listed AND fully detailed; anything not yet purchasable is shown (never hidden) with a **Coming soon** (or **Invite only**) badge and a disabled, non-clickable CTA. Implements the "Pricing page (normal page, every section listed)" section of `docs/MARKETING-BUILD-PLAN.md`; prices are the GA defaults in `lib/pricing/settings.ts` (`PRICING_DEFAULTS`).

**Context.** The prior `/pricing` template + `LegacyPricing` fallback both sold a beta/founder campaign ("Free during beta", "Founder pricing locked", a `betaFree` struck-price look, Crew as the featured card). The owner asked for a normal pricing page: Member featured (free, forever), and the full inventory listed in three sections, Membership (people), For Spaces (practitioners/businesses/orgs), and Add-ons, with not-yet-sellable plans shown at their real prices and marked Coming Soon. Billing stays gated OFF by the `billing_live` master flag (ADR-362), so nothing charges; the page is honest about that without fake scarcity.

**Decision.**
1. **Tiers block extended (`components/page-editor/blocks/collections.tsx`).** The `badge` field grows from `none | founder` to `none | founder | comingSoon | inviteOnly` (a select), and `ctaStyle` grows from `primary | secondary` to add `disabled`. A `comingSoon` / `inviteOnly` badge renders a muted, tokenized chip beside the tier name; a `disabled` CTA renders a non-clickable `<span aria-disabled>` (muted, `cursor-default`) instead of a `<Link>`, so a not-yet-purchasable card carries no dead link. Purely additive: existing tiers (`none` badge, `primary`/`secondary` CTA) are unchanged. Tokens only, no hex.
2. **Template rewritten (`lib/page-editor/templates/pricing.ts`).** Beta Hero/Statement/founder framing removed. Now: a Hero (Member-first, "No card today. Leave anytime."), three sections as four Tiers blocks (Membership 3 cards with Member `featured`; For Spaces split 3 + 4 across two Tiers blocks for readability, 7 plans; Add-ons 3 cards), a "what your membership funds" section (Heading + a concrete-cost FeatureGrid: the room's lights, insurance, the thermal circuit), a "Connection should circulate" Statement, a "Host, Guide, and Mentor are earned, not bought" note (Heading + Text), a risk-reversal FeatureGrid, an Accordion FAQ, and a CallToAction. Every unbuilt plan shows its real price + a `comingSoon`/`inviteOnly` badge + a disabled CTA. Voice per CONTENT-VOICE §10; no em or en dashes.
3. **Route updated (`app/(marketing)/pricing/page.tsx`).** Renders `getPublishedData('pricing')` if `isRenderable`, ELSE the git TEMPLATE via `getTemplate('pricing')` if `isRenderable`, ELSE the kept-but-unused `LegacyPricing`. This makes the new git content LIVE without a DB publish (the template fallback wins over the legacy coded component). Metadata reframed to normal pricing. The FAQPage JSON-LD now mirrors the template's Accordion FAQ (a `PRICING_FAQ` array kept in sync) so structured data matches what visitors read.

**Coming Soon vs hidden, and the billing gate.** "Coming soon" is a DISPLAY state on a fully-detailed card, deliberately not a hidden tier: the price is real and shown, only purchase is deferred. This is consistent with the runtime gates (`memberTierSellable` / `spacePlanSellable` fail-safe FALSE while `billing_live` is OFF), so the page's disabled CTAs match the system's actual sellability today. No countdown, no scarcity copy.

**Consequences.** Three files changed: `components/page-editor/blocks/collections.tsx` (Tiers block + its `ComponentConfig` fields), `lib/page-editor/templates/pricing.ts` (rewrite), `app/(marketing)/pricing/page.tsx` (template-fallback render + metadata + FAQ schema). `LegacyPricing` is retained but no longer the default renderer (the template wins). No schema change, no migration, no new authz surface (a marketing read). Template verified `isRenderable` (13 blocks, all known types). `tsc` / `eslint` (changed files) green; no `lib/page-editor` vitest exists. **Operator note:** if a stale `published_data` row exists for the `pricing` slug, it OVERRIDES this template (the route prefers a renderable published doc); clear or republish it to surface the new content. Instructional "how to edit a marketing page" guidance is unchanged and lives in the Notion Training DB.

---

## ADR-389: Admin reorg Phase 3 - the two net-new Resonance surfaces (Playbooks + Resonance Graph)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-24). The two NET-NEW operator surfaces the Resonance Engine needed but had no UI for: a Playbooks registry and a consent-first Resonance Graph. Phase 3 of `docs/ADMIN-BUILD-PLAN.md` (the admin reorg) and the visible front door for the Resonance Engine data layer (ADR-382 to 387). Strictly ADDITIVE: two new read-only routes + two thin surface-read modules, no change to any Resonance Engine data layer, no migration. Both compose the page-framework templates (never hand-rolled), gate server-side, and degrade to a calm empty state when the prod tables are empty.

**Context.** The Resonance Engine shipped its loop (ADR-382), cockpit (ADR-383), safety (ADR-384), graph scoring (ADR-385), gamification fuel (ADR-386), and per-Space upsell (ADR-387), but two capabilities had data + no operator surface: the Playbook Registry (operators could not see the saved plays, their autonomy grade, the circuit-breaker state, or the run history) and the Resonance Graph (operators could not see who resonates with whom). ADMIN-IA-PROPOSAL step 11 + ADMIN-BUILD-PLAN Phase 3 specified both, side by side under the unified CRM domain's Resonance AI group.

**Decision.**
1. **Playbooks (`/admin/crm/playbooks`, gate `requireAdmin('janitor')`).** An Index-style `AdminTemplate`: a StatCard row (total playbooks, runs this week, autonomy mode, circuit-breaker state), the playbook CATALOG read from the CODE registry (`lib/playbooks/registry.ts`, the source of truth, no IO), and a recent-run history. READ-ONLY for v1 ("run now" + the autonomy slider stay behind the existing governed execute path + per-Space slider). The page states plainly that the platform default is SUGGEST ONLY (`autoExecutionAllowed(null)` is false) so nothing member-facing fires on its own.
2. **Resonance Graph (`/admin/crm/graph`, gate `requireAdmin('janitor', { staff: 'insights', staffLevel: 'read' })`).** A Dashboard-style `AdminTemplate`: a metric row (consented members, live double-opt-in edges, mean Resonance Health from `getPlatformHealth`) and the relationship view as a RANKED, ACCESSIBLE list of the strongest consented connections, each rendered with a semantic-token strength meter and a per-node link to Member Intelligence. NO heavy graph-viz dependency (the build-plan rule).
3. **Two thin surface-read modules.** `lib/playbooks/overview.ts` (`getPlaybookActivity`: fail-safe platform read over `playbook_runs` for the history + week tallies; the catalog stays the code registry) and `lib/resonance/graph-overview.ts` (`getGraphOverview` + `getStrongestConnections`: fail-safe reads over `resonance_consent` + `resonance_edges`). Both follow the established untyped-admin-client + fail-safe pattern (ADR-246): any error / missing table / empty prod table returns zeros / an empty list, so the pages never crash.
4. **CONSENT IS MANDATORY (defense-in-depth).** The data layer already only writes edges over the opted-in pool, but `getStrongestConnections` re-checks `resonance_consent.opted_in` for BOTH ends of every edge on read and DROPS any edge where either side is not currently opted in, so a consent withdrawal between nightly runs is honored immediately. The graph can never fabricate or over-surface a connection; the empty state explains the double-opt-in gate.
5. **Nav registration (two of the three systems).** Both pages register under CRM's Resonance group in `app/(main)/admin/sections.ts` (the dashboard UX + admin search index) and `lib/admin/nav.ts` (the sub-header mega-menu Engine group). `lib/nav-areas.ts` is NOT touched: these are sub-pages, not top-level rail domains (the rail already carries the Resonance CRM domain root, ADR-382). The page-chrome map already returns `'none'` for all `/admin/*`, so no rail registration is needed per page.

**Rationale.** The registry is the source of truth for the playbook CATALOG (so the surface never drifts from the declared plays); the surface modules read only the RUNTIME signal the registry cannot know (what ran, who is connected). Both fail SAFE to silence because the failure mode that matters is a crashing operator page, and because the tables are expected to be empty on prod today (the autonomy engine starts suggest_only; matching is opt-in default-deny), so the honest first state IS the empty state. The consent re-check on read is the trust moat made structural: the page can only ever show fewer edges than exist, never more.

**Consequences.** Additive + read-only; no Resonance Engine data layer file changed, no migration (the surfaces read the existing `playbooks` code registry, `playbook_runs`, `resonance_consent`, `resonance_edges`, `member_engagement_scores`). No new mutation, so no new authz gate; both surface modules carry `authz-delegated` notes (the gate lives at the staff-gated page). New code: `lib/playbooks/overview.ts`, `lib/resonance/graph-overview.ts`, `app/(main)/admin/crm/playbooks/page.tsx`, `app/(main)/admin/crm/graph/page.tsx`. Changed (nav only): `app/(main)/admin/sections.ts`, `lib/admin/nav.ts`. No Notion page (a build-log; the operator "how to use the reorganized admin" page is tracked separately in the Training DB per ADMIN-BUILD-PLAN). `tsc` / `eslint` (new + changed files) / the targeted `lib/playbooks` + `lib/resonance` + `lib/admin` + `app/(main)/admin` vitest (87 passed) / `pnpm build` (both routes generated) green. UNCERTAINTY: the `playbooks` / `playbook_runs` / `resonance_*` tables may be empty on prod, in which case both pages render their (intended) empty states.

## ADR-390: Unified navigation — standardized, role-gated menu containers

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-24). Standardizes ALL site navigation into editable, role-gated containers built on the DB-backed menu system (`lib/menus/`): four primary containers — **`header`** (the mega-menu for general site nav), **`left`** (the in-app rail / "user menu in the feed"; admin section entry points live here), **`footer`**, **`profile`** (the account dropdown) — plus a fifth, the contextual **`admin_header`**. Every group/page/link is assignable to any container and movable between them; visibility is purely role-based (a basic member sees a little; complexity scales up to Janitor). Shipped additively + fail-safe (a surface with no DB rows renders from rich code defaults).

**Context.** Navigation was defined in five ad-hoc menu "surfaces" (`public_discover`, `public_explore`, `admin_subheader`, `left_rail`, `marketing_footer`) plus a hardcoded account dropdown (`components/layout/user-menu.tsx`), with the admin nav hand-maintained in three code modules that had drifted. The owner directive: collapse this into standardized containers an operator can fully reshape, with any item movable to any container and role-based visibility as the spine. Load-bearing risk: admin pages gate on TWO axes — a community/role floor PLUS a staff capability domain (`staffDomain`/`staffLevel`, ADR-127/ADR-208) — but the menu tables could only express `min_access` + per-role `role_modes` (the editor even hard-pinned `min_access='visitor'`), so moving a staff-gated page into a container would have silently lost its gate.

**Decision.**
1. **Two-axis gate on menu data.** Migration `20260801100000_menu_gate_columns.sql` adds `staff_domain` + `staff_level` to `menu_items`, and `min_access` + `staff_domain` + `staff_level` + `icon` + `blurb` to `menu_categories` (additive, nullable, untyped `menuDb()` handle so no type regen).
2. **One union gate, every renderer.** `components/layout/menu-role.ts` `canSeeMenuItem`/`canSeeMenuCategory`/`canSeeMenuEl` decide visibility as the UNION of the menu-access path (`effectiveMode` — `role_modes` + `min_access` floor) and the staff axis (`staffCan`), taking the FULL viewer (community token + staff role) so a functional operator (e.g. a Marketer whose community token is just `member`) still sees their domains. Never weakens a gate; pages still re-gate server-side.
3. **Editor exposes the gate.** `item-editor.tsx` stops pinning `min_access='visitor'`; a shared `GateControls` (min access + staff domain/level) is wired into the link editor and the category header. The per-role `role_modes` matrix stays as the finer override layer.
4. **Four primary containers.** `MenuSurfaceKey`, `MENU_SURFACES`, `SURFACE_KEYS`, `defaults.ts` reduce to `header`/`left`/`footer`/`profile` (+ `admin_header`). `header` merges the two public mega surfaces (Discover + Explore become its top-level dropdown categories, `MegaBar` `triggerLevel='category'`); `left` = the rail (admin entry points via `NAV_AREAS`, role-gated); `footer` = renamed `marketing_footer`; `profile` drives both the public `UserMenu` and the in-app `AccountDropdown` link lists (framed by fixed Profile/Invite/Report/theme/Sign-out chrome). Migration `20260801120000_menu_four_containers.sql` renames `left_rail`→`left` / `marketing_footer`→`footer` in place and drops the structurally-reshaped `public_*` / `admin_subheader` rows (verified pristine seeds in prod) to re-render from the new defaults.
5. **Contextual admin header (the fifth surface).** `admin_header` (migration `20260801130000`) is the admin mega sub-nav: clicking a `left` admin entry lands on that section's dashboard, and the shell (`adminSectionForPath`, longest href-prefix) re-roots the `admin_header` menu to the active section's children so `MegaBar` draws only THAT section's sub-pages as tabs/dropdowns, with the admin/Vera `AdminSearchBar`. Built from `ADMIN_NAV` via `adminHeaderMenu()`; a `GRID_SURFACES` member.
6. **Put any page anywhere.** `moveItem` / `moveCategory` server actions (re-stamping `menu_id` across the whole subtree so nothing orphans) + a `MenuMoveField` "Move to…" dropdown in the link editor and group header.

**Rationale.** The gate-fidelity work is the security-load-bearing core: getting it in first means every later step — merging surfaces, the contextual admin header, the cross-container move — can relocate a staff-gated item safely. Unioning the two axes (rather than collapsing staff into the single token) keeps department operators seeing their surfaces while never granting anything the page itself would refuse. Naming each container for what it is (header/left/footer/profile/admin_header) is what makes "assign any page to any slot" legible to an operator.

**Consequences.** Migrations `20260801100000` / `20260801120000` / `20260801130000` (all applied + verified on prod `azsqfeonabsbmemvddqd`). New: `components/admin/menu/gate-controls.tsx`, `components/admin/menu/menu-move-field.tsx`, the `menu-role` gate helpers, `defaults.ts` builders (`headerMenu`/`leftMenu`/`footerMenu`/`profileMenu`/`adminHeaderMenu`). Changed: `types.ts`/`read.ts`/`actions.ts`/`known-routes.ts`/`active-surface.ts`, `primary-nav`/`marketing-header`/`site-header`/`app-shell`/`user-menu`, and the marketing/help/main/root layouts. `tsc` + `eslint` + `next build` + `check:authz` + full vitest (1973) green; fail-safe fallback verified. The admin **dashboard pages** (`ADMIN_GROUPS`) stay code-driven (out of scope), so nav and dashboards may drift — accepted. `lib/admin/nav.ts` (`ADMIN_NAV`) still feeds the `admin_header` default; the old `menu_config` left-rail bridge is retained.

## ADR-391: Starter Circles surfaced as geo-located, claim-able virtual circles (directory + map)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-24). The twelve staff-authored Starter Circle blueprints (`circle_templates`, ADR-reserved foundation in `docs/STARTER-CIRCLES.md`) are now surfaced inside the main `/circles` directory and on its map as **virtual, claim-able circles scattered within ~10 miles of each viewer**, so a member anywhere sees startable Circles near them. No new tables, no migration; gated by the existing `circle_templates_enabled` master flag.

**Context.** The blueprints lived only in the dedicated `/circles/templates` gallery. The owner wanted them to "look available" everywhere a member browses: listed in the main directory with a special badge, and dropped on the circle map near the viewer's location, each opening a circle-style preview with a "Claim this circle" action. The hard constraint: viewer location is **client-only** (IP geo via `getApproxLocationByIP`, resolved inside the map component) — the server `/circles` page never knows it. Starters also must not masquerade as real circles with members, and the hot `/circles` query must stay untouched.

**Decision.**
1. **Per-viewer virtual projection, never persisted.** `lib/circles/starter-projection.ts` (pure, client-safe) projects each active template to a stable point inside a uniform disk around the viewer — `mulberry32(hashSeed(seedKey | rounded-location | templateId))`, `r = R·√u`. Placement is deterministic per viewer (rounded to 2dp so GPS jitter doesn't reshuffle) and differs between viewers. A `StarterSeed` type (six fields) keeps the client payload tiny.
2. **Clean server/client split.** The **directory list** injects Starter cards **server-side** (no geo needed) — `app/(main)/circles/page.tsx` reads `getActiveTemplates()` when `templatesEnabled()`, maps them to `CircleCardData` with `isStarter: true`, honors the same facets (Pillar channel / type / search; interest + online hide them), and orders them members → Starters → discovery. The **map markers** are projected **client-side** in `components/circles/circles-map.tsx` (where viewer geo lives), passed into `circle-map.tsx` as a separate **unclustered violet layer** (`#7C5CD6`) distinct from the amber real-circle clusters, with its own popup linking to the preview.
3. **Circle-style preview + Claim.** New route `app/(main)/circles/starter/[slug]/page.tsx` (Detail template) renders the blueprint read-only (identity, audience, the four Pillars inside, the Meetup/Gathering/Thread rhythm, agreements, size) behind the same flag. `components/circles/starter-claim.tsx` runs the existing `remixTemplateAction` (Remix = "Claim this circle, or make it your own", NAMING.md) → private draft → builder. Synthetic card/marker ids (`starter-<slug>`) never collide with real circle ids; the Join form is replaced by a Claim link so no membership write is ever attempted against a non-existent circle.
4. **New `StarterBadge`** (`components/ui/starter-badge.tsx`) — primary-amber Sparkles pill, distinct from the warning-gold Demo and signal-teal Featured badges.

**Rationale.** Projecting at render time (not seeding rows) is the only honest way to place a *shared* blueprint at a *per-viewer* location, and it keeps the blueprints clearly "Starters to claim" rather than fake circles with fake members. Doing the geo work client-side respects that viewer location never reaches the server, while the badge + card injection is pure server data. The isolated projection module + a separate map layer mean the hot directory query and the real-circle clustering are untouched.

**Consequences.** No migration. Touches: `lib/circles/starter-projection.ts` (+`StarterSeed`), `app/(main)/circles/page.tsx`, `components/circles/{circle-card,circles-map,circle-map,starter-claim}.tsx`, `components/ui/starter-badge.tsx`, `app/(main)/circles/starter/[slug]/page.tsx`. `/circles` now always does one extra (indexed, fail-safe) `platform_flags` read for the gate. `tsc` (pre-existing missing-optional-dep errors only), `eslint`, `check:authz`, and `vitest lib/circles` green. When the flag is off, every surface renders exactly as before. The `/circles/templates` gallery stays as the browse-by-Pillar surface; this adds the in-directory + on-map discovery path on top of it.

## ADR-392: Marketplace foundation go-live — prod schema applied, verticals activated

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-25). The Marketplace foundation (commerce core + connect-only listings + housing matching, ADR-39X/39Y) is **live in production**: the additive schema is applied to the `azsqfeonabsbmemvddqd` project and the `housing` / `maker` / `shop` verticals are flipped `enabled: true`. The `market` (General) vertical was already live (`market_listings`, ADR-148).

**Context.** The first attempt (#1047) shipped the verticals enabled but registered nav surfaces (`housing`/`maker`/`shop`) that were absent from `lib/core/access-matrix.ts`. The shared shell (`app/(main)/layout.tsx`) maps `accessTo(area.surface, hats)` over `NAV_AREAS` on every authenticated route, so the missing surfaces made `accessTo` throw — a full `/feed` outage (2026-06-24, reverted #1049). #1051 re-landed the foundation **dormant** (`enabled: false`) with the root cause fixed: `accessTo` default-denies an unknown surface instead of throwing, the three surfaces are registered, and `composeNavAreas` skips a malformed placement. Activation was deliberately deferred until the backing schema was in prod.

**Decision.**
1. **Apply migrations first, then activate** (the post-incident ordering). The four `20260815*` migrations were applied to prod via the Supabase MCP as purely **additive** DDL (9 new tables, all RLS-enabled; 2 housing RPCs; the `space_storefront` feature gate). Every dependency was verified present beforehand (`resonance_neighbors`, `resonance_consent`, `profiles.home_geocell_*`, `set_updated_at`, `get_my_profile_id`, `is_space_member`, `entities`/`spaces`/`circles`). Nothing was altered or dropped.
2. **Record the exact repo versions** (`20260815000000`–`…300`) in `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`, so a later `supabase db push` skips them rather than re-running non-idempotent statements (triggers).
3. **Migration-lineage note (operational).** Prod's migration history is a *different lineage* than the repo: the same migrations appear under renumbered versions and re-decomposed (e.g. repo `20260803…_starter_circles_2_seed` = prod `20260624200356`; repo `resonance_consent`/`embeddings`/`edges`/`matches` are folded into prod's single `20260624035543_activate_resonance_engine`). Prod is **schema-current** but its migration files are NOT applied verbatim. The repo's marketplace files are also future-dated (`20260815`, above prod's `20260624` max), so they now sit at the top of prod's recorded history. **Follow-up:** if the team relies on strict `db push` ordering, re-date these four files to the current timeline (a fresh, no-op rename) — left as-is here to avoid touching already-applied migrations.
4. **Activation is safe by construction.** With the surfaces registered and `accessTo` crash-proof, mounting the verticals into `NAV_AREAS` cannot reproduce the outage: a fault in a marketplace page degrades that page only (Next.js error boundary), never the shell. `lib/verticals/marketplace-resilience.test.ts` keeps the crash-path guarantees and now also asserts the verticals are active and present in `NAV_AREAS`.

**Rationale.** The outage was a shell-level throw, not a data problem — so the fix is structural (default-deny) and the activation only needed the tables to exist. Applying additive DDL and recording matching versions is the least-surprising way to make prod queryable without guessing the team's bespoke migration process.

**Consequences.** Browse + post work today across all four verticals; Housing is fully functional (connect-only, no payment). Maker/Shop checkout settles **only once billing is enabled** (`billing_live` is currently off, so the Stripe webhook 503s by design). Touches `lib/verticals/{housing,maker,shop}.ts` (`enabled: true`) and the resilience test. Prod gained 9 tables + 2 RPCs + 1 feature gate; `take_rate` deliberately unchanged (ADR-393).

## ADR-393: Keep the platform take_rate at 8/5/3%; defer the marketplace rake ladder

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-25). The `pricing_settings.take_rate` stays at its current value — **practitioner 8% / business 5% / organization 3%**. The storefront migration's bundled rake cut was NOT applied.

**Context.** An earlier draft of `20260815000200_pricing_storefront_gate.sql` did two things: insert the `space_storefront` feature gate, and `UPDATE pricing_settings.take_rate` to practitioner 3% / business 2% / organization 1%. The migration's own header flagged the UPDATE as "apply only with explicit owner sign-off." `take_rate` is the application-fee rate on **every** Stripe Connect sale (tips, event tickets, storefront), not a marketplace-only knob — so the cut would have re-priced the whole platform. It is also distinct from the locked marketplace category rake (5/3/2/1%), which is a separate mechanism.

**Decision.** Apply only the feature-gate INSERT; keep `take_rate` at 8/5/3%. The migration file is edited to remove the UPDATE (so a fresh environment never silently lowers the rake), and the prod row is left untouched. The marketplace rake ladder is treated as its own deliberate change, to be made at `/admin/pricing` or in a dedicated, signed-off migration.

**Rationale.** Going live with the marketplace must not quietly change platform-wide economics. Billing is currently off, so the rate has no live effect today — which is exactly why it must be set intentionally before billing flips on, not inherited from a marketplace migration.

**Consequences.** No pricing change ships with the marketplace. `take_rate` (old value preserved for the record: `practitioner_bps 800 / business_bps 500 / organization_bps 300`) remains operator-tunable. When the marketplace rake is decided, it lands as a focused change with its own ADR.

## ADR-394: One Marketplace hub — General on `/market`, new `listings` tables for Housing, every surface connected

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-25). After activating the verticals (ADR-392), a connection audit found the new `/marketplace` surfaces were duplicating the entrenched General marketplace and linking to routes that did not exist. This ADR records the cleanup that makes the marketplace one coherent, no-dead-link hub.

**Context.** Two General marketplaces existed in parallel: the established `/market` (`market_listings`, `lib/marketplace.ts`, kinds, contact-via-DM, owner controls, edit, operator-editable header, wired into the member footer / Studio listing builder / density analytics / help) and a thinner newcomer at `/marketplace` (the new `listings` table, `lib/listings`) with no detail page, no author, no edit. All three new tables (`market_listings`, `listings`, `commerce_products`) were empty, so there was no data to preserve. The audit also found dangling links: `ListingCard` → `/marketplace/[id]`, Housing's "List housing" → `/marketplace/housing/new`, Makers' "Open a storefront" → `/marketplace/makers/sell`, and the Maker/Shop product cards → detail routes — none of which existed.

**Decision.**
1. **General marketplace = `/market`** (the entrenched surface). The redundant new General hub is retired: `/marketplace` and `/marketplace/new` are thin `redirect()`s to `/market`, so any link still resolves to the canonical hub.
2. **The new `listings` / `housing_listings` tables + `lib/listings` serve Housing only.** Built the missing pages on them: `/marketplace/housing/new` (post = `createListing` + `upsertHousingDetail`) and `/marketplace/housing/[id]` (detail + contact-via-DM + owner controls). `getListingWithOwner` adds the owner join for the contact handoff.
3. **Makers & Shop got their detail + create pages** on the commerce core: `/marketplace/makers/sell` (create a `owner_kind='profile'` product), `/marketplace/makers/[id]` and `/shop/[id]` (product detail + a `BuyButton`). The buy flow calls `createCommerceCheckout`, which FAIL-SAFEs to a friendly "payments aren't on yet" while billing is off — never a half-charge.
4. **One faceted nav across every surface.** `components/marketplace/facet-nav.tsx` (`MarketplaceFacets`) renders All · Housing · Makers · Shop and is mounted on `/market`, `/marketplace/housing`, `/marketplace/makers`, and `/shop`, so the areas read as one hub regardless of entry point. `ListingCard` took a `basePath` prop so its links target the right detail route.

**Rationale.** With every table empty, keeping the established `/market` (and its CMS/Studio/footer/analytics wiring) and pointing the new tables at Housing — their genuinely novel purpose — removes duplication with the least disruption and no data migration. Building the real target pages (rather than hiding CTAs) is what "make it live" means; the billing-off fail-safe keeps Makers/Shop honest until payouts are enabled.

**Consequences.** No 404s remain across the marketplace. `tsc` + `eslint` clean; the 40 marketplace/access tests stay green. Touches `app/(main)/marketplace/*` (redirects, housing/maker pages, `actions.ts` repurposed to Housing, new `commerce-actions.ts` + `buy-button.tsx`), `app/(main)/shop/*`, `app/(main)/market/page.tsx` (facet bar), `components/marketplace/{facet-nav,listing-card}.tsx`, and `lib/listings/index.ts` (`getListingWithOwner`). The post-purchase `success_url` (`/orders`) is a known follow-up (only reachable once billing is on). The General path in `lib/listings` (`vertical='market'`) is left intact as a base but is unused by the UI.

## ADR-395: Marketplace is operable end-to-end — member + operator surfaces, every menu wired

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-25). Beyond browse/list (ADR-392/394), a member and an operator can now actually *run* the marketplace: buyers see orders, makers manage a storefront and get paid, seekers find roommates, anyone reports abuse, and operators stock the Shop, oversee Space storefronts, refund orders, and triage reports. Every relevant menu links to these surfaces.

**Context.** Activation made the marketplace browsable, but several end-to-end journeys had data layers with no UI (`commerce_orders`, `marketplace_reports`, `housing_seeker_profiles` + matching RPCs) and the first-party Shop had no way to be stocked except direct SQL. "Usable by a user or admin" required closing those.

**Decision.**
1. **Member surfaces.** `/orders` (buyer history); `/marketplace/makers/manage` (seller storefront: publish/unpublish/sold-out/delete + payout readiness via `getConnectStatus`, linking to the existing `/settings/billing` Connect onboarding + recent sales); `/marketplace/housing/roommates` (seeker profile form + consent-gated matches, called with the **authed** Supabase client so `auth.uid()` resolves); and a quiet `ReportButton` (T&S) on housing/maker detail pages.
2. **Operator surfaces.** `/admin/marketplace` (Shop catalog CRUD + stats + Space-storefront oversight), `/admin/marketplace/[id]` (edit), `/admin/marketplace/orders` (all orders + refund), `/admin/marketplace/reports` (moderation queue). All gated `requireAdmin('admin', { staff: 'platform' })`; mutations gated `authorizeAction(caller, 'admin', 'platform')`.
3. **Data layer.** New `lib/commerce/orders.ts` (buyer/seller/all reads + status counts) and `lib/commerce/reports.ts` (file/list/triage); `lib/commerce/products.ts` gained seller/operator reads, `updateProduct`, `deleteProduct`, and an ownership gate; `lib/listings/housing.ts` gained `getSeekerProfile`.
4. **Every menu wired.** Admin left rail (`admin-marketplace`, `lib/nav-areas.ts`) + admin mega-nav section (`lib/admin/nav.ts`, Catalog / Orders / Reports); member footer (Housing/Makers/Shop); account menu (`lib/menus/defaults.ts`: My orders + My storefront, with `Receipt` added to the menu icon vocabulary); public mega-nav (`lib/site.ts`: Marketplace under The Community). The left in-app rail already carried the four areas via the vertical registry.

**Rationale.** Compose the existing seams (Admin templates, `requireAdmin`/`authorizeAction`, the menu registries, `DataTable`/`StatCard`/`EmptyState`) rather than invent new patterns, so the marketplace admin reads like the rest of `/admin`. The buy/refund paths reuse the audited `lib/billing` + `lib/commerce/checkout` money rails; nothing here changes the charge model.

**Consequences.** `tsc` + `eslint` clean; 45 marketplace/access/rail tests green. Billing-off behavior is honest throughout (listing works; checkout + refund fail-safe to a friendly message). Known follow-ups: order/report rows show ids rather than joined buyer/target names (operator can still act); refund surfaces no inline error yet (unreachable while billing is off — no paid orders exist). Roommate matching needs the member to have opted into resonance + saved a seeker profile, by design (consent-gated).

## ADR-396: Per-area marketplace visibility — an operator on/off so areas can be built privately

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-25). Each marketplace area (General / Housing / Makers / Shop) has an operator on/off switch. OFF = invisible to members (dropped from nav, footer, menus, AND the page is gated) while operators still see and edit it. Toggled at `/admin/marketplace`.

**Context.** Beyond per-item drafts (a product is `draft` until published), the owner wanted to rework a whole *area* without members seeing a half-built feature, then publish. The honest version of "invisible" includes hiding the nav entry, which lives in the shared app shell — the surface that caused the 2026-06-24 outage — so it had to be done with the same defensiveness.

**Decision.**
1. **Backed by `platform_flags`** (`marketplace_<area>_published`), read through `lib/marketplace/visibility.ts` (`marketplaceVisibility`, React-`cache`d). **Fail-open:** a missing row or read error reads as PUBLISHED, so a transient DB hiccup never blanks a live area. No migration (the kv table exists).
2. **Shell gate reuses the existing patterns.** The `(main)` layout already hides nav by setting `navAccess[key]='none'` (the Space block) and already redirects off-limits routes. For a non-operator, an unpublished area's nav key is set to `'none'` (drops it from rail + footer) and a request under its route prefix redirects to `/feed`. The whole block is `try/catch` fail-open, and the **redirect is thrown OUTSIDE the try** so a caught error can never swallow Next's redirect signal. Operator detection respects view-as (a steward previewing down sees the member view).
3. **Operator who's an operator** = platform staff (`web_role` admin/janitor) or the `platform` staff domain — the same gate as the marketplace admin. They see hidden areas with a `MarketplaceHiddenBanner` ("hidden from members") on each area's root page.
4. **Toggle UI** is an "Area visibility" section on `/admin/marketplace`; each switch calls `setAreaVisibilityAction` → `setPlatformFlag` (audited via `platform_flag_events`). The flag is read per request, so the change takes effect on the next navigation.

**Rationale.** Extending the shell's *own* established nav-hide + redirect idioms (rather than inventing a new mechanism) keeps the blast radius tiny and the failure mode safe; fail-open guarantees the worst case is "an area the operator wanted hidden stays visible," never a crash or a blanked app.

**Consequences.** `tsc` + `eslint` clean; the 40 resilience/access tests stay green (the shell change is runtime, not in `NAV_AREAS`/`accessTo`). Defaults preserve today's state (everything published). One extra cached flag read per non-operator request. The public marketing mega-nav link to `/market` is not flag-gated (a hidden General area redirects a visitor who follows it); gating the static marketing nav is a minor follow-up.

## ADR-397: Marketing CTA recast to builder framing ("Start a Circle"), and the exemplar gains a multi-CTA contract

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-25). The shared acquisition CTA label flips from "Join the Beta" to **"Start a Circle"** site-wide, paired with a quiet secondary "or just join as a member." The Community page (`/the-community`, the exemplar template) is reworked to place the primary at three high-intent moments instead of one at the bottom.

**Context.** The Community page argued the emotional *why* repeatedly but gave a sold reader nowhere to act until the footer, and the one CTA ("Join the Beta") framed the visitor as a tester. `CONTENT-VOICE.md §2b/§7b` is explicit that the Latent Leader is the reader the whole growth model runs on and that the activation frame is "you don't have to build a community, host one Circle, we'll hand you the format." A report (authored without repo/canon visibility) proposed removing the "Channel" noun and a 3-CTA system; reviewed against the locked canon, only the CTA half was adopted.

**Decision.**
1. **`BETA_CTA_LABEL = "Start a Circle"`** (was "Join the Beta") in `lib/site.ts`, so every shared consumer (marketing header nav, mobile menu, hero, mid-page, close) re-labels at once. Added `BETA_CTA_SECONDARY_LABEL` / `BETA_CTA_SECONDARY_HREF` for the Seeker's lighter path. Destination is unchanged (`/onboarding/beta`, the open induction); the hero `note` ("We're just opening. The first Hosts set the tone.") carries honest founding status so the builder framing is not a bait.
2. **The exemplar template now ships a multi-CTA contract.** `the-community.ts` places the primary at the hero, a mid-page `CallToAction` after the new "How you start" steps (highest intent), and the ink close. Rule: never stack two buttons; the secondary is a quiet text link, not a button. This supersedes the prior "ONE primary CTA on the page" note in that file's header, which other page templates copy.
3. **"Channel" is KEPT and explained** (Pillar > Channel > Circle), per `docs/NAMING.md` (locked). The report's proposal to drop the noun was rejected because the canon is locked and the nav already drifted to "Interests"; scrubbing the term on one page would worsen site-wide consistency. A formal Channel rework, if ever wanted, is a separate canon decision (NAMING.md + ADR) first.
4. **Source of truth = git → publish.** Edits land in `lib/page-editor/templates/the-community.ts`; the live `pages.published_data` is then re-published from it (reconciling the 2026-06-25 operator-doc drift). Other primary pages whose published DB doc bakes the literal "Join the Beta" keep it until re-published — a known transient inconsistency to clear as the marketing sweep reaches each page.

**Rationale.** Changing one shared constant is the lowest-blast-radius way to recast the action everywhere; placing CTAs at proven intent moments (after the reader sees the night, the safety-net path, and the three concrete steps) is standard conversion practice and was the page's biggest gap. Keeping "Channel" honors the locked canon over an out-of-band report.

**Consequences.** Template compiles (vitest dump) and resolves the constants; 3 primary + 3 secondary CTAs, "Channel" retained, zero em dashes. The marketing header / mobile menu / user menu now read "Start a Circle" on deploy. Pages with a published DB doc containing the old literal will show mixed labels until swept/re-published. Follow-ups: sweep the remaining primaries (Home, Quest, Lab, Spaces, About), reconcile the nav "Interests" vs canon "Channel" drift, and source a true "one person holding a small gathering" hero asset (the library skews to crowd/beach/party; the calm breathwork-circle shot was promoted to the hero as the best available).

## ADR-398: Starter Circle headers are drawn, Pillar-coded vector art (with an optional photo override)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-25). Each of the twelve Starter Circle blueprints now ships a bespoke **drawn header** rendered as inline SVG in the house illustration language, themed by the template's primary Pillar. An operator can override any one with an uploaded photo from the admin editor; the drawing is the default, never a bare gradient.

**Context.** The blueprints surfaced everywhere a member browses (ADR-391) but had no imagery — the gallery cards and the Starter preview opened on plain type, and `circle_templates.image_url` was null for all twelve. The image library holds only Lab-space and community photos (nothing topical), and the session's egress policy denies every stock-photo host, so sourcing photographs was infeasible. The owner directed a drawn set instead, "to match the rest of the slides" — i.e. the marketing vector mockups (commit 060e014) built from `components/marketing/illustrations`.

**Decision.**
1. **One art module, twelve bespoke scenes.** `components/circles/template-art.tsx` holds a slug-keyed set of inline-SVG scenes (open book, stacking coins, a board, a runner, peaks + trail, a paddle, a seated figure, two figures + a thread, a set table, a paint palette, notes on a waveform, lined paper + pen) authored in the existing illustration grammar: simple line-art + flat shapes, `viewBox="0 0 240 110"`, `preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice"`, `role="img"` + `aria-label`. Full-bleed field rect + a central safe-zone motif so the same art crops cleanly into both a wide hero band and a 16:9 card.
2. **DAWN tokens only, Pillar-coded.** Palette mirrors `components/journey/v2/pillar-chip.tsx`: Mind→`info`, Body→`success`, Spirit→`primary`, Expression→`signal`. A `text-<ink>` wrapper makes `currentColor` the Pillar ink for every child (ink fills/strokes via `currentColor`, white via `fill-surface`/`stroke-surface`, one darker pop via the `accent` class). No hex ships; reads in light and dark. A faint concentric "frequency" ripple ties the set together.
3. **`TemplateCover` = photo or drawing.** It renders the uploaded `image_url` via `next/image` when set, else `TemplateHeaderArt`. Wired into the gallery cards (`/circles/templates`), the Starter preview hero (`DetailTemplate.hero` on `/circles/starter/[slug]`), and the directory Starter cards (`CircleCard` gained an optional `primaryPillar` so a photo-less Starter falls back to the drawn header instead of the generic icon).
4. **Upload path in the admin editor.** The plain "Image URL" input in `components/admin/circle-templates/template-editor.tsx` is replaced by the reusable `ImageUpload` control (upload-or-paste, controlled, persisted with the form via a hidden `image_url` field) plus a live preview of the drawn header shown when no image is set.

**Rationale.** Drawing the headers keeps everything in-repo, license-clean, and resolution-independent — and matches the established marketing illustration language rather than introducing a second visual register. Pillar-coding the palette makes a header legible as its Pillar at a glance. Keeping the photo override means a real photograph can replace any drawing later without code, satisfying the "way to upload a header image" requirement.

**Consequences.** No migration (the drawing renders whenever `image_url` is null, which is the seeded state for all twelve). New: `components/circles/template-art.tsx`. Changed: `components/circles/circle-card.tsx` (optional `primaryPillar` + drawn fallback), `app/(main)/circles/page.tsx` (pass `primaryPillar` on Starter cards), `app/(main)/circles/templates/page.tsx` (card cover), `app/(main)/circles/starter/[slug]/page.tsx` (hero), `components/admin/circle-templates/template-editor.tsx` (uploader + preview). Uploaded covers use the client `ImageUpload` → `event-media` public bucket (already in `next.config` remotePatterns), the same path circle-settings covers take. `tsc` (pre-existing missing-optional-dep errors only) + `eslint` green for all touched files.

## ADR-399: Circle "health" shows Zaps earned THROUGH the circle, not members' personal totals

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-25). The circle detail page's "Circle health" rail stops presenting members' personal, platform-wide Zaps/badges as the circle's own. It now shows a single honest, circle-scoped points figure — "Zaps earned here" — and hides the panel when a circle has earned none. A freshly-claimed circle with no adopted Journey/Practices reads zero.

**Context.** The panel computed "Total zaps / Avg zaps / Badges earned" by summing each member's `current_season_zaps` and `achievement_count` — whole-platform personal totals. On a just-claimed Starter Circle (the claimant is the only member, nothing adopted), "Total zaps" was literally the host's own season total, so the circle appeared to have earned the founder's personal points. The platform has no per-circle points accumulator; circle "points" everywhere else (e.g. the circle leaderboard) likewise just rank members by personal season Zaps. So there was no honest "earned through this circle" number being shown.

**Decision.**
1. **New read `lib/circles/earned.ts` `getCircleEarnedZaps(circleId)`** sums two genuinely circle-scoped sources, with NO economy award-path surgery: (a) `practice_logs.zaps_awarded` for logs stamped with this `circle_id` (set when a member logs the circle's practice via `LogPracticeButton` → `logPracticeAction` → `logPractice`; un-logging deletes the row, so the sum self-corrects), and (b) `zap_transactions.amount` for rows whose `metadata->>'circleId'` is this circle (the Expression Challenge done in person at a Circle already stamps `{ circleId }`, `lib/quest/expression.ts`). No double count: circle-practice logs write their `zap_transactions` WITHOUT a `circleId` stamp (counted once via `practice_logs`); Expression zaps never write a `practice_logs` row. Founding/joining bonuses (`circle_start`, `circle_join`) carry no `circleId` and aren't practice logs, so they're correctly excluded — founding a circle is not activity earned through it. Fail-safe: any error returns 0.
2. **The panel uses it.** `app/(main)/circles/[slug]/page.tsx` drops the personal aggregates ("Avg zaps", "Total zaps", "Badges earned") and renders "Zaps earned here" (`circleEarnedZaps`) alongside the circle's own member signals ("Active streaks", "New this week"). The whole panel is gated on `circleEarnedZaps > 0` (previously `totalZaps > 0`), so a circle with no circle-earned activity shows no health panel. All the health reads now sit behind `showsHealth`, so non-managers never trigger them (a small perf win over the prior unconditional read).
3. **Indexes (`20260816000000`).** Partial indexes on `practice_logs (circle_id)` and `zap_transactions ((metadata->>'circleId'))` so the panel's two filtered reads never seq-scan the hot ledger tables.

**Rationale.** Attributing points to a circle from data that already carries the circle linkage (practice_logs.circle_id, the Expression circleId stamp) is correct and low-risk — it touches no award/idempotency path, and historical personal Zaps are never retro-credited. Going forward, any award that wants to count as circle-earned just stamps `metadata.circleId` (or writes a circle-stamped practice log) and it lands here automatically.

## ADR-400: Splash Funnels — one onboarding library, with publish state stored in the `data` jsonb (no migration)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-25). The onboarding "beta splash" / "sequence" surface is recast as **Splash Funnels**: one admin library (`/pages/sequences`) where the default flow is the first entry, presented as the reusable "Splash Funnel template", and every custom funnel carries a real lifecycle (draft → published, duplicate, delete, create-from-template). The default template's copy is genericized off the beta-launch framing.

**Context.** Onboarding had two disjoint admin surfaces: `/pages/splash` edited the default flow (slug `beta-default`), and `/pages/sequences` listed/created audience "versions" built in a wizard. There was no publish/unpublish: any `sequence_overrides` row was served live the instant it existed, so a half-built funnel's `?seq=` link showed unfinished copy. Operators wanted a single library, a "create from template" path, and the ability to take a funnel offline.

**Decision.**
1. **Publish state lives INSIDE the existing `sequence_overrides.data` jsonb, not a new column.** `SequenceOverride.status: 'draft' | 'published'`; `sequenceStatus()` reads it with a missing value defaulting to `'published'`, so every pre-existing funnel stays live with zero migration. The funnel list is tiny, so reading all rows and filtering in JS costs nothing — a column + index would be over-engineering. This deliberately keeps the change out of `supabase/migrations/` (no DDL, no prod-before-code deploy timing, no collision with concurrent migration work).
2. **Serving gate in `resolveSequence(slug, { preview })`.** With `preview` off (the public `/onboarding/beta` route and the completion tag-stamp), a `draft` funnel falls back to the default flow, so a paused funnel's link still lands somewhere sensible. The builder loads real draft content with `preview: true`. New funnels start as drafts; `saveSequenceVersion` carries the current status forward so a wizard save never silently publishes.
3. **Actions** (`builder-actions.ts`, janitor-gated): `createFromTemplateAction` (clone the default into a new draft), `duplicateSequenceAction`, `setSequenceStatusAction`. `uniqueSlug` now checks existing DB funnels too, closing a latent bug where a new/duplicated funnel could overwrite another via the slug primary key.
4. **Identifiers are kept** across the rename: the `beta-default` slug, the `/onboarding/beta` route, and the `beta_early_adopter` marketing tag (a registered cohort trait) do not change — only the visible copy and admin labels move to "Splash Funnels".

**Rationale.** The publish flag is low-cardinality, read in the same query that already loads the override, and never needs SQL-side filtering at this scale, so jsonb is the right home and avoids a migration entirely. Falling back to the default (rather than 404ing) on an unpublished link keeps any already-shared link safe. Keeping the wired identifiers stable means analytics cohorts and every existing CTA/link keep working through a purely presentational rename.

**Consequences.** Migration `20260816000000_circle_earned_zaps_indexes.sql` (two partial indexes, no data change). New `lib/circles/earned.ts`; changed `app/(main)/circles/[slug]/page.tsx`. Journey-run completion Zaps (`journey_finished`) are NOT yet circle-attributed (the completion path doesn't carry circle context); counting those is a follow-up that would stamp `circleId` on the run-completion award. `tsc` (pre-existing missing-optional-dep errors only) + `eslint` green for the touched files.

## ADR-401: Retire the Beta demo seed — demo_mode off, demo-metro geography purged

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-25). The Beta "demo seed" program is retired. `demo_mode` is turned off, the leftover demo-metro **geography** (regions/nexuses/hubs the seeds created but never flagged `is_demo`) is purged from prod, and the national-metros seed migration is no-op'd so a fresh database never recreates it.

**Context.** The demo seed (`20240303…seed_demo_circles` + `20260603…demo_0..3`) created ~34 demo Circles across San Diego North County and five national metros (Austin, Boulder, Asheville, Brooklyn, Bend), plus Houston/Portland. The owner wants the live product to carry only real Circles (MoFlow Encinitas, The Reprogramming Circle, Meditation Circle). The demo CONTENT (`is_demo` circles/profiles/posts/events) had already been cleared from prod, but two residues remained: `demo_mode` was still `true`, and the GEOGRAPHY the seeds created (regions/nexuses/hubs) was never `is_demo`-tagged, so the national-metro + Houston/Portland geo lingered as "real" rows with no Circles.

**Decision.**
1. **`demo_mode` off.** `platform_flags.demo_mode = false` (prod set directly 2026-06-25; the value is a boolean column, and `demoModeEnabled()` returns it verbatim).
2. **Purge the demo-metro geography, keep the real tree.** Deleted by slug in FK order (NO ACTION FKs): hubs `bayou-city-hub`/`rose-city-hub` → nexuses `houston-nexus`/`portland-nexus` → the Houston/Portland outposts → metro regions (`austin, houston-r, bend, portland-r, boulder, brooklyn, asheville`) → state regions (`texas, oregon, colorado, new-york, north-carolina`). KEPT: the Americas → United States → California → San Diego tree, the flat Encinitas/San Diego/Carlsbad/North County tags, and Los Angeles + San Francisco (owner's call). Verified first that no Circle references the deleted hubs and no profile is homed in a deleted region.
3. **Migration `20260817000000_retire_demo_seed.sql`** makes (1) + (2) durable and reproducible on every environment (idempotent: each `DELETE … WHERE slug IN (…)` no-ops once gone). Prod was cleaned directly; this keeps fresh DBs and other envs in step.
4. **National-metros seed retired.** `20260603000004_demo_3_national.sql` is emptied to a `SELECT 1;` no-op (comment preserved) so a fresh database never recreates the national demo set; the data it left on existing envs is removed by the retire migration.

**Rationale.** Deleting by explicit slug (not by `is_demo`) is necessary because the geo rows were never `is_demo`-flagged, and it lets us keep the real-aligned San Diego/Encinitas geography the live community uses while removing only the empty demo metros. Doing the prod cleanup directly (then mirroring it in a migration) gets the live database clean immediately while keeping repo + every env consistent.

**Consequences.** New migration `20260817000000_retire_demo_seed.sql`; `20260603000004_demo_3_national.sql` no-op'd. The legacy `20240303…seed_demo_circles` and `20260603000003_demo_2_sandiego` migrations are intentionally LEFT in place: they seed the real-aligned San Diego/Encinitas geography (and demo_2 the practice-adjacent hubs) the live app uses; their dormant `is_demo` Circles stay hidden with demo off, and the retire migration strips any demo-metro geo they touch. Fully extracting the real geo into a non-demo seed so those two can also be retired is a follow-up. No code/schema change; the `is_demo` columns + `demo_mode` infrastructure remain (still referenced across the app).
## ADR-402: The Pages workspace runs on the module-assignment engine; the entity Settings drawer drops the generic page-content group

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-25). `/pages` (the operator "find any page and edit it" workspace) is now module-driven like every other arrangeable surface, and the shell Settings drawer no longer shows the generic "Page" settings group on entity detail pages.

**Context.** `/pages` was a hand-built `DashboardTemplate` whose four areas (in-app Member, in-app Focus surfaces, Splash funnels, Marketing pages) were reordered by a bespoke bottom-right "Sort areas" dock (`AdminPageDock` + a per-operator cookie in `pages-areas.ts` / `pages-order-actions.ts`). That was a parallel layout mechanism that didn't match the module engine (ADR-270/271/272/294) every other arrangeable page uses, and it surfaced as "Page Settings" cards in the wrong corner. Separately, the shell Settings drawer rendered the generic `PageSettingsModule` (Basics title/header, Status & visibility, SEO) on entity detail pages (Circle/Hub/Nexus/Event/channel/profile) ON TOP OF each entity's own settings block, so a Circle showed a confusing second "Basics" panel for a page whose identity is the entity's own name/cover/status.

**Decision.**
1. **`/pages` is module-driven.** The four areas are self-fetching, role-gated layout modules (`pages-in-app-member`, `pages-in-app-focus`, `pages-splash-funnels`, `pages-marketing`) in `lib/widgets/{modules,registry}`; `/pages` is listed in `module-routes.ts` and carries its set in `ROUTE_MODULE_IDS`, so the page is `AdminTemplate` + `<PageModules route="/pages">`. Operators arrange the areas from the on-page Settings → Layout panel. The splash + marketing blocks self-gate to janitor; the in-app blocks render for any operator.
2. **The legacy mechanism is removed from this surface:** the `AdminPageDock` mount in the `/pages` layout, the `PagesSectionSorter`, and the `pages-areas.ts` / `pages-order-actions.ts` cookie files are deleted. The dock + `SectionSorter` stay for the `/admin` dashboards (their own migration is later).
3. **`/pages` drops the member right rail** via `page-chrome` `railFor → 'none'`, like every admin workspace; its operator chrome is the Settings → Layout drawer.
4. **The entity Settings drawer suppresses the generic group on entity scopes** (`scopeKindForPath != null` in `settings-drawer.tsx`). Each entity owns its identity through its own settings block; entity PROFILES at `/spaces/*` are a separate, module-driven scope and keep their Layout editor. Drawers that would now be empty collapse (`hasContent` no longer counts the suppressed group), so a non-managing operator never opens an empty drawer on an entity page. (Complementary, PR #1076: on content-editable pages that ALSO render the richer "Page content" editor, the generic group's Basics PANE is dropped via a `hideBasics` prop since the two overlap; Status / SEO / Layout stay.)

**Rationale.** One arrangement system (the module engine + Settings → Layout) instead of two; the cookie/dock was a one-off that couldn't carry per-role visibility or the scope cascade. The entity-settings cleanup removes a duplicate, meaningless control rather than adding one. No DB migration (layout state already lives in `page_settings`).

**Consequences.** New `components/widgets/pages/*`; `lib/widgets/{modules,registry,module-routes}` carry the `/pages` set; `page-chrome.ts` drops the rail; `settings-drawer.tsx` gates the generic group; `pages-areas.ts` + `pages-order-actions.ts` deleted. `tsc` 0 / `eslint` / widgets module test 13/13 / `pnpm build` green.

## ADR-403: The Leadership dashboard (/lead) is module-driven; "Circles you lead" is additive, and the floor admits anyone who actually leads

**Status:** Accepted (2026-06-25). `/lead` is rebuilt on the module engine, the "Circles you host: 0" bug is fixed, and the entry floor opens to any real leader.

**Context.** `/lead` is the consolidated home for community leaders.
