- Moving pgvector/pg_trgm relocates the operator classes that existing `ivfflat`/`hnsw`/`gin_trgm_ops` indexes and every semantic-search / `ILIKE`-acceleration RPC depend on; each would need `extensions` added to its `search_path` or fully-qualified operators, and indexes may need rebuilding. High blast radius across resonance, practices, help, library embeddings, and discover search.
- PostGIS specifically pins objects to `public` and Supabase's own guidance warns that `ALTER EXTENSION postgis SET SCHEMA` frequently fails or breaks; moving it is a known-hazard operation.
- The security benefit is negligible: these expose types/operators, not `SECURITY DEFINER` data paths. The real definer-attack vector — `search_path` injection — is already closed by ADR-004 (helpers pin `search_path`).

**Consequences.** The three WARN advisories are consciously accepted, not fixed. This is the correct trade for a live product: real security is governed by RLS + the `SECURITY DEFINER` helpers with pinned `search_path` (ADR-004), the `am_participant`-style gates (ADR-014), `auth.uid()` scoping inside definer functions, and the `REVOKE EXECUTE` service-role lockdown pattern (applied where a function must not be caller-invokable). The remaining advisor noise is by-design and documented: `rls_enabled_no_policy` = deny-all (the safe default for a service-role-only table), and anon `EXECUTE` on an `auth.uid()`-scoped definer returns nothing without a session. If a future dedicated `extensions` schema is ever introduced, do it at project setup on a fresh branch, never as an in-place move on the live DB.

## ADR-508: Unified entity block system — blocks are Loom Apps, one data model, per-surface display (Spotlight + Spaces internal via module engine; Puck for marketing + external websites)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-02). Re-aims the Spaces editor epic (S1-S3, ADR/Epic 1.7) toward one shared system across Spotlight AND Spaces. Owner-directed strategy alignment.

**Context.** Four surfaces display an entity's data (`lib/page-editor/surfaces.ts`, ADR-500: `website | space | spotlight | user`). Today Spotlight (member) and Space (entity) profiles both render + edit through Puck. The owner's findings: Puck's flexibility is great for MARKETING pages, but its output "never looks good and doesn't fit the center page area" of the in-app surfaces, and the prior template + block system (the module engine, `<PageModules>`) worked well there. The Spaces block-picker work (S1-S3) proved the module-engine direction for one entity; this ADR generalizes it.

**Decision.** One block system, split by surface:

- **Every content block is a Loom App** (the LP1 App contract, `lib/apps/*`). A block's core function is bound to the entity's LIVE data, it is managed in Loom, and it can be applied to ANY entity. "Any element/block we build is an app in Loom, appliable to any space" is the standing rule.
- **One data model, per-surface display.** An entity's data (a member's or a space's) is the single source. Each surface SELECTS which apps show and how; Spotlight, the Space, and the user's external Website all read the same data with per-surface visibility.
- **Internal surfaces (Spotlight + Spaces) use the module engine, NOT Puck.** A shared, entity-agnostic block set + a Discord/Instagram-style **grid block-picker** editor (pick a template, drop blocks into slots, drag to rearrange), mobile-first. Spotlight and Spaces share the SAME blocks + editor; only the entity + its data differ. Cutover is fresh-default-for-all (no Puck-doc carry-over).
- **Puck stays for MARKETING + EXTERNAL websites.** Frequency's own marketing pages edit in Puck as a fullscreen experience with the main header still visible. Users' external websites use Puck TEMPLATES that read the same entity data, so a user's info syncs across Spotlight -> Space -> Website from one database, with per-surface show/hide.

**Consequences.** The Spaces S1-S3 blocks become entity-agnostic Loom Apps shared with Spotlight; the S3 vertical-list editor is superseded by the grid block-picker; Spotlight moves off its Puck linktree onto this system; Spotlight + Spaces cut over together. The §10 boundary is refined: module engine = internal in-app profiles (Spotlight/Spaces); Puck = marketing + external user websites (never the in-app center area). Roadmap: U1 entity-agnostic block apps; U2 grid block-picker; U3 wire + cut over Spotlight + Spaces; U4 Puck fullscreen-with-header + external template sync. Supersedes the space-only S4/S5 framing of Epic 1.7.

**U3 (2026-07-03):** Shipped the LIVE CUTOVER as a REVERSIBLE RENDER SWAP (fresh-default-for-all, owner-confirmed). The space Home body (`app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/(profile)/page.tsx`) now renders `<SpaceProfileModules grid=…>` instead of `<SpaceLanding>` (Puck); the public member Spotlight (`app/spotlight/[handle]/page.tsx`) now renders `<MemberProfileModules grid=…>` inside the shared `SpotlightShell` (identity header + theme + background + join CTA preserved) instead of the Puck body. The effective grid is `mergeEntityLayout(defaultIds, savedLayout, kind)` where the space `defaultIds` are the grid EDITOR's exact palette (`blocksForKind('space')` minus feature-locked) so editor default == live default and no operator-placed block is dropped, and the member `defaultIds` are `defaultMemberLayout()`. Both renders are FAIL-SAFE and REVERSIBLE: the stored Puck docs (`spaces.preferences.pageDocs` + the S3 `profileLayout`) and `profiles.meta.spotlight` are never deleted or overwritten — reverting is a one-line swap back to `<SpaceLanding>` / `<SpotlightPage>`, both kept intact. The member grid is read fail-safe off `profiles.meta.entityGrid` (added to `SpotlightData.grid`, no extra query). Closed the S2 team-data gap: `getSpaceTeam` reads active `space_members` with an operator role (editor/moderator/admin) joined to their profile, so the `team` block renders (fail-safe empty). Primary edit affordances re-homed to the grid editors (space `settings/profile/grid`, member `people/[handle]/profile-preview/edit`); the classic Puck editors stay reachable but demoted.

**U4-A (2026-07-03):** Made the MARKETING page editor a fullscreen experience with the main site header still visible (owner directive in the U4 line). Moved the route from `app/edit/[slug]` (outside the `(main)` group, so it mounted no app shell and had no site header) to `app/(main)/edit/[slug]` (URL unchanged), and registered `/edit/<slug>` in `FULL_WIDTH_EDITOR_PATTERNS` + `DASHBOARD_NONE_PATTERNS` (`lib/layout/page-chrome.ts`) so the shell drops both rails + page gutters but KEEPS the header — the same proven pattern the Space landing editor (`/spaces/<slug>/edit-page`) already uses. No editor-component change: the in-house Puck-free `DesktopEditor` is reused as-is (it stays `h-[100dvh]`, shared with the full-viewport Spotlight builder which must keep hiding the header). Only importer of the moved server actions (`components/page-editor/editor.tsx`) was repointed. Marketing pages now edit in-app with the site nav around them, matching the Spaces editor.

**U4-B (2026-07-03):** Shipped the FOUNDATION for a Space's EXTERNAL website (owner-approved scope: Spaces first, path-based `/sites/<slug>`, render + surface-sync foundation, no new editor). A Space's public micro-site lives at `app/sites/[slug]/page.tsx` (a top-level route OUTSIDE the `(main)` group, so no app shell / auth chrome, `force-dynamic`). It renders the Space's EXISTING Home Puck doc through the SHARED `BlockRender`, reading the SAME live rows the in-app profile reads (`getSpaceContentData` with the identical identity + `profileData` inputs SpaceLanding passes) — proving **one data model, two renders**: the module-engine grid in-app (per U3) and the Puck-templated site externally (per the ADR §10 boundary: Puck for external websites). The new pure **per-surface visibility model** (`lib/spaces/surface-visibility.ts` + `.test.ts`) is the sync primitive: `surfaceVisibility` at `spaces.preferences` keys each surface (`website | space | spotlight`) to a list of hidden block TYPE names (stable across doc edits, not instance ids); `filterDocForSurface(data, 'website', prefs)` returns a NEW doc with those types removed at the top level AND inside `SpaceLayout` main/side slots. Fail-safe: a malformed blob hides nothing. The route is **FAIL-CLOSED** (the #1 requirement): it resolves the Space as an ANONYMOUS viewer and 404s + noindexes unless BOTH `getSpaceVisibility(slug) === 'network'` (never a Private Space) AND `readWebsitePublished(preferences) === true` (`lib/spaces/website.ts`, fail-safe false) — no "not public yet" copy. Publish is a boolean toggle: the owner/admin/editor-gated `setWebsitePublished` server action (reusing the existing `authorizeEditor` + `writePreferences` merge in the manage/layout actions) writes `preferences.websitePublished` and revalidates `/sites/<slug>` + `/spaces/<slug>`; the customize panel (`space-page-panel.tsx`) exposes the toggle + a live-link to the site. The site paints the Space's brand accent via `AccentScope` (white-label, tokens only) inside a deliberately light public chrome (`components/spaces/space-website-shell.tsx`: brand bar + "Made on Frequency" footer). JSON-LD (`spaceSchema` + `breadcrumbSchema`) emits for the network + published site only. **Follow-ups:** a dedicated website TEMPLATE + external editor (this increment reuses the profile doc, no new editor), the `member` surface, and subdomain / custom-domain hosting (path-based only for now).

## ADR-509: White-label website builder + multi-tenant hosting — dedicated sites apex, Vercel Domains API, in-house Puck editor, anonymous tenant sites

**Status:** Accepted (owner-approved 2026-07-03). Full technical plan: [`docs/WHITE-LABEL-SITES.md`](WHITE-LABEL-SITES.md).

**Context.** ADR-508's U4-B shipped a Space's external website at `/sites/[slug]` (a Puck render of the Space's Home doc reading the one data model, per-surface visibility, fail-closed). The owner wants to develop this into a full white-label site builder + hosting service: every Space gets a branded subdomain, a premium custom-domain tier with automated TLS, the full visual editor, and full integration with the existing system — with minimal ongoing maintenance and support. A codebase recon + external research (Vercel Platforms, Cloudflare for SaaS, Next.js ISR, OWASP subdomain-takeover/CSP) informed the decision.

**Decision.**
- **Subdomains** at a DEDICATED new apex (owner-registered; placeholder `frequencysites.com`), NOT a subdomain of `frequency.app`, so public sites' cookie scope is isolated from the app by construction. Served via a Vercel **wildcard domain** + a host router in `proxy.ts` (the Next 16 middleware, which does no host rewriting today).
- **Custom domains** (premium tier) via the **Vercel Domains API** (programmatic add + TXT/CNAME verification + automatic TLS; unlimited domains on Pro/Enterprise) — the lowest-ops path. **Cloudflare for SaaS** ($0.10/custom hostname/mo) is held as the scale option; self-managed ACME is rejected (on-call burden contradicts the minimal-support goal).
- **Editor** = extend the EXISTING in-house Puck fork (`DesktopEditor` + `BlockRender`), un-gated to per-Space multi-page authoring. `@measured/puck` is NOT re-adopted (would undo ADR-493).
- **Public tenant sites are ANONYMOUS** — the router skips the session refresh and sets no app cookie on tenant hosts, sidestepping cross-domain auth entirely.
- **Domains model**: a new `space_domains` table (subdomain + custom, each with a verification token + status), superseding the single overloaded `spaces.domain` column. Render from the `pages` table (un-gate the `space_id` seam; add `pages.space_id` + `unique(space_id, slug)`), cached with per-site ISR tags revalidated on publish.
- **Billing**: the premium custom-domain tier rides the existing default-deny entitlements + Stripe per-Space subscriptions; a `custom_domain` add-on (or the existing `space_whitelabel` Branding gate) gates bind-time; downgrade redirects the custom domain to the subdomain.
- **Audience**: Spaces first (P0–P4); members generalize in P5.

**Rejected.** A subdomain of `frequency.app` (shared cookie scope); Cloudflare for SaaS as day-one (a second system before per-domain cost justifies it); re-adopting npm Puck (reverts ADR-493); self-managed ACME (ops burden); a non-anonymous tenant site (cross-domain session complexity for no product gain).

**Consequences.** Two greenfield pillars to build: the domains model + `proxy.ts` host router wired into `resolveSpaceForHost`, and Vercel domain provisioning/verification. Six shippable phases (P0 foundations → P5 members). The Stripe slice waits on the Stripe MCP connector being authorized. Custom-domain economics at scale (Vercel Enterprise ~$20-25K/yr vs Cloudflare $0.10/host) must be modeled before pricing P3. Supersedes the "follow-ups" note left at the end of ADR-508's U4-B.

## ADR-510: Reconcile the new-user signup trigger drift — codify prod's `handle_new_auth_user`, never block signup but surface orphans

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-03). Migration: `supabase/migrations/20261013000000_reconcile_signup_trigger.sql`.

**Context.** A production signup failure was reported ("can't create a new account", after DB changes "not fully synced"). A fanned-out investigation (Supabase logs + live schema/trigger inspection + a code-channel recon) found: signup is passwordless only (magic link + Google OAuth), and the sole profile-provisioning path is the `AFTER INSERT ON auth.users` trigger `public.handle_new_auth_user()`. The live DB is currently HEALTHY (25 auth users, 0 orphaned, recent signups succeed); the one recurring `postgres` ERROR (`reward_grants_rule_key_profile_id_key` duplicate) is BENIGN — it is the intended claim-then-pay reward idempotency (insert-and-catch-duplicate) in `lib/journeys/grants.ts` / `lib/quest/*`, not a signup fault. The real, actionable finding is DRIFT: the live `handle_new_auth_user()` runs a hardened body (pinned `search_path`, sanitized + collision-safe handle, and a blanket `EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN RETURN NEW` swallow) that NO migration reproduced — the only repo definition was the naive original in `20240101000000_initial_schema.sql`. That drift is a latent outage (a redeploy / `db reset` / re-apply installs the fragile naive trigger, which a future NOT-NULL-without-default column or a failing chained trigger would turn into GoTrue "Database error saving new user" on every channel), and the prod swallow silently converts any provisioning failure into an orphaned auth user with no log trail.

**Decision.** Codify the working prod behavior into a migration so repo == prod and a redeploy cannot regress signup, with two improvements that CANNOT block a signup: (1) pin `search_path to 'public'` (security-definer hygiene, matching the `lockdown_secdef_*` posture); (2) replace the SILENT swallow with `RAISE WARNING ... RETURN NEW` — signup still never fails at the auth level, but an orphaned account now leaves a warning in the postgres log instead of failing invisibly. The trigger keeps `revoke execute ... from public, anon, authenticated` (it only fires in GoTrue's insert context). Never applied by hand; mirrored per docs/WORKFLOW.md.

**Rejected.** Fail-loud (drop the swallow so provisioning failures error the signup): correct in principle but risky as a hotfix — if any latent provisioning issue exists, applying it would turn currently-silent-successful signups into hard failures, so we keep the never-block guarantee and only add visibility. Chasing the `reward_grants` log error: it is expected idempotency noise, not a bug.

**Consequences.** Repo and prod stop diverging on the single most safety-critical trigger. Orphaned-account failures become visible. Follow-ups for the team (out of this PR): apply the three repo migrations not yet on prod (`20261006000000_drop_redundant_space_subscription_items_index`, `20261010000000_library_sequence_kind`, `20261011000000_app_overrides` — none signup-related), and decide whether to move the provisioning posture to fail-loud once orphan-warning telemetry confirms provisioning never fails.

## ADR-511: Splash pages into Loom — catalog + govern as read-only indexes that deep-link OUT

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-03). Shipped as `lib/library/splash-templates.ts` + `lib/library/splash-registry.ts` + `app/(main)/admin/library/splash-lane-view.tsx` + `components/admin/library/splash-lane.tsx` (the `?lane=splash` lane, docs/LOOM-PLATFORM.md §4, docs/PAGE-FRAMEWORK.md §10).

**Context.** A splash page (a micro-site landing or a QR landing) is a first-class thing operators want to browse, find, and account for, but the real editors already exist and are the right home: micro-sites are Puck (`public.pages` → /edit/<slug>, ADR-500), QR splashes are the constrained QR form. The Loom Studio Splash lane shipped read-only but shallow: only templates opened a drawer, live splashes were inline-only, the where-referenced resolver (`listSplashUsages`, reading `public.library_usages`) was written but unused, the lanes were siloed behind `?lane=`, and no ADR recorded the boundary. Deepening it risked two anti-patterns: making the Loom the splash block editor (a second editor to drift), or persisting the code-backed templates as authoritative `library_assets` rows (a stale DB copy of code).

**Decision.** The Splash lane is a pair of read-only INDEXES over surfaces that stay authoritative elsewhere: a CATALOG (the code-backed `SPLASH_TEMPLATES`, code is the source of truth per ADR-501) and a GOVERNANCE list (the live splashes across `public.pages` ∪ `qr_codes`). Every edit affordance DEEP-LINKS OUT to the real editor; the lane never renders `<PageModules>` on a splash, never makes a splash a module route, never registers one as an App, and never writes a template into `library_assets`. This PR deepens the lane within that boundary: (A) wire the existing `listSplashUsages` "Used in" index into template cards; (B) add a live-splash detail drawer (source / status / target / schedule / deep-link-out Edit / usages) mirroring the template drawer; (C) surface matching splash templates from the top-level Loom search as a labeled "From the Splash lane" strip that links INTO `?lane=splash`. All reads are staff-gated (`isJanitor`) and fail-safe to `[]`; untyped `library_usages` access uses the documented `createAdminClient() as unknown as {...}` cast, never `as SupabaseClient`.

**Rejected.** (1) Move splashes to the module engine / make the Loom the block editor — violates §10 and creates a second editor that drifts; the Puck micro-site editor and the QR studio stay the single home. (2) Persist templates as authoritative `library_assets` rows — violates ADR-501's derive-direction (code → catalog) and the lane's no-drift invariant; templates are cataloged from code, never copied to the DB. (3) A migration for the "Used in" index — unneeded; `library_usages` / `library_versions` and the reserved template/flow kinds already exist.

**Consequences.** The lane is now a full Loom citizen for discovery and governance without owning any splash content. QR splashes have no `library_usages` context (its enum is page/space_brand/spotlight/email/other), so their "Used in" is empty by design. Follow-ups deliberately deferred to keep this PR read-only + additive: (a) broaden governance beyond micro-sites + QR to `/start/[flow]` and `/beta/[slug]` (plan Phase D); (b) fold the onboarding Splash Funnels / sequences into this lane — that belongs with the separate onboarding-into-Loom work, not here.

## ADR-512: Repo & database topology — one app = one repo + one Supabase project; Frequency isolated

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-03). Full model: [`docs/REPO-AND-DB-TOPOLOGY.md`](REPO-AND-DB-TOPOLOGY.md).

**Context.** The owner wants Frequency to be its own thing (repo + database), with other apps (Onesky/Energetics, future) organized separately. A read-only inventory found one Supabase org "Frequency™" (`hkveprznovcteywuczcv`) holding two projects — Frequency Community (`azsqfeonabsbmemvddqd`, the live Frequency prod DB) and hook (`qakbtenvporcfkznivdh`, a separate practitioner/coach OS). `frequency-web` targets only Frequency Community, with zero code/config/env references to any other project (clean 1:1). The open question was whether "everything else" should be separate repos sharing one database.

**Decision.** One app = one repo + one Supabase project. Frequency stays isolated (its own repo + Frequency Community), referenced by nothing else. For other apps: separate database per app is the DEFAULT (mirror Frequency); a SHARED database is reserved for a genuine suite that shares users/data, and in that case those apps live in a MONOREPO (or one designated schema-owner repo) so the schema has a single migration owner. "Many independent repos, one shared database" is explicitly discouraged — it carries the coupling of a shared DB (coordinated migrations, shared blast radius, cross-app security exposure) without the coordination of a shared repo.

**Rejected.** Separate repos + one shared database across independent apps (the coupling-without-coordination trap). A Frequency monorepo absorbing other apps (violates the isolation intent). Leaving both projects in one org long-term (does not match "Frequency is its own thing").

**Consequences.** Owner follow-ups (dashboard actions, not scriptable from a repo): split the Supabase org so `hook`/other apps live in a separate org from Frequency Community; wire the Onesky/Energetics repo to its own project (hook, or a fresh one) with its own env + config + migrations. `frequency-web` needs no change — it is already isolated. The `resonance/` sub-app currently shares the Frequency project via a `resonance` schema; if it becomes its own product it gets its own project.

## ADR-513: Space profiles join the standardized admin rail via the dormant spaceType/spaceFunction App plumbing

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-04). Shipped as the `space` scope kind (`lib/core/capabilities.ts` + `lib/layout/page-chrome.ts`), the `SPACE_EDITOR_APPS` catalog lane (`lib/apps/catalog.ts`) + its `appsForScope` resolver branch (`lib/apps/for-scope.ts`), and the trigger swap in the Space profile chrome (`app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/(profile)/layout.tsx`). Retires `components/spaces/space-customize-{button,drawer}.tsx` + `OPEN_SPACE_CUSTOMIZE`.

**Context.** Every other manageable entity (circle, event, hub, nexus, practice, profile) opened the SAME standardized admin bar (`openAdminBar` → `AdminBar`, docs/ADMIN-RAIL.md), but a Space profile used a bespoke parallel rail: a `SpaceCustomizeButton` dispatching its own `OPEN_SPACE_CUSTOMIZE` window event into a self-contained `SpaceCustomizeDrawer`. Two rails, two events, two chromes to keep in sync. The App contract already carried the plumbing for Space apps end to end — the `{ on:'spaceType' }` placement scope, the `{ system:'spaceFunction', fn }` gate, and `AppViewer.canUseSpaceFn` — but nothing populated it, so it sat dormant. A Space lives OUTSIDE the community Capability spine (its authority is SpaceRole + `spaceFunctionAccess` over `spaces.entitlements`/`feature_roles`, never a `Capability`), which is exactly why it needed the second, function-gated App lane rather than a Capability-gated one.

**Decision.** Make a Space profile's owner "Customize" trigger open the standardized `AdminBar`, pointed at a new `space` scope kind, showing the Space's 9-spine surfaces (`SPACE_SURFACES`) as browse-first **link-rows** into the existing `/spaces/<slug>/settings/*` sub-pages. Concretely: (1) add `space` to the `Scope` union with an EMPTY capability set (Space authority is not a Capability) and an explicit `case 'space'` so `resolveCapabilities` stays exhaustive; (2) resolve `/spaces/<slug>` to a `space` admin scope; (3) derive `SPACE_EDITOR_APPS` from `SPACE_SURFACES` keyed by `{ on:'spaceType' }` + a `spaceFunction`/`none` gate (spine-sorted so the resolved order matches `spaceSurfacesFor`); (4) branch `appsForScope` on `space` to resolve by type, carrying the Space `type` on the scope and the viewer's usable `spaceFns` on `canUseSpaceFn` (both serializable across the CustomEvent boundary via `OpenAdminBarDetail`); (5) render those apps as link-rows (option (a)), NOT inline editors. The `/manage` console stays a full-page workspace and is NOT converged — the rail deep-links into the same sub-pages the console does, via the one `hrefForSurface` map (lifted to the pure `lib/spaces/surface-hrefs.ts` so the client rail and the server console share it without dragging server deps into the client bundle). FAIL-SAFE: Basics / Page / Mode / Services / Danger carry gate `none`, so an owner always sees a non-empty rail even if function resolution returns nothing; the trigger is owner-gated (`canSeeAsOwner`) and the `AdminBar` `hasContent` guard hides a truly empty bar, so a visitor never sees or triggers it.

**Rejected.** Expressing Space authority as a `Capability` (it is SpaceRole + function access, a different world — so we carry `spaceFns` instead). Converging the `/manage` console into the rail (the console is a deliberate full-page operator workspace; the rail is the quick in-context surface — they share sub-pages, not chrome). Inline editors in the rail now (deferred as a Phase 3: link-rows reuse every existing `/settings/*` sub-page with zero rebuild). Keeping the bespoke `SpaceCustomizeDrawer` (the whole point is one rail chrome across every entity).

**Consequences.** One admin-rail chrome for every entity, including Spaces; the dormant spaceType/spaceFunction App plumbing is now exercised, so a future Space surface is one `SPACE_SURFACES` row (it flows into both the console and the rail automatically). `hrefForSurface` now lives in a pure lib module (`console.tsx` re-exports it, so `console.test.ts` is unchanged). The rail's Space link-rows use the surface's spine-slot icon (the console keeps its richer per-surface icons). Deferred: inline Space editors in the rail (Phase 3); attainable-but-locked rows for plan-gated Space functions (`lockedAppsForScope` matches `scopeKind`, not `spaceType`, so Space apps the viewer can't use are simply hidden, matching today's console). Minor deviation from strict console parity: the Offerings surface (gate `none`) shows a rail link even for a commerce-less type, where the console's `spaceSurfacesFor` hides it — acceptable (the Offerings page adapts/empties), and exact parity holds for every offering-bearing type.

## ADR-514: Inline-first admin rail — config renders inline, feature workflows link out; the bar flattens to one spine-ordered list; the `render` classification

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-04). Shipped as a new `surfaces.editor.render: 'inline' | 'link'` axis on the App contract (`lib/apps/types.ts`), carried from `AdminModule.render` (`lib/admin/modules/registry.ts`) and `SpaceSurface.render` (`lib/admin/entities/registry.ts`) through the catalog (`lib/apps/catalog.ts`) and round-tripped by `toAdminModule` (`lib/apps/adapters.ts`); the single render branch in `settings-panel`'s `nodesForAppIds` + the entity-agnostic `SurfaceLinkRow`; the flattened `sections` model + the rewritten `admin-bar-body.tsx`; three Space inline wrappers (`components/admin/modules/space-{basics,mode,page}-module.tsx`) over three read-gated getters (`app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/manage/rail-getters.ts`).

**Context.** ADR-513 put Space profiles on the standardized rail but rendered EVERY Space surface as a link-row into `/settings/*` — a regression from the old inline "Customize" drawer, which edited cover, accent, business info, pages, and mode in place. The owner's directive: the standardized rail should render SETTINGS INLINE ("everything in view"), and ONLY feature workflows should link out. The existing two-level drill-down (HOME categories → CATEGORY detail, ADR/Phase 3) also fought "everything in view": a manager had to drill into each category to see its editors. And the inline-vs-link decision could not overload ADR-138's `surface: 'inline' | 'sidebar'` (that is the *tune-on-the-page vs manage-in-the-dock* axis — a different meaning).

**Decision.** (1) Add a SEPARATE `render: 'inline' | 'link'` field to the editor surface — how the standardized bar DRAWS an editor. `inline` mounts the App's editor component in the bar; `link` draws a compact `SurfaceLinkRow` out to the App's own management page. It is the single decision point in `nodesForAppIds`, branching per-App on `render` (never on entity kind), so a Space can mix inline editors with link-rows. (2) Classify: every core/personal module `inline` (behavior-preserving — they already rendered inline); a Space's config surfaces **Basics · Mode · Page** `inline`, its feature workflows **Offerings · Services · Members · CRM · QR · Email · Insights · Billing · Danger** `link`. (3) Flatten the bar: replace the drill-down with one flat, spine-ordered list — each populated category is a header (`SPINE_META`) followed by its nodes, all in view; the `SettingsPanelModel` sheds `flat`/`categories`/`content` for `sections`. Keep the search box pinned at the top as a FILTER (results scroll to their section), keep the resize handle. (4) Restore the Space inline editors by mounting the EXISTING `SpaceSettingsForm` / `ModeSettings` / `SpacePagePanel` via thin client wrappers that self-fetch through read-only getters, mirroring `circle-settings-module` → `getCircleAdminData`: each getter re-gates server-side (`resolveSpaceManageAccess` + the surface's function check) and returns `null` for a non-manager, so the wrapper renders nothing. No write action changed (each editor already re-gates its own writes). `OpenAdminBarDetail` stays serializable (enums/strings only — the slug comes from the live path, values from the getters).

**Rejected.** Overloading `surface: 'inline' | 'sidebar'` for the render decision (different, ADR-138 meaning — would conflate two axes). Threading the fetched values through the CustomEvent detail (breaks serializability; the getters + live path already supply them). Keeping the two-level drill-down (fights "everything in view"; search is retained as the Hick's-Law mitigation for the taller bar). Adding core/personal link-rows now (out of scope; all core/personal surfaces are `inline` this PR — a `hrefForEntitySurface` is the deferred follow-up). Rebuilding the Space editors for the bar (the existing server-fed components are reused verbatim; only a client-callable getter was added).

**Consequences.** The rail is inline-first: a manager opens straight into every config editor, no drilling. Adding a surface still means one registry row — now with a `render` classification. The Space getters are the fail-safe boundary (re-gate + null), so the flatten never weakens a gate; a signed-out viewer still gets nothing and an owner a non-empty bar (the always-on `none` floor is unchanged). `space.mode` landed **inline** (a `getSpaceModeData` getter reassembles the same `ModeView` the `/manage/mode` page builds). Deviation: the inline Space Page panel edits the **Home** page's blocks only (dropped `useSearchParams` to avoid a Suspense-boundary build constraint) — the full per-page block editor stays on `/manage/layout` and the "Edit your profile" grid link; the Pages manager (create/rename/reorder/delete) still works inline. Deferred to a follow-up: core-entity + personal link-outs (a `hrefForEntitySurface` map + classifying those surfaces `link` where a full page is warranted).

**Three-tier reorg follow-up (2026-07-04, shipped in the same ADR).** The owner's redesign directive ("the admin menu is correct but disorganized — reorder by most-used/importance; standard content at top; primary features link to 'view more'; extra features obscured in a simple dropdown") landed as a THIRD axis on the editor surface: `surfaces.editor.tier: 'standard' | 'primary' | 'extra'` + a within-tier `priority` (`lib/apps/types.ts`), carried from `AdminModule.tier`/`priority` (`lib/admin/modules/registry.ts`) and `SpaceSurface.tier`/`priority` (`lib/admin/entities/registry.ts`) through the catalog (`lib/apps/catalog.ts`, both editor lanes) and round-tripped by `toAdminModule` (`lib/apps/adapters.ts`) — exactly like `render`. `tier` and `render` stay ORTHOGONAL: the inline-vs-link decision in `settings-panel`'s per-app `nodeForApp` still dispatches purely on `render`; `tier` only decides the band. The pure grouping seam is `lib/admin/modules/spine.ts`: `TIER_ORDER` (`standard → primary → extra`), `tierForApp` (fail-safe: untagged → `primary`, untagged `danger` → `extra` so a destructive surface never renders expanded at top), and `groupIntoTiers` (partition by band → sort within band by `priority`, personal-before-management + fixed spine order as tiebreaks so "You" leads each band → group into per-`(tier, slot)` sections). `settings-panel` feeds it the resolved editor Apps + the folded inline extras (page-content → standard, quest/Layout tuner → primary, event Danger → extra) and tags each search row with its band; `admin-bar-body` renders **standard** then **primary** sections inline, then ONE native `<details>` **"More"** disclosure (default CLOSED, plain-noun sentence-case label, no em dash) holding every **extra**-band section. **Tier assignment applied.** Space: Basics/Page/Mode → standard (10/20/30); CRM/Members/Offerings/Services/Email → primary (CRM leads at 10, then 20/30/40/50); QR/Insights/Billing/Danger → extra (10/20/30/99). Personal "You": Profile → standard; Appearance/Notifications/Connections → primary; Account-and-privacy/Billing → extra. Core entities: Basics(settings/text/mode) → standard; Place/People/Engage → primary; Insights/Danger → extra. **Judgment calls (stated):** the operator Layout tuner (an extra folded into the `layout` slot) → primary; the page-content editor → standard; the event Danger zone → extra — each matching its slot's band. The account slot deliberately spans all three bands; the `${tier}:${slot}` ref key keeps sections unique. **Danger reachability** is preserved (risk hold): Danger sits under a closed "More" but is always mounted and stays in the search index, and a search reveal of any extra-band result opens "More" BEFORE scrolling. Tests: `spine.test.ts` (`tierForApp` fail-safe + `groupIntoTiers` band/priority ordering + "You" leads + slot-spans-bands), `registry.test.ts` (the exact Space tier/priority tags + CRM-leads + Danger-in-extra), `catalog.test.ts` (tier/priority flow through both editor lanes + the untagged fail-safe defaults + Danger-in-extra-group-AND-search-index).

**Keep-it-in-the-rail follow-up (Phase 2, 2026-07-04, shipped in the same ADR).** The owner's "keep everything IN the rail" directive landed as compact SUMMARY CARDS on the primary Space feature `link` surfaces plus a standard-tier identity strip — the signal stays in the rail while the deep workflow still opens its page. New pure client-boundary map `SURFACE_SUMMARIES` (`components/admin/modules/surface-summaries.ts`, mirroring `module-map.tsx`) binds a surface id to a read-gated getter + its singular/plural stat COPY (no em dashes). The card-vs-plain rule is baked into `nodeForApp`: a `render: 'link'` surface draws `<SurfaceSummaryCard>` IFF `SURFACE_SUMMARIES[id]` exists, else the plain `<SurfaceLinkRow>` (lifted from `settings-panel.tsx` into its own module `surface-link-row.tsx` so the panel + card share it without a client-import cycle). Cards + stat + source: `space.people` → "N members" (`listSpaceMembers`, active only); `space.engage.crm` → "N in your pipeline" (`getDeals` length, the lean one-query read, NOT the 4-read `getSpaceCrmFunnel`); `space.services` → "N services listed" (`readProfileData(prefs).offerings` filtered by `isServiceListed`, no extra query); `space.comms` → "N campaigns" (`listSpaceCampaigns` length). Plain link-rows (no card): `space.offerings` (adaptive, no honest single stat) and every extra-tier surface (`space.reach`/`space.insights`/`space.billing`/`space.danger` — **Danger never carries a stat**). Five new read-gated getters in `manage/rail-getters.ts` (`getSpaceIdentityData` + the four `getSpace*Summary`) each re-gate exactly like `getSpaceBasicsData` (`resolveSpaceManageAccess` + the surface's per-Space function) and return a TINY serializable payload (`{ count } | null`, or the strip's plain strings) — no React/Icons. `SurfaceSummaryCard` self-fetches (slug from `usePathname`, getter in an effect, fixed-height skeleton, no await in render) and FAIL-SAFE degrades to `<SurfaceLinkRow>` on `null`/throw/no-slug. The compact identity strip (`space-identity-strip.tsx` over `getSpaceIdentityData`) — cover thumbnail + logo chip + name + an "Edit" link to `/settings/basics` — is pinned at the top of the standard tier and renders nothing for a non-manager. Both the strip and the cards ride the CLIENT-built `SettingsPanelModel` (a new `identityStrip: ReactNode | null` slot); `OpenAdminBarDetail` stayed serializable (stats never thread the trigger detail). `admin-bar-body` renders the strip above the inline sections in the NON-search branch only (search results unaffected). No registry/spine/href change. Tests: `surface-summaries.test.ts` (the four-key card-vs-plain rule + Offerings/extra/Danger excluded + the singular/plural stat copy + no em dashes). Deviation: the loading skeleton matches `SurfaceLinkRow`'s one-line height (per plan), so a card that resolves to data grows one line — accepted (no CLS *during* the loading state; the plan specified matching the link-row height).

**Phase C/D follow-up (2026-07-04, shipped in the same ADR).** The deferred classification landed. (C) **Core entities** (circle · hub · nexus · event · practice · channel) stay ALL `render: 'inline'`: each entity's inline module IS its dedicated editor — the `/manage` + `/settings` consoles merely re-compose the SAME module components (`console.tsx` binds e.g. `circle.basics → <CircleSettingsModule/>`), so there is no separate page to link to, and where a genuine feature-workflow page exists (the event guest dashboard `/events/[slug]/manage`) the inline module (`EventPeopleModule`) ALREADY deep-links to it internally. Flipping any of them to a bare link-row would REGRESS a working inline editor (losing the inline approve/invite/counts), so none is reclassified. (D) **Personal "You"** grew from Appearance-only to the full set: the **config** surfaces **Profile · Appearance · Notifications · Connections and location** render `inline` — three new thin wrappers (`components/admin/modules/personal-{profile,notifications,connections}-module.tsx`) over three read-gated getters (`app/(main)/settings/rail-getters.ts`) that mount the EXISTING `/settings/*` forms (`ProfileForm`, `NotificationsForm` + `SmsForm`, and the five connection controls); the **feature workflows** **Account and privacy** and **Billing** are `render: 'link'`. Account and privacy links (rather than inlines) because it has no single reusable form — it is a composite of a data-fetched blocked-members list + data export + account deletion (a management/danger workflow, not lightweight config); Billing is a portal workflow. The **link resolver** is the new pure `hrefForEntitySurface(appId, scope)` (`lib/admin/entity-surface-hrefs.ts`, the core/personal twin of `hrefForSurface`), wired into `nodesForAppIds`'s non-Space `link` branch; it resolves personal surfaces off the global scope only (fail-safe) and returns `null` for every core-entity id (no dead rows). Each personal getter re-gates on the AUTHED viewer (`supabase.auth.getUser()` / `getMyProfileId()`) and returns `null` when signed out, so the wrapper renders nothing — the flatten never weakens a gate. No write action changed; `OpenAdminBarDetail` stayed serializable (slug/ids from the live path, values from getters).

## ADR-515: Uniform admin rail — core settings inline, a second layer to the bottom bank, layout chooser owner-visible everywhere (supersedes parts of ADR-514)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-04). Phase 1 (the keystone) shipped as a new `surfaces.editor.placement: 'inline' | 'bank'` axis on the App contract (`lib/apps/types.ts`), carried from `AdminModule.placement` (`lib/admin/modules/registry.ts`) and `SpaceSurface.placement` (`lib/admin/entities/registry.ts`) through the catalog (`lib/apps/catalog.ts`, both editor lanes) exactly like `render`/`tier`; a new pure `lib/admin/rail-bank.ts` (`bankForScope`, unit-tested); the split-by-placement + bank population in `components/layout/settings-panel.tsx`; the sticky search + bottom bank grid in `components/layout/admin-bar/admin-bar-body.tsx`; the collapsed title header in `components/layout/admin-bar/admin-bar.tsx`; and the core-entity manage-console cases wired into `lib/admin/entity-surface-hrefs.ts`.

**Context.** ADR-514 made the rail inline-first and banded it into three tiers, but every scope still hand-shaped its own body and had no consistent "where do I go from here?" affordance: the manage console, CRM, Insights, and Billing were reachable only by hunting through link-rows, and the Layout / template chooser was gated on the STAFF `isOperator` axis (an owner could not see it). The redesign directive is a UNIFORM contract every rail obeys: a sticky search pinned to the top, the first content section as the top of the box, core settings inline, a promotable second layer to a fixed bottom bank of primary areas, and an owner-visible layout chooser. This keystone establishes the MECHANISM without mass re-tagging (later phases opt surfaces into the bank).

**Decision.** (1) Add a SEPARATE `placement: 'inline' | 'bank'` field to the editor surface — where a surface lives on the rail. `inline` (the default everywhere) renders it in the body via `render`/`tier` (unchanged); `bank` promotes it into the bottom bank button-grid instead. `settings-panel` splits the resolved apps by `placement`: inline apps feed the existing `nodeForApp` body path byte-for-byte; bank apps resolve an href (Space → `hrefForSurface`; core/personal → `hrefForEntitySurface`) and merge into the bank. Nothing is tagged `bank` this PR, so the body path is unchanged (non-breaking). (2) New pure `bankForScope(scope, viewer, extra)` returns the FIXED per-scope primary-area quick-links MERGED with any `placement: 'bank'` surfaces: space → Manage console + CRM + Insights + Billing; personal/global (and a person profile) → All settings + Billing, plus Operator + CRM + Insights for staff; event/hub/nexus/circle/practice → their `/{section}/<slug>/manage` console; unknown/channel → `[]`. Fail-safe: de-dupes by href, drops any Danger/destructive href, empty-safe for a null/unknown scope. (3) Sticky search (`sticky top-0 z-20` with a `bg-surface` backdrop bleeding over the container padding via negative margins) kept mounted in both search + browse states; the bank renders as the LAST block in the browse branch only. (4) Collapse the chrome "Settings" title header on desktop + mobile to just the close button, so the sticky search + first section are the top. (5) De-operatorize the circle/event Layout injection: gate it on the ENTITY EDIT capability (`viewer.caps.has('circle.editSettings')` / `event.editSettings`), not `isOperator`, keeping the `isModuleRoute` guard, so the template chooser is an owner-visible guaranteed section on the scopes that have one. (6) Wire `event.* → /events/<id>/manage`, `hub.* → /hubs/<slug>/manage`, `nexus.* → /nexuses/<slug>/manage` into `hrefForEntitySurface` (keyed on `scope.id`, the seam its own TODO anticipated) so a future `placement: 'bank'` core surface resolves its bank href; circle/practice consoles are thin, so their surfaces stay body-inline.

**Rejected.** Re-tagging surfaces to `bank` in this PR (deferred to later phases — the keystone only establishes the mechanism, so it stays non-breaking). Building the per-entity `*.layout` registry rows now (later phases; this PR only de-operatorizes the existing circle/event injection). A hardcoded bank per rail (the pure `bankForScope` + the `placement` merge is the durable seam). Ever admitting a Danger link to the bank (the bank is navigation, never a place to delete from).

**Consequences.** Every rail now renders the same PLUS a sticky search, a collapsed header, and a bottom bank of primary areas — additive, no surface removed from any body. Adding a bank quick-link is one `bankForScope` case or one `placement: 'bank'` registry tag. The layout chooser is owner-visible. Deferred: tagging second-layer surfaces to `bank`, the per-entity layout registry rows, and emptying the current `extra`/"More" disclosure (later phases). Tests: `rail-bank.test.ts` (per-scope bank + ≥1 link for space/personal/event + never a Danger href + de-dupe + null/unknown → []); `entity-surface-hrefs.test.ts` updated for the event/hub/nexus console resolution.

**Phase 3 follow-up — the Space rail (2026-07-04, shipped in the same ADR).** The owner directive (verified survey): **a feature that paints on the public Space profile keeps its editor INLINE in the rail body; a back-office destination becomes a BOTTOM-BANK button.** So in `SPACE_SURFACES` (`lib/admin/entities/registry.ts`) the five back-office feature workflows are tagged `placement: 'bank'` — **CRM (`space.engage.crm`) · Email (`space.comms`) · QR codes (`space.reach`) · Insights (`space.insights`) · Plan and billing (`space.billing`)** — while every profile-painting surface stays inline: **Basics · Mode and focus · Page · Offerings · Services · Members**. **Danger (`space.danger`) stays INLINE** as the de-emphasized last body item — a destructive action must never be a quick-link (the bank drops any Danger href by construction, so this is belt-and-braces). No new mechanism was needed: the keystone's `placement` axis flows through the catalog (`catalog.ts`, both editor lanes) and `settings-panel` already splits inline-vs-bank and resolves each banked Space surface's href via `hrefForSurface(id, slug)`, merging them (de-duped by href) with the fixed `bankForScope` base (Manage console · CRM · Insights · Billing). Result body: identity strip → Basics → Mode → Page → Offerings → Services → Members → [Danger]; result bank: Manage console · CRM · Email · QR · Insights · Billing. **Deviation (stated):** Insights currently shares the QR sub-page (`/settings/qr` — there is no standalone insights route yet), so QR + Insights collapse to ONE bank button by the href de-dupe; both destinations remain reachable. Members + Services keep their inline summary cards (`SURFACE_SUMMARIES`); the CRM/Email summary-card entries are now dead (those surfaces are banked, so `nodeForApp` never runs for them) but left in place, harmless, so a future un-bank restores the card. **The inline template chooser (owner directive: "every admin bar should have the layout chooser").** The 7 structural `TEMPLATES` (`lib/widgets/templates.ts`), previously reachable only inside the standalone grid editor, are surfaced as a compact `TemplateThumbnail` picker leading the Page panel's quick tweaks (`components/spaces/space-page-panel.tsx`, above cover size). Picking a tile writes the chosen `template` onto the `template` key of `preferences.profileLayout` (the SAME `EntityLayout` node `mergeEntityLayout` + the grid editor read/write) via a NEW `setSpaceProfileTemplate(slug, template)` server action (`app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/manage/layout/actions.ts`), mirroring `setSpaceCoverSize`/`setSpaceAccent`: it validates the id (`isTemplateId`), re-gates the owner via `authorizeEditor` (`canEditProfile`, staff preview fails closed — no gate weakened), and preserves every other `profileLayout` key (slots / hidden / order). `TemplateThumbnail` is now exported from `layout-editor.tsx` and reused; `readProfileTemplate` (`manage/layout/preferences.ts`, pure) feeds the current choice through `getSpacePageData` + the `/manage/layout` page. **Fail-safe preserved:** the new action re-gates server-side; the inline module + getter re-gate on manage access and render nothing for a non-manager; `OpenAdminBarDetail` stays serializable (the panel self-fetches its template; nothing new rides the trigger detail). Tests: `registry.test.ts` (the inline/bank placement per Space surface + Danger never banked); `rail-bank.test.ts` (the space bank absorbs CRM/Email/QR/Insights/Billing, QR+Insights collapse, never a Danger href); `catalog.test.ts`/`for-scope.test.ts` unchanged (placement flows through, appsForScope still reproduces `spaceSurfacesFor`).

**Phase 4 follow-up — the circle + event rails (2026-07-04, shipped in the same ADR).** The owner directive (verified survey): **the rail body is the page's own core admin — one control for every on-screen function; the second layer / back-office and the create paths go to the bottom bank; every rail carries the layout chooser.** CIRCLE: two on-screen functions had no rail control, so each gets a first-class module. (1) **This week's practice** (`circle.practice`, engage/primary, `lib/admin/modules/registry.ts` + `components/admin/modules/circle-practice-module.tsx`) — the host-assigned practice paints on the circle page (the "This week's practice" card + the member log button), so the picker is extracted OUT of Circle Quest (`circle-quest-module.tsx` loses the `SetCirclePractice` block — it would otherwise render twice in the engage section) into its own rail module. It self-fetches a new `getCirclePracticeAssignData(slug)` (`app/(main)/circles/admin-actions.ts`) that re-checks `circle.assignTask` (the engage authority + the SAME capability the module declares) and returns null for anyone else; setting the practice reuses `setCirclePracticeAction` (gated `circle.editSettings`, co-granted to a circle leader — so the write gate is never weaker than the read gate). (2) **Insights** (`circle.insights`, insights/extra) — circle health (Zaps earned here · active streaks · new this week) is an on-screen readout, so the rail carries an at-a-glance module mirroring `hub.insights` / `nexus.insights` / `practice.insights`; it self-fetches `getCircleInsightsData(slug)` (re-checks `circle.editSettings`, reuses `getCircleEarnedZaps`). The circle **bank** (`lib/admin/rail-bank.ts`, its own `case 'circle'`) is **Manage console · New event · New announcement** — the two create quick-actions (`/events/new?circle=<id>` · `/broadcast?compose=true&scope=<id>`) mirror the header `CircleHostMenu` byte-for-byte, keyed on the same circle id the scope carries. **Deviation (stated):** the plan named Insights as a BANK LINK, but a circle has **no standalone insights page** — pointing a bank button at `/circles/<slug>` is circular and the health panel has no anchor, so per the plan's own fallback Insights is an **inline** health module instead (which also keeps the ADR-515 Phase 2 invariant that no non-global module is `placement: 'bank'`). EVENT: the body keeps Settings · Place & Time · People · Engage inline and the **Danger zone inline (de-emphasized — never banked)**; the **bank** (its own `case 'event'`) is a single **Manage dashboard** (`/events/<id>/manage`) — the second-layer console that already carries the roster, approvals, questionnaire, sent Dispatches, AND the analytics, so Insights folds into it (a separate Insights button would dedupe to the same href) and the on-page Dispatch composer stays the compose path (there is no standalone dispatch-compose route). The `EventPeopleModule`'s in-body "Open the guest dashboard" link-card was **removed** — the canonical affordance is now the bank button; the module keeps only the inline approve/counts work. **Layout choosers:** both stay owner-visible via the keystone de-operatorization — gated on `viewer.caps.has('circle.editSettings')` / `event.editSettings` behind the `isModuleRoute` guard, rendering as a `layout`-slot primary section (`settings-panel.tsx`, unchanged). **Fail-safe preserved:** every new getter re-gates server-side and renders nothing when unauthorized; no write action's gate was weakened; `OpenAdminBarDetail` stays serializable (the modules self-fetch). Tests: `registry.test.ts` (the two new circle rows in scope-kind order; the core-entities-render-inline invariant still holds); `rail-bank.test.ts` (the circle bank's three links + the event Manage dashboard); `access.test.ts` (Insights joins the editSettings-gated circle editor set). No new capability (reused `circle.assignTask` / `circle.editSettings`), no migration.

**Phase 5 follow-up — the hub / nexus / practice / channel rails (2026-07-04, shipped in the same ADR).** Same owner directive as Phase 4. Each entity keeps its existing inline body (Settings · People · Insights · Danger, de-emphasized where destructive) and gains the missing layout affordance; nothing new is banked. **PRACTICE** gets the REAL owner-visible LayoutEditor: the practice detail body is `<PageModules>`-driven (stats · intro · guide · tags · used-in, ADR-116), so the circle/event `layoutBlock` injection (`components/layout/settings-panel.tsx`) generalizes to a third noun `'practice'` — `showPracticeLayout = isPractice && isModuleRoute(pathname) && viewer.caps.has('practice.editSettings')`, a `layout`-slot primary section, de-operatorized exactly like circle/event (owner-visible on the entity-edit capability, not the staff `isOperator` axis). It renders exactly when the practice settings module does (host+/staff owner via the existing `manager` gate), so no gate is weakened. **HUB + NEXUS** have HAND-BUILT detail pages (fixed sections: identity → insight → circles/hubs), NOT `<PageModules>`-driven, so there is no block set to reorder and a real LayoutEditor would be a broken picker. Instead each gets a minimal `*.layout` registry module (`hub.layout` / `nexus.layout`, `layout`-slot primary, gated `hub.manage` / `nexus.manage`; `components/admin/modules/{hub,nexus}-layout-module.tsx`) that self-fetches `getHubAdminData` / `getNexusAdminData` (null unless the manage capability → renders nothing otherwise, fail-safe), states the page uses a standard fixed layout, and links to the Manage console. **Deviation (stated):** hub/nexus get a minimal affordance, not a real picker — the constraint's allowed fallback for hand-built pages, preferred over fabricating a broken control. **CHANNEL** is platform-curated (no per-channel owner → `channel.manage` is staff-only) and the member page had no admin trigger. (a) The detail page (`app/(main)/channels/[id]/page.tsx`) now mounts an `OpenAdminBarButton scope={{ kind: 'channel', id }}` gated on `channel.manage`, resolved via a new `getChannelCapabilities` (`lib/core/load-capabilities.ts`) — so a staff viewer reaches the single Channel settings module in place. (b) A new staff **Insights** readout (`channel.insights`, insights/extra — tuned-in + circles, reusing the tables the detail page counts; `getChannelInsightsData` gates on staff and returns null otherwise). (c) The channel **bank** is a new `case 'channel'` in `bankForScope`: staff → the operator `/admin/channels` directory + `/admin/moderation` (channels are governed from the `/admin` hub, not a per-entity console); a non-staff viewer never reaches the rail, so the bank is `[]`. **Deviation (stated):** channel gets NO layout chooser — its detail page is not `<PageModules>`-driven, a genuine exception the constraints permit over a forced broken picker. The plan also floated "Propose to library" as a practice bank quick-action; it stays the on-page owner control instead, because the bank is a PURE navigation structure (`BankLink { label, icon, href }` with a Danger-drop + dedupe keyed on `href`) and "Propose to library" is a mutating server action (`submitToLibrary`) with no navigable route — banking it would require a fake href and violate the bank's contract. **Fail-safe preserved:** every new getter re-gates server-side and renders nothing when unauthorized; no write action's gate was weakened; `OpenAdminBarDetail` stays serializable (every new module self-fetches); Danger is never banked (the channel bank carries only `/admin/channels` + `/admin/moderation`, neither destructive). Tests: `registry.test.ts` (hub/nexus scope-kind order gains `*.layout`; channel scope-kind gains `channel.insights`; the core-entities-render-inline invariant still holds — the new modules are `render: 'inline'`, `placement` unset); `rail-bank.test.ts` (the staff channel bank + the non-staff empty bank). No new capability (reused `hub.manage` / `nexus.manage` / `practice.editSettings` / `channel.manage`), no migration.

**Phase 2 follow-up — the personal "You" rail (2026-07-04, shipped in the same ADR). REVERSES ADR-514 Phase C+D.** The owner directive: the personal rail is ONLY the member's own core admin. So the personal BODY is now exactly three inline surfaces — **Profile** (`account.profile`, identity/contact/bio), a **condensed Spotlight** (`account.spotlight`, new), and a **Layout** link (`account.layout`, new) — and the secondary account surfaces are tagged `placement: 'bank'`: **Appearance · Notifications · Connections and location · Account and privacy · Plan and billing** LEAVE the sidebar body and render as bottom-bank buttons that link to their `/settings/*` page (resolved via `hrefForEntitySurface`, which already mapped all five). This DIRECTLY REVERSES ADR-514 Phase C+D, which had inlined Appearance/Notifications/Connections via thin wrappers; those three wrappers (`personal-{appearance,notifications,connections}-module.tsx`) and their getters (`getNotificationsRailData` / `getConnectionsRailData` in `app/(main)/settings/rail-getters.ts`) were RETIRED — only `getProfileRailData` remains (it already carried the spotlight flags + the handle), feeding all three inline surfaces. New: `personal-spotlight-module.tsx` (condensed — a Published/Draft status pill, a Publish/Unpublish toggle reusing `setSpotlightPublished`, and a "Build your page" link to `/people/<handle>/profile-preview/edit`; when not enabled, a single line + "Turn on your Spotlight" reusing `setMySpotlightEnabled`; renders nothing when `canEnableSpotlight` is false) and `personal-layout-module.tsx` (self-fetches the handle, renders a `SurfaceLinkRow` to the profile grid editor). `ProfileForm` gained a `hideSpotlight` prop so the rail's `PersonalProfileModule` suppresses the big Spotlight block (the condensed section stands in); the full `/settings/profile` page keeps it. **Deviation (stated):** the plan named only Appearance/Notifications/Connections for the bank, but Account-and-privacy + Plan-and-billing were ALSO tagged `placement: 'bank'` (rather than left as `render: 'link'` `extra`-tier body rows), because the directive is that the inline body is ONLY Profile/Spotlight/Layout — leaving them as "More" link-rows would keep them in the body. Billing dedupes against the base bank's Billing link. **Fail-safe preserved:** every getter re-gates on the authed viewer and returns null when signed out (renders nothing); no write action changed; `OpenAdminBarDetail` stays serializable (the modules self-fetch; nothing new rides the trigger detail). **Round-trip fix:** `toAdminModule` (`lib/apps/adapters.ts`) now carries `placement` back like `render`/`tier` (the keystone added it to the forward composition only), so the catalog still round-trips byte-for-byte. Tests: `registry.test.ts` (the personal set now Profile/Spotlight/Layout inline + the five bank surfaces; the moved three are `placement: 'bank'`); `rail-bank.test.ts` (the personal/global bank absorbs the moved surfaces as extras + dedupes Billing); `catalog.test.ts` (the band+priority invariant is over INLINE apps — a bank surface carries none); `entity-surface-hrefs.test.ts` retitled for the bank resolution.

**Phase 6 follow-up — the net-new Journey rail (2026-07-04, shipped in the same ADR). THE FINAL PHASE.** Same owner directive as Phases 4/5: the rail body is the page's own core admin, one control for every on-screen function; second-layer / data-heavy features go to the bottom bank; every rail carries a layout affordance. Journey was the one core editable entity with NO standardized rail (only the global `journey.create` gate existed). Now it has one, mirroring the channel/practice wiring. **New capability + scope.** `journey.editSettings` joins the `Capability` union and a `{ kind: 'journey'; journeyId; authorId; viewerManagesScope? }` variant joins `Scope` (`lib/core/capabilities.ts`); the resolver's `case 'journey'` grants it to the author (`scope.authorId === profileId`) OR staff OR a parent-scope manager, **precisely mirroring the event/practice cases**. `getJourneyCapabilities(journeyId)` (`lib/core/load-capabilities.ts`, mirrors `getPracticeCapabilities`) loads `journey_plans.author_id` and resolves; `journey` is wired into the `loadCapabilitiesForScope` dispatcher. `adminScopeFor` gains `{ prefix: /^\/journeys\/([^/]+)/, kind: 'journey' }` (`lib/layout/page-chrome.ts`) — the 2nd segment is the slug (like circles); the bare `/journeys` index falls through to `global`, and the sibling list routes (`/journeys/mine`, `/journeys/new`) resolve to a journey scope whose module getters fail-safe to null (no plan by that slug), so they show no chrome. The `/journeys/<slug>/edit` MINI-RAIL (`MINI_RAIL_PATTERNS`, immersive builder starts collapsed) is orthogonal and untouched. `journey → 'journeys'` joins `SECTION_BY_KIND` (`lib/apps/for-scope.ts`) and `SECTION_FOR_KIND` + `ENTITY_KINDS` (`rail-bank.ts` / `settings-panel.tsx`). **Rail composition (4 registry rows, all gated `journey.editSettings`, all `render: 'inline'`, `placement` unset).** Settings (`journey.settings`, basics) mounts the self-contained `JourneySettings` editor INLINE — identity · delivery · rewards · discovery · how the Circle gathers · publishing; **Builder and layout** (`journey.builder`, layout) is a minimal LINK-OUT affordance to the full-page builder `/journeys/<slug>/edit`; Export (`journey.export`, reach) mounts the light `JourneyExport` control INLINE; Danger (`journey.danger`, danger) mounts `JourneyDangerZone` INLINE, **never banked**. Each is a thin wrapper (`components/admin/modules/journey-{settings,builder,export,danger}-module.tsx`) that self-fetches ONE read-gated getter, `getJourneyRailData(slug)` (`app/(main)/journeys/admin-actions.ts`), which re-resolves `journey.editSettings` via `getJourneyCapabilities` and returns null otherwise — so every row renders nothing for a non-owner (fail-safe); the underlying autosave/delete/export actions each re-check ownership server-side (`assertOwner`), so no write gate is weakened. **The bank** is a new `case 'journey'` in `bankForScope`: a single **Open builder** link to `/journeys/<slug>/edit`, slug-keyed via `slug ?? scope.id`. A Journey has no thin `/{section}/<slug>/manage` console — its console IS the immersive builder — so the bank points there. **Deviation, stated (the data-heavy exception, mirroring Phase 5's hub/nexus "minimal Layout"):** the Journey Builder + Advanced/`page_config` are DATA-HEAVY (blocks · practices · pillars · Vera review, built for the full-page edit route), so `journey.builder` LINKS OUT rather than mounting a real `LayoutEditor` inline — the constraint's allowed fallback for a non-`<PageModules>`-driven surface, preferred over fabricating a broken picker. Consequently `settings-panel.tsx`'s `layoutBlock` is NOT extended to `'journey'` (no real inline chooser). By contrast `JourneySettings` IS self-contained given its props, so it mounts truly inline — the "lightweight Settings inline" the directive prefers. Export stays inline (NOT banked) because the bank is pure navigation (`BankLink { label, icon, href }`) and export is a mutating client action with no navigable route. **Trigger.** The member-facing Journey home for an author is the LEARN page (`/journeys/<slug>/page.tsx` redirects a published-journey author to `/learn`), so `app/(main)/journeys/[slug]/learn/page.tsx` now mounts an `OpenAdminBarButton scope={{ kind: 'journey', id: plan.id }}` (DB id on `scope.id`, the slug≠id contract; the bank resolves the slug from the path) gated on `getJourneyCapabilities(plan.id).has('journey.editSettings')` — so an author OR an operator reaches the rail in place, mirroring the channel page. No new migration (reuses `journey_plans.author_id`). Tests: `registry.test.ts` (the four journey rows in `order`, journey-scope-only, all inline + `journey.editSettings`, Danger in the danger slot); `rail-bank.test.ts` (the journey bank is the slug-keyed builder link, never a danger href); `for-scope.test.ts` (journey joins `ENTITY_KINDS`, so the catalog round-trips its rows).

## ADR-516: The page-aware smart rail — rail content is f(archetype, scope); a freeform rows page-builder for Profile + Space; settings pages become a stats + quick-links Hub

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-04). Phased; Phase A shipped. Builds ON ADR-515 (the uniform rail) and ADR-508 (the entity-block layout model).

**Context.** ADR-515 made every scope's rail uniform, but the rail's content is still `f(scope)` ALONE: the personal "You" editor (identity, incl. the profile header-image upload) is injected against a hard-coded `{ kind:'global' }` for any signed-in viewer regardless of the page (`components/layout/settings-panel.tsx:247-258`). Nothing consults "what page am I on / does this control's subject live here." A page-×-function trace found six mismatches (the class the owner flagged): (A) the full Profile editor incl. header-upload shows on `/settings` and every authed page; (B) Spotlight/Layout inject everywhere; (C) `/settings/profile` renders the `ProfileForm` twice (rail + page body); (D) the operator Page group shows on the settings index; (E) the personal "You" editor shows on the Space Customize rail; (F) the Space settings/manage cog resolves NO space controls (path-derived scope has no `spaceType`). Separately, the owner wants the profile/space page itself buildable from the rail (freeform rows, each split into 1-4 columns, each slot a block/function), replacing the fixed 7-template picker (ADR-508's `TemplateId` enum + two `switch(template)` renderers), with a few starter layouts and everyone starting on a basic layout; and settings pages to be "a stats and quick-linking box," not a duplicated editor.

**Decision.** (1) Add a SECOND axis to the rail: `railArchetypeFor(pathname) → 'builder' | 'hub' | 'manage'` (a pure sibling of `railFor`/`adminScopeFor` in `lib/layout/page-chrome.ts`), plus a per-module `surfaces?: readonly RegExp[]` predicate on `AdminModule` ("mount this inline editor ONLY where its subject's page matches"), threaded through `lib/apps/catalog.ts` into `surfaces.editor` and applied in `settings-panel.tsx`. Rule: mount an inline editor iff archetype is `builder`/`manage` AND the module's `surfaces` predicate matches (absent predicate = today's behavior); on a `hub` archetype mount NO inline editors — the rail is `bankForScope` (promoted from the pinned foot to the body) + a `StatCard` grid + completeness nudges. This one seam fixes mismatches A-F. (2) Model the profile/space page as a FREEFORM ROWS layout on the existing `EntityLayout` JSON (`lib/entity-blocks/layout.ts`, stored opaque in `profiles.meta.entityGrid` / `spaces.preferences.profileLayout` — NO DB migration): `rows: RowDef[]` where `RowDef = { id; columns: 1|2|3|4; slots: (blockId|null)[] }`. `template` is demoted to the NAME of the starter seed a layout began from; legacy `template`+`slots` stays a back-compat read path (like `order`). Three starters per kind (`basic|showcase|minimal`); `basic` == the legacy default (a no-op visual change). (3) The in-rail builder is an OUTLINE editor (rows → column-slots), NOT a mini-canvas — the live page behind the same-route slide-over is the WYSIWYG surface, so a shared client layout context makes edits paint the page instantly (optimistic at 0ms, persist debounced), no iframe/postMessage needed (our rail is same-document, unlike Shopify's separate preview). Dual reorder (drag + up/down arrows + "Move to position…", full `aria-live` a11y); per-block cluster (handle + ▲▼ + ⋯: move-column, edit, hide, move-to-bench, remove); a collapsible **Bench** tray (remove-to-bench ≠ delete); starters as schematic thumbnails. The standalone grid pages (`/people/<handle>/profile-preview/edit`, `/spaces/<slug>/settings/profile/grid`) are deleted and 301'd into the rail (later phase). (4) Hub stats/nudges reuse existing signals only (member: profile completeness · Spotlight state · Zaps+streak · connections · plan; space: members · pipeline · services · page state · plan) — the sole new data code is a pure completeness helper, a `friendships` count getter, a space-tier label, and a search-scoring tweak.

**Rejected.** A full Puck editor in the rail (Puck stays for freeform marketing per ADR-508; the constrained rows model is simpler, safer, on-brand, responsive-by-construction). An iframe + postMessage preview (unnecessary — our rail shares the page's route/React tree, so shared state is simpler and MORE live). Per-cell column-span/ratio metadata on `RowDef` (deferred — see the equal-column tradeoff below). A DB migration (the layout store is already opaque JSON). Forking a new quick-links list for the Hub (reuse `bankForScope`).

**Consequences (Phase A — the rows model + live renderer, shipped 2026-07-04).** `EntityLayout` gains `rows?: RowDef[]` with positional validators — `parseRows`/`sanitizeRows` (bounded ≤24 rows; `columns` ∈ {1,2,3,4}; `slots` clamped/padded to `columns`; each cell null or a KNOWN registry block id, deduped across all rows; row `id` matched to `/^r[0-9a-z]+$/i` else regenerated, so raw input is never used as an object key — the positional model needs no slot-key allowlist, and the legacy `slots` map stays guarded by the existing `KNOWN_SLOT_IDS`). Added `templateToRows(template, slots, kind)` (the 7-template back-compat bridge), `STARTER_LAYOUTS`/`starterRows`, and `resolveRows(layout, kind)` (rows → else legacy template→rows → else `basic`). `EntityGrid` rewritten from `switch(template)` to a generic `rows`+`renderBlock` renderer; both live callers (`member-profile-modules.tsx`, `space-profile-modules.tsx`) feed `resolveRows`. The old editor pages are untouched and still compile/save (changes are purely additive). **Proof of unchanged live render:** the dominant `single` default converts to one 1-col row per block and `EntityGrid` renders a 1-col row's block directly inside the same `<div class="@container space-y-6">` wrapper — byte-identical, asserted by `entity-grid.test.tsx` ("renders the legacy single default byte-identically") + `layout.test.ts` (the live merged member-default path). Starters: member.basic `[1:about][1:stats][1:links][1:topfriends]`, showcase `[1:about][2:stats,links][2:topfriends,∅]`, minimal `[1:about]`; space.basic `[1:about][1:stats][1:offerings][1:events][1:updates]`, showcase `[1:about][2:offerings,events][2:stats,reviews]`, minimal `[1:about]`. **Equal-column tradeoff (stated):** the rows model is equal-column by construction (N-up, capped at 4), so a legacy asymmetric `main-side` (3:2) family template converts to a 1:1 two-column row — block grouping + order preserved, ratio not; the `single` default (the vast majority of live profiles/spaces) is byte-identical. Per-cell span metadata is a deferred enhancement if asymmetric splits are wanted. Tests: 82 pass across `lib/entity-blocks`, `components/entity-blocks`, `components/widgets`, `lib/widgets`. No new capability, no migration.

**Consequences (Phase B — the page-awareness seam + the Hub, shipped 2026-07-04).** Added `railArchetypeFor(pathname) → 'builder' | 'hub' | 'manage'` (a pure client-safe sibling of `railFor`/`adminScopeFor` in `lib/layout/page-chrome.ts`, unit-tested in `page-chrome.test.ts`): builder = `/people/<handle>` (+ profile-preview[/edit]) and the Space profile ROOT `/spaces/<slug>`; hub = `/settings*`, `/spaces/<slug>/settings*|/manage*`, and any generic content page (the default); manage = an entity-detail scope (a non-global `adminScopeFor` kind). Added `surfaces?: readonly RegExp[]` to `AdminModule` (+ `SpaceSurface` for symmetry), threaded through `catalog.ts` into `surfaces.editor` and back via `adapters.ts` `toAdminModule` (round-trips byte-for-byte — the round-trip test stays green), and set on the three personal rows (`account.profile`/`account.spotlight` → `[/^\/people\/[^/]+/, /^\/settings\/profile(?:$|\/)/]`; `account.layout` → `[/^\/people\/[^/]+/]`). `settings-panel.tsx` now reads the archetype and applies it: a personal/inline editor mounts iff (archetype ≠ hub) AND its `surfaces` predicate matches (scope-gated `mgmtApps` are unpredicated, so untouched) — **fixes A + B** (the profile editor + header upload no longer paint on `/settings` or any non-profile page). On the `hub` archetype NO inline personal editor mounts and the body is the **Hub** (`components/layout/admin-bar/hub-rail.tsx`): a `StatCard` grid + one completeness nudge + `bankForScope` promoted from the pinned foot into the "Go to" body block (the one bank array, not a fork) — **fix C** falls out (`/settings/profile` is a hub, so the inline `ProfileForm` is suppressed and the page body remains the sole editor). **Fix D**: the operator Page group is gated off the SETTINGS hub (`isSettingsHub`) but kept on a content hub. **Fix E**: the personal "You" set is injected only in the member's own global/profile context, never on a Space Customize rail or any non-global entity scope. **Fix F**: on `/spaces/<slug>/settings|/manage` (hub) the rail renders the Space **Hub** (stats + the Manage/CRM/Insights/Billing bank), which needs no path-derived `spaceType` (option (b)); the full typed-space spine still comes via the profile-page **"Customize"** trigger (which carries `spaceType`), treated as `manage`. Hub stats ship from EXISTING signals only: member = profile completeness % (new pure `lib/profile/completeness.ts` over the 7 fields + Spotlight flag) · Spotlight state · Zaps + streak (`profiles.lifetime_zaps`/`current_streak`) · connections (new cheap `friendships` HEAD count) · plan (`deriveTier`); space = members · pipeline · services · page state · plan (a small `spaces.plan` label map). All new getters re-gate on the authed viewer/scope and fail-safe to null; the completeness helper is pure. Tests: `railArchetypeFor` mapping, the surface-predicate filtering, the surfaces round-trip, completeness units, and a Hub-renders-on-hub render test. No new capability, no migration, no write.

**Consequences (Phase C — the in-rail Profile builder + live preview, shipped 2026-07-04).** The `account.layout` module is no longer a link-row: on the member's OWN `/people/<handle>` its rail section renders the full **ProfilePageBuilder** (`components/entity-blocks/profile-page-builder.tsx`) — a rows/slots OUTLINE editor (collapsible row strips with a drag handle + `[1][2][3][4]` column control + collapse chevron + row ⋯ menu; each slot a pill holding a block cluster or a "+ Add block" picker; a persistent + inline Add row; a collapsible **Bench** tray). Reorder is available THREE ways — HTML5 drag, always-on ▲▼ arrows, and a "Move to" / "Move to position" menu — with a real keyboard grab + `aria-live` pattern (Enter/Space to grab, arrows to move, Enter/Escape to drop/cancel; announcements via an `sr-only role=status` region). The picker (`block-picker.tsx`, pure `filterPickerBlocks`) is a searchable command-list grouped Suggested → All, excluding placed/hidden blocks. Starters (`STARTER_LAYOUTS`) render as schematic wireframe thumbnails on a default/empty layout, plus "Start from blank". Pure mutation helpers live in `lib/entity-blocks/rows-ops.ts` (`addRow`/`removeRow`/`moveRow`/`setRowColumns`/`placeBlock`/`moveBlock`/`benchBlock`/`hideBlock`/`unhideBlock`/`removeBlock`/`swapCells`, all immutable + `normalize`d to the Phase A invariants; `deriveBench` = palette − placed − hidden, never stored) with thorough vitest. **Live preview:** a shared client `ProfileLayoutContext` (mounted in the app shell, wrapping BOTH `{children}` and the rail) holds the working `{rows,hidden}`; the builder edits it, and the owner's `OwnerProfileLayoutPreview` → `LiveProfileGrid` (fed a keyed node map from the new `renderMemberBlockNodes`, every candidate member block rendered once server-side) repaints INSTANTLY from context — zero round-trip for structural edits. Persistence: every edit schedules a debounced (600ms) `saveMemberGridLayout({rows,hidden})` (already `sanitizeEntityLayout`-guarded, session-derived, self-only; revalidates `/people/<handle>`). Structural edits need no `router.refresh` (the nodes already exist). The preview is the member (self) surface only — the public Spotlight still renders through Puck (ADR-508), and it seeds from `getPublishedSpotlight`, so a member with no published Spotlight edits the outline without an in-page preview (the builder itself works regardless). The standalone `/people/<handle>/profile-preview/edit` page is deleted and replaced by a thin `redirect()` route to `/people/<handle>`; every in-code link that pointed there (profile-form "Build your page", the Spotlight rail module, the owner Spotlight header link, the Spotlight-blocks edit pencil) now points at the profile / Spotlight settings. Tests: rows-ops (every mutation + edges), the save action accepts/sanitizes rows, LiveProfileGrid renders from the persisted layout, the picker excludes placed blocks, the redirect. No new capability, no migration, no read widened; the builder renders nothing for a non-owner.

**Deferred phases (Profile + Space first).** ✅ B (2026-07-04). ✅ C (shipped 2026-07-04, above). D — the Space builder (drop the personal "You" from the space rail, fix E; space cog carries `spaceType`, fix F). E — the intelligence polish (nudges, relevance ranking, contextual entry, search scoring).

## ADR-517: Universal Space functions — every profile is the same functionally; Modes become optional starter presets, freemium TIER gates usage (not Mode)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-04). Phase F shipped (this entry). Supersedes the per-type function restriction from ADR-461/464 (Space Modes) and the CRM/email plan-gate default-deny from the CRM strategy (P3). The freemium TIER wiring is Phase G (separate).

**Context (owner directive).** "Take out all the Mode functions and make every profile the same functionally. Each profile has access to the same functions and can choose how much they use after freemium." A Space's `type` (its Mode) was restricting WHICH functions/surfaces it had: `SPACE_FUNCTIONS[].types` scoped each function to a subset of types (`availability`→practitioner, `tickets`→event_space, `crm`→practitioner/business/coaching, …), and `SPACE_SURFACES[].types` + `OFFERING_SECTIONS[].types` scoped the rail spine + the Offerings composition per type. Separately, the pure `spaceFunctionAccess` resolver DEFAULT-DENIED the two "plan-gated" functions (`crm`, `email`) on the `spaces.entitlements` blob, so a fresh Space saw no CRM/Email during the beta (nothing grants those keys yet). Net: profiles were NOT the same functionally, and CRM/Email were dark.

**Decision.** (1) **Universal function ACCESS.** Every `SpaceFunctionKey` is DEFAULT-ON in the pure resolver (`lib/spaces/functions.ts` `spaceFunctionEnabled` no longer branches on `fn.entitlement`; only an explicit `entitlements[key] === false` turns a tool off), and every function's `types` is `['*']`. So `spaceFunctionAccess(space, fn, role)` returns the FULL set for a manager of ANY Space, subject only to the ROLE ladder (unchanged) — every profile is the same functionally. (2) **The freemium TIER seam stays intact for Phase G.** `crm`/`email` keep their `entitlement` VALUE, but it is now ONLY the Phase-G tier key that the LIVE seam (`lib/spaces/function-access.ts` `spaceFunctionAccessLive` → `featureAllowed({plan},{billingLive})`) reads to gate USAGE/LIMITS once billing is live.
