While `billing_live` is OFF (the beta) `featureAllowed` short-circuits to true, so everything resolves to available — correct. `lib/spaces/entitlements.ts` is untouched (the full-website upsell lock, the AI-depth ladder, and the autonomy dial remain the clean seam). (3) **Modes → optional starter PRESETS.** `lib/spaces/modes.ts` + the Mode picker stay, but a Mode only SEEDS suggested defaults (pipeline, nav emphasis, lexicon) on provision / focus-switch; it never gates a function or a surface. The "Mode and focus" surface is reframed as a "starter preset" on-canon (no em dashes; "a preset never locks anything, every tool stays available"). (4) **Rail + Offerings universalized.** `SPACE_SURFACES[].types` and `OFFERING_SECTIONS[].types` are all `['*']`, so every console type resolves the identical full spine and every Space can configure every offering (each Offerings section body re-checks its own — now universal — function gate and keeps money dormant until Phase G). (5) **Pipeline seed E1.** `ensureSpaceStages(spaceId, type, variant?)` now seeds from the resolved Mode preset's `pipeline` (`lib/crm/stage-templates.ts` `seedStagesForSpace` → `resolveMode(type, variant).pipeline`), the SAME set the "Suggested pipeline" preview shows, so the preview and the seed can never disagree; a type with no Mode falls back to a GENERIC funnel. Idempotent (never re-clobbers an edited pipeline). The per-type templates in `stage-templates.ts` are retired (only the generic fallback + the mode-sourced seed remain). (6) **Vera autonomy in the rail (GAP 2).** A new inline `space.autonomy` `SPACE_SURFACES` module (engage slot, `requiredFunction: 'crm'`) reuses the existing `setSpaceAutonomy` write via a thin self-fetching wrapper (`components/admin/modules/space-autonomy-module.tsx` + `getSpaceAutonomyData`), owner-gated (`caps.canManageMembers`, the SAME gate the setter enforces).

**Where Phase G (freemium tiering UI) plugs in.** The tier gate lives entirely in the LIVE `featureAllowed` seam. Phase G (a) turns `billing_live` on + defines the plan→feature ladders in `lib/pricing/gates.ts` (the `space_crm`/`space_email`/… keys `FUNCTION_FEATURE_KEY` already maps to), and (b) routes the per-Space CRM/Email/… gate call SITES that currently use the PURE `spaceFunctionAccess` (rail-getters `resolveSummarySpace`, the `spaceSurfacesFor` `canUse` binding) through `spaceFunctionAccessLive` so the plan ladder is enforced past the pure availability check. No schema or resolver change is needed for G — only the seam it already targets.

**Migration?** NONE. Function availability is COMPUTED, not stored per-Space: no column enumerated a Space's enabled functions (the `entitlements`/`feature_roles` blobs are sparse OVERRIDES, default-ON). Existing Spaces see the full set from the code change alone. No `supabase/migrations/*.sql` added or applied.

**Risks flagged for owner review (not guessed).** (a) **Offerings section bodies now render for every type** — a practitioner's Offerings page stacks Tickets/Memberships/Donations/etc. Each section is `<Suspense>`-wrapped, re-gates its own (now universal) function, and keeps money dormant, so the runtime risk is bounded; but confirm each section body (`availability`/`memberships`/`donations`/`enroll`/`tickets`/`checkin` `section.tsx`) is type-agnostic. Clean revert lever: `OFFERING_SECTIONS[].types` alone restores type-shaping without touching function access. (b) **Public PROFILE_BLOCKS keep type-shaping** (booking→practitioner, business-presence→business/org) — the public default page layout is intentionally NOT universalized here (it renders type-specific member-facing widgets); the block editor still lets an owner add blocks. Universalizing the public page is a separate product call.

**Consequences.** `spaceFunctionAccess`/`enabledFunctionKeys`/`partitionSpaceBlocks` return the full function set for a manager (no block locked by Mode); the Phase D space builder receives the full set; the rail shows the identical spine for every console type + the inline autonomy control; the pipeline seed matches the Mode preview. Tests updated: `functions.test.ts` (universal default-ON + full per-type set + owner clears every function), `profile-blocks.render.test.ts`, `capabilities.test.ts` (admin of a plain Space now gets the CRM board), `offerings.test.ts` (every type gets every section), `registry.test.ts` (one full spine for every type + the autonomy surface tier/placement), `catalog.test.ts` (autonomy inline), `console.test.ts` (autonomy grouped in people, href → /crm), and new `stage-templates.test.ts` (seed == the Mode preview + generic fallback). Gate green: tsc, eslint (touched files), the spaces/crm/admin/apps/entity-blocks vitest (1164 pass), check:canon, check:authz. No gate weakened — every write stays owner-gated (`resolveSpaceManageAccess`/`canManageMembers`); making functions "available" never bypasses the manage/owner check.

**Consequences (Phase D2 — the Space Basics / identity redesign, four owner requests, shipped 2026-07-04).** (#4 — brand accent is now a REAL color picker.) The Space brand accent is no longer chosen only from named preset tokens whose suggestions borrowed **alert/semantic** labels (Success/Warning/Info — wrong for a brand accent). `brand_accent` now stores EITHER a curated DAWN token NAME **or** a 6-digit hex the owner picks. This is a conscious, scoped reversal of the earlier D6 "the accent is tokens-only, never a hex" for `brand_accent` specifically — SAFE because `brand_accent` is NOT wired into any server `<style>` tag (`lib/theme/server/resolve.ts` leaves it decorative); it only ever reaches a React inline `style` via `AccentScope`, and the strict `/^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$/` is re-validated at BOTH the write (`isValidAccent`) and the builder (`accentVars`), so no injection surface exists. (The admin `SpaceBrandEditor` already stored a hex here; `accentVars` merely ignored it — this change also activates that path.) `lib/spaces/accent.ts` gains `HEX_ACCENT`, `isValidAccent` (the ONE rule both accent entry points share), and a hex branch in `accentVars` that derives the full `--color-primary*` family from the one hex: `color-mix` for the -hover/-strong shades, a translucent `color-mix(... transparent)` -bg (theme-tolerant on any surface), and a luminance-picked readable text color (WCAG 0.179 black/white crossover). Both write actions (`updateSpaceProfile`, `manage/layout/actions.ts setSpaceAccent`) swap their `TOKEN_ALLOWLIST.has` gate for `isValidAccent` — **owner gate unchanged**. The shared `AccentPicker` (`components/spaces/space-form.tsx`) is rebuilt: a native color well + a validated hex text input + a "Default" clear, beside a tight 8-swatch grid `ACCENT_SWATCHES` of on-palette hexes relabeled to warm, non-alert names (Sunrise #E2912F, Bronze #9A5E12, Clay #B07515, Rose #BA3B30, Forest #0F8E78, Spruce #11827A, Ocean #1EB6C5, Harbor #2F6FB0 — all real `app/globals.css` values, none a semantic/alert word). It is consumed by BOTH the batched settings form and the immediate-save `space-page-panel` "Theme and accent" section (which drops its inline token buttons + the `ACCENT_TOKENS` import). (#5 + #6 — Logo URL → Logo Upload, in the identity-images group.) The Basics form's raw "Logo URL" text field is replaced by the shared `ImageUpload` control, writing the SAME `brand_logo_url` column via the existing owner-gated `uploadSpaceImage` + `setSpaceImages`; it now sits in an **Images** group alongside the header (cover) image uploader, so a Space sets its logo where it sets its other identity images (not buried in the business form). Images save on upload (like the business form), separate from the batched Save. `SpaceSettingsValues` gains `coverImageUrl` (built in all three `initial` constructors + the rail getter). (#7 — Basics redesign.) `SpaceSettingsForm` is regrouped with `SectionHeader` kit sections — **Identity** (name, tagline, about + the two Vera drafts) · **Images** (header + logo uploaders) · **Brand** (the color picker) · **Visibility** — single-column-friendly for the ~360px rail and the full `/settings/basics` page; every existing field + its persistence + the staff-preview read-only fieldset kept. Tests updated: `accent.test.ts` (hex family derivation, luminance text pick, malformed-hex rejection, `isValidAccent`), `profile-settings.test.ts` (a valid hex now writes through; a malformed accent still rejected). tsc + eslint + the spaces/admin/lib vitest (3500 pass) + check:canon + check:authz all green. No migration (the `text` column already held hex from the admin path); no read widened; no gate weakened.

**Consequences (Phase F2 — the CRM pipeline stages are now EDITABLE by the owner, shipped 2026-07-04).** The audit's #10 ("evolve the suggested pipeline") + GAP 1 ("the on-screen pipeline gets an admin function in the bar"): `crm_stages` were read-only everywhere despite copy claiming otherwise. **New write layer** `lib/crm/stages.ts` (no `'use server'`, so it also exports the pure helpers + invariants) with client-callable wrappers `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/crm/stage-actions.ts`: `createStage` / `renameStage` / `setStageKind` / `reorderStages` / `deleteStage`. **Authz (unchanged posture):** every action re-resolves the Space BY SLUG and gates through one shared seam `resolveStageManagerAccess(slug)` — `resolveSpaceManageAccess.canManage` (owner/admin/editor; a staff previewer is NOT canManage, so fail-closed) PLUS `spaceFunctionAccess(space,'crm',role)` — then writes through the **service-role admin client** SCOPED to `space_id` (an INSERT stamps it; every UPDATE/DELETE filters BOTH `id` AND `space_id`, so a cross-space id is a no-op). `crm_stages` stays RLS-enabled with **no policies** (service-role only); the owner gate is the authority — **NO migration, NO RLS change** (a policy would duplicate a gate the server action already enforces and widen the write surface past `canManage`). **Invariant:** at least one `won` and one `lost` stage always remain — a delete or kind-change that would drop the last of either is rejected with a clear message (pure `canDeleteStage`/`canSetStageKind`, enforced server-side + mirrored in the UI for disabling). **Delete-with-deals rule:** deleting a stage that holds deals REASSIGNS them to an adjacent `open` stage first (`pickReassignStage`, nearest open, preferring the preceding one), setting `status='open'`/`closed_at=null`; when no open stage would remain to catch them the delete is BLOCKED with a clear message (never orphan a deal). A kind-change also re-syncs the deals sitting in that stage to the new kind so metrics stay honest. **UI:** `components/spaces/crm/stage-editor.tsx` — an owner-only "Edit stages" mode on the CRM board's Pipeline view (`SpacePipeline` gained `editable`/`slug`, mounted for a manager via `caps.canEditProfile`; the Focus CRM surface + non-managers stay read-only): rename inline, set kind, reorder (drag + keyboard-accessible up/down arrows mirroring the rail builder's `ReorderControls`), add, delete (confirm + the Won/Lost guard surfaced). **Rail module (GAP 1):** a new inline `space.pipeline` `SPACE_SURFACES` row (engage slot, `requiredFunction:'crm'`, tier `primary`, priority 12) → `SpacePipelineModule` (`components/admin/modules/space-pipeline-module.tsx` + `getSpacePipelineData`, self-fetching, re-gated through the SAME `resolveStageManagerAccess` seam so a non-manager gets null) shows the stages compactly and links into the full editor; `hrefForSurface` maps it to `/crm?view=pipeline`; wired into `MODULE_COMPONENTS` + the console group/icon maps. **Copy:** the Mode settings "Suggested pipeline" line now describes a real path ("Edit them any time on your pipeline board, and switching focus keeps a pipeline you have already changed"). Tests: new `lib/crm/stages.test.ts` (pure invariants + each action owner-gated + space-scoped + the Won/Lost floor + reorder permutation + reassign-on-delete + the shared gate null for a non-manager); `registry.test.ts` / `catalog.test.ts` extended for the `space.pipeline` row (inline, primary, crm-gated). No gate weakened; no schema/RLS change.

## ADR-518: Feature tier range + placeholder price points — the reusable "after freemium" affordance (billing on hold)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-04). Phase G (freemium tiering UI) of the ADR-458/472 pricing ladder, building on ADR-517 Phase F (universal Space functions + the clean tier seam). Billing stays ON HOLD; this ships the UI with PLACEHOLDER prices wired to the REAL entitlement tiers. Nothing charges.

**Context (owner directive #9).** "All features need to have a range selector after freemium with upgrade price points." Phase F made every Space function universally available and left the tier gate entirely in the LIVE `featureAllowed` seam (`spaceFunctionAccessLive` → `featureAllowed({plan},{billingLive})`), permissive while `billing_live` is OFF. The model: every feature is AVAILABLE, but usage/limits + advanced depth are gated by freemium tier, and each gated feature shows a range across the tiers (Free → paid) with an upgrade price point. Billing is not live, so real prices are not set yet.

**Decision.** (1) **One feature → tier config.** `lib/pricing/feature-tiers.ts` (PURE, client-safe) is the single source: per tier-gated feature it holds the ordered rungs, what each rung unlocks (a limit/quota or an on/off), and a PLACEHOLDER price point per tier. Prices come from two placeholder maps — `PLACEHOLDER_SPACE_PRICE_CENTS` (Pro $19 / Business $49 / Nonprofit $12 seat / Org from $199) and `PLACEHOLDER_MEMBER_PRICE_CENTS` (Crew $9) — that mirror the code catalog founding rates (`lib/billing/pricing-keys.ts` CATALOG) and are formatted through the existing display convention (`formatCents`, `lib/pricing/display.ts`). No component invents a price. The ladders cover all 12 tier-gated `FEATURE_GATES` keys (space_crm/email/automation/team/multi_pipeline/whitelabel, the three AI-depth keys, and the personal vault_cash_in/gamification_full/vera_unlimited); `feature-tiers.test.ts` asserts every gated gate key has a ladder whose `minTier` equals the gate's `minEntitlement` and that `meetsGate` agrees with each rung's `unlocked` flag, so the display ladder can never drift from the real gate. (2) **The reusable selector.** `components/pricing/feature-tier-range.tsx` (client) renders a segmented RANGE across a feature's tiers: an accessible radiogroup (roving tabindex, arrow/Home/End keys, `aria-checked`), the viewer's current tier highlighted ("Your plan"), each tier's unlock + placeholder price, and a single upgrade CTA. It is DISPLAY + NAVIGATION only — no server action, no mutation — so it weakens no gate. The server seam `components/pricing/feature-tier-upsell.tsx` resolves `billingLive()` (CTA copy only) and mounts it; it is a no-op for a feature with no ladder. (3) **Surfaced on the gated features.** Wired through the standard locked/teaser states: `FeatureLockedNotice` (gained optional `featureKey`/`currentPlan`, renders the range on a PLAN gap for a manager — covers Email and any plan-gated function surface), the CRM board `LockedCrm` (space_crm), and the CRM snapshot teaser `CrmUpsell` (space_crm). Keyed off the feature → tier config, one reusable mechanism, not a bespoke widget per feature.

**Nothing charges — the go-live switch.** `PLACEHOLDER_PRICING = true` (feature-tiers.ts) is the single, obvious flag: while true every price is a preview and the selector's CTA is a plain Link to `/spaces/<slug>/settings/billing` (or `/upgrade`), never a checkout. `billing_live` stays false (no Stripe activation), so `featureAllowed` still grants everything during the beta and the range is informational/preview. To go live: set the real amounts in the two placeholder maps (or point the builder at the operator catalog), flip `PLACEHOLDER_PRICING` to false, and turn on `billing_live` — that is the whole switch. The selector READS the real `plan`/tier for the highlight; it enables no real gating or charging.

**Migration?** NONE. The config is code; the ladders derive from the existing entitlement tiers + code catalog. No `supabase/migrations/*.sql` added or applied.

**Owner input needed before go-live (flagged, not guessed).** The per-tier numeric LIMITS (CRM contact caps, monthly email send volume, seat counts) are left QUALITATIVE in the unlock copy on purpose — those numbers, and the real prices, are an owner decision. Set them (and the real amounts) when billing goes live.

**Consequences.** A consistent "after freemium" range appears on every tier-gated surface via one config + one component; the personal-axis ladders (vault/gamification/Vera) are built and tested and ready to drop onto their personal upsell surfaces (not yet placed — Space CRM/Email were the directed minimum). Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint (touched files), the pricing/spaces/components/app vitest (1042 pass in scope; 2 new test files, 15 new tests), check:canon, check:authz. No gate weakened — the selector is display + navigation only, the manager-only checks reuse `caps.canManageMembers`, and no write path was added.

**Superseded framing (ADR-519).** The per-feature "unlocks feature X" ladder framing is superseded by the USAGE-METER model in ADR-519. `feature-tiers.ts` stays as the price/tier source (and the FeatureTierRange still backs non-metered on/off capabilities), but the primary surfaces now show ALLOWANCES, not unlocks.

## ADR-519: Usage meters, not feature gates — "pay to play", every feature available, higher tiers raise the allowance (billing on hold)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-04). The metered reframe of the freemium value ladder, owner directive #4: "Monetize using MORE of a feature as needed, rather than pay per feature. All functionality available to any space. They just have to pay to play." Builds on ADR-517 (universal Space functions) + ADR-518 (the per-feature range UI). Billing stays ON HOLD; nothing charges, nothing is newly blocked.

**Context.** ADR-518 shipped a per-feature tier ladder whose rungs read as "unlocks feature X". That still frames the model as feature-GATES (whether you can use a thing). The owner directive is the opposite: every feature is available at every tier (already true post-ADR-517 Phase F), and the freemium ladder is about HOW MUCH of a feature you can use. The free tier gives an ALLOWANCE; higher tiers raise the limit. You pay to use more as you grow, not to unlock.

**Decision.** (1) **One usage-meter config.** `lib/pricing/feature-meters.ts` (PURE, client-safe, sibling of feature-tiers.ts) defines, per METERABLE feature, a usage DIMENSION + a per-tier ALLOWANCE (a numeric cap, or `null` = unlimited). Dimensions are drawn only from the real feature set: CRM = contacts; Email = sends per month; Team = seats; Automations = active automations; Pipelines = pipeline count; governed playbooks = playbook runs per month; Resonance Graph = AI matches per month; Vera (personal) = messages per day. Prices come straight from feature-tiers.ts (one price source); the ALLOWANCES are the new constant block. **Non-metered features are named explicitly** in `NON_METERED_FEATURES` with a reason — branding/white-label (on/off capability), the read-only resonance VIEW (its AI usage is metered on `space_crm_resonance_ai`), spending Gems (`vault_cash_in`, an on/off Crew unlock), and the full rewards loop (`gamification_full`, an on/off experience) have no natural quantity, so they stay available with no meter. A coverage test asserts every tier-gated `FEATURE_GATES` key is either metered or consciously non-metered. (2) **The reusable meter selector.** `components/pricing/feature-meter-range.tsx` (client) renders the allowance ladder: an accessible radiogroup (roving tabindex, arrow/Home/End, `aria-checked`), each tier's allowance line ("Up to 100 contacts", "Unlimited contacts") + placeholder price, the viewer's current tier highlighted, an OPTIONAL "X of N used" readout, and a single "Upgrade for more" CTA (a Link, never a checkout). The server seam `feature-meter-upsell.tsx` resolves `billingLive()` (note copy only) and mounts it; it FALLS BACK to the ADR-518 tier ladder for a non-metered capability so that surface still explains which tier turns it on. (3) **Lock → usage-nudge reframe.** The three lock surfaces are reframed from "Unlock X / locked" to "you're on the free allowance, upgrade for more": `FeatureLockedNotice` (plan reason, Gauge icon), the CRM board `LockedCrm`, and the CRM snapshot `CrmUpsell`. Nothing is presented as locked; a viewer within allowance is not blocked.

**Nothing charges / nothing hard-blocks — the enforcement seam.** `withinAllowance(feature, tier, usage, { billingLive })` (feature-meters.ts) is THE single enforcement point. While `billing_live` is OFF it ALWAYS returns true — the meters are informational/preview, so nothing charges and no usage is hard-blocked (a test asserts even usage far over the allowance passes while billing is off, and that a non-metered/unknown feature is never blocked). Once billing goes live the SAME helper compares `usage` to the tier's allowance (`null` = unlimited = always true). That is the whole seam a real hard limit plugs into later; nothing consults it to block today.

**The ONE place to set real numbers + the go-live switch.** Real allowances: the `allowances` maps in `RAW_METERS` (feature-meters.ts), guarded by `PLACEHOLDER_ALLOWANCES` (true today). Real prices: the two placeholder maps in feature-tiers.ts, guarded by `PLACEHOLDER_PRICING`. Go-live = set the real numbers in those two blocks, flip both placeholder flags to false, and turn on `billing_live`. Enforcement then flips on automatically through `withinAllowance` at whatever call sites choose to consult it (none do yet — the seam is clean and unwired).

**Migration?** NONE. The meter ladder is code; it derives from the existing entitlement tiers + the ADR-518 price maps. No `supabase/migrations/*.sql` added or applied.

**Owner input needed before go-live (flagged, not guessed).** Every per-tier allowance number (CRM contact caps, monthly email/playbook/resonance volume, seat counts, Vera daily cap) is a PLACEHOLDER marked `@placeholder` in `RAW_METERS`. The real quantities, and the real prices, are the owner's call — set them (and flip the flags) when billing goes live.

**Optional usage readout.** `FeatureMeterRange` accepts a `usage` prop and renders "X of N used" via the pure `allowanceReadout`. It is not wired to a live count on the CRM upsell surfaces because those render BEFORE any CRM data is fetched (the nudge shows when a space has no CRM data yet), so no cheap count is in hand there. The capability is built and tested; wire a count in where one is already fetched (e.g. the working CRM board's deal/contact counts) if a live readout is wanted.

**Consequences.** The freemium ladder now reads as allowances, not unlocks, via one config + one component; the lock surfaces are usage nudges. `feature-tiers.ts` stays as the price/tier source and still backs non-metered on/off capabilities through the fallback. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint (touched files), the pricing/spaces/components vitest (1014 pass in scope; 2 new test files, 24 new tests), check:canon, check:authz. No gate weakened — the selector is display + navigation only, the manager-only checks reuse `caps.canManageMembers`, no write path was added, and `withinAllowance` cannot block while billing is off.

## ADR-520: The Space menu regroup — 7 member-facing groups over the 9-slot spine, Mode demoted to a Starter chip, usage the visible monetization surface, no lock path for a Space

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-04). Owner item 5. Reorganizes the Space admin rail now that ADR-517 made every Space function universal and ADR-519 made monetization metered. IA/display only — no gate weakened, no scope but `space` touched, no migration. Builds on ADR-514/515/516 (the standardized rail) + ADR-519 (usage meters).

**Context.** The Space rail rendered the engineering 9-slot spine (Basics · Place · People · Layout · Engage · Reach · Comms · Safety · Insights · Billing · Danger) as section headers. Post-ADR-517 nothing is type-gated and nothing is locked for a Space; post-ADR-519 the value story is "use more of a tool", not "unlock a tool". The spine headers no longer match how a member thinks about running a Space, and the rail still carried a lock/unlock band (empty, but present) and buried usage.

**Decision — the 7 groups (approach: per-scope slot→label relabel, NOT a new grouping key).** Each member-facing GROUP re-purposes ONE distinct spine slot for the Space scope; the rail relabels that slot's section header from `SPACE_GROUP_META` (lib/admin/modules/spine.ts) **only when the scope is a Space**, so every other scope's SPINE_META header is untouched. This reuses the whole existing tier/section/search plumbing (which keys on `slot`) with zero new grouping axis. Group order in the rail is driven by (tier, priority), not spine order, so the 7 groups render exactly:

| Group (rail header) | Spine slot | Tier | Surfaces | Placement |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Identity** | basics | standard | Basics (+ identity strip) | inline |
| **Page** | layout | standard | Page builder | inline |
| **Audience** | people | primary | Members (card), CRM (usage card), Vera autonomy, Pipeline | inline (CRM also in base bank) |
| **Offerings & money** | engage | primary | Offerings, Services | inline |
| **Reach** | reach | primary | QR codes, Email | bank (group is bank-only) |
| **Growth** | insights | extra | Insights, Plan and usage | bank (group is bank-only) |
| **Danger** | danger | extra | Danger zone | inline, under "More" |

**Before → after surface map** (SPACE_SURFACES row edits, lib/admin/entities/registry.ts):

| Surface | before (slot · tier · priority · placement) | after |
| --- | --- | --- |
| space.basics | basics · standard · 10 · inline | unchanged |
| space.mode | basics · standard · 30 · inline | **pulled to Starter chip** (excluded from sections) |
| space.layout (Page) | layout · standard · 20 · inline | unchanged |
| space.people (Members) | people · primary · 20 · inline | people · primary · **10** |
| space.engage.crm (CRM) | engage · primary · 10 · **bank** | **people** · primary · **15** · **inline** |
| space.autonomy | engage · primary · 15 · inline | **people** · primary · **20** |
| space.pipeline | engage · primary · 12 · inline | **people** · primary · **25** |
| space.offerings | engage · primary · 30 · inline | unchanged |
| space.services | engage · primary · 40 · inline | unchanged |
| space.reach (QR) | reach · **extra · 10** · bank | reach · **primary · 50** · bank |
| space.comms (Email) | **comms** · primary · 50 · bank | **reach** · primary · **55** · bank |
| space.insights | insights · extra · 20 · bank | unchanged (own href now, below) |
| space.billing → **"Plan and usage"** | **billing** · extra · 30 · bank | **insights** · extra · 30 · bank, relabeled |
| space.danger | danger · extra · 99 · inline | unchanged |

**Starter chip.** `space.mode` is pulled OUT of the section list and rendered as a compact "Starter: {preset}" chip pinned just under the Identity strip (`components/admin/modules/space-starter-chip.tsx`, self-fetching via the read-gated `getSpaceStarterChip`, fail-safe to nothing for a non-manager), with a "Change" affordance to `/manage/mode`. On-canon reframe: a Starter arranges your page and suggests a pipeline; every tool stays available; change any time. It is still `space.mode` — just presented as a chip, never a settings section.

**Retired: the Space lock path.** Under ADR-517 nothing is locked for a Space and the model is metered usage, so a Space surface must never render as a lock. `useSettingsPanel` now computes `lockedApps` only when `!isSpace` (the path stays for the personal / core-entity axis). It was already empty over the live catalog (Space apps are `{on:'spaceType'}`-scoped, which `lockedAppsForScope` never matches); this makes the "no lock on a Space" rule explicit.

**Usage the visible monetization surface (ADR-519 reuse).** (a) **Inline meter on the CRM card.** CRM is kept an INLINE usage card in Audience (the deviation from a literal "CRM → bank": usage must be visible in the body per the directive; it still appears as a base-bank quick-link). `SurfaceSummaryCard` gained an optional `meterKey`: when set and the getter returns the Space `tier`, the card shows a thin `count / allowance unit` line with a quiet fill bar and a subtle "Upgrade" nudge once usage crosses `USAGE_UPGRADE_THRESHOLD = 0.8` (a single constant in feature-meters.ts, **flagged for owner tuning**). The live count is the cheap proxy for the metered dimension (CRM deals ≈ contacts). `space_crm` is wired to CRM, `space_email` to Email (Email is banked, so its card only shows if surfaced; its ladder lives in the hub). (b) **The "Plan and usage" hub (P3).** `space.billing` is reframed to "Plan and usage": the billing page now mounts `FeatureMeterRange` for EVERY plan-axis metered feature under a "Usage" section — the single "where am I on the ladder" answer, current plan highlighted, nothing charged/blocked. (c) **Insights / QR href split (P3).** `hrefForSurface('space.insights')` now returns `/settings/qr#scans` (a `#scans` anchor added to the QR page's Scans section) so Insights no longer dedupes into the QR codes bank button — both stay reachable.

**P4 (page-aware foregrounding) — DEFERRED, documented.** Leading a Space sub-page's rail with that page's group requires threading the Space `type` onto path-derived sub-page scopes (today the Space management rail resolves only from the profile-root "Customize" trigger, which carries the typed detail; a path-derived Space scope has no type, so `appsForScope` returns [] and no Space rail resolves on `/settings/*`). That is deeper plumbing than P1-P3 warranted, and the strategy allowed shipping P1-P3 and leaving P4 as a follow-up. Follow-up: carry the type on sub-page scopes + a sub-page→group map feeding `contextualEntrySlot`/`railArchetypeFor`.

**Other scopes unchanged.** SPINE_META is untouched (only the Space-scoped `SPACE_GROUP_META` overlay was added); `SPINE_ORDER` is untouched; only SPACE_SURFACES rows changed. The core `ENTITY_SURFACES`/`ADMIN_MODULES` and every non-space rail (member/circle/event/hub/nexus/practice/channel/journey) render identically. The `/manage` console groups by surface id (its own CONSOLE_GROUP_FOR), so it is unaffected by the slot remaps.

**Authz.** Unchanged — IA/display only. Every rail getter stays owner-gated (`resolveSpaceManageAccess` + the same per-Space function its sub-page re-checks); the Starter chip getter re-gates on manage access; the CRM card getter re-gates manage + `crm`; no write path added; a11y preserved (semantic tokens, radiogroup meters, fill bars `aria-hidden`).

**Migration?** NONE.

**Consequences.** The Space rail reads as 7 member-facing groups with usage visible where it matters; Mode is a one-line Starter chip; a Space never shows a lock. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint (touched files), the lib/admin · components/admin · components/layout · components/spaces · app/(main)/spaces · lib/pricing vitest (all pass; registry/rail-bank/surface-summaries tests updated for the new grouping, 1 new starter-chip surface + 1 meter test added), check:canon, check:authz. No gate weakened.

## ADR-521: Server-authoritative active timer session + selectable warm-up (countdown, flash, haptics)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-04). Zap-timer fixes. Two related changes to the one timer door (ADR-229): (1) a server row makes a running sit / walk PERSIST cross-device until logged or cancelled, resuming as RUNNING (no prompt); (2) the warm-up pre-roll becomes selectable (3/5/10s) with a count, a flash, and haptics, for both engines. One migration (additive).

**Context — THE BUG.** A running timer (the Mindless sit `components/on-air/session.tsx`, the Get Moving run `components/on-air/movement-session.tsx`) held its whole state in React memory plus a localStorage crash-recovery record (`lib/on-air/live-session.ts`). Elapsed is wall-clock from an epoch `startedAt`, so no time is lost, but on any reload / navigation / second device the member saw a "Resume / Discard" PROMPT (or a fresh setup), not the timer simply carrying on. The owner tracked a walk and every browser reset re-prompted. There was no server record of "a timer is running right now", so nothing could resume cross-device.

**Decision.**
- **`practice_timer_sessions`** (migration `20261014000000`): ONE active row per member (`profile_id` UNIQUE), `practice_id` nullable, `mode` (`mindless`|`movement`), `setup` jsonb (the opaque per-engine payload + `resumeFromSec`), `started_at` (pause-adjusted wall clock), `paused_at` nullable, `seconds_target` nullable. RLS on; members READ their own row; ALL writes go through owner-gated service-role server actions (no client write policy — mirrors `tips` / `supporter_contributions`).
- **Owner-gated actions** (`app/(main)/on-air/timer-session-actions.ts`, self-gated via `getMyProfileId`, no caller-supplied id): `startTimerSession` (upsert), `pauseTimerSession`, `resumeTimerSession` (clears `paused_at`, adopts the shifted `started_at`), `cancelTimerSession` (delete), `getActiveTimerSession` (reader → a `LiveSessionRecord`). `completeSession` clears the row (logging ends the run). All idempotent + fail-safe.
- **Global resume, running not prompt.** `MindlessProvider` reads the server session on load: a localStorage record auto-resumes same-browser; else the SERVER row is fetched and passed as `resumeRecord` to the matching engine. Each engine's mount effect now calls `resumeFromRecord(rec, viaGesture=false)` (auto-resume RUNNING) instead of `setResumePrompt(rec)`; the old Resume-prompt screen is removed. Wake-lock / fullscreen / AudioContext need a gesture, so on an automatic resume the clock resumes immediately and a small non-blocking "Tap to restore sound" chip re-arms audio/screen on the next tap. localStorage stays the fast cache (write-through); the SERVER row is the cross-device source of truth. The pre-roll save-gap is closed (the record is written from `start()` through warm-up).
- **Warm-up (3/5/10s) + flash + haptics.** `OnAirPrefs.warmupSec` (default 5) rides the full prefs pipeline (`completeSession` merge, `session-data` read, the crash-recovery setup payload). A selector sits beside the sit's cue toggles; the main live screen shows a prominent count; the final "one" fires a full-screen flash (semantic tokens only, `motion-reduce` hides it) as `begin()` runs; each warm-up second buzzes (a stronger pulse + optional `countBeep` on "one"), gated on the existing `haptics` / `bell` prefs. Movement mirrors the armed pre-roll (item was a WALK) using the shared `warmupSec` pref.

**Authz.** Every new action is self/owner-gated (session-derived profile, never a target id); `getActiveTimerSession` returns only the caller's row. check:authz passes. `practice_timer_sessions` is not in the generated types yet — reached via the untyped admin cast (ADR-246) until `lib/database.types.ts` is regenerated.

**Migration?** YES — `supabase/migrations/20261014000000_practice_timer_sessions.sql`. WRITTEN, not applied (owner reviews + applies, then regenerates types).

**Consequences.** A running sit / walk persists on Frequency and keeps running across resets, reloads, navigation, and devices, until the member LOGS or CANCELS — no more re-prompt. Warm-up is a real, felt countdown. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint (touched files), vitest (`components/on-air` · `lib/on-air` · `app/(main)/on-air` · `lib/`, all pass; new tests: the active-session actions self-gated + record reconstruction from `started_at`, the warm-up clamp round-trip), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-522: In-app personal profile unified onto the grid engine for owner + visitor, decoupled from the Spotlight publish gate

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-04). Builds on ADR-508 (unified entity blocks) + ADR-516 (freeform rows page-builder). No migration.

**Context.** On the in-app profile (`app/(main)/people/[handle]/page.tsx`) the member's page-builder blocks rendered THREE ways: the OWNER saw the freeform grid (`OwnerProfileLayoutPreview` → `LiveProfileGrid` over `resolveRows(meta.entityGrid,'member')`), while a VISITOR got `ProfileSpotlightBlocks`, which passed a HARDCODED flat order (`profileSpotlightLayout()`) and NO grid — so `meta.entityGrid` was ignored for visitors. Both paths also read through `getPublishedSpotlight`, returning nothing unless the member had a PUBLISHED Spotlight (Crew+ only). Net: the in-rail Layout builder displayed for the OWNER ONLY, and profiles looked different member-to-member by tier / publish flag.

**Decision.**
- **One engine for owner + visitor.** The visitor path (`ProfileSpotlightBlocks`) now renders the member's `resolveRows(meta.entityGrid,'member')` through the SAME `MemberProfileModules` grid branch the owner preview uses — a static render of the identical grid (same arrangement / columns / hidden choices). `MemberProfileModules` enters grid mode whenever the `grid` prop is SUPPLIED (including `null`, which `resolveRows` maps to the default starter), so a null-grid visitor still renders the sensible default. The owner keeps `OwnerProfileLayoutPreview` (its live-edit `LiveProfileGrid`). The old `profileSpotlightLayout()` flat order is retired.
- **Decoupled from the publish gate.** New reader `getMemberProfileModules(handle)` (`lib/spotlight/data.ts`) shares the EXACT safe read of `getPublishedSpotlight` — admin client, the `SPOTLIGHT_SELECT` column allowlist, layout validated on read — minus the `readSpotlightPublished` gate. Both in-app render paths read through it, so every member's grid shows regardless of tier / `meta.spotlight.published`, falling back to the default starter when `meta.entityGrid` is empty. The read stays safe/gated; no write gate weakened.
- **Chrome vs about/stats duplication.** The identity band already shows the bio and the Standing card shows Zaps/Gems/Streak/Rank, so `about` + `stats` would double-render. They are removed from the in-app MEMBER default: the member `STARTER_LAYOUTS` (basic/showcase/minimal) now lead with `links`/`topfriends`, and the member builder locks `about`/`stats` out of its palette + bench (`MEMBER_CHROME_BLOCK_IDS`, applied via the builder's existing `lockedIds`). They stay VALID registry member+space blocks (so a SPACE profile keeps its own about/highlights and the generic layout mechanics + their tests are untouched) — the lock lives at the in-app builder layer, not the registry.
- **Personal editing de-dup.** The full Spotlight enable/publish block inside `ProfileForm` is no longer shown on `/settings/profile` (the page now mounts it with `hideSpotlight`, as the rail wrapper already did). The canonical in-app control is the rail `account.spotlight` module; identity settings and Spotlight are decoupled. `ProfileForm` remains one editor with two entry points (rail + page) — that duplication is intentional and untouched.

**Deviation (stated).** Fix 3 named `defaultMemberLayout()` as a place to drop about/stats, but `defaultMemberLayout()` is SHARED with the public `/spotlight` mini-site (`mergeEntityLayout(defaultMemberLayout(), …)`, which renders the bio via the `about` block with `showBio={false}`). Removing about there would regress the deferred public page (bio would vanish). So the in-app default is de-chromed at the member STARTER + the builder PALETTE instead, and `defaultMemberLayout()` / the shared `MEMBER_PROFILE_BLOCKS` render map are left intact to protect the public surface. The in-app render never merges `defaultMemberLayout()` (it reads `resolveRows(data.grid)` directly), so it never re-acquires about/stats.

**Follow-up (deferred, NOT in this change).** The public `/spotlight/<handle>` Puck page is a separate mini-site with its own published gate; unifying it onto the same grid engine is a later pass.

**Consequences.** Owner-preview and visitor-view show the SAME page; every member's in-app profile has a uniform look independent of tier / publish. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint (touched files), vitest (`components/` · `lib/` · `app/(main)/people/` · `app/(main)/settings/`), check:canon, check:authz. Tests updated: member starter layouts (basic/minimal/mutation), a new chrome-block registry assertion.
## ADR-523: Admin-rail wiring cleanup (D1-D4, stale space-preview registry, console IA unify)

**Status:** Accepted · corroborated by the touched files below
**Context:** An audit surfaced a batch of small admin-rail wiring inconsistencies, all
anchor-verified. None change authorization; each is a mechanical dedup/retire.
**Decision (SAFE subset applied):**

- **D1 — duplicate rail trigger.** `page-admin-bar.tsx` `isEntityDetail` only matched
  `circles|events|practices`, so on `hubs|nexuses|channels|journeys` detail pages (which now
  mount their own `OpenAdminBarButton`) the generic shell cog ALSO fired. Extended the regex
  to include those four so the generic caps-blind cog is suppressed wherever a page owns its
  trigger (single-trigger parity with circles/events/practices).
- **D2 — dead `space.mode` inline module.** `SpaceModeModule` never rendered (the rail
  unconditionally filtered `space.mode` out of `inlineApps`). Deleted the component, its
  `module-map` entry + import, the `getSpaceModeData` getter (+ now-unused imports), and the
  filter line (nodeForApp returns null for a component-less inline app, so it drops on its own).
  KEPT the `space.mode` SPACE_SURFACES row — it still feeds the console link, `surface-hrefs`,
  the Starter chip's Change affordance, and the `catalog.test` INLINE assertion. Mode is edited
  via the Starter chip → `/manage/mode`. Also deleted the orphaned `lib/apps/bindings.tsx`
  (`componentFor` seam, zero importers).
- **D3 — duplicate circle challenge editor.** `CircleQuestModule` mounted `CircleChallenges`
  (adopt/drop) in the SAME engage slot as the first-class `circle.engage` module. Dropped that
  mount (kept the read-only Journeys/Practices lists), mirroring the earlier practice-picker
  dedup, so challenges are editable in exactly one place.
- **D4 — journey detail trigger parity.** Added an owner/operator-gated
  `OpenAdminBarButton scope={{ kind: 'journey', id: plan.id }}` to the journey DETAIL page's
  actions slot (gated on `getJourneyCapabilities(...).has('journey.editSettings')`), mirroring
  `/learn`, so the scoped journey rail is reachable from the root page, not only the player.
- **Stale space-preview fork retired.** The staff `/profile-preview` render passed no `grid`, so
  it fell through to the TYPE-shaped `defaultProfileLayout` while the live page renders the
  FUNCTION-only grid. Fixed the preview to compute + pass the same grid the live page uses
  (`blocksForKind('space')` filtered by `requiresFunction`), and retired the per-TYPE gate from
  `defaultProfileLayout` (now function-only, matching the live palette) so the flat fallback is
  uniform too. Fixed the stale "nothing live reads this yet" comment (it IS live). Files stay
  (their `ProfileBlockId`/`profileBlockById`/`PROFILE_BLOCKS`/`toProfileContext` exports are used
  elsewhere), so only the type gates were removed, per the audit's conditional.
- **Console IA unified to the ADR-520 7 groups.** `manage/console.tsx` still rendered the
  pre-ADR-520 5-group IA (Space · People · Offerings · Reach · Billing and insights · Danger).
  Re-grouped it to cluster by spine SLOT and label from the shared `SPACE_GROUP_META` (Identity ·
  Page · Audience · Offerings & money · Reach · Growth · Danger), so the console and the in-rail
  bar agree. Same links/targets; grouping/label change only.

**Authz.** Unchanged. check:authz passes.
**Consequences.** Live renders verified unchanged where intended: the live space profile page
still passes its own grid (untouched); the Starter chip still reads `getSpaceStarterChip` (kept).
Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint (touched files), vitest (`components/` · `lib/admin/` ·
`app/(main)/` · `lib/apps/`, 401 pass; plus the updated `profile-blocks*` + `console` tests),
check:canon, check:authz. No em dashes in touched copy; semantic tokens only.

## ADR-524: Spotlight Puck retired — the grid is the single engine end to end (public /spotlight renders the grid; editor → grid builder)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-04). The deferred follow-up named in ADR-522. Builds on ADR-508 (unified entity blocks) + ADR-516 (freeform rows builder). No migration.

**Context.** ADR-522 unified the IN-APP profile (owner + visitor) onto the grid engine but deliberately left the PUBLIC `/spotlight/<handle>` mini-site and its Puck EDITOR for a later pass. Two engines still coexisted for the personal Spotlight: the grid (`meta.entityGrid`, the in-rail builder) and the legacy Puck editor (`/settings/profile/spotlight`, writing `meta.spotlight.layout/theme/background/themes/draft`). This retires the Puck engine from the personal Spotlight so the grid is the single engine end to end. (ADR-508 keeps Puck for freeform MARKETING + SPACE landing pages — untouched here.)

**Decision.**
- **Public render → grid.** `app/spotlight/[handle]/page.tsx` renders the member's grid through the SAME `MemberProfileModules` grid path the in-app visitor uses (`resolveRows(meta.entityGrid,'member')`, falling back to the default starter when null), inside the unchanged shared `SpotlightShell` (identity header + theme + background + join CTA). The public mini-site now shows the member's actual builder arrangement, never Puck.
- **Publish gate kept.** The public page is opt-in, so it still reads through `getPublishedSpotlight` (the `readSpotlightPublished` gate). A not-enabled / not-published member 404s exactly as before. Only the RENDER changed (grid, not Puck); the in-app profile stays ungated per ADR-522.
- **Safety: no migration, no Puck fallback needed.** The grid render sources content-block data from the RETAINED validated `data.layout` (the member's `meta.spotlight` blocks, still read + validated on the security boundary in `lib/spotlight/data.ts`), grouped by type by `resolveMemberBlockData`, while `meta.entityGrid` only controls ARRANGEMENT. So a member who only ever used the old Puck editor (Puck blocks, empty `entityGrid`) still renders their authored content, in the default starter order (`resolveRows(null)` is always non-empty) — never blank. A migration to preserve exact Puck ORDER is impossible anyway: the grid is TYPE-keyed (one `heading`/`text`/... cell that renders all authored blocks of that type), not instance-keyed. `SpotlightShell` also always renders the identity header + CTA, so the page can never be visually empty. Proven by `lib/spotlight/grid-safety.test.ts`.
- **Editor retired → redirect.** `/settings/profile/spotlight` is now a thin owner-only redirect to the in-rail grid builder on the member's own profile (`/people/<handle>`). Every "Build your Spotlight" link (profile-form, people/[handle] owner header, personal-spotlight-module) points at the grid builder. `page-chrome.ts` no longer treats the route as a full-viewport/full-width editor takeover (it keeps the standard global rail).
- **Code deleted vs kept.** DELETED (unreachable after retirement): `components/spotlight/{puck-editor,theme-editor,theme-slots,publish-bar,spotlight-live-preview,spotlight-identity,profile-theme-picker,spotlight-chrome,puck-render,spotlight-page}.tsx`, and the `meta.spotlight` WRITER actions (`saveSpotlightLayout/Background/Theme`, `saveSpotlight*ThemeSlot*`, `saveSpotlightDraft`, `publishSpotlightDraft`). KEPT: `SpotlightShell` (public grid chrome); the READ path in `lib/spotlight/data.ts` incl. `validateSpotlightLayout` (still the content source + security boundary); `setSpotlightEnabled/Published` (now gate the GRID's public visibility); `saveMemberGridLayout` (the grid builder); `uploadSpotlightImage` + Top Friends actions (used by the marketing linktree block / the `spotlight_top_friends` table); and ALL of `lib/spotlight/puck/*` + `lib/page-editor/*` (shared with the KEPT marketing/space Puck). NOT touched: any Space or marketing Puck usage.

**Follow-up (deferred).** The retired Puck editor was the only UI to edit page THEME/BACKGROUND and pick TOP FRIENDS. Existing values still render (theme/background via `SpotlightShell`, Top Friends via the `topfriends` block); the server actions are retained. A grid-side theme/background/Top-Friends editor is a later pass.

**Consequences.** One engine (the grid) drives the in-app profile AND the public mini-site; the personal Spotlight has no Puck. No published page goes blank. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint (touched files), vitest (`app/` · `components/` · `lib/`), check:canon, check:authz. New test: `lib/spotlight/grid-safety.test.ts`; updated: `lib/layout/page-chrome.test.ts` (the retired route is no longer an editor takeover).

## ADR-525: Spotlight appearance (theme/background/Top Friends) gets a grid-side rail admin surface, replacing the retired Puck editor's controls

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). Closes the deferred follow-up named in ADR-524. No migration.

**Context.** ADR-524 retired the Puck Spotlight editor and, with it, the ONLY UI to edit the member's profile SKIN, the Spotlight page THEME (header framing + fonts), the page BACKGROUND (image + focus/dim/zoom), and TOP FRIENDS. Those values still render on the public `/spotlight/<handle>` page via `SpotlightShell` (`data.theme`/`data.background` from `lib/spotlight/data.ts`, `profile.profile_theme` via `resolveProfileSkin`), and the Top Friends + image-upload actions were retained — but the theme/background WRITER actions were deleted and no surface exposed any of it. This gives the four functions a home in the personal admin rail (the canonical place now that the editor is gone), on the grid-side.

**Decision.**
- **Restored owner-gated writers.** `setSpotlightTheme(theme)` and `setSpotlightBackground(background)` in `app/(main)/settings/profile/spotlight-actions.ts` write the SAME validated `meta.spotlight.theme` / `meta.spotlight.background` shape `lib/spotlight/data.ts` reads back (`spotlightThemeStyles` + `SpotlightShell` consume it), so the public read path is unchanged. Both are SESSION-DERIVED (bind to the authed user's own row via `auth_user_id`, no target id), require Spotlight enabled, VALIDATE on write (`validateSpotlightTheme` / `validateSpotlightBackground`, the asset path pinned to the owner's folder), reuse the retained `withSpotlightTheme` / `withSpotlightBackground` merge helpers, and `revalidatePath('/people/<handle>', '/spotlight/<handle>')`. They restore the minimal shape of the deleted `saveSpotlightTheme`/`saveSpotlightBackground` (dropping the retired draft/theme-slot machinery). The profile SKIN reuses the ALREADY-RETAINED `updateProfileTheme` (validated against the `PROFILE_SKINS` allowlist) rather than adding a duplicate.
- **A rail Appearance module.** A new global-scope personal module `account.spotlightAppearance` ("Spotlight look") — `components/admin/modules/personal-appearance-module.tsx`, registered in `lib/admin/modules/registry.ts` + `module-map.tsx` — renders inline in the personal "You" body next to Spotlight/Layout, mounting on the member's own `/people/<handle>` and `/settings/profile` (the ADR-516 surface predicate). It self-fetches the read-gated `getAppearanceRailData` (in `rail-getters.ts`; re-gates the authed viewer, returns null when signed out or the member cannot enable Spotlight) and edits: the profile **skin** (`updateProfileTheme`), the Spotlight **header** show/height/focus + fonts (`setSpotlightTheme`), the **background** image via the retained `uploadSpotlightImage` + focus/dim/zoom (`setSpotlightBackground`), and **Top Friends** via the retained `setTopFriends`/`reorderTopFriends`/`removeTopFriend` with a picker sourced from `getAcceptedFriendsForPicker` (caller's own accepted friends only). It is NOT the browser-palette `account.appearance` (which stays a bank link) — this is the Spotlight page's own look.
- **Reuse, not duplication.** The Top Friends actions, `uploadSpotlightImage`, `updateProfileTheme`, and the pure `spotlight-flags`/`theme` validators are all reused unchanged. `lib/spotlight/puck/*` and all Space/marketing Puck are untouched. No gate is weakened; every write re-checks auth server-side.

**Consequences.** The four appearance functions the retired editor owned are editable again, from the personal rail, with no engine regression — the public `/spotlight` read path is byte-for-byte unchanged. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint (touched files), vitest (`app/(main)/settings/profile/` · `lib/spotlight/` · `components/admin/` · `lib/admin/` · `components/spotlight/`), check:canon, check:authz. New tests: `spotlight-actions.appearance.test.ts` (the writers, self-gated + validated) and `rail-getters.appearance.test.ts` (the getter); updated `lib/admin/modules/registry.test.ts` (the new inline personal module).

## ADR-526: The two profile kinds get distinct in-rail editors — member = single-column block LIST, space = two-column layout editor with a 66/33 ratio

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration (the layout blob is opaque `profiles.meta.entityGrid` / `spaces.preferences.profileLayout` JSON; new fields are additive and every read fails safe).

**Context.** ADR-516 built ONE freeform-rows page builder (`EntityPageBuilder`) shared by the member (Spotlight) and space (Spaces) profiles: both got the same rows-with-columns editor. In practice the two profile kinds want DIFFERENT shapes. A member profile is a personal page whose frame (header image, identity band, Standing card) is chrome; the content underneath reads best as a simple vertical list, and a member should not be composing multi-column grids. A space profile is a business page (Facebook/LinkedIn grammar) that genuinely benefits from a two-column layout with a wider lead column. The shared 4-column editor over-served the member and under-specified the space (no ratio control), and the space page panel still carried three now-obsolete controls: a Type/Focus switcher, a structural template picker (irrelevant under freeform rows), and a Header-vs-Hero cover-size toggle.

**Decision.**
- **Per-kind max columns (the authority).** `maxColumnsForKind(kind)` in `lib/entity-blocks/layout.ts` — `member: 1`, `space: 2`. The server `sanitizeEntityLayout(raw, kind)` and the renderer's `resolveRows(layout, kind)` CLAMP every row to the kind's max (dropping overflow blocks to the derived bench), so a member layout can never persist or render more than one column and a space never more than two, regardless of any stale saved value.
- **Member = single-column block LIST.** `EntityPageBuilder` branches on `maxColumns`: for the member it renders a plain reorderable list (no rows, no columns, no starters) — each placed block is a strip with on/off (hide), up/down, drag-reorder, and a menu; an "Add block" row appends an unplaced one. The member palette is `blocksForKind('member')` minus the chrome blocks (about/stats), so the Spotlight/authored blocks (Top Friends, Links, Heading, Text, Image, Gallery, Quote, Embed, Divider) are all visible and arrangeable. The member profile always uses the Header cover image (`ProfileCover`); there was never a member Hero option.
- **Space = two-column layout editor with a ratio.** The space keeps the rows editor, capped at `[1][2]` columns, plus a per-row SPLIT control on a 2-column row: 50/50 (even) or 66/33 (`ratio: 'lead'`, a wider lead column). The new optional `RowDef.ratio` (`'even' | 'lead'`, only meaningful at `columns === 2`, sparse — only `lead` is stored) flows through parse/sanitize/resolve/normalize and `setRowRatio`; `EntityGrid` renders `lead` as `sm:grid-cols-[2fr_1fr]`. Clicking a block opens its controls (the label is a button). The space profile ALWAYS uses the Hero cover (`readCoverSize` now returns a constant `'hero'`).
- **Space page-panel cleanup.** Removed the Type/Focus switcher (Mode stays a free preset via the Starter chip, ADR-517), the structural template picker (superseded by freeform rows), and the cover-size toggle (always Hero now). Their server actions (`setSpaceCoverSize`, `setSpaceProfileTemplate`) and the dead `getSpacePageData` fields (`coverSize` / `profileTemplate` / `focus`) were deleted; the Hero scrim control now always shows.

**Consequences.** Each profile kind gets an editor matched to what it is: members manage a clean single-column list, spaces compose a two-column page with a real 66/33 option. The clamp makes the model self-correcting for any legacy layout. No migration and no public-render regression for existing single-column profiles (a 1-column rows layout is unchanged). Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint (touched files), vitest (`lib/entity-blocks/` · `components/entity-blocks/` · `lib/spaces/`), check:canon, check:authz. New/updated tests cover the per-kind clamp, the 66/33 ratio through parse/sanitize/resolve, `setRowRatio`, and the `sm:grid-cols-[2fr_1fr]` render.

## ADR-527: Cleanup — retire the rail Starter chip and the code orphaned by the ADR-526 profile redesign

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration; no user-facing feature removed beyond the rail chip.

**Context.** ADR-526 split the profile editors and removed the space page panel's Type/Focus switcher, template picker, and cover-size toggle. That left three orphans: the rail "Starter" chip (ADR-520, a compact "Starter: {preset}" chip that duplicated Mode framing in the Space rail), the legacy `BlockGridEditor` (the pre-ADR-516 template+slots editor, superseded by the freeform `EntityPageBuilder` and with zero remaining importers), and `readProfileTemplate` (the dead reader for the retired template picker).

**Decision.**
- **Removed the rail Starter chip.** Deleted `components/admin/modules/space-starter-chip.tsx` and `getSpaceStarterChip` (rail-getters), and unwired `starterChip` from the settings-panel model + `admin-bar-body` render. Space **Mode stays a creation-time preset** — it still drives labels, the seeded CRM pipeline (`lib/crm/stage-templates.ts`), nav emphasis, marketing personas, and the creation wizard — and is still editable on the full `/spaces/<slug>/manage/mode` page reachable from the manage console. Only the redundant rail chip is gone; `lib/spaces/modes.ts` (`resolveMode` et al.) is untouched.
- **Deleted dead code.** `components/entity-blocks/block-grid-editor.tsx` (no importers) and `readProfileTemplate` (unused after ADR-526). The legacy `template` key is still tolerated on READ by the entity-layout model (`resolveRows`), so any old saved layout keeps rendering.

**Consequences.** The Space rail is leaner (identity strip, then the sections — no Mode chip) and the tree carries no dead editor/reader. Mode as a concept is unaffected. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint (touched files), vitest, check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-528: Inline per-block content + style editor in the rail (blocks author their own content, no Puck)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration (content + style ride the opaque EntityLayout blob; additive, fail-safe on read).

**Context.** ADR-516/526 let an operator ARRANGE blocks (rows, columns, on/off) but a block's CONTENT still came from the Puck Home doc (`resolveSpaceAuthoredContent`), and there was no per-block styling. The operator asked to (1) split a 2-column row 1/2, 2/3, or 1/3; (2) edit each block inline when clicked (text/link, on/off, the info each box needs); (3) style each block (background on/off + basic layout); (4) make every editor block a real content block on the page.

**Decision.**
- **Row split gains a third option.** `RowRatio` is now `even | lead | trail` — 50/50, 66/33 (wider first), or 33/66 (wider second); `EntityGrid` renders `trail` as `sm:grid-cols-[1fr_2fr]`. The rail split control shows 1/2 · 2/3 · 1/3.
- **Per-block content + style on the layout blob.** `EntityLayout` gains `content` (Record<blockId, props>) and `style` (Record<blockId, {background,pad,align}>), keyed by block id, validated on parse AND write by `lib/entity-blocks/block-content.ts`: a block-id key is allowlisted against the registry (blocks prototype pollution), every field is coerced to its schema and bounded, every URL passes `safeUrl` (http(s)/mailto/tel/relative only — no `javascript:`/`data:`). They thread through `BuilderLayout`, the shared store, and both save actions (which already run `sanitizeEntityLayout`), and are carried onto the effective grid by `mergeEntityLayout`.
- **Inline editor.** Clicking a block in the rail opens `BlockEditPanel`: a CONTENT block (heading/text/links/image/gallery/quote/embed/divider) gets its authored fields; a DATA block gets on/off + quick title/intro + a "Manage" link to that feature's own console (deep data stays in its manager, per the chosen scope). Every block gets style controls (card background, spacing, alignment). Edits apply to the store (instant repaint + debounced save) and schedule a `router.refresh()` so the server-rendered live preview reconciles the new content/style.
- **Own render, no Puck.** `ContentBlockView` + `BlockStyleFrame` (server-safe) render the authored content and wrap every block in its style frame. The space renderer reads content/style off the effective grid; a content block falls back to any legacy Puck-doc content when its inline bag is empty, so existing profiles do not regress. Puck stays for the external website only.

**Consequences.** Every block on a profile is now inline-editable and styleable in the rail, with the page previewing live; content authoring no longer requires Puck. Fail-safe + injection-safe throughout; no migration. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint, vitest (3927 passed), check:canon, check:authz. New: `lib/entity-blocks/block-content.ts` (+ test), `components/entity-blocks/content-block-view.tsx`, `components/entity-blocks/block-edit-panel.tsx`; content/style coverage added to the layout, rows-ops, and entity-grid tests. Follow-up: wire the member profile renderer to the same content store (the model + editor already support it; only the member render path still reads legacy content), and richer per-content-type controls.

## ADR-529: Profile editor refinements — instant style, curated core palette, per-block admin areas, ratings split

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration.

**Context.** After the inline content/style editor shipped (ADR-528), five refinements were requested from the live Space profile: (1) the block background toggle appeared to do nothing; (2) each block's settings should be relevant to it; (3) the rating was buried inside the "Find us online" links card; (4) a function-backed block should link to its own admin area; (5) the palette should be narrowed to a best-practice, non-overwhelming set.

**Decision.**
- **Instant live style (1).** The owner live-preview node map now renders each block UNSTYLED (`renderSpaceBlockNodes` → `styled=false`), and `LiveProfileGrid` applies each block's `BlockStyleFrame` CLIENT-side from the shared store. A background / spacing / alignment edit repaints immediately instead of waiting on a server refresh (the visitor render still styles server-side). The card-background toggle stays available on every block (owner directive).
- **Relevant settings (2).** The edit panel already branches by category — content blocks show their authored fields, data blocks show on/off + a section title/intro + a Manage link; `divider` shows style only. Combined with (4), each block's settings map to what it is.
- **Ratings split (3).** The operator-entered rating moved out of the Business ("Find us online") block into the Reviews block, which now renders a compact rating card when a rating is set (else the coming-soon card). "Find us online" is links-only.
- **Per-block admin areas (4).** A data block's "Manage" link points at that FEATURE's own admin surface (`about`/`contact`/`reviews` → basics, `offerings` → offerings, `booking` → availability, `team` → members), falling back to the Space console; content blocks get no link.
- **Curated core palette (5).** `profilePaletteForKind` narrows the offered palette to `CORE_PROFILE_BLOCK_IDS` — About, Offerings, Booking, Events, Team, Reviews, Contact (+ member Top Friends) and the four essential content blocks (Heading, Text, Links, Image). Retired from the OFFER: Highlights, Practices, Circles, Business, FAQ, Updates, Gallery, Quote, Embed, Divider. NON-DESTRUCTIVE: a retired block that is still placed on an existing profile keeps rendering (fail-safe); it just cannot be re-added, and the fresh-default starters now seed the curated core.

**Consequences.** The Space editor is leaner and each block's controls are relevant and wired to a real admin area; style edits preview instantly; ratings stand on their own. No migration; existing profiles are unaffected beyond the narrowed offer. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint, vitest (3929 passed), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-530: Final polish pass — member content render wired, editor simplified for mobile, rail search gap closed

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration.

**Context.** A final polishing pass on the admin rail + profile editor: make sure every function is editable and connected, simplify the interfaces, and work on mobile. Two read-only audits (rail wiring; mobile + simplicity) drove the fixes.

**Decision.**
- **Member content + style now render (connection gap).** The member profile renderer read the legacy member data bag, so a member's inline-authored content/style (ADR-528/529) never showed. `renderMemberBlock` now renders a content block from the layout's `content` bag (falling back to the member component) and applies the per-block style frame — rendered UNSTYLED into the node map so `LiveProfileGrid` applies style client-side (instant), exactly like the space path. The member preview seeds `content`/`style` into the store.
- **Rail search gap (audit 1).** The wiring audit found the rail fully connected (no dead links, no stub getters) except `space.mode`, which is console-only (ADR-527) yet appeared in the rail SEARCH index as an inert result. It is now excluded from `searchApps`.
- **Editor simplified for the narrow rail (audit 2).** The row strip carried two look-alike segmented controls (columns + split) plus row up/down + a menu, overflowing the 288px rail on a 2-column row. The **column split (1/2 · 2/3 · 1/3) moved into the row menu** (it only applies to a 2-column row), the redundant **"Move to position" grid was removed** (up/down covers it), the **dead hover-only "Add row"** between rows was removed (it never revealed — its `<li>` had no `group` class — and the persistent Add row already covers it), the per-block **Style controls collapsed behind a "Style" disclosure** so the panel leads with content, and the **column control's touch target** was enlarged.

**Consequences.** Members' inline edits now render; the rail search no longer surfaces a dead result; the block builder fits the narrow/mobile rail and reads as fewer, clearer controls. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint, vitest (3929 passed), check:canon, check:authz. Deferred (documented, lower priority): touch drag-handles remain desktop-only (up/down arrows + the Move-to / Place-in menus are the touch reorder path), and a further 44px-touch-target sweep across every builder icon button.

## ADR-531: The per-block background toggle fills WHITE, not an elevated tinted card

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration.

**Context.** The per-block style control (ADR-528) offered a "Card background" switch that painted `bg-surface-elevated` (#FAF6EC, the warm cream elevated tone) behind the block. Against the page canvas (`--color-canvas` #FBF8F1, also warm cream) the two tones are nearly identical, so switching it "on" read as stacking a second faintly-tinted layer on top of the canvas rather than giving the block a distinct background — the operator's feedback: "turn the white background on / off, not add another background layer."

**Decision.** `BlockStyleFrame`'s background fill is now the plain white surface (`bg-surface`, #FFFFFF) instead of `bg-surface-elevated`. White reads as a crisp, deliberate card against the warm canvas, so the toggle is an honest "white background on / off." The control is relabeled **"White background"** to match. Everything else about the frame is unchanged (same `rounded-2xl border`, same padding default, applied client-side in `LiveProfileGrid` for instant preview).

**Consequences.** The background toggle now does what it says — one white surface, no stacked tint. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint, vitest (3929 passed), check:canon.

## ADR-532: Condense + reorder the Space Basics rail — one name per header, pictures first, section-level block move

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration.

**Context.** Operator feedback on the Space admin rail (Basics editor + page builder): the rail stacked three near-synonymous labels before the first field ("IDENTITY" group → "Basics" module → "Identity" section), pinned a redundant identity strip (cover + logo thumbnail + name + Edit) that duplicated fields already in the form, buried the header/logo uploaders below the text, wrapped the brand swatches beside the color well, and made moving a page block between sections awkward (the block menu's "Move to" only listed empty column slots, and drag is desktop-only).

**Decision.**
- **One name per header (no doubles).** The `SpaceBasicsModule`'s own "Basics" title + description is dropped: the rail already labels the group "Identity" (`SPACE_GROUP_META`), so the form's own section headers carry the structure. The form's leading section is renamed **"Identity" → "Name & bio"** so nothing echoes the group label.
- **Remove the identity strip.** `SpaceIdentityStrip` (the "cover + logo + name + Edit" card pinned atop the rail) is deleted along with its `getSpaceIdentityData` getter and the `identityStrip` field on the admin-bar model — the Basics form itself now shows and edits those images at the top.
- **Pictures first, header full-width.** The Basics form reorders to **Pictures (header banner + logo) → Name & bio → Brand → Visibility**. The header image spans the full width (a true banner preview, not a squeezed half column); the logo sits beneath at a constrained square size.
- **Brand swatches on a full line.** `AccentPicker` stacks: the color well + hex + Default on top, the suggested on-brand swatches as one full-width row (`grid-cols-8`) beneath.
- **Section-level block move (touch-friendly).** The page-builder block menu replaces the empty-slot-only "Move to (Row X, column Y)" list with **"Move to section N"** for every row plus **"New section"**. Moving fills the target row's first empty slot, or — if that row is full — drops the block into a fresh section just below it; "New section" appends a row at the end. Both no-op cleanly at the `MAX_ROWS` cap. This works with a tap (no drag), so it functions on mobile; up/down arrows and desktop drag are unchanged.

**Consequences.** The Space Basics rail reads top-to-bottom with a single header per level and the visitor-facing images up top; blocks relocate section-to-section on any device. Member (single-column) editor is untouched (its block menu passes no sections). Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint, vitest (3929 passed), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-533: Space rail rework, Stage 1 — one "Business info" + one "Branding" section (kill the identity/business duplication)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration. First of a staged Space-rail rework.

**Context.** The Space admin rail had grown THREE overlapping identity/business editors: the top "Identity" Basics form (name / tagline / About / header image / logo / accent / visibility), a separate Business info form buried in the Page module's "More page settings" disclosure (Story / address / hours / phone / email / website / socials + its OWN header + logo uploaders), and the Page module's own "Cover style" + "Theme and accent" sections. Header image was editable in three places, logo and accent in two, and "About" (the `spaces.about` column) vs "Story" (the `profileData.about` blob) were two different stores both meaning "about". Operator feedback: duplicate content, out of order, needs condensing.

**Decision.** Consolidate every identity/business field into exactly TWO rail sections, each field with one editor:
- **Business info** (Section 1, the `basics` slot relabelled from "Identity"). A single new `SpaceBusinessInfoForm` holding every WORD: brand name, tagline, **About** (short intro → the `spaces.about` column, `updateSpaceProfile`), **Story** (longer narrative → the `profileData.about` blob, `setSpaceBusinessInfo`), the contact block (address, hours, phone, email, website, rating, socials), and visibility. One Save fires both writes; both re-gate server-side.
- **Branding** (Section 2, a NEW `space.branding` surface on the repurposed `place` spine slot, relabelled "Branding" for the Space scope in `SPACE_GROUP_META`). A new `SpaceBrandingForm` with every VISUAL field: header image, logo, cover style (Shade/Blend, compact buttons), and theme accent. Each control saves on its own action.
- The Page module's `SpacePagePanel` **loses** its Cover style, Theme/accent, and Business info sections (now in Branding + Business info); it keeps the block editor, Pages, and External website. The old `SpaceBusinessForm` is deleted (orphaned).
- The `/manage` console gains a Branding card (icon + href to `/settings/basics` interim, since Branding has no standalone page yet); `groupForSurface` clusters the `place` slot into the Identity/Business-info console group so the console keeps its seven ADR-520 groups.

About↔Story needs **no migration** — both stores already exist and already render (the `profileData.about` blob is the public "Our story" card; the `spaces.about` column feeds the About module), so this only gives each one editor and one label.

**Deferred to later stages:** the page-editor rework (Section 3), a "More Pages" coming-soon section (Section 4), services rendered as one expandable summary-block section each, and pinning Billing + Danger at the bottom. The standalone `/settings/basics` + `/settings` pages still use the older combined `SpaceSettingsForm` and will be migrated to the split forms in a follow-up.

**Consequences.** The Space rail now leads with two clean, non-overlapping sections; every identity/business/brand field has exactly one home. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint, vitest (3929 passed), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-534: Space rail rework, Stage A — three admin-rail bug fixes (squish, cover re-select, load UX)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration.

**Context.** Operator-reported bugs on the Space edit rail: (1) refreshing with the rail open came back with the rail GONE but the content still squished; (2) the cover-style buttons let you pick Blend but would not let you switch back to Shade until a reload; (3) the rail loaded slowly, did not always start at the top, and gave no feedback while a section was fetching.

**Decision.**
- **Squish/rail mismatch (bug 1).** The bar persists `open` in localStorage and reported it to the shell, which sizes the content column down. But a Space rail resolves its apps from the click-time `detail.spaceType`, which a hard refresh loses, so the panel rendered nothing (`hasContent` false) while the shell still squeezed the content. Two fixes: the bar now reports `open && hasContent` (the squeeze tracks what actually renders), AND it **persists the last typed-open `detail`** (scope + caps + spaceType + spaceFns, all serializable) to `sessionStorage` keyed by path, restoring it on hydrate so the Space rail **repopulates on refresh** instead of coming back empty.
- **Cover re-select (bug 2).** `SpaceBrandingForm`'s scrim buttons keyed their active/disabled state off the server `coverScrim` prop, which only updates on `router.refresh()`, so after one pick the other button stayed disabled until a reload. The buttons now track the selection in **optimistic local state** (synced back from the prop with the render-time adjust-on-prop-change pattern).
- **Load UX (bug 3).** The bar **scrolls its body to the top** whenever it opens or its scope changes; each self-fetching rail module now shows a shared **`RailModuleLoading` ("Working…")** indicator (spinner + polite live region) instead of a bare pulse skeleton; and the detail-restore above means the rail's functions reload on refresh rather than staying blank.

**Consequences.** Refresh no longer strands squished content behind a missing rail, and the rail restores its scope + content; cover style switches both ways instantly; the rail starts at the top and shows honest "Working…" feedback. Full app speed-up of the per-module self-fetch is deferred to the inline-workspace stage. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint, vitest (3929 passed), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-535: Space rail rework, Stage B — the standardized sections (Identity & Branding · Info & Connect · Settings)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration. Regroups ADR-533's two sections into the owner's standard set.

**Context.** Owner directive: the rail should open with a small, standard set of sections that any rail can reuse, split by WHAT the content is: what shows in the header hero, the forward-facing marketing/connect info, and the lower settings. ADR-533's "Business info + Branding" split (text vs visual) did not match that; name/tagline belong with the hero, and ratings/visibility are settings, not marketing.

**Decision.** Regroup into three standardized sections (Spaces first; the block set generalizes to other rails later):
- **Section 1 — Identity & Branding** (`space.branding`, `place` slot, priority 10, renders FIRST): everything in the header hero — brand name, tagline, header image, logo, cover style, accent. Name + tagline save on BLUR; images/cover/accent each save on their own action (no Save button). `SpaceBrandingForm` gained the name + tagline fields; `getSpaceBrandingData` now returns them.
- **Section 2 — Info & Connect** (`space.basics`, priority 15): the forward-facing content a Spotlight/profile shows — About, Story, contact (address/hours/phone/email/website), and social links. One Save writes the About column + the profileData blob. Renamed the form `SpaceBusinessInfoForm → SpaceInfoConnectForm`; dropped name/tagline (→ Identity & Branding) and ratings/visibility (→ Settings).
- **Lower Settings** (`space.settings`, NEW, `safety` slot, priority 70 — sorts late): the knobs pulled out of the forward-facing sections — rating + count, and visibility. New `SpaceSettingsForm` + module + `getSpaceSettingsData`. `setSpaceBusinessInfo` MERGES, so ratings here and Story/contact in Info & Connect never clobber each other.
- **Page settings visible.** The Page panel's "More page settings" `<details>` disclosure is unwrapped — Pages + External website render directly on the rail (owner directive: visible, not hidden).
- **Console:** the new `place`/`safety` slots cluster into the identity console group so the `/manage` console keeps its seven ADR-520 groups; `space.settings` gets an icon + href.

**Consequences.** The rail reads Identity & Branding → Info & Connect → Page → …services… → Settings, a standard shape. Every field still has exactly one editor. Deferred: the page-organizer rework (Stage C) and the inline-workspace (Stage D). The standalone `/settings/basics` page still uses the older combined form pending its migration. Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint, vitest (3929 passed), check:canon, check:authz.
