## ADR-536: Space rail rework, Stage C — the simplified page organizer (curated connected palette, two-way split, arrange-on-top)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration.

**Context.** The page organizer offered ~20 blocks but only ~6 rendered — the rest were data blocks with no wired source (practices, circles, faq, updates, highlights) or rarely-used content blocks (gallery, quote, embed, divider), so the operator kept placing blocks that showed nothing ("19-20 listed, 6 working"). The split control had three ratios, and the "Add row" control sat below the rows. Owner directive: make it super simple — only blocks that connect to real profile info, one split adjuster (50/50 or Main/Side), and arrange controls above the blocks.

**Decision.**
- **Curated, connected palette.** `CORE_PROFILE_BLOCK_IDS` is narrowed to the blocks that show live profile info or a wired feature: About, Offerings, Book, Events, Team, Reviews, Contact, **Find us online** (`business`, re-added — it renders the Info & Connect socials), plus the content blocks Heading, Text, Image. A new `KIND_PALETTE_EXCLUSIONS` drops the authored `links` list from the SPACE palette (Find us online covers links) while keeping it for the MEMBER (whose bio-link list is `links`). Retired blocks still RENDER if already placed (fail-safe); they just cannot be re-added.
- **Two split options.** The 2-column split control offers only **50/50** and **Main / Side** (a wider main column on the LEFT). The legacy wider-right `trail` still renders if a layout has it, but the control coerces it to Main / Side, so there is never a third choice.
- **Arrange controls on top.** The primary "Add row" action moved ABOVE the rows outline (it was below), so building the page reads top-down; the duplicate bottom control was removed.

**Consequences.** The organizer offers ~11 blocks that all render something real, one clear split adjuster, and a top-down arrange flow. Click-to-edit (the inline block panel) and the section-level move (ADR-532) are unchanged. Deferred to follow-ups: splitting About (short) vs Story (long) into two distinct blocks, and the inline-workspace stage (services + deeper settings rendering in the page body). Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint, vitest (3929 passed), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-537: About and Story are two distinct profile blocks

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration.

**Context.** ADR-535 split a space's identity text into two fields — About (a short intro, the `spaces.about` COLUMN) and Story (the longer narrative, the `preferences.profileData.about` BLOB) — but the page organizer still had ONE `about` block, which rendered the BLOB ("Our story" card). So the short About column had no block, and the two fields could not be placed independently.

**Decision.** Two page blocks, one per field:
- **`about` block** renders the SHORT About (the `spaces.about` column) under "About this space"; null when empty.
- **`story` block** (new) renders the LONG Story (the `profileData.about` blob) as the "Our story" card (the former `about` block body).

The short column is threaded fail-safe into the render bag: `getSpaceAbout(spaceId)` (an untyped admin read of `spaces.about`, empty ⇒ `''`) joins the existing request-cached `getSpaceLiveContent` pass and is exposed as `SpaceContentData.aboutShort`. `story` is registered (`lib/entity-blocks/registry.ts` order 15, `ProfileBlockId`, `PROFILE_BLOCKS`), added to `CORE_PROFILE_BLOCK_IDS`, and wired into `SPACE_PROFILE_BLOCKS`. Space-only (a member profile never sees `story`).

**Consequences.** Operators can place About and Story independently, matching the two Info & Connect fields. No migration (both stores already existed). Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint, vitest (3929 passed), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-538: Space rail rework, Stage D1 — services open INLINE in the space page (?panel), starting with Members

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration. First sub-stage of the inline workspace (Stage D).

**Context.** Owner directive: the space page is the single workspace. The hero + tab menu stay fixed at the top; a primary function (a service manager, a deeper settings editor) should open in the BODY below them, never navigating the operator off the page. Today those managers are separate routes (`/spaces/<slug>/crm`, `/settings/*`). Confirmed decisions: the panel REPLACES the profile body; it must be fast and never leave the page; CRM stays full-width at its own route (the one exception); the standalone routes keep working as deep links (the "full admin" reachable from the console), while primary editing happens inline.

**Decision (the seam, proven on Members).** The hero + menu live in the route-group layout `(profile)/layout.tsx`, and an App Router layout does NOT re-render on a query-string change, so a `?panel=<id>` soft-navigation swaps ONLY the page body while the hero + menu persist. Concretely:
- `(profile)/page.tsx` reads `searchParams.panel`; for a viewer who passes the SAME owner/manage gate that already picks `OwnerSpaceLayoutPreview`, and a known panel id, it renders `<SpaceBodyPanel>` INSTEAD of the profile body (else unchanged). Visitors never see a panel.
- `components/spaces/workspace/surface-panels.ts` is the registry (`SURFACE_PANELS`, `isPanelId`, `PANEL_SURFACE_TO_ID`) — one row per panelable surface (D1: `members`).
- `SpaceBodyPanel` renders a light inline header (label · "Back to page" → `/spaces/<slug>` · "Open full page" → the standalone route) over the manager's chrome-free body.
- Each manager body is extracted into a chrome-free, self-gating Server Component (`settings/members/members-body.tsx`); the standalone route keeps rendering it inside its `FocusTemplate`, so deep links are unchanged.
- The RAIL links via a new `panelHrefForSurface` (a panelable surface → `?panel=`, else the normal route); `hrefForSurface` is UNTOUCHED, so the `/manage` console + the bottom bank keep their full routes (the "full admin sub-menu"). CRM is never a panel.

**Consequences.** Clicking Members in the rail now opens the roster inline under the persistent hero + menu; the standalone `/settings/members` route still works for deep links. The seam (registry + `panelHrefForSurface` + chrome-free body) is the template the remaining services (D2), deep-settings editors (D3), and menu polish (D4) reuse — CRM excepted (full-width). Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint, vitest (3932 passed), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-539: Space rail rework, Stage D2 — the remaining services open inline (Offerings, Services, QR, Email, Billing)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration. Extends ADR-538 (D1) to the rest of the service managers.

**Context.** D1 (ADR-538) proved the inline-workspace seam on Members: a rail link opens a `?panel=<id>` that swaps the profile body (hero + menu persist) with the manager's chrome-free, self-gating body, while the standalone route stays a deep link. D2 replicates that for the remaining panelable services.

**Decision.** Add inline panels for **Offerings** (`space.offerings`), **Services** (`space.services`), **QR codes** (`space.reach`), **Email** (`space.comms`), and **Plan & usage / Billing** (`space.billing`), mirroring the D1 pattern exactly:
- Each route's body is extracted into a chrome-free, self-gating `<X>Body` Server Component (`settings/<x>/<x>-body.tsx`); the route `page.tsx` gates + wraps it in its existing template, so the standalone route renders byte-identically (including the feature-locked variants for QR/Email/Billing and Billing's locked/unlocked title swap).
- `surface-panels.ts` stays PURE (label + `fullHref` + `PANEL_SURFACE_TO_ID`, imported by the client rail); the server-only body components live in a `PANEL_BODIES` map inside `SpaceBodyPanel` (a Server Component), so no server body import leaks into the client bundle. Body type accepts async Server Components.
- `hrefForSurface` + `lib/admin/rail-bank.ts` are untouched — the `/manage` console and the bottom bank keep linking QR/Email/Billing to their full routes; only the RAIL body opens the panel via `panelHrefForSurface`.
- **CRM stays full-width** at its own route (never a panel), per the owner directive; Insights (shares the QR route) and Danger are left as full-route/inline as before.

**Consequences.** Every primary service now opens inline in the space page under the persistent hero + menu; each standalone route still works as a deep link. Deferred: deep-settings full editors as panels (D3), menu active-state + polish (D4), optional route redirects (D5). Gate green: `tsc --noEmit`, eslint, vitest (3932 passed), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-540: Space rail rework, Stage D4 — inline workspace panel polish

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration. Polish on the D1/D2 inline service panels.

**Decision.** Three polish items on the `?panel=` inline service panels: (1) the panel body streams behind a `<Suspense>` `ProfileBodySkeleton` so switching panels shows an instant skeleton, not a blank swap; (2) the panel header (label · Back to page · Open full page) is sticky beneath the profile menu, so the exit stays reachable while scrolling a long manager; (3) `SpaceProfileMenu` reads `?panel` and drops Home's active pill when a panel is open, plus a compact "You are editing <label>" affordance (aria-current) so the open panel reads as the current surface. Standalone routes byte-identical. Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest (3932), check:canon, check:authz.

**Consequences.** The inline service panels feel finished. NOTE: this stage polished the SERVICE panels; it did NOT address the page-layout editor, which is tracked separately (the owner's core ask — see the next planning cycle).

## ADR-541: Space rail rework, Stage D5 — CRM bounded panel + the manage console folds on-page

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration. Extends the D1/D2 `?panel=` inline-workspace seam to the CRM and the /manage console cards.

**Decision.** Two changes complete the "everything happens on the space page" model for the remaining deep surfaces. (1) **CRM opens INLINE, bounded.** A new chrome-free `crm-body.tsx` renders the default CRM board (People view) directly in the profile body under the persistent hero + menu; `space.engage.crm` maps to panel id `crm` in `PANEL_SURFACE_TO_ID`, and `space-body-panel.tsx` wraps it in an `overflow-x-auto` bounded container (the board stays width-contained in the page rather than going full-bleed — the owner: "bounded in the page for now, we might try full screen later"). The Pipeline/Cockpit views and the `?contact=`/`?stage=` deep sub-views stay on the full `/crm` route, reachable via the panel's "Open full page". (2) **The manage console folds onto the page.** The `/manage` console cards now resolve their hrefs through `panelHrefForSurface` instead of `hrefForSurface`, so every panelable surface (members, offerings, services, qr, email, billing, crm) opens as an on-page panel when clicked from the console, matching the rail. The `/manage` route + its redirect are unchanged; the console page itself stays a deep link, but its cards are now on-page entry points. Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest (3933), check:canon, check:authz.

**Consequences.** Settings area #2 (the deep, on-page admin) now covers the CRM and the full manage console, so navigating any previous manage surface from the side rail or the console keeps the owner on the space page. This closes Stage D. The page-layout editor rebuild (the owner's core ask) remains the separate, next planning cycle.

## ADR-542: The page-editor rebuild — column stacks, four free-form blocks, on-page WYSIWYG editing

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No DB migration (the layout still lives in `spaces.preferences.profileLayout`); the persisted shape gains a `cells` field with full back-compat for the old `slots` shape.

**Context.** The owner asked repeatedly to fix the page editor. Their definitive spec: a simple STACKED-ROW editor built ON the page (WYSIWYG), where rows have 1 or 2 columns (2-col = 50/50 or Main/Side), a column holds MULTIPLE stacked content boxes, boxes are user-data blocks plus a few core free-form blocks, all other content features removed, the layout picker made legible. Prior stages kept editing in the cramped 288px admin rail; this rebuild moves it onto the page and changes the model so a column is a stack, not a single slot.

**Decision.**
- **Model (column stacks).** `RowDef` changes from `slots: (string|null)[]` (one block per column) to `cells: string[][]` (each column is an ordered STACK of block ids; empty column = `[]`; invariant `cells.length === columns`). Global dedup still holds — a block id appears at most once across the layout (a v1 simplification: multiple instances of the SAME free-form block is deferred). `MAX_STACK = 12`. `parseRows` reads the new `cells` and MIGRATES the legacy `slots` shape (`slots:[a,null]` → `cells:[[a],[]]`), so every saved layout keeps rendering. `parse/sanitize/resolve/templateToRows/starters` and all of `rows-ops.ts` are ported to stacks; `swapCells` (adjacent-cell swap) is replaced by `nudgeBox` (reorder one step within a column stack), `placeBlock` gains an optional stack index and APPENDS instead of displacing the occupant to the bench, and `setRowColumns` narrowing MERGES the dropped columns' boxes onto the last kept column (nothing is lost). `EntityGrid` renders each column's stack.
- **Blocks + palette.** The SPACE palette is now the 9 connected data sections (about, story, offerings, booking, events, team, reviews, contact, business) + 4 free-form blocks: **Callout** (authored title / body / button / image), **Image Gallery** (reuses the existing `gallery`), **Journeys** (a DATA block that auto-pulls the space's hosted journeys from `data.practices.journeys`), and **Features** (authored repeating icon / title / text items). The legacy authored blocks (heading / text / links / image) and the never-wired data blocks (highlights / practices / circles / faq / updates / quote / embed / divider) are excluded from the SPACE palette (they stay registry entries so member profiles + any existing placement still render). New `block-content.ts` schemas + sanitizers cover callout and a new `features` field type; `content-block-view.tsx` renders callout + features; a new `JourneysBlock` renders the hosted-journeys group via `EXTRA_DATA_BLOCKS`.
- **On-page WYSIWYG editor.** Editing moves onto the page: the owner arranges blocks directly on the live grid (driven by the existing shared `EntityLayoutProvider` store, which already repaints the page instantly + saves debounced). The space rail stops being the layout editor (the member rail block-list editor is unchanged).

**Consequences.** A space column can hold a real stack of boxes; the four free-form blocks give the owner authored content that renders; the palette only offers blocks that actually connect to something. Back-compat is total (old `slots` layouts migrate on read). DEFERRED to a follow-up: multiple instances of the same free-form block (needs instance ids + an instance-keyed, CodeQL-safe content sanitizer). Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest, check:canon, check:authz.

**Revision (2026-07-05, same cycle).** The owner rejected editing chrome ON the page: "I don't want to see edit functions on the page. I want a simple page arranger in the side bar with the page showing what is changed there." So the on-page editing overlay (`on-page-editor.tsx`) is removed; the page renders the LIVE RESULT only (no chrome, visitor and owner identical). The layout is arranged from the SIDEBAR again — the `SpacePageBuilder` (rows + columns + the curated palette) re-mounts in the `space.page` rail module, and its edits still repaint the on-page preview instantly through the shared `EntityLayoutProvider` store. The column-stack model, the four free-form blocks, and the palette cleanup from ADR-542 are unchanged. Additionally, the builder's per-section up/down arrows now carry a section BETWEEN rows (nudge within its column stack, and at the stack edge jump to the adjacent row), so moving a section from one row to another is a one-tap arrow (in addition to the existing "Move to section" menu and drag). Removed: `on-page-editor.tsx`, `space-layout-rail-note.tsx`, `box-position.ts`.

## ADR-543: Modular menu system, P0 — the universal SPACE module contract + manifest

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). Additive, no migration, no render change. Foundation for the modular menu program (docs/MODULAR-MENU.md).

**Context.** The admin menu should be a set of INDEPENDENT modules, one per primary area or service (CRM, Booking, Members, …), on one modular template that expands to every scope, plus an admin area to manage which modules/features a space runs. Today two parallel systems exist: `lib/admin/modules/registry.ts` (`ADMIN_MODULES` + `modulesForScopeKind`) covers 8 scopes (global, circle, hub, nexus, event, practice, channel, journey) on a `requiredCapability: Capability` gate; SPACE is the outlier — its services are `render:'link'` link-rows in `lib/admin/entities/registry.ts` (`SPACE_SURFACES`) gated on a `SpaceFunctionKey` (space has NO `Capability` values). This phase brings space onto the module contract.

**Decision.** A new PURE module `lib/admin/modules/space-modules.ts` declares the SPACE menu as a catalog of independent modules (`SPACE_MODULES: SpaceModule[]`) on the universal shape: id, label, Icon, `family` (space | audience | offerings | reach | growth | system), `slot`, `gate` (`always` for a shell area | `feature` for a service gated on a `SpaceFunctionKey`), `featureKey` (the toggle the Module Manager will flip), `render`, `deepLink(slug)`, `order`, `tier`. The catalog is the full independent set: the shell (Identity & Branding, Info & Connect, Page, Settings, Danger) + Members + CRM (to absorb Vera autonomy + Pipeline in P1) + the SEVEN independent commerce modules (Booking, Memberships, Donations, Enrollment, Tickets, Check-in, Store — the owner's directive, un-merging the old collapsed "Offerings" surface) + QR + Email + Insights + Plan & Billing. `spaceModuleManifest(entitlements, {hidden, order})` is the single PURE resolver: filters by feature gate (default ON — a service shows unless `spaces.entitlements` maps it to `false`), drops owner-hidden modules, and orders by owner preference then catalog order. Unit-tested (11 cases): catalog completeness vs every `SPACE_FUNCTION`, the commerce split, default-on gating, and the manifest's hide/order overrides. ADDITIVE: nothing renders from this yet — the live SPACE_SURFACES rail/console path is untouched.

**Consequences.** The contract + manifest are the durable seam the rest of the program builds on: P1 renders the rail + console from `spaceModuleManifest` (service split + CRM consolidation go live), P2 wires each module's on-page body + deep link, P3 is the Module Manager (writes `hidden`/`order` + feature entitlements), P4 levels up the other scopes, P5 retires the duplicate `SPACE_SURFACES` registry. Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest, check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-544: Modular menu, P1 — the manage console renders from the module manifest

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration. First render surface onto the P0 contract; the rail is deferred to P1b.

**Decision.** The Space `/manage` console renders from `spaceModuleManifest` (ADR-543) instead of `spaceSurfacesFor`/`SPACE_SURFACES`. `manage-board.tsx` builds the module list and gates it by the SAME authoritative `canUse(fn)` (role + plan + entitlement via `spaceFunctionAccess`), so a space with a function off drops that module. `console.tsx` is retyped `SpaceSurface → SpaceModule`: it folds the finer module slots into the existing 7 member-facing console groups (`groupForModule`), renders each row from the module's own `Icon`/`label`/`desc`, keeps Mode as a secondary within-group signal (a module's function = `gate.kind==='feature' ? gate.fn : null`), and keeps the Danger inline delete. A new pure `panelHrefForModule(module, slug)` resolves each row's href panel-first (a module-id→panel-id map for members/crm/Store/qr/email/billing, so the Stage-D5 on-page `?panel=` behavior does NOT regress) and falls through to `module.deepLink(slug)` for the modules without a panel yet (the 7 commerce services open their existing `/settings/*` pages). This makes the **service split** (7 independent commerce modules) and the **CRM consolidation** (one `space.crm`, no separate autonomy/pipeline rows) live in the console. `hrefForSurface`/`panelHrefForSurface` are untouched for their other callers.

**Consequences.** The console is the first surface on the unified contract; adding/removing a space module is now one catalog entry. The rail still renders from `SPACE_SURFACES` (its `lib/apps` catalog layer is coupled to the legacy surfaces + `MODULE_COMPONENTS` + summaries + their tests) — retargeting it is P1b. Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest (3948), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-544b: Modular menu, P1b — the SPACE rail shows the independent modules (service split + CRM consolidation)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration. Brings the SPACE admin RAIL to the same independent-module set the P1 console already shows (docs/MODULAR-MENU.md — P1b). SPACE scope only; no other scope's rail changes.

**Context.** P1 (ADR-544) split the manage console into independent modules from `spaceModuleManifest`, but the rail still rendered the legacy merged commerce surface. The rail's data path is NOT `SPACE_SURFACES` directly — it flows through the `lib/apps` catalog: `SPACE_EDITOR_APPS` (lib/apps/catalog.ts) derives one editor `App` per `SPACE_SURFACES` row keyed by `{on:'spaceType'}` + a `spaceFunction`/`none` gate; `appsForScope` resolves them for the viewer's per-Space functions; `settings-panel.tsx` renders the body (inline editors + `render:'link'` link-rows, hrefs via `panelHrefForSurface`) and the bottom bank (`placement:'bank'` surfaces + fixed `rail-bank` links). `MODULE_COMPONENTS`, `SURFACE_SUMMARIES`, `rail-bank`, `surface-hrefs`, and their tests are all coupled to the `SPACE_SURFACES` ids.

**Decision.** Take the sanctioned MINIMAL path: change the rail's surface SOURCE (`SPACE_SURFACES` in lib/admin/entities/registry.ts) rather than retarget the whole `lib/apps` layer, keeping every piece of rail plumbing intact. The merged `space.offerings` row is removed and replaced with the SEVEN independent Offerings & money surfaces — Booking (`availability`), Memberships (`memberships`), Donations (`donations`), Enrollment (`enroll`), Tickets (`tickets`), Check in (`checkin`), and the existing Store (`space.services`, relabelled from "Services", `null`-gated) — each gated on its OWN per-Space function, so a commerce surface shows exactly when its function is usable (the per-type shaping `canUse(fn)` already encodes; no adaptive-visibility special-case). The standalone `space.autonomy` + `space.pipeline` rows are removed: CRM is now a SINGLE `space.engage.crm` row (desc updated to fold in Vera autonomy), matching the console's one `space.crm` module — autonomy + pipeline return as CRM sub-areas in P2, and `MODULE_COMPONENTS` drops their (now unmounted) inline bindings. `spaceSurfacesFor` drops the Offerings exception (each surface gates on `requiredFunction`); `hrefForSurface` gains the six commerce deep-links to the existing `/settings/*` pages; the legacy `space.offerings` href case stays mapped (the adaptive page + Mode next-best-actions still reference it). The rail keeps its exact tier/priority/placement (QR · Email · Insights · Plan and usage stay banked; Danger stays inline under "More"), and every inline shell editor (Identity & Branding · Info & Connect · Page · Settings) mounts unchanged. `catalog.ts`, `settings-panel.tsx`, `for-scope.ts`, `SURFACE_SUMMARIES` (aside from the Store copy), and `rail-bank` are untouched.

**Alternatives.** (1) Retarget `SPACE_EDITOR_APPS` at `spaceModuleManifest` (the "rail literally on the manifest" ideal) — rejected for this phase: it forces rail-only presentation facets (bank placement, the danger link fallback, panel-href-by-module-lookup, priority ordering) back into the pure P0 manifest or a parallel map, reorders the shipped rail, and rewrites the catalog + for-scope parity tests — strictly more runtime-critical churn for the same visible result. The full manifest convergence + `SPACE_SURFACES` retirement stays P5's job. (2) Keeping "Services" as the label — rejected: "Store" is the canon module name the console already shows.

**Consequences.** The rail now shows the same independent module SET as the console: seven Offerings & money rows and one CRM row, per-type-shaped by each tool's function. The rail's CRM id stays `space.engage.crm` (the console's is `space.crm`); both render one CRM row, so the divergence is internal only and collapses when P5 retires `SPACE_SURFACES`. Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest (3949), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-545: Modular menu, P2 — full inline bodies + snapshots for the SPACE commerce services

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration. Gives the six independent commerce service modules a full on-page `?panel=` body + a rail snapshot, so all seven Offerings & money services (with Store) open INLINE like Members/CRM/Store/QR/Email/Billing (docs/MODULAR-MENU.md — P2). SPACE scope only.

**Context.** P1b (ADR-544b) split the rail + console into the seven independent commerce modules, but only Store already opened inline (`?panel=services`); the other six (Booking / Memberships / Donations / Enrollment / Tickets / Check in) still deep-linked to their `/settings/*` route. Those six routes were already refactored to redirect into the unified Offerings surface, with each service's chrome-free UI living in its `settings/<svc>/section.tsx` (composed by `offerings-body.tsx`, which self-gates then stacks the type-applicable sections). The inline-panel seam (Stage D1–D5) dispatches a panel id → a self-gating `{ slug }` body via `PANEL_BODIES` (space-body-panel.tsx), keyed off the pure `SURFACE_PANELS` / `PANEL_SURFACE_TO_ID` registry and the `panelHrefForModule` / `panelHrefForSurface` maps (surface-hrefs.ts).

**Decision.** Add a chrome-free, self-gating `*-body.tsx` per commerce service (availability/memberships/donations/enroll/tickets/checkin) that mirrors `members-body.tsx`'s gate EXACTLY — resolve the Space (fail closed), `resolveSpaceManageAccess` → null for a non-manager/non-staff — then delegate to the EXISTING `*Section` (which re-checks its own per-Space function gate + owns every write action). Reusing the section verbatim means zero UI duplication and no gate weakened. Register each in `SURFACE_PANELS` (panel id → `{label from the module catalog, fullHref → the `/settings/*` route}`), `PANEL_BODIES`, `PANEL_SURFACE_TO_ID` (rail surface id → panel id), and `MODULE_PANEL_ID` (module id → panel id, so `panelHrefForModule` opens the panel from the console). The standalone `/settings/*` routes are UNCHANGED (they still redirect into the unified Offerings surface, which composes the same section). **Snapshots:** add three cheap, read-gated single-table counts to `manage/rail-getters.ts` — Booking (availability windows), Memberships (tiers), Tickets (ticket tiers) — each re-gated via `resolveSummarySpace` (manage access + the surface's own function) and fail-safe to null, wired into `SURFACE_SUMMARIES` so the rail card shows a glanceable line. **CRM sub-areas:** Vera autonomy + Pipeline are already folded into the CRM panel — `crm-body.tsx`'s `CrmViewTabs` surfaces People / Pipeline (the `SpacePipeline` editor) / Cockpit (the `AutonomyControl` autonomy dial), each linking to its full view from the inline panel.

**Alternatives.** (1) Convert the six redirect pages into full standalone template-wrapped pages — rejected: it changes standalone behavior (a single-service page instead of the unified Offerings surface) and risks the redirect tests, for no panel benefit. (2) Snapshots for Donations / Enrollment / Check-in — deferred: Donations is one ask + Enrollment is one program (no natural count), and Check-in's count is a capture scan; none is a clean cheap single-stat, so their panels stay plain link-rows. (3) Full inline `?view=` switching inside the CRM panel — deferred (kept from D5): threading `?view=` through the `?panel=` seam collides with the profile query param, so the CRM tabs link out to the full board (the "links clearly" bar).

**Consequences.** All seven commerce services open inline in the profile body (hero + menu persist), matching D1–D5. Booking / Memberships / Tickets carry a rail snapshot; the other three stay plain link-rows. No server-side gate changed: each body self-gates on manage access and its section re-checks the per-Space function. Tests: new `surface-panels.test.ts`; extended `surface-hrefs.test.ts` (the six panel + module mappings) and `surface-summaries.test.ts` (the three snapshots). Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest, check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-546: Modular menu, P3 — the Module Manager (owner-gated menu + feature control)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-05). No migration. The owner-facing admin area that persists the menu overrides the P0 manifest already reads (docs/MODULAR-MENU.md — P3). SPACE scope only; ships space-first.

**Context.** P0–P2 made `spaceModuleManifest(entitlements, { order, hidden })` the pure resolver for the SPACE menu and rendered the manage console from it, but nothing yet WROTE the `{ order, hidden }` overrides or gave the owner a place to flip a feature on or off. Two write paths already existed and are REUSED rather than reinvented: (1) `setSpaceFeatureEnabled(slug, fn, enabled)` (settings/features/actions.ts, ADR-518 Phase G) — owner/admin-gated, writes the universal on/off switch into `spaces.entitlements` (sparse: enabling deletes the key, disabling sets `false`; a plan-gated feature can only be turned ON within the plan); (2) `saveSpaceGridLayout` / `saveSpaceProfileLayout` (settings/profile/actions.ts) — the guarded merge-into-`spaces.preferences.<node>` template (untyped admin client, ADR-246, preserving every other key).

**Decision.** A new `preferences.moduleMenu` node `{ order?: string[]; hidden?: string[] }` holds the menu presentation overrides. A pure, fail-safe reader `readModuleMenuPrefs(preferences)` (lib/spaces/module-menu.ts) validates every id against `SPACE_MODULES`, de-dupes, and DROPS any unhideable id from `hidden` (the shell config surfaces Identity/Info/Page/Settings, Danger, and the Module Manager itself — `UNHIDEABLE_MODULE_IDS` / `isModuleHideable`, new pure helpers on the catalog), so a partial / stale / hostile blob can never break a render or strand the owner. A guarded server action `saveSpaceModuleMenu(slug, { order, hidden })` mirrors `saveSpaceGridLayout` exactly: resolve the Space, gate OWNER/ADMIN (`getSpaceCapabilities.canManageMembers` — an editor / staff previewer is refused, matching `setSpaceFeatureEnabled`'s authority), sanitize the lists, merge the node into `spaces.preferences` SCOPED to the resolved space id (`.eq('id', …)`), preserving every other key. The manage CONSOLE (`manage-board.tsx`) now reads `readModuleMenuPrefs(space.preferences)` and feeds `{ order, hidden }` into `spaceModuleManifest`, so a hidden module drops and the owner order applies. The Module Manager itself is a new catalog module `space.modules` (family `system`, slot `safety`, always-on, no `featureKey`, deep-linking to `/spaces/<slug>/manage/modules`); the console gates it to OWNER/ADMIN (`caps.canManageMembers`), narrower than the console's editor-level manage gate, so a mere editor never sees the menu-management entry point. The UI is a centered Focus page (`manage/modules/page.tsx`, gated exactly like settings/features) rendering `ModuleManager`, a client grid of every catalog module grouped by family with, per row: the module icon + label, a tier badge, a feature ON/OFF `Switch` (reused `components/ui/switch`; hidden for shell modules, an upgrade nudge for a plan-gated locked one), a hide-from-menu toggle (hard-disabled for unhideable modules), and up/down reorder controls (within the module's family, so families stay coherent). Feature toggles call `setSpaceFeatureEnabled`; reorder/hide call `saveSpaceModuleMenu`; both are optimistic with rollback.

**Alternatives / scope.** (1) A rail INLINE module vs the Focus page — chose the Focus page: the feature-toggle sibling (settings/features) is already a Focus page composing the same action + `FocusTemplate`, so this is the consistent, lowest-risk home, and a full module grid reads better centered than crammed inline. The `space.modules` catalog entry still surfaces it in the console menu. (2) Wiring `{ order, hidden }` into the RAIL — DEFERRED to P3b: the rail still renders from `SPACE_SURFACES` via the `lib/apps` catalog (not the manifest), so honoring the owner order/hidden there is the same retarget P5 owns; the CONSOLE (the primary Module Manager surface) is wired fully, and hiding/ordering takes effect there today. (3) Enforcing hideability in the manifest — rejected: the manifest stays a dumb pure resolver (it drops whatever `hidden` contains); the unhideable guard lives in the reader + action + UI, so a bad stored blob is corrected on read.

**Consequences.** The owner can turn each service on or off, reorder their menu, and hide a module, and the console reflects it immediately; the shell / Danger / Module Manager can never be hidden or turned off. No existing gate weakened: `setSpaceFeatureEnabled` is reused unchanged, and `saveSpaceModuleMenu` self-gates owner/admin and scopes its write. Tests: new `lib/spaces/module-menu.test.ts` (fail-safe read + sanitizers) and extended `space-modules.test.ts` (the `space.modules` module + hide/family metadata); the manifest's existing order/hidden coverage stands. Rail-order remains P3b. Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest (3989), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-546b: Modular menu, P3b — the SPACE rail renders from the manifest + honors the Module Manager overrides

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-06). No migration. The coupled refactor P1b/P3 deferred: the SPACE admin RAIL now derives from `spaceModuleManifest` and honors the owner's per-space `preferences.moduleMenu` (order + hidden), so hiding / reordering a module in the Module Manager (P3) takes effect in the RAIL, not just the /manage console (docs/MODULAR-MENU.md — P3b). SPACE scope only; every other scope's rail is byte-identical.

**Context.** Through P3 the rail's SPACE app list still derived from `SPACE_SURFACES`: `SPACE_EDITOR_APPS` (lib/apps/catalog.ts) mapped one editor `App` per surface row, `appsForScope` (lib/apps/for-scope.ts) resolved them for the viewer's per-Space functions, and `settings-panel.tsx` rendered the body + bottom bank. The console had already moved onto `spaceModuleManifest` (ADR-544), but the two menus derived from parallel sources, and the Module Manager's `{ order, hidden }` reached only the console — the rail could not honor them because its app list was a static catalog with no per-space overrides threaded to it.

**Decision.** Retarget ONLY the SPACE scope's rail app derivation at the manifest, leaving every other scope's lane untouched. (1) `SPACE_EDITOR_APPS` is rebuilt from `SPACE_MODULES` (not `SPACE_SURFACES`): one App per INDEPENDENT module, keyed `{ on:'spaceType', type:'*' }` + a `spaceFunction`/`none` gate, so the rail resolves the SAME set the console + Module Manager show (service split, one CRM module, and the new `space.modules` Module Manager row). `render` maps `inline` → an inline editor (MODULE_COMPONENTS, ids unchanged) and `panel`/`link` → a link-row (href panel-first via the new `panelHrefForModuleId` → `panelHrefForModule`, so the Stage-D5 `?panel=` behavior does NOT regress). (2) `SpaceModule` gains two additive RAIL fields — `priority` (within-band order) and `placement` (`bank`) — carried onto the App exactly like `tier`, set to the legacy surface values so the shipped rail layout is byte-identical: Settings stays a late footer in the primary band, and QR · Email · Insights · Plan and usage stay banked; Danger stays a link-row under "More" (its `render` flips `inline`→`link`, unused by the console which special-cases `space.danger` by id). (3) The owner overrides ride the `AdminScope` (a new `moduleMenu` field) from the Customize trigger (the space `(profile)/layout.tsx` reads `readModuleMenuPrefs(space.preferences)` and passes it through `OpenAdminBarButton` → the AdminBar detail → `settings-panel`), and a PURE `applySpaceModuleMenu` in `appsForScope`'s space branch drops HIDDEN modules and reassigns each survivor's within-`(tier, slot)`-band priority by the owner ORDER — so the default (catalog) order maps priorities back to themselves (the rail is unchanged) and only a genuine reorder permutes a band, while the owner-ordered list also drives the bottom bank order. The CRM rail summary is re-keyed from the surface id `space.engage.crm` to the module id `space.crm`.

**Alternatives / scope.** (1) Threading `{ order, hidden }` into the pure `spaceModuleManifest` / catalog rather than at resolve time — rejected: the catalog is a per-request-invariant superset (the search index reads it), so per-space overrides belong at `appsForScope`, keyed off the scope, exactly where the viewer's function gate already lives. (2) Retiring `SPACE_SURFACES` now — DEFERRED to P5: `spaceSurfacesFor` / the `SpaceSurface` type + the surface-id map (`hrefForSurface`, the Mode next-best-actions in `lib/spaces/modes.ts`, and `rail-bank`'s fixed CRM link) still consume the legacy surface ids, so full retirement stays P5's job; P3b retargets only the rail lane and leaves `SPACE_SURFACES` in place. (3) Owner/admin-gating `space.modules` in the rail as narrowly as the console (`canManageMembers`) — the rail is caps-blind by design (the Customize trigger is already manager-gated and the `/manage/modules` page self-gates owner/admin server-side), so it shows as an always-on shell module and the page stays the authority.

**Consequences.** One menu source: hiding or reordering a module in the Module Manager now takes effect in the rail (body + bank) and the console alike. The only visible change to the default rail is the added "Menu and features" row under "More"; every band, the bank membership, the inline shell editors, and the on-page `?panel=` service bodies are unchanged, and `space.mode` (already a no-op rail row) drops out. Other scopes are byte-identical (verified: the `appsForScope` parity test for the 8 entity scopes still passes; only the space branch calls `applySpaceModuleMenu`, only the space lane changed in the catalog, and the settings-panel edits are inside the `space`/`spaceSlug` guards). No gate weakened. Tests: `lib/apps/catalog.test.ts` + `lib/apps/for-scope.test.ts` retargeted to the manifest (+ hidden/order coverage), `space-modules.test.ts` extended (priority/placement), `surface-summaries.test.ts` re-keyed. Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest (3995), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-547: Modular menu, P4 — retire the duplicate `SPACE_SURFACES` registry (space fully on the ONE module system)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-06). No migration. Pure de-duplication with NO intended behavior change to the space menu (docs/MODULAR-MENU.md — P4). SPACE scope only; every other scope untouched.

**Context.** Since P1 (console, ADR-544) and P3b (rail, ADR-546b) the space menu has rendered entirely from ONE source — `SPACE_MODULES` + `spaceModuleManifest` (lib/admin/modules/space-modules.ts). The parallel `SPACE_SURFACES` registry (the `SpaceSurface` type + the `spaceSurfacesFor` resolver in lib/admin/entities/registry.ts) that P0 declared alongside it had already stopped driving any render path: a grep of `lib/ components/ app/` for the symbols returned only the registry's own DEFINITIONS and its unit test. The rest of the earlier "consumers" (`hrefForSurface`, the Mode next-best-actions in `lib/spaces/modes.ts`, `rail-bank`, `surface-panels`, `module-map`, the `lib/apps` catalog) reference plain surface-id STRINGS or stale comments, never the `SPACE_SURFACES` array or the `SpaceSurface` type. So the registry was dead weight kept only by its test.

**Decision.** Delete the SPACE-specific exports from `lib/admin/entities/registry.ts`: the `SpaceSurface` interface, the `SPACE_SURFACES` array, the `spaceSurfaceAppliesToType` helper, and the `spaceSurfacesFor` resolver — plus the now-unused `SpaceType` / `SpaceFunctionKey` imports the space spine needed. The GENERIC core path stays exactly as is: `EntitySurface`, `ENTITY_SURFACES`, `surfacesFor`, `managesEntity`, `ManagedEntity`, and `SPINE_ORDER` still drive the circle/hub/nexus/event/practice consoles (they gate on `Capability`, not a space function, so they are a different system). `hrefForSurface` (lib/spaces/surface-hrefs.ts) is left in place unchanged: it is a self-contained `switch` on surface-id strings — it never imported `SPACE_SURFACES` — and is still the id→href map the /manage console + the bottom bank (`rail-bank.ts`, CRM/Insights/Billing links) call, so the fixed bank and Mode links keep working. `ModeAction.surface` (lib/spaces/modes.ts) is left as-is: the Mode settings view renders the label only (`manage/mode/page.tsx` drops `surface`), so the ids are vestigial hints, not live routes — re-pointing them would be churn with no behavior effect. The registry's own unit test drops the entire `spaceSurfacesFor` describe block (the tier/priority/placement/commerce-independence contract it locked is already covered, on the manifest, by `space-modules.test.ts`). Every remaining textual `SPACE_SURFACES`/`SpaceSurface` mention across the tree was scrubbed to neutral phrasing so a future grep is clean, except the one deliberate retirement note.

**Alternatives / scope.** (1) A thin `SpaceSurface` shim kept for the Mode engine — rejected: nothing actually resolves `ModeAction.surface` to a route, so no consumer needed migrating and no shim is warranted; the field stays as a plain-string hint. (2) Re-pointing the Mode next-best-actions + `hrefForSurface` at module ids / `deepLink` — deferred as unnecessary: `hrefForSurface` is detached from the deleted symbols and still serves the console + bank, and `space.offerings` (the adaptive commerce page) has no single module equivalent, so re-keying it would change what the (already label-only) action would open. (3) Removing the now-unused `panelHrefForSurface` and the `'billing'` `SPINE_ORDER` entry — left alone: out of this phase's scope (symbol retirement) and zero-risk to leave.

**Consequences.** `SPACE_SURFACES`, `spaceSurfacesFor`, and the `SpaceSurface` type are FULLY deleted — no shim remains. The space menu is behavior-identical: the console, rail, bottom bank, `?panel=` bodies, inline editors, and Mode suggestions all render from the same `spaceModuleManifest` they already used, and `hrefForSurface` is byte-identical. No gate weakened (the deletion is pure metadata; `check:authz` unaffected). Tests: `lib/admin/entities/registry.test.ts` now exercises only the core `ENTITY_SURFACES` path; the manifest contract lives in `space-modules.test.ts`. Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest (370 files / 3979 tests), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-548: Modular menu, P5 — the other entity scopes + Account/"You" + global on the one contract (program complete)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-06). No migration. Closes the modular menu program (docs/MODULAR-MENU.md — P5). Consistency + convergence audit; no new capability, no scope's menu behavior changed beyond the intended copy fix.

**Context.** P0–P4 brought SPACE fully onto the `spaceModuleManifest` and retired the duplicate `SPACE_SURFACES` registry. P5's remit: (1) level the OTHER eight non-space scopes (global, circle, hub, nexus, event, practice, channel, journey) up to the same consistent "full" module treatment SPACE now carries, and (2) confirm the personal `account.*`/global set and the whole tree resolve through ONE seam, closing any remaining "retire duplication" loose end. The audit found the program had ALREADY achieved structural convergence across the earlier phases: the eight non-space scopes render from `ADMIN_MODULES` via `modulesForScopeKind`, and `appsForScope` (lib/apps/for-scope.ts) resolves every scope — space via `spaceModuleManifest`, the rest (incl. global's personal "You" set) via the scope-kind match — through that ONE resolver, proven byte-for-byte by the `appsForScope` parity test (`for-scope.test.ts`) for all eight entity kinds plus global. A grep of `lib/admin`/`lib/apps` for the retired `SPACE_SURFACES` / `spaceSurfacesFor` / `SpaceSurface` symbols returned zero live code paths — only a single historical note comment in `space-modules.ts` and the docs — so P4 had fully closed the duplication; no code still branches on them.

**Decision.** The only substantive divergence the audit surfaced was COPY: the five entity `*.settings` module descriptions and the `channel.settings` description carried an em dash (`— edited in place.` / `— platform-curated, staff only.`), a hard CONTENT-VOICE violation, whereas SPACE's module descriptions (space-modules.ts) and the file's own `journey.settings` (`… and publishing. Edited in place.`) were already em-dash-free. Normalize the six to the canon-compliant, SPACE-matching pattern (a period, not an em dash): circle/hub/nexus/event/practice settings end with a separate "Edited in place." sentence, and channel ends "… and visibility. Platform-curated, staff only." Pure member-facing copy fix, no field/gate/order/render touched. The modules that link to a full-page manager (`hub.layout`, `nexus.layout`, `journey.builder`) already carried a clear "Manage its sections from the console" / "in the full-page builder" description, so they were left as-is (already clean). No snapshots added (the remit barred expensive new queries, and the audit found no cheap-single-stat gap worth one). `ENTITY_SURFACES` (the generic capability-gated /manage console path for circle/hub/nexus/event/practice) is LEFT intact: it is a distinct renderer that already reads one clean registry, not a duplicate of the rail's `ADMIN_MODULES`, and deleting it would be a behavior change to the console for no convergence gain — out of the bounded, safe remit.

**Alternatives / scope.** (1) Deleting `ENTITY_SURFACES` "so it is one renderer everywhere" (the doc's original P5 framing) — declined: it is the /manage console's own capability-gated catalog, already single-sourced, and collapsing it into `ADMIN_MODULES` would change the console's section set + risk its tests for a cosmetic "one array" win the rail/console already share at the `appsForScope` seam. (2) Recasing entity nouns (circle/hub/practice) as proper nouns in descriptions — declined: lowercase "your circle" / "this practice" is the established convention across SPACE ("your space", "your team") and the personal set, so recasing would be churn with no canon mandate (only the em dash is a hard rule). (3) Adding a `deepLink` field to `AdminModule` to mirror `SpaceModule` — declined: the entity modules render their manager inline (self-fetching wrappers) and already say where the full page lives in their copy; a new field is capability, not the bounded consistency polish P5 scoped.

**Consequences.** The modular menu program is COMPLETE: one `AdminModule`/`SpaceModule` contract, one `appsForScope` resolver (space on `spaceModuleManifest`, every other scope on `modulesForScopeKind`), and a Module Manager the owner drives, with the eight non-space scopes' copy now on the same em-dash-free canon SPACE carries. No user-visible menu behavior changed beyond the six corrected descriptions; the `appsForScope` parity test still passes for the eight entity scopes; no gate weakened. Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest, check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-549: Space page arranger — client-side content preview, merge-safe save, and the edit-the-real-header model

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-06). No migration. Reworks the arranger's edit / live-preview / save engine + a batch of owner-reported bugs (ADR-542 family).

**Context.** The owner reported the sidebar page arranger felt broken: edits did not show live and saving was slow; filling several Callout fields saved only the title; the About "Section Title" added a header ABOVE instead of editing the block's real title; the white card could not be turned off; the row control was a bare 1/2 toggle; a block moved into a 2-column row stuck half-width; image upload was stuck.

**Decision.**
- **Instant preview, no blanket refresh.** The arranger no longer fires a full `router.refresh()` on every edit. `LiveProfileGrid` renders CONTENT blocks (Callout / Features / Gallery) and every block's STYLE frame CLIENT-SIDE from the shared store, so content + style edits repaint the page the instant they are typed. DATA blocks keep their server node (their live-data body cannot be reproduced client-side); only their authored header/body reconcile through one debounced, data-block-scoped refresh.
- **Merge-safe content save.** The store gained `applyContent`/`applyStyle` that fold each field over a `latest` ref (always the freshest layout) via the immutable ops, replacing the stale render-time `layout` snapshot the builder merged against. This fixes "only the title saved" (a burst of Callout field edits now all persist) and the "stuck upload" (appended image URLs no longer clobbered). Regression tests cover the burst-merge.
- **Edit the block's REAL header.** The render-above `DataQuickHeader` is removed. Every data block exposes `eyebrow` + `title` fields (About/Story also `body`), sanitized allowlist-based; `resolveDataHeader` folds the override over the block default and `space-profile-modules.tsx` threads it into each block's own header, so an edit replaces the rendered eyebrow/title/body rather than stacking a second header.
- **Per-box background on/off.** `BlockStyle.background` is tri-state (`true` force card / `false` strip the self-drawn white card to transparent / absent = block default); a prominent "Background" toggle sits above the Style disclosure and reads true to the page.
- **One 3-way row picker.** The `[1][2]` + separate ratio control becomes a single Full / 50-50 / Main-Side picker; choosing Full merges the row's columns back to one (nothing lost), which also unsticks a half-width block.
- **Image gallery + upload** verified end to end (gated `uploadSpaceBlockImage` → `event-media` bucket → public URL; the upload button shows progress and appends). **Spacing:** a right gutter on the live-preview so the page does not crowd the rail.

**Consequences.** The arranger edits feel instant and save reliably, every offered block edits its real content, and the row/background/gallery controls behave. DEFERRED: a deeper rail-load speedup (item 9) needs merging the builder + panel getters into one payload (`manage/rail-getters.ts`) — a follow-up. Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest (3987), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-550: One-resolve Space rail bundle — kill the ~5 duplicate heavy resolves behind the slow sidebar

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-06). No migration. Delivers the rail-load speedup ADR-549 deferred as "item 9". Read-only refactor; no gate weakened, no menu behavior changed.

**Context.** The owner reported "loading the sidebar takes forever" on a Space profile root. The standardized admin rail mounts several Space modules that EACH self-fetch through their OWN `'use server'` action on mount — `getSpaceBasicsData`, `getSpaceBrandingData`, `getSpaceSettingsData`, `getSpacePageData`, and (via the page module's `SpacePageBuilder`) `getSpaceLayoutRailData`. Because every server action is a SEPARATE request, React's per-request `cache()` never dedupes across them, so each one repeats the whole heavy chain: `getCallerProfile` → `getVisibleSpaceBySlug` → `resolveSpaceManageAccess` → `getSpaceCapabilities` → `readProfileExtras`/`readProfileData`. That is ~5 modules × the same resolve = the slow rail.

**Decision.** Resolve the chain ONCE for the whole Space rail and distribute the result.
- **One bundled getter.** `getSpaceRailBundle(slug)` (app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/manage/rail-getters.ts) runs the resolve a single time, self-gates identically (returns null for a non-manager / non-staff viewer, the fail-safe), then assembles every per-module slice `{ basics, branding, settings, page, layout }`. Each existing getter's assembly body was refactored into a pure, synchronous `buildXData(space, caps, staffViewing, extras, …)` helper that BOTH the individual getter AND the bundle call, so there is zero behavior drift and the standalone getters keep working for isolation.
- **A client provider that fetches once + distributes.** `SpaceRailDataProvider` (components/admin/modules/space-rail-data.tsx) is mounted at the ONE point the rail body renders every module (`AdminBarBody`); on a `/spaces/<slug>` route it calls `getSpaceRailBundle(slug)` once and holds it in context. Each Space module reads its slice via `useSpaceRailSlice(slug, select, fallback)`. CRUCIAL isolation fallback: with no provider (a module mounted outside the rail) or an errored bundle, the hook self-fetches through the module's own getter exactly as before, so every module still works standalone.
- **Builder seed prop.** `EntityPageBuilder`/`SpacePageBuilder` accept an optional pre-fetched `seed` (rows/hidden/matchId/customized/lockedIds) and skip their own `getSpaceLayoutRailData` fetch when it is supplied; the Page module feeds `seed` from the same bundle. `undefined` keeps the self-fetch path (member builder + any standalone mount); an explicit `null` means "the viewer cannot edit" so the builder renders nothing without a round-trip.

**Alternatives / scope.** (1) Wrapping each getter in `React.cache()` — declined: cache is per-request, and these are five separate client-invoked server actions, so it cannot dedupe across them. (2) Collapsing the modules into one server component — declined: the rail is a client-composed flat section list (ADR-514); a provider + slice hook keeps every module independently mountable and testable. The member profile builder (a different path, `getMemberLayoutRailData`) and the modular-menu system are untouched.

**Consequences.** The Space rail now does ONE heavy resolve per open instead of ~5 — the modules render from the shared bundle, and the builder no longer adds its own layout fetch. Behavior is identical: every module shows the same data, same read-only gating, same fail-safe-null for non-managers; every write action still self-gates; `check:authz` unaffected (the bundle re-gates through `getCallerProfile` + `resolveSpaceManageAccess` exactly like the getters). Tests: `rail-getters.bundle.test.ts` pins the gate (null for non-manager / missing space) and that each bundle slice deep-equals its standalone getter (the no-drift contract). Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest (371 files / 3991 tests), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-551: Space arranger follow-ups — reliable data-block reconcile, a robust background strip, and the team card

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-06). No migration. A second round of owner-reported arranger bugs on top of ADR-549.

**Context.** Three defects survived ADR-549: (1) authoring a DATA block's title (e.g. Business presence → "Find me online") appeared to do nothing, then "worked after a LONG time" on a retry, with no working indicator; (2) the per-box Background on/off toggle did nothing for data sections; (3) the Team card had regressed to a bare avatar with no name.

**Decision.**
- **Data-block reconcile chained to the save, not a timer.** A DATA block's header/body is server-rendered from the space's live data, so an authored eyebrow/title/body edit cannot repaint purely client-side — it needs a `router.refresh()`. ADR-549 fired that refresh on a fixed 900ms timer, which RACED the ~600ms debounced save: when the refresh landed first the server re-read the OLD content and nothing changed until a later edit won the race. Now the builder marks the data edit dirty and reconciles the instant the store's `saving` flag falls true→false (the save persisted), so the refresh never reads stale. A `reconciling` flag holds the "Saving" indicator up from the keystroke through the reconciled paint. CONTENT + STYLE edits still repaint instantly from the shared store with no round-trip (ADR-542).
- **Background strip by class, at any depth.** The tri-state Background toggle flattens a section onto the page canvas. The old strip used a direct-child selector (`[&>*]`), but a data section nests its white card INSIDE a `ModuleSection` `<section>`, so the strip only hit the empty wrapper and did nothing. Replaced with an unlayered `.entity-bg-strip [class*='bg-surface']` rule (globals.css) that flattens every `bg-surface`/`bg-surface-elevated` card nested at ANY depth to transparent (no border, no shadow); `BlockStyleFrame` adds the class when `background:false`. Reliable across every block regardless of nesting.
- **Team card shows the full name.** The member card's name wrapper lacked `flex-1`, so with `min-w-0` + `truncate` the name collapsed to nothing in a narrow (Main/Side) column. The wrapper is now `flex-1 min-w-0` and the name wraps (`break-words`, no truncate), so the card is a rectangle that always shows who the person is.

**Consequences.** Presentation + client-store timing only — no schema, no server actions, no gate weakened. Gate green: `tsc`, eslint, vitest (entity-blocks), check:canon, check:authz.

## ADR-552: Two programs, one simple system — collapse spaces to Business / Non Profit, usage-metered

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-06). Approved owner direction. Finalizes the space model into two
types and two paid programs. Full plan + phased build: `docs/BUSINESS-MODEL-PLAN.md`. Ships
behind `billing_live = OFF`; nothing charges until an operator flips it.

**Context.** The space model accumulated two orthogonal ladders — 8 `spaces.type` values and a
5+ plan tier stack (`free/pro/business/nonprofit/organization` + whitelabel + an add-on loadout)
— on top of a system that ADR-517/519 had already made functionally universal (every tool on for
every space; monetize usage, never access). The result was a lot of surface for a model whose
intent is now simple: one page free, every tool on but capped, pay to use more. The owner directed
a strip-down to the simplest thing that can grow later.

**Decision.**
- **Two types:** `business` and `nonprofit` (plus the hidden platform `root`). Every removed type
  (practitioner, coaching, organization→nonprofit, event_space, lab, partner) folds into a free
  **Focus** preset under Business (or the nonprofit set). Focus stays free framing — starter
  layout, CRM pipeline seed, lexicon — and never gates.
- **Two programs:** `business` and `nonprofit`, plus `free`. Free-vs-paid is **not a separate plan
  name** — it is a usage state within Business. You stay "Business" and pay as you use more + per
  seat. This retires `pro`, `organization`, and `whitelabel` as tiers (archived, never deleted, so
  grandfathered subscriptions still resolve).
- **Naming:** "Business" and "Non Profit" are each simultaneously the type and the plan — one word,
  no third vocabulary. Recorded in `docs/NAMING.md`.
- **Paywall = usage + seats + take-rate**, never a feature lock. Free is a genuine taste with caps
  that make upgrading a no-brainer at the moment of success (see the free allowance table + the
  "you'd have saved $X" take-rate math in the plan doc).
- **Defaults chosen (reversible):** `organization` DB value renamed to `nonprofit`; free take-rate
  5% / Business 3%; a small daily Vera AI taste on free; Focus presets kept.

**Alternatives.** (1) Keep the 4-tier ladder (ADR-472) — rejected: more surface than the intent
needs, and it reintroduces the tier vocabulary the owner wants gone. (2) Scale Business price by
unlocking features (per-feature tiers) — rejected: contradicts ADR-519's "all features available,
never show a lock" and reintroduces paywalls; the usage-meter engine already models the right thing
(pay for more, not for access). (3) Keep all 8 types, only collapse the plans — rejected: the type
sprawl is most of the admin/wizard complexity, and every capability is already universal, so the
types earn nothing but a starter preset, which Focus already provides.

**Consequences.** A large but overwhelmingly mechanical collapse: type/label edits + a plan-catalog
prune + one genuine logic move (the `entity-cta.tsx` transactional fork re-bases from `type` onto
`mode_variant`). It also unlocks a round of admin simplification (one menu+features surface, delete
~9 dead settings routes, a one-field create wizard). Executed as the phased build in
`docs/BUSINESS-MODEL-PLAN.md` (Phase 0 docs → 1 types → 2 plans → 3 usage numbers → 4 ease-of-use →
5 profile polish → 6 copy/go-live). No behavior changes for a live space until `billing_live` is
flipped; every retired catalog item is archived so existing subscriptions keep resolving.

## ADR-553: The admin menu contract is locked and machine-enforced

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-06). Full spec: `docs/MENU-CONTRACT.md`. Closes the admin-menu
standardization (the menu-consolidation #1572 + cleanup Phases B/C). No behavior change from this
ADR itself; it adds the guardrail.

**Context.** The operator admin menu + rail had drifted across iterations: a rail that was already
unified (appsForScope over one App catalog) but fed by parallel source registries, a `/manage`
console that was unified only for Space while core entities rendered from a thin legacy
`ENTITY_SURFACES`, and a scatter of dead/duplicated menu code. The owner asked for one system used
the same way for every scope AND a way to keep it from being accidentally overwritten (including by
an AI agent) while still allowing small tweaks and further development.

**Decision.** Standardize on ONE source of truth and enforce it in CI.
- **One catalog set.** `SPACE_MODULES`, `ADMIN_MODULES`, `LAYOUT_MODULES` are the only module
  catalogs. Every surface derives from them: the rail via `appsForScope`, the Space console via
  `resolveSpaceMenu`, the entity consoles via `resolveEntityConsole` (new, wrapping the same
  `appsForScope`) rendered by the shared `EntityManageConsole`. `ENTITY_SURFACES` retired (Phase C).
- **Machine-enforced lock.** `pnpm check:menu` (`scripts/check-menu.mjs`, a new CI step) fails a PR
  that hand-rolls a parallel `*_MODULES` catalog or reintroduces a retired parallel registry
  (`SPACE_SURFACES`/`ENTITY_SURFACES`); escape hatch `// menu-ok: <reason>` or an allowlisted file
  with a reason. Runtime drift-guard tests (`space-menu.test.ts`, `entity-console.test.ts`) assert
  the console and rail resolve the identical module set per scope. An `AGENTS.md` rule points every
  agent at the contract.
- **Extension model.** A menu tweak = editing a catalog row (label/icon/order/gate/group). Adding a
  scope = registering modules in `ADMIN_MODULES`. Neither touches the renderers. This is why "little
  tweaks" are safe by construction and the guard blocks turning a tweak into a rewrite.

**Alternatives.** (1) Merge the two gate systems (Capability vs SpaceFunctionKey) / fully unify the
console+rail into one renderer — declined for now: Space's function-gating and its panel-style
console vs the entities' inline-editor console are real differences; forcing one renderer risked the
proven surfaces. The contract locks the SHARED source + resolvers; per-surface presentation stays.
(2) CODEOWNERS path protection — not available (solo-repo template). The CI gate + tests are the
load-bearing lock.

**Consequences.** The admin menu is now one system every scope resolves through, with the leftovers
gone and the standard enforced by CI, so it can be developed further (add catalog rows) without
being overwritten. Gate green: tsc, eslint, vitest (+ check:menu + the two drift guards), check:canon,
check:authz. `docs/MENU-CONTRACT.md` is the reference; `AGENTS.md` carries the one-line rule.

## ADR-560: Per-Space contact at-risk / churn scoring (the win-back gap)

**Status:** Accepted (2026-07-06). Migration file-only until applied via the Supabase SQL editor.

**Context.** The platform already scores at-risk in three places, none of them the space CONTACT:
a member's practice-streak `atRisk` (`lib/practice-streak.ts`), re-activation playbook markers
(`lib/spaces/ai-usage.ts` `REACTIVATION_MARKERS`), and the per-PROFILE `member_engagement_scores`
churn_risk matview for the platform cockpit (ADR-383). A Space owner looking at their CRM board had
no signal for which of THEIR contacts were going cold, and no win-back hook off it.

**Decision.** Add a per-Space contact churn signal on the contact itself, a pure scorer, and a
cockpit surface with a manual win-back trigger.
- **Shape (lowest-risk).** Three PROJECTION columns on `public.contacts` mirroring the existing
  `engagement_score numeric` column: `risk_score numeric [0,100]`, `at_risk boolean`, `risk_factors
  jsonb`, plus a partial `(space_id, risk_score desc) where at_risk` index
  (`20261017000000_contact_at_risk.sql`). A side table / matview was rejected (ADR-560 §3): it would
  duplicate the space_id/RLS surface for no v1 benefit, since the contact row already carries every
  raw signal the v1 rules need. A `not valid` [0,100] check guards writes without an apply-time scan.
- **RLS.** `contacts` already carries the canonical per-Space policies (ADR, 20260905000000: read via
  `is_space_member(space_id)` or staff, writes via `can_write_space_content(space_id)`). RLS gates the
  ROW, so the new columns inherit those policies with NO new policy; the migration re-asserts the
  enable + read policy idempotently. A cross-space contract test
  (`test/contract/tenancy-entity-modules.test.ts`) proves the new cockpit read binds `space_id` on the
  RLS-bypassing admin path (filter-presence + a two-space leak oracle).
- **Scoring (rules first, ML seam).** `lib/spaces/contact-risk.ts` is a PURE, unit-tested rules-based
  scorer: recency of last contact/activity (ramped, saturating), engagement decay, unsubscribe,
  payment-overdue/dunning, no-show, and the streak-at-risk signal, producing a `[0,100]` score, an
  `atRisk` flag (threshold 50), and the contributing factors. Fail-safe + no risk-by-absence: a signal
  we do not have contributes nothing. `scoreContactRisk(signals)` takes a plain struct, so a future
  model can replace or blend with no caller change.
- **Surface.** `lib/spaces/crm-funnel.ts` gains an `atRisk` summary (count + worst offenders) derived
  LIVE by the scorer over the raw contact signals, so it works before any writer persists into
  `risk_score`. The CRM funnel panel renders a "Going cold" list with the factors + a "Win back"
  button that runs the existing value-led `reengage_winback` play through the already-governed
  `runSpacePlaybookAction` (suggest-by-default, circuit-breaker, autonomy gate all intact).

**Deferrals.** (1) No writer/cron persists `risk_score`/`at_risk` yet — the cockpit derives live; the
columns are the durable seam for a batch writer or ML model. (2) `lib/database.types.ts` NOT
regenerated (no live-DB access in this workflow); the new columns are read UNTYPED per ADR-246, as
`contacts` already is in this module. (3) The win-back is the MANUAL leg; enrolling an at-risk contact
into a win-back SEQUENCE automatically lands with the automation work, with no change to the button.

**Consequences.** Space owners see which contacts are going cold and can act in one click. Gate green:
tsc, eslint, vitest (+ the new scorer/summary/contract suites), check:authz, check:rls.
## ADR-561: Space automation — scheduled campaign send job + per-Space rule/drip UI (R4/R5)

**Context.** Business-accounts Automation had per-Space campaigns that were composable + schedulable
(`campaigns.scheduled_for`) and a `crm.space.automation` entitlement gate, but no job actually FIRED a
scheduled campaign, and there was no operator surface to build automation rules or drip sequences over a
Space's own contacts. The root `automation_rules` + `nurture_sequences` tables are platform-scoped
(engagement events / per-persona), not per-Space.

**Decision.**
- **R4 — scheduled send job.** A Vercel Cron route `/api/cron/space-campaigns` (every 5 min, guarded by
  `CRON_SECRET`, following the existing `publish-scheduled`/`nurture` cron pattern) drives a pure
  `sendDueCampaigns()` (`lib/spaces/campaigns-send-due.ts`). Idempotency is a CLAIM: for each due
  campaign a conditional update flips `status 'scheduled' -> 'sending'` while re-asserting
  `status='scheduled'` in the WHERE, so exactly one overlapping run wins and sends; the loser sees no
  due row. The winner resolves the campaign's stored `audience_filter` over the Space's OWN contacts and
  delivers through a NEW session-less seam `sendSpaceCampaignSystem` (extracted from `sendSpaceCampaign`
  by lifting the post-authz body into `deliverSpaceCampaign`), which re-runs every anti-spam gate
  (email-function, kill-switch, daily cap, consent + suppression, per-recipient unsubscribe, the
  `outreach_sends` ledger). `scheduleSpaceCampaign` now persists the chosen audience + requires a
  subject/body. New column `campaigns.audience_filter jsonb` + a partial due-index; `'sending'` is a new
  transient status value (status is free-text, no enum change).
- **R5 — automation rule + drip UI.** Three new Space-scoped, service-role-only tables
  (`space_automation_rules`, `space_drip_sequences`, `space_drip_steps`) with RLS enabled + NO policies,
  reached only through gated server actions (`lib/spaces/automation.ts` + `automation-actions.ts`,
  mirroring `lib/spaces/segments.ts`): every read/write filters `space_id`, every write gates on
  `canEditProfile`. The operator surface is a Focus page at `/spaces/<slug>/settings/automation`
  (rules panel + drip-sequence editor), self-gated on the `crm.space.automation` entitlement (an upgrade
  notice when the plan lacks it). It is mounted by ADDING ONE ROW to `SPACE_MODULES` (`space.automation`,
  a `link` module in the reach family riding the `crm` feature gate), honoring the locked
  MENU-CONTRACT — no hand-rolled menu, `pnpm check:menu` + drift guards stay green.

**Alternatives.** (1) Reuse `nurture_sequences` for drips — declined: it is per-persona + platform
service-role, not per-Space; bending it would break its tenancy model. (2) A new `automation`
SpaceFunctionKey for the menu gate — declined: automation is a CRM amplifier gated on an entitlement,
not a function; the catalog row rides the existing `crm` gate and the surface enforces the real
entitlement. (3) A DB advisory lock / queue table for idempotency — the conditional-claim UPDATE is
simpler, needs no new infra, and Postgres already serializes the racing updates.

**Consequences.** Scheduled Space campaigns now actually send (exactly once), and operators can author
rules + drip sequences. The rule/drip RUNNER (firing rules off Space CRM events, dripping sequence steps
on a schedule) is a follow-on: the surface persists + edits them now, but nothing yet enrolls contacts or
fires the steps. New cron wiring in `vercel.json`. Contract test in
`test/contract/tenancy-entity-modules.test.ts` locks the `space_id` binding on every automation
read/write; unit tests cover the pure validators, permission gating, and the idempotent claim. Gate
green: tsc, eslint, vitest (4015), check:menu, check:authz.

---

## ADR-562: Optional per-row headers on module-driven pages

**Status:** Accepted · corroborated by `lib/page-settings/layout.ts` (`SlotConfig.header` /
`headerEnabled`, `resolveSlotHeaders`), `components/widgets/page-modules.tsx` (renders the header),
`components/admin/page-settings/layout-editor.tsx` (the per-row field + toggle).

**Context.** The interior page-layout engine (ADR-270/271/272) lays a route's modules into a
template's AREAS (slots / rows). A row had no way to carry a heading, so operators could not label a
section of the page (e.g. a "Featured" row over a stack of blocks). We wanted an owner-toggleable
row header that renders on the public page, defaulting OFF so every existing page is unchanged.

**Decision.** Extend the EXISTING per-slot layout jsonb, no new table. Each slot gains two optional
fields on `page_settings.layout.slots[slotId]`:
- `header?: string` — the heading text, trimmed + bounded to `ROW_HEADER_MAX` (80).
- `headerEnabled?: boolean` — persisted only as `true`; absent/false = the header-less default.

A header renders only when `headerEnabled === true` AND the text is non-empty (`slotHasHeader`),
and only above a row that has visible content (`resolveSlotHeaders` feeds the renderer, which skips
empty rows). The public render uses the kit's `SectionHeader` primitive (semantic `<h2>`, token
classes, no hardcoded hex). The arranger exposes a text field + on/off toggle per row, saved through
the existing `savePageLayout` action (which iterates the CONSTANT template slots, so a crafted slot
key can't inject a property — same hardening as the module/role write).

**Alternatives.** (1) A new `page_row_headers` table — declined: the header is a property of a slot
that already lives in the layout jsonb; a table would fragment one config across two stores and need
its own RLS + cascade. (2) Storing the header as a pseudo-module — declined: it is row chrome, not a
block, and would pollute the module set + role gates. (3) Rendering the header even over an empty
row — declined: a lone heading with no content reads as a bug.

**Consequences.** Zero migration (jsonb-carried); existing pages render identically (default OFF).
`hasLayoutConfig` now counts an active header, so a page whose ONLY customization is a row header
still resolves as a saved config (not the coded default). The header is independent of per-module
role gates — it labels the ROW, so it shows whenever the operator turned it on, even if individual
blocks in the row are gated. Copy passes NAMING + CONTENT-VOICE (no em dashes). Gate green: tsc +
eslint clean on the changed files, vitest (row-header suite added to `layout.test.ts`), check:authz,
check:menu, check:rls.

## ADR-563: Owner-editable Space header CTA (the one dominant hero button)

**Context.** The Space profile hero's single primary CTA was fixed: its label came from the per-type
default (`defaultPrimaryCtaLabel` in `lib/spaces/profile-config.ts`, e.g. "Become a member") and its href
was hard-wired to the reserved `/book` transactional surface. An operator could not retitle the button or
point it anywhere else, even though several of their own surfaces already exist (contact, offerings,
booking).

**Decision.**
- **A new PURE module `lib/spaces/header-cta.ts`** owns the CTA override: a closed set of IN-HOUSE
  functions (`book` / `contact` / `tickets` / `donate` / `join` / `offerings`), each resolving to a
  surface that ALREADY exists — `#contact` and `#offerings` jump to the profile Home section anchors
  (`SpaceContact` / `SpaceOfferings` blocks render as `<section id="…">`, per `section-anchors.ts`); the
  rest open the type-branched `/book` surface. A CUSTOM override stores an operator URL + label instead.
  The module carries a tolerant normalizer (`readHeaderCtaPreference`, drops an unknown function key, a
  blank label, or an unsafe URL), a strict URL guard (`isValidCtaUrl`: only absolute http(s) or a
  same-origin `/path`, never `javascript:` or protocol-relative), a total resolver (`resolveHeaderCta`,
  always a label + href, external flag for a custom off-site link), and a non-destructive merge
  (`nextHeaderCtaPreferences`, `null` clears the override).
- **Persistence reuses `spaces.preferences` jsonb** under a new top-level `headerCta` node (alongside
  `coverScrim` / `moduleMenu`), so NO new column and NO SQL. The write is a new server action
  `setSpaceHeaderCta` in `app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/manage/layout/actions.ts`, re-gated through the same
  `authorizeEditor` + `writePreferences` seam as `setSpaceCoverScrim` (so `check:authz` stays green) and
  re-validating the override server-side.
- **The control lives in the Identity and Branding module** (`components/spaces/space-branding-form.tsx`),
  BETWEEN "Cover style" and "Theme accent": a three-way mode picker (Default / Built in / Custom link),
  the in-house function grid + an optional label input, or a custom URL + label, saved on a "Save button".
  Data flows through `buildBrandingData` (`rail-getters.ts`) and the `SpaceBrandingModule` wrapper. This
  is a control INSIDE an existing module's render, not a new menu item, so the MENU-CONTRACT is untouched.
- **The header renders the resolved CTA** (`app/(main)/spaces/[slug]/(profile)/layout.tsx`): both the
  desktop action row and the mobile band read `resolveHeaderCta(readHeaderCtaPreference(preferences), …)`,
  falling back to the per-type default when unset. A custom off-site URL opens in a new tab with safe rel.
- **Spacing (unrelated polish shipped together).** The gap between the Space admin menu and the first
  content block is DOUBLED: `PageAdminBar` self-suppressed its divider on Space profiles (owner directive:
  no rule under the menu), so it now returns a rule-less token spacer (`h-10 sm:h-12`, 2x the standard
  `mb-5 sm:mb-6` divider gap) instead of `null` — token spacing only, no hardcoded values.

**Alternatives.** (1) A new `spaces.header_cta` column — declined: `preferences` is the existing override
blob for exactly this class of framing choice, and ADR-246 keeps it untyped. (2) Map "Contact" to
`startConversation` / a DM — declined: that action requires an accepted friendship, so it is not a valid
public CTA; the `#contact` section anchor is the in-house, public, no-relationship-required surface. (3) A
`/spaces/<slug>/offerings` route — it does not exist; the offerings surface is the `#offerings` Home anchor.

**Consequences.** Owners can retitle the hero button and point it at any of their own surfaces or a custom
link; the default is unchanged when they set nothing. Copy passes NAMING + CONTENT-VOICE (plain verb
phrases, sentence case, no em dashes). Unit tests cover the normalizer, URL guard, resolver, href map, and
merge (`lib/spaces/header-cta.test.ts`). Gate green: tsc, eslint, vitest (4057), check:menu, check:authz,
check:rls, build.

## ADR-564: Space automation RUNNER — enrollment ledger, trigger dispatch, drip fire job (ADR-561 follow-on)

**Context.** ADR-561 shipped the automation SURFACE (rules + drip sequences an owner defines) but no
RUNNER: nothing enrolled a contact on a trigger or fired a drip step on schedule. The root nurture
runner (`lib/nurture/runner.ts` + `/api/cron/nurture`) and the R4 campaign send job are the exact
patterns to mirror, per-Space.

**Decision.**
- **Enrollment model.** One new Space-scoped, service-role-only table `space_drip_enrollments`
  (`20261021000000_space_drip_enrollments.sql`): `(space_id, sequence_id, contact_id, email,
  current_step, next_run_at, status enrolled|sending|done|stopped, last_sent_at, timestamps)`, RLS
  enabled + NO policies (added to `scripts/rls-deny-all.txt`; the ADR-561 tables were also added there,
  fixing a `check:rls` miss in the surface PR). Unique `(sequence_id, contact_id)` makes a re-fired
  trigger a no-op; a partial due-index on `(status, next_run_at) where status in ('enrolled','sending')`
  keeps the cron hot path small. Enrolling = insert at the sequence's first ENABLED step with
  `next_run_at = now() + step0.delay_hours` (`enrollContactInSequence`, `lib/spaces/drip-enroll.ts`),
  re-reading BOTH the sequence AND the contact pinned to `space_id` so a cross-space id enrolls nothing.
